- tests/fixtures/salary-small.csv: not an included source/documentation extension or secret-like filename

### Safety Rules

- `.env*`, credential, secret, private-key, Firebase admin SDK, and service-account style files are excluded.
- Dependency folders, build output, reports, generated exports, logs, lockfiles, common media, and large files are excluded.
- Known API keys, tokens, private keys, and database URL passwords are redacted before writing this export.

## Code Files

31 | AI_MAX_TOKENS=[REDACTED_SECRET]

41 | ML_ANALYTICS_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES=500
42 | MAX_JSON_BODY_BYTES=20971520
43 | 
44 | # Security (Production)

46 | # RATE_LIMIT_MAX=100
47 | # RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS=60000

### FILE: .github/workflows/ci.yml

Source path: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

```yaml
 1 | name: CI
 2 | 
 3 | on:
 4 |   push:
 5 |     branches: [main, develop, gen-ai-working]
 6 |   pull_request:
 7 |     branches: [main, develop, gen-ai-working]
 8 | 
 9 | jobs:
10 |   lint-and-test:
11 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
12 |     timeout-minutes: 15
13 | 
14 |     steps:
15 |       - name: Checkout code
16 |         uses: actions/checkout@v4
17 | 
18 |       - name: Setup Node.js
19 |         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
20 |         with:
21 |           node-version: "20"
22 |           cache: "npm"
23 | 
24 |       - name: Install dependencies
25 |         run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
26 | 
27 |       - name: Lint frontend
28 |         run: npm run lint
29 |         working-directory: apps/frontend
30 |         continue-on-error: true
31 | 
32 |       - name: Type check frontend
33 |         run: npx tsc --noEmit
34 |         working-directory: apps/frontend
35 |         continue-on-error: true
36 | 
37 |       - name: Run tests
38 |         run: npm run test
39 |         working-directory: apps/frontend
40 |         continue-on-error: true
41 | 
42 |       - name: Build frontend
43 |         run: npm run build
44 |         working-directory: apps/frontend
45 | 
46 |   build-verify:
47 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
48 |     needs: lint-and-test
49 |     timeout-minutes: 10
50 | 
51 |     steps:
52 |       - name: Checkout code
53 |         uses: actions/checkout@v4
54 | 
55 |       - name: Setup Node.js
56 |         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
57 |         with:
58 |           node-version: "20"
59 |           cache: "npm"
60 | 
61 |       - name: Install dependencies
62 |         run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
63 | 
64 |       - name: Verify full build
65 |         run: npm run build
66 | 
67 |   security-scan:
68 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
69 |     needs: lint-and-test
70 | 
71 |     steps:
72 |       - name: Checkout code
73 |         uses: actions/checkout@v4
74 | 
75 |       - name: Setup Node.js
76 |         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
77 |         with:
78 |           node-version: "20"
79 |           cache: "npm"
80 | 
81 |       - name: Install dependencies
82 |         run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps
83 | 
84 |       - name: Audit dependencies
85 |         run: npm audit --audit-level=high
86 |         continue-on-error: true
```

### FILE: .github/workflows/deploy.yml

Source path: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

```yaml
 1 | name: Deploy
 2 | 
 3 | on:
 4 |   push:
 5 |     branches: [main, gen-ai-working]
 6 |   workflow_dispatch:
 7 | 
 8 | jobs:
 9 |   deploy-frontend:
10 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
11 |     if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/gen-ai-working'
12 | 
13 |     steps:
14 |       - name: Checkout code
15 |         uses: actions/checkout@v4
16 | 
17 |       - name: Setup Node.js
18 |         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
19 |         with:
20 |           node-version: "20"
21 |           cache: "npm"
22 | 
23 |       - name: Install dependencies
24 |         run: npm ci
25 | 
26 |       - name: Build frontend
27 |         run: npm run build
28 | 
29 |       - name: Deploy to Vercel
30 |         uses: amondnet/vercel-action@v25
31 |         with:
32 |           vercel-token: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
33 |           vercel-org-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
34 |           vercel-project-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_PROJECT_ID }}
35 |           vercel-args: "--prod"
36 | 
37 |   deploy-backend:
38 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
39 |     if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/gen-ai-working'
40 | 
41 |     steps:
42 |       - name: Checkout code
43 |         uses: actions/checkout@v4
44 | 
45 |       - name: Setup Node.js
46 |         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
47 |         with:
48 |           node-version: "20"
49 |           cache: "npm"
50 | 
51 |       - name: Install dependencies
52 |         run: npm ci
53 |         working-directory: apps/backend
54 | 
55 |       - name: Deploy to Vercel
56 |         uses: amondnet/vercel-action@v25
57 |         with:
58 |           vercel-token: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_TOKEN }}
59 |           vercel-org-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_ORG_ID }}
60 |           vercel-project-id: ${{ secrets.VERCEL_BACKEND_PROJECT_ID }}
61 |           vercel-args: "--prod"
62 |           working-directory: apps/backend
```

 96 | ## 🔧 Architecture Overview
 97 | 
 98 | ### Backend Stack

100 | ```
101 | apps/backend/
102 | ├── src/
103 | │   ├── core/server.js              # HTTP server setup
104 | │   ├── routes/                     # 18+ route handlers
105 | │   │   ├── index.js               # Main router
106 | │   │   ├── datasets.js            # Dataset CRUD
107 | │   │   ├── chat.js                # Chat endpoints
108 | │   │   ├── analytics.js           # Analytics queries
109 | │   │   ├── ai.js                  # AI provider routes
110 | │   │   ├── machine-learning.js    # ML training/predict
111 | │   │   ├── agentic-models.js      # Model selection
112 | │   │   └── ... (13 more)
113 | │   ├── services/                   # Business logic
114 | │   │   ├── analytics-service.js
115 | │   │   ├── ai-analyst/           # Agentic AI services
116 | │   │   │   ├── schema-trained-ai-service.js
117 | │   │   │   ├── dashboard-plan-engine.js
118 | │   │   │   ├── llm-schema-dashboard-planner.js
119 | │   │   │   └── schema-fingerprint.js
120 | │   │   └── ... (10+ more)
121 | │   ├── middleware/                 # Request/response handlers
122 | │   │   ├── cors.js
123 | │   │   ├── error-handler.js
124 | │   │   └── request-logger.js

127 | │   │   ├── helpers.js
128 | │   │   └── logger.js

132 | │   │   └── constants.js
133 | │   └── database/
134 | │       └── dataset-repository.js   # SQLite persistence
135 | └── package.json
136 | ```

162 | ---
163 | 
164 | ## 🚀 Key Features & Implementations
165 | 
166 | ### 1. **Schema-First AI Analysis**
167 | 
168 | - **No raw data sent to APIs** - only schema metadata
169 | - Gemini AI for intent detection and SQL generation
170 | - Local SQL execution for safety and privacy
171 | - Fallback to local analysis if API unavailable
172 | 
173 | ### 2. **Agentic AI System**
174 | 
175 | - Dynamic model routing based on API key availability
176 | - Gemini (Google) as primary
177 | - Ollama support for local models
178 | - Cascading fallback chain
179 | - Model selection configuration
180 | 
181 | ### 3. **Machine Learning**
182 | 
183 | - AutoGluon integration for automated ML
184 | - Local model training and prediction
185 | - Feature importance analysis
186 | - Model persistence and caching
187 | 
188 | ### 4. **Data Pipeline**
189 | 
190 | - CSV/Excel import with validation
191 | - Automatic schema detection
192 | - Data quality profiling
193 | - Anomaly detection using Z-score
194 | - Data cleaning suggestions
195 | 
196 | ### 5. **Persistence Layer**
197 | 
198 | - SQLite for local data storage
199 | - Chat history preservation
200 | - Cache system (global + per-dataset)
201 | - Repository pattern for data access
202 | 
203 | ### 6. **Frontend UI**
204 | 
205 | - React 18 with TypeScript
206 | - TailwindCSS for styling
207 | - Radix UI components
208 | - Recharts for visualizations
209 | - TanStack Query for data fetching
210 | 
211 | ---
212 | 
213 | ## 📋 Verified Endpoints (40+)
214 | 
215 | ### State Management
216 | 
217 | - `GET /api/state` - Returns dataset, messages, analysis
218 | 
219 | ### Dataset Operations
220 | 
221 | - `POST /api/datasets/demo` - Load demo sales data
222 | - `POST /api/datasets/import` - Import CSV/Excel
223 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id` - Get dataset info
224 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/schema` - Get schema
225 | - `PATCH /api/datasets/:id/rows/:rowId` - Update row
226 | - `DELETE /api/datasets/:id` - Delete dataset
227 | 
228 | ### Chat
229 | 
230 | - `POST /api/datasets/:id/chat` - Send query
231 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/chat/history` - Get history
232 | - `DELETE /api/datasets/:id/chat/history` - Clear history
233 | 
234 | ### Analytics
235 | 
236 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai-correlations` - Pearson correlations
237 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai/profile` - Data profiling
238 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai/anomalies` - Anomaly detection
239 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai/relationships` - Column relationships
240 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai/cleaning` - Cleaning suggestions
241 | 
242 | ### ML/AutoML
243 | 
244 | - `POST /api/ml/train` - Train model
245 | - `POST /api/ml/predict` - Make prediction
246 | - `GET /api/ml/feature-importance` - Feature analysis
247 | - `GET /api/ml/health` - ML service status
248 | 
249 | ### AI Services
250 | 
251 | - `POST /api/ai/test` - Test AI generation
252 | - `GET /api/ai/status` - AI provider status
253 | - `GET /api/cascade/status` - Cascade chain status
254 | 
255 | ### Export
256 | 
257 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/export/json` - JSON export
258 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/export/csv` - CSV export
259 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/export/md` - Markdown export
260 | 
261 | ---
262 | 
263 | ## 🔐 Security Features
264 | 
265 | ✅ **CORS Protection** - Configured origin/methods/headers  
266 | ✅ **Schema Validation** - All inputs validated  
267 | ✅ **Error Handling** - Comprehensive error responses  
268 | ✅ **No Raw Data to AI** - Schema-only metadata sent  
269 | ✅ **Rate Limiting** - Cache prevents abuse  
270 | ✅ **Input Sanitization** - SQL injection prevention
271 |

274 | ## 🛠️ Development Commands

### FILE: AGENTIC_SCHEMA_REFACTORING_PLAN.md

Source path: `AGENTIC_SCHEMA_REFACTORING_PLAN.md`

```md
  1 | # 🤖 AI Agentic Schema-Based Data Analytics Refactoring Plan
  2 | 
  3 | **Status**: Comprehensive Analysis & Implementation Blueprint
  4 | **Version**: 2.0
  5 | **Last Updated**: June 2, 2026
  6 | 
  7 | ---
  8 | 
  9 | ## Executive Summary
 10 | 
 11 | Your codebase has foundational agentic capabilities scattered across multiple services and routes.
 12 | This document consolidates them into a **unified, enterprise-grade AI Agentic Schema-Based Data Analytics Framework**.
 13 | 
 14 | ### Current State
 15 | - ✅ Schema detection systems exist
 16 | - ✅ Multiple AI providers configured (Ollama, Gemini, OpenAI, Claude)
 17 | - ✅ Route handlers for agentic models and schema agents
 18 | - ⚠️ **Critical Gap**: No unified agentic orchestration layer
 19 | - ⚠️ **Critical Gap**: Schema-based agents operate independently
 20 | - ⚠️ **Critical Gap**: No multi-agent collaboration framework
 21 | - ⚠️ **Critical Gap**: Deterministic analytics not fully integrated with agentic reasoning
 22 | 
 23 | ### Target State
 24 | A **multi-layered agentic schema framework** where:
 25 | 1. **Schema Agent** extracts & understands data structure
 26 | 2. **Analytics Agent** plans insights & metrics
 27 | 3. **Tool Router Agent** selects optimal analysis methods
 28 | 4. **Critic Agent** validates dashboard decisions
 29 | 5. **Orchestrator Agent** coordinates multi-agent workflow
 30 | 6. **Memory Agent** learns from patterns across datasets
 31 | 
 32 | ---
 33 | 
 34 | ## Part 1: Current Architecture Analysis
 35 | 
 36 | ### 1.1 Existing Agentic Components
 37 | 
 38 | ```
 39 | BACKEND STRUCTURE:
 40 | ├── routes/
 41 | │   ├── agentic-models.js          ← Model config & health
 42 | │   ├── agentic-data-science.js    ← Python ML gateway
 43 | │   ├── schema-agent.js            ← Schema profiling & dashboard spec
 44 | │   └── schema-trained-ai.routes.js ← Schema-aware chat/analysis
 45 | │
 46 | ├── services/
 47 | │   ├── agentic/
 48 | │   │   ├── ollama-agent-router.js
 49 | │   │   ├── analytics-agent-planner.js
 50 | │   │   ├── analytics-tool-router.js
 51 | │   │   ├── analytics-critic-agent.js
 52 | │   │   └── chief-analyst-orchestrator.js  ← Multi-agent coordinator
 53 | │   │
 54 | │   ├── schema-*
 55 | │   │   ├── schema-detector.js (59 KB - core schema analysis)
 56 | │   │   ├── schema-ai-service.js
 57 | │   │   ├── schema-dashboard-engine.js
 58 | │   │   ├── schema-packet-builder.js
 59 | │   │   └── schema-agent/ (subdirectory with profiler, planner, etc)
 60 | │   │
 61 | │   └── Other Services:
 62 | │       ├── analytics-service.js (33 KB - traditional analytics)
 63 | │       ├── analytics-brain-service.js (26 KB - insights)
 64 | │       └── dashboard-ai-agent.js (11 KB - AI dashboard planning)
 65 | │
 66 | └── config/
 67 |     ├── agentic-models.js  ← Model definitions
 68 |     └── environment.js     ← AI provider config
 69 | ```
 70 | 
 71 | ### 1.2 Problems Identified
 72 | 
 73 | | Problem | Impact | Priority |
 74 | |---------|--------|----------|
 75 | | No unified schema-agentic pipeline | Each component works in isolation | 🔴 CRITICAL |
 76 | | Duplicate schema processing logic | Code redundancy, maintenance overhead | 🟠 HIGH |
 77 | | AI providers not agent-aware | Can't adapt models based on task | 🟠 HIGH |
 78 | | No agentic reasoning memory | Agents don't learn across requests | 🟠 HIGH |
 79 | | Schema ↔ Analytics coupling loose | Hard to trace agentic reasoning | 🟠 HIGH |
 80 | | Frontend doesn't show agentic flow | Users see black boxes | 🟡 MEDIUM |
 81 | | No agent performance metrics | Can't measure agent effectiveness | 🟡 MEDIUM |
 82 | | Tools hardcoded, not dynamic | New analysis types require code changes | 🟡 MEDIUM |
 83 | 
 84 | ---
 85 | 
 86 | ## Part 2: Unified Agentic Schema Framework Architecture
 87 | 
 88 | ### 2.1 Core Framework Design
 89 | 
 90 | ```
 91 | ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 92 | │                    INSIGHTFLOW AGENTIC SYSTEM                        │
 93 | └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 94 | 
 95 | LAYER 1: DATA INGESTION & SCHEMA DETECTION
 96 | ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
 97 | │  Dataset Upload  →  Schema Detector Agent │  ← Identifies structure
 98 | │  (CSV/Excel/PDF)    (Profiling + Types)   │
 99 | └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
100 | 
101 | LAYER 2: SCHEMA NORMALIZATION & MEMORY
102 | ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
103 | │  Schema Normalizer  ↔  Semantic Memory   │  ← Learns similar schemas
104 | │  (Consolidate, Standardize, Index)       │
105 | └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
106 | 
107 | LAYER 3: MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION
108 | ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
109 | │                   Chief Analyst Orchestrator                       │
110 | │       (Coordinates agents, routes decisions, aggregates results)   │
111 | ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
112 | │  ANALYTICS  │  SCHEMA    │  TOOL     │  CRITIC   │  EXECUTION     │
113 | │  AGENT      │  VALIDATOR │  ROUTER   │  AGENT    │  AGENT         │
114 | │  (Plan      │  (Ensures  │  (Selects │ (Validates│  (Computes     │
115 | │  metrics &  │  valid     │  methods) │  quality) │   analytics)   │
116 | │  insights)  │  schema)   │           │           │                │
117 | └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
118 | 
119 | LAYER 4: DETERMINISTIC ANALYTICS ENGINE
120 | ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
121 | │  Query Planner  ← Agentic Plans          │
122 | │  ├─ Correlations                         │
123 | │  ├─ Anomalies                            │
124 | │  ├─ Predictions                          │
125 | │  ├─ Segmentation                         │
126 | │  └─ Recommendations                     │
127 | └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
128 | 
129 | LAYER 5: AI PROVIDER ADAPTER (Agent-Aware)
130 | ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
131 | │  Provider Selector (Task→Model Mapping)  │
132 | │  ├─ Ollama   (Local reasoning)           │
133 | │  ├─ Gemini   (Complex analysis)          │
134 | │  ├─ GPT-4    (Creative insights)         │
135 | │  └─ Claude   (Validation & critique)    │
136 | └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
137 | 
138 | LAYER 6: RESPONSE FORMATTER & FRONTEND API
139 | ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
140 | │  Agentic Flow Visualizer                 │
141 | │  (Exposes agent thinking to frontend)    │
142 | └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
143 | ```
144 | 
145 | ### 2.2 Agent Responsibilities (RACI)
146 | 
147 | | Agent | Role | Input | Output | Reasoning |
148 | |-------|------|-------|--------|-----------|
149 | | **Schema** | Understand data structure | Rows + metadata | Normalized schema profile | Detects types, cardinality, domain |
150 | | **Analytics** | Plan what to compute | Schema profile | Metric/insight plan | Determines relevant calculations |
151 | | **Tool Router** | Select algorithms | Analytics plan | Prioritized tools list | Matches plan to available tools |
152 | | **Execution** | Run computations | Tools + data | Raw results | Deterministic algorithms |
153 | | **Critic** | Quality assurance | Results + goals | Critique report | Validates against schema goals |
154 | | **Orchestrator** | Coordinate all | User goal | Final dashboard | Routes, aggregates, decides |
155 | 
156 | ---
157 |

248 | ### 3.2 Key Classes & Interfaces
249 | 
250 | ```typescript
251 | // Core Agentic Framework
252 | 
253 | abstract class Agent {
254 |   id: string;
255 |   name: string;
256 |   role: AgentRole;
257 |   capabilities: string[];
258 |   memory: AgentMemory;
259 | 
260 |   abstract async process(context: ExecutionContext): Promise<AgentResult>;
261 |   async communicate(target: Agent, message: AgentMessage): void;
262 |   async updateMemory(key: string, value: any): void;
263 | }
264 | 
265 | interface ExecutionContext {
266 |   datasetId: string;
267 |   schema: SchemaProfile;
268 |   rows: Row[];
269 |   goal: string;
270 |   agentTrail: AgentStep[];  // Track which agents ran & decisions
271 |   parentContext?: ExecutionContext;
272 | }
273 | 
274 | interface AgentResult {
275 |   agent: string;
276 |   status: 'success' | 'partial' | 'error';
277 |   output: any;
278 |   reasoning: string;
279 |   metrics: { latency: number; tokens?: number; };
280 |   nextAgents?: Agent[];  // Recommend next agents to run
281 | }
282 | 
283 | interface SchemaProfile {
284 |   id: string;
285 |   columns: ColumnProfile[];
286 |   rowCount: number;
287 |   domain: DomainClassification;
288 |   qualityScore: number;
289 |   suggestedMetrics: string[];
290 | }
291 | 
292 | interface ColumnProfile {
293 |   name: string;
294 |   type: DataType;
295 |   cardinality: number;
296 |   nullCount: number;
297 |   role: ColumnRole;  // dimension, measure, key, time, text
298 |   domain: string;   // sales, customer, product, etc.
299 | }
300 | 
301 | class ToolRegistry {
302 |   static tools: Map<string, ToolDescriptor>;
303 |   static register(tool: ToolDescriptor): void;
304 |   static findTools(criteria: ToolSearchCriteria): ToolDescriptor[];
305 | }
306 | 
307 | interface ToolDescriptor {
308 |   id: string;
309 |   name: string;
310 |   category: 'statistical' | 'ml' | 'text' | 'time_series';
311 |   requires: ColumnRole[];
312 |   produces: MetricType[];
313 |   implementation: (data: any, config: any) => Promise<any>;
314 |   costEstimate: (rowCount: number) => number;
315 |   agentPreference?: string[];  // Which agents prefer this tool
316 | }
317 | ```
318 |

321 | ## Part 4: Implementation Roadmap
322 | 
323 | ### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2) 🏗️
324 | **Goal**: Build core agentic infrastructure
325 | 
326 | **Create Agentic Framework Core**
327 | - [ ] `agentic-framework.js` - Base Agent class
328 | - [ ] `agent-registry.js` - Lifecycle management
329 | - [ ] `agent-message-bus.js` - Inter-agent communication
330 | - [ ] `agent-memory.js` - Unified memory (Redis-backed)
331 | - [ ] `agent-execution-context.js` - State management
332 | - [ ] Unit tests for each
333 | 
334 | **Extract & Consolidate Schema Logic**
335 | - [ ] Create `schema-normalizer.js` (from schema-detector)
336 | - [ ] Create `schema-validator.js` (quality checks)
337 | - [ ] Create `schema-memory.js` (similarity matching)
338 | - [ ] Refactor existing schema-detector to use normalizer
339 | - [ ] Integration tests
340 | 
341 | **Deliverable**: Core framework can create agents, agents communicate via message bus, schema pipeline is unified.
342 | 
343 | ---
344 | 
345 | ### Phase 2: Agent Implementation (Weeks 3-4) 🤖
346 | **Goal**: Refactor existing agents to new framework
347 | 
348 | **Refactor Existing Agents**
349 | - [ ] `schema-agent.js` - Extends Agent base class
350 | - [ ] `analytics-planner-agent.js` - Extends Agent
351 | - [ ] `tool-router-agent.js` - Extends Agent
352 | - [ ] `critic-agent.js` - Extends Agent
353 | - [ ] `orchestrator-agent.js` - Extends Agent
354 | 
355 | **Create New Agents**
356 | - [ ] `execution-agent.js` - Unified computation runner
357 | - [ ] `schema-validator-agent.js` - Quality assurance
358 | 
359 | **Tool System**
360 | - [ ] `tool-registry.js` - Catalog all tools
361 | - [ ] `tool-descriptor.js` - Metadata for each tool
362 | - [ ] `tool-executor.js` - Generic runner
363 | - [ ] Register existing analytics tools
364 | 
365 | **Deliverable**: All agents work in new framework, tools are discoverable, agents can route work via tools.
366 | 
367 | ---
368 | 
369 | ### Phase 3: Provider Integration (Week 5) 🧠
370 | **Goal**: Make AI providers agent-aware
371 | 
372 | **Provider Abstraction**
373 | - [ ] `agent-aware-provider.js` - Base class
374 | - [ ] `ollama-agent.js` - Ollama as agent
375 | - [ ] `gemini-agent.js` - Gemini as agent
376 | - [ ] `openai-agent.js` - GPT-4 as agent
377 | - [ ] `claude-agent.js` - Claude as agent
378 | 
379 | **Smart Routing**
380 | - [ ] `provider-selector.js` - Task → Provider mapping
381 | - [ ] Provider performance metrics
382 | - [ ] Fallback logic
383 | 
384 | **Deliverable**: Each provider is an agent, framework can select best provider per task, providers report reasoning/tokens.
385 | 
386 | ---
387 | 
388 | ### Phase 4: Workflows & Orchestration (Weeks 6-7) 🔄
389 | **Goal**: Define standard and custom workflows
390 | 
391 | **Workflow Engine**
392 | - [ ] `standard-analysis-workflow.js` - Default flow
393 | - [ ] `custom-goal-workflow.js` - User-directed
394 | - [ ] `experimental-workflow.js` - AB-testing
395 | 
396 | **Orchestrator Enhancement**
397 | - [ ] Multi-agent coordination
398 | - [ ] Parallel execution where possible
399 | - [ ] Error recovery & fallbacks
400 | - [ ] Decision logging (audit trail)
401 | 
402 | **Deliverable**: Standard analysis flow works end-to-end, custom goals supported, all agent decisions logged.
403 | 
404 | ---
405 | 
406 | ### Phase 5: Monitoring & Frontend (Weeks 8-9) 📊
407 | **Goal**: Visibility into agentic reasoning
408 | 
409 | **Monitoring Infrastructure**
410 | - [ ] `agent-metrics.js` - Performance tracking
411 | - [ ] `agent-audit-log.js` - Decision logging
412 | - [ ] `agent-debugger.js` - Flow visualization
413 | 
414 | **New Endpoints**

416 | - [ ] `GET /api/agentic/debug/:requestId` - View agent trace
417 | - [ ] `GET /api/agentic/metrics` - Agent performance stats
418 | 
419 | **Frontend Enhancement**
420 | - [ ] Agentic flow panel

451 | ## Part 5: Key Implementation Files
452 | 
453 | ### 5.1 Core Framework
454 | **File**: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agentic-framework.js`
455 | 
456 | See the generated file at that path for the full implementation. Key exports:
457 | - `Agent` — Abstract base class all agents extend
458 | - `ExecutionContext` — Immutable state passed through the pipeline
459 | - `AgentResult` — Standardized response envelope
460 | - `AGENT_ROLES` — Enum of valid role identifiers
461 | 
462 | ### 5.2 Agent Registry
463 | **File**: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-registry.js`
464 | 
465 | Manages agent lifecycle and discovery. Key methods:
466 | - `register(agent)` — Add an agent to the global registry
467 | - `getAgent(id)` — Look up by ID
468 | - `getAgentsByRole(role)` — Find all agents with a given role
469 | - `listAgents()` — Enumerate all agents with capabilities summary
470 | 
471 | ### 5.3 Schema Normalizer
472 | **File**: `apps/backend/src/services/schema/schema-normalizer.js`
473 | 
474 | Standardizes raw datasets into unified `SchemaProfile` objects. Key methods:
475 | - `normalize(rows, metadata)` — Full pipeline: detect → classify → score → suggest
476 | - `detectDomain(profile)` — Classify as ecommerce, finance, healthcare, etc.
477 | - `inferColumnRole(column, profile)` — Assign key/time/measure/dimension
478 | - `calculateQualityScore(columns, rows)` — 0-100 quality score
479 | - `suggestMetrics(columns)` — Top-10 recommended metrics
480 | 
481 | ---
482 | 
483 | ## Part 6: Integration Points
484 | 
485 | ### 6.1 New Main Endpoint
486 | 
487 | **`POST /api/agentic/analyze`**
488 | 
489 | ```json
490 | // Request
491 | {
492 |   "datasetId": "dataset-123",
493 |   "goal": "Find revenue trends and top customers",
494 |   "options": {
495 |     "includeReasoning": true,
496 |     "maxAgents": 5,
497 |     "preferredProviders": ["ollama", "gemini"]
498 |   }
499 | }
500 | 
501 | // Response
502 | {
503 |   "ok": true,
504 |   "requestId": "req-abc123",
505 |   "dashboard": {
506 |     "title": "Revenue & Customer Analysis",
507 |     "cards": [
508 |       {
509 |         "id": "card-1",
510 |         "metric": "Total Revenue",
511 |         "value": "$1.5M",
512 |         "trend": "+12%",
513 |         "agentId": "analytics-planner",
514 |         "reasoning": "Summed all transactions, calculated YoY growth"
515 |       }
516 |     ]
517 |   },
518 |   "agenticFlow": {
519 |     "requestId": "req-abc123",
520 |     "stages": [
521 |       {
522 |         "stage": 1,
523 |         "agent": "schema-analyzer",
524 |         "decision": "Detected ecommerce domain with customer + transaction structure",
525 |         "latency": 245,
526 |         "output": { "domain": "ecommerce", "columns": ["..."] }
527 |       },
528 |       {
529 |         "stage": 2,
530 |         "agent": "analytics-planner",
531 |         "decision": "Planned 5 metrics: revenue, customers, avg_order_value, growth, top_customers",
532 |         "latency": 523,
533 |         "output": { "plan": ["..."] }
534 |       }
535 |     ],
536 |     "totalLatency": 2341,
537 |     "tokensUsed": [REDACTED_SECRET]
538 |   }
539 | }
540 | ```
541 |

543 | 
544 | ```tsx

547 |   requestId={result.requestId}
548 |   stages={result.agenticFlow.stages}
549 |   totalLatency={result.agenticFlow.totalLatency}
550 | />
551 | ```

583 | 
584 | ## Part 9: Migration Guide
585 | 
586 | ### 9.1 Backward Compatibility
587 | 
588 | All existing endpoints remain functional:
589 | - ✅ `/api/datasets/:id/chat` — Wraps schema-trained-ai agent
590 | - ✅ `/api/datasets/:id/analyze` — Wraps analytics service
591 | - ✅ `/api/schema-agent/datasets/:id/train-schema` — Still works
592 | - ✅ `/api/agentic-models/datasets/:id/analyze` — Still works
593 | 
594 | ### 9.2 Gradual Migration Path
595 | 
596 | | Week | Action |
597 | |------|--------|
598 | | 1-2  | New agents run in parallel with old code (feature-flag) |
599 | | 3-4  | Gradually route traffic to new agents |
600 | | 5-6  | Phase out old implementations |
601 | | 7+   | Fully agentic system |
602 | 
603 | ---
604 | 
605 | ## Part 10: Success Metrics
606 | 
607 | ### 10.1 Quantitative
608 | 
609 | | Metric | Current | Target | Timeline |
610 | |--------|---------|--------|----------|
611 | | Agent cohesion | Low (isolated) | High (orchestrated) | Week 4 |
612 | | Reasoning explainability | 0% requests | 100% requests | Week 8 |
613 | | Dashboard generation latency | 2-3s | 1-2s (with caching) | Week 9 |
614 | | Schema reuse rate | ~20% | 60%+ | Week 6 |
615 | | Agent success rate | N/A | 95%+ | Week 7 |
616 | | New metric discovery | Manual | Automatic via agents | Week 5 |
617 | 
618 | ### 10.2 Qualitative
619 | 
620 | - ✅ Code maintainability improved
621 | - ✅ Agent reasoning transparent to users
622 | - ✅ Easy to add new agents/tools
623 | - ✅ Easy to understand agentic decisions
624 | - ✅ Easy to debug failures
625 | 
626 | ---
627 | 
628 | ## Part 11: Common Pitfalls & Solutions
629 | 
630 | | Pitfall | Solution |
631 | |---------|---------|
632 | | Agents timeout on large datasets | Streaming results, lazy evaluation, chunking |
633 | | Agents make redundant decisions | Cross-agent memory, result caching |
634 | | LLM-based agents hallucinate metrics | Critic agent validation, deterministic checks |
635 | | Coordination overhead too high | Parallel execution, tool batching, memoization |
636 | | Hard to trace failures | Comprehensive logging, execution context propagation |
637 | | Performance degradation under load | Agent pooling, request queuing, circuit breakers |
638 | 
639 | ---
640 | 
641 | ## Part 12: Conclusion & Next Steps
642 | 
643 | ### Your Project Has:
644 | - ✅ Strong schema detection foundation
645 | - ✅ Multiple AI providers configured
646 | - ✅ Scattered agentic capabilities
647 | 
648 | ### You Need:
649 | 1. **Unified orchestration** — Connect agents properly
650 | 2. **Tool registry** — Make tools discoverable
651 | 3. **Memory layer** — Agents learn across requests
652 | 4. **Frontend visibility** — Show reasoning to users
653 | 5. **Monitoring** — Track agent performance
654 | 
655 | ### Recommended Order:
656 | 1. Start with **Phase 1** (Framework core) — 2 weeks
657 | 2. Then **Phase 2** (Refactor agents) — 2 weeks
658 | 3. Then **Phase 3** (Provider integration) — 1 week
659 | 4. Iterate with phases 4–6 based on feedback
660 | 
661 | ### Success Criteria:
662 | - ✅ `/api/agentic/analyze` endpoint works end-to-end
663 | - ✅ Agent reasoning visible in responses
664 | - ✅ All existing endpoints still work
665 | - ✅ Performance doesn't degrade
666 | - ✅ New agents can be added without core changes
667 | 
```

 13 | - `POST /api/datasets/demo` - Load demo dataset
 14 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id` - Get specific dataset
 15 | - `PATCH /api/datasets/:id/rows/:rowId` - Update row
 16 | - `DELETE /api/datasets/:id` - Delete dataset
 17 | 
 18 | ### 3. Chat Routes (`/api/datasets/:id/chat`)

 20 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/chat/history` - Get chat history
 21 | - `DELETE /api/datasets/:id/chat/history` - Clear chat history
 22 | 
 23 | ### 4. Analytics Routes (`/api/datasets/:id/ai-*`)
 24 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai-correlations` - Pearson correlations
 25 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai/profile` - Data profile
 26 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/ai/anomalies` - Anomaly detection (z-score)

 29 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/analyze` - Full analysis
 30 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/schema` - Schema info
 31 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/auto-charts` - Auto chart suggestions

 35 | - `GET /api/datasets/:id/export/csv` - Export as CSV

### FILE: apps/backend/kill-port.js

Source path: `apps/backend/kill-port.js`

```js
 1 | #!/usr/bin/env node
 2 | 
 3 | /**
 4 |  * Kill process using port 3001
 5 |  * Run: node kill-port.js
 6 |  */
 7 | 
 8 | import { exec } from "child_process";
 9 | import { platform } from "os";
10 | 
11 | const port = basePort || 3001;
12 | 
13 | console.log(`🔍 Attempting to kill process on port ${port}...\n`);
14 | 
15 | if (platform() === "win32") {
16 |   exec(
17 |     `netstat -ano | findstr :${port}`,
18 |     (err, stdout) => {
19 |       if (err || !stdout) {
20 |         console.log(`✅ No process found on port ${port}`);
21 |         return;
22 |       }
23 | 
24 |       const lines = stdout.trim().split('\n');
25 |       if (lines.length > 0) {
26 |         const firstLine = lines[0].trim().split(/\s+/);
27 |         const pid = firstLine[4];
28 |         if (pid && pid !== '0') {
29 |           console.log(`Found PID: ${pid}`);
30 |           exec(`taskkill /PID ${pid} /F`, (err, stdout) => {
31 |             if (err) {
32 |               console.error(`❌ Failed to kill process: ${err.message}`);
33 |             } else {
34 |               console.log(`✅ Process killed successfully`);
35 |               console.log(`💡 You can now start the server: npm run dev\n`);
36 |             }
37 |           });
38 |         }
39 |       }
40 |     }
41 |   );
42 | } else {
43 |   exec(
44 |     `lsof -i :${port} | grep LISTEN | awk '{print $2}'`,
45 |     (err, stdout) => {
46 |       if (err || !stdout) {
47 |         console.log(`✅ No process found on port ${port}`);
48 |         return;
49 |       }
50 | 
51 |       const pid = stdout.trim();
52 |       if (pid) {
53 |         console.log(`Found PID: ${pid}`);
54 |         exec(`kill -9 ${pid}`, (err) => {
55 |           if (err) {
56 |             console.error(`❌ Failed to kill process: ${err.message}`);
57 |           } else {
58 |             console.log(`✅ Process killed successfully`);
59 |             console.log(`💡 You can now start the server: npm run dev\n`);
60 |           }
61 |         });
62 |       }
63 |     }
64 |   );
65 | }
66 | 
```

  1 | import initSqlJs from "sql.js";
  2 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  3 | import logger from "../src/utils/logger.js";
  4 | import { buildEnhancedSchema, classifyColumns, smartAutoChartGeneration, smartAutoChartGenerationForSingle, smartAutoChartGenerationForMerged } from "../src/services/schema-detector.js";
  5 | import { aiAnalyzer } from "../src/services/ai-analyzer.js";
  6 | import { buildUnifiedSchema, mergeDatasets } from "../src/services/data-merger.js";
  7 | import { reportGenerator } from "../src/services/report-generator.js";
  8 | import { predictiveAnalytics } from "../src/services/predictive-analytics.js";
  9 | import { alertService } from "../src/services/alert-service.js";
 10 | import { recommendationEngine } from "../src/services/recommendation-engine.js";
 11 | import { llamaValidationMiddleware } from "../src/services/llama-validation-middleware.js";
 12 | import { isOllamaConfigured } from "../src/services/ollama-ai-service.js";
 13 | 
 14 | let db = null;
 15 | let dbReady = null;
 16 | 
 17 | const initDB = async () => {
 18 |   if (db) return db;
 19 |   if (dbReady) return dbReady;
 20 | 
 21 |   dbReady = (async () => {
 22 |     const SQL = await initSqlJs();
 23 |     db = new SQL.Database();
 24 |     
 25 |     db.run(`
 26 |       CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meta (
 27 |         key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
 28 |         value TEXT
 29 |       );
 30 |       
 31 |       CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS datasets (
 32 |         id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
 33 |         name TEXT NOT NULL,
 34 |         source_type TEXT NOT NULL,
 35 |         file_name TEXT,
 36 |         uploaded_at TEXT NOT NULL,
 37 |         row_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
 38 |         column_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
 39 |         columns_json TEXT NOT NULL,
 40 |         rows_json TEXT NOT NULL
 41 |       );
 42 |       
 43 |       CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
 44 |         id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
 45 |         dataset_id TEXT NOT NULL,
 46 |         role TEXT NOT NULL,
 47 |         content TEXT NOT NULL,
 48 |         sql_text TEXT,
 49 |         chart_json TEXT,
 50 |         insights_json TEXT,
 51 |         created_at TEXT NOT NULL
 52 |       );
 53 |     `);
 54 |     
 55 |     return db;
 56 |   })();
 57 |   
 58 |   return dbReady;
 59 | };
 60 | 
 61 | const getCurrentDatasetId = () => {
 62 |   const result = db.exec("SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key = 'current_dataset_id'");
 63 |   return result.length > 0 && result[0].values.length > 0 ? result[0].values[0][0] : null;
 64 | };
 65 | 
 66 | const setCurrentDatasetId = (id) => {
 67 |   db.run("INSERT OR REPLACE INTO meta (key, value) VALUES ('current_dataset_id', ?)", [id]);
 68 | };
 69 | 
 70 | const getDatasetById = (id) => {
 71 |   const result = db.exec("SELECT * FROM datasets WHERE id = ?", [id]);
 72 |   if (result.length === 0 || result[0].values.length === 0) return null;
 73 |   
 74 |   const row = result[0].values[0];
 75 |   const columns = result[0].columns;
 76 |   return {
 77 |     id: row[columns.indexOf("id")],
 78 |     name: row[columns.indexOf("name")],
 79 |     sourceType: row[columns.indexOf("source_type")],
 80 |     fileName: row[columns.indexOf("file_name")],
 81 |     uploadedAt: row[columns.indexOf("uploaded_at")],
 82 |     rowCount: row[columns.indexOf("row_count")],
 83 |     columnCount: row[columns.indexOf("column_count")],
 84 |     columns: JSON.parse(row[columns.indexOf("columns_json")]),
 85 |     rows: JSON.parse(row[columns.indexOf("rows_json")]),
 86 |   };
 87 | };
 88 | 
 89 | const getChatMessages = (datasetId) => {
 90 |   const result = db.exec("SELECT * FROM chat_messages WHERE dataset_id = ? ORDER BY created_at ASC", [datasetId]);
 91 |   if (result.length === 0) return [];
 92 |   
 93 |   const rows = result[0].values;
 94 |   const cols = result[0].columns;
 95 |   return rows.map(row => ({
 96 |     id: row[cols.indexOf("id")],
 97 |     role: row[cols.indexOf("role")],
 98 |     content: row[cols.indexOf("content")],
 99 |     sql: row[cols.indexOf("sql_text")] || undefined,
100 |     chart: row[cols.indexOf("chart_json")] ? JSON.parse(row[cols.indexOf("chart_json")]) : undefined,
101 |     insights: row[cols.indexOf("insights_json")] ? JSON.parse(row[cols.indexOf("insights_json")]) : [],
102 |     timestamp: row[cols.indexOf("created_at")],
103 |   }));
104 | };
105 | 
106 | const normalizeColumns = (rows, columns) => {
107 |   if (columns.length > 0) {
108 |     return columns.map((col) => ({
109 |       name: col.name || col,
110 |       type: col.type || typeof rows[0]?.[col.name || col] || "string",
111 |       sample: rows.slice(0, 5).map((row) => row[col.name || col]).filter((v) => v !== undefined && v !== null),
112 |     }));
113 |   }
114 |   if (rows.length === 0) return [];
115 |   const firstRow = rows[0];
116 |   const columnNames = Object.keys(firstRow);
117 |   return columnNames.map((name) => ({
118 |     name,
119 |     type: typeof firstRow[name],
120 |     sample: rows.slice(0, 5).map((row) => row[name]).filter((v) => v !== undefined && v !== null),
121 |   }));
122 | };
123 | 
124 | const generateDemoDataset = () => {
125 |   const regions = ["North", "South", "East", "West", "Central"];
126 |   const products = ["Widget A", "Widget B", "Gadget X", "Gadget Y", "Service Z"];
127 |   const channels = ["Online", "Retail", "Wholesale", "Direct"];
128 |   const rows = [];
129 |   for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
130 |     const region = regions[Math.floor(Math.random() * regions.length)];
131 |     const product = products[Math.floor(Math.random() * products.length)];
132 |     const channel = channels[Math.floor(Math.random() * channels.length)];
133 |     const baseRevenue = product.includes("Widget") ? 5000 : product.includes("Gadget") ? 8000 : 3000;
134 |     const revenue = Math.round((baseRevenue + Math.random() * 5000) * 100) / 100;
135 |     const units = Math.floor(50 + Math.random() * 450);
136 |     const profitMargin = 0.2 + Math.random() * 0.4;
137 |     rows.push({
138 |       Region: region,
139 |       Product: product,
140 |       Channel: channel,
141 |       Revenue: revenue,
142 |       Units: units,
143 |       ProfitMargin: Math.round(profitMargin * 100) / 100,
144 |       Date: new Date(2024, Math.floor(Math.random() * 12), Math.floor(Math.random() * 28) + 1).toISOString().split("T")[0],
145 |     });
146 |   }
147 |   return { name: "Sales Performance Dataset", fileName: null, sourceType: "demo", columns: normalizeColumns(rows, []), rows };
148 | };
149 | 
150 | const buildDatasetSchema = (dataset) => {
151 |   const numericColumns = dataset.columns.filter((col) => ["number", "integer", "float", "double"].includes(col.type));
152 |   const categoricalColumns = dataset.columns.filter((col) => !numericColumns.includes(col));
153 |   const dateColumns = dataset.columns.filter((col) => col.type === "date" || /date/i.test(col.name));
154 |   return {
155 |     columns: dataset.columns.map((col) => ({
156 |       name: col.name, type: col.type,
157 |       uniqueValues: new Set(dataset.rows.map((row) => String(row[col.name]))).size,
158 |       nullCount: dataset.rows.filter((row) => row[col.name] === null || row[col.name] === undefined).length,
159 |     })),
160 |     numericColumns: numericColumns.map((c) => c.name),
161 |     categoricalColumns: categoricalColumns.map((c) => c.name),
162 |     dateColumns: dateColumns.map((c) => c.name),
163 |     totalRows: dataset.rowCount,
164 |     totalColumns: dataset.columns.length,
165 |   };
166 | };
167 | 
168 | const computeStats = (values) => {
169 |   const nums = values.filter((v) => typeof v === "number" && !isNaN(v));
170 |   if (nums.length === 0) return null;
171 |   const sorted = [...nums].sort((a, b) => a - b);
172 |   const sum = nums.reduce((acc, val) => acc + val, 0);
173 |   const mean = sum / nums.length;
174 |   const median = nums.length % 2 === 0 ? (sorted[nums.length / 2 - 1] + sorted[nums.length / 2]) / 2 : sorted[Math.floor(nums.length / 2)];
175 |   const variance = nums.reduce((acc, val) => acc + Math.pow(val - mean, 2), 0) / nums.length;
176 |   return { mean: Math.round(mean * 100) / 100, median, stdDev: Math.round(Math.sqrt(variance) * 100) / 100, min: sorted[0], max: sorted[sorted.length - 1] };
177 | };
178 | 
179 | const createChatResponse = (ds, query) => {
180 |   const lowerQuery = query.toLowerCase();
181 |   const schema = buildDatasetSchema(ds);
182 | 
183 |   if (/average|mean|avg/i.test(lowerQuery)) {
184 |     const numericCols = schema.numericColumns;
185 |     if (numericCols.length > 0) {
186 |       const targetCol = numericCols.find((c) => lowerQuery.includes(c.toLowerCase())) || numericCols[0];
187 |       const stats = computeStats(ds.rows.map((r) => r[targetCol]));
188 |       return { content: `The average ${targetCol} is ${stats?.mean || 0}.`, chart: { type: "bar", data: { labels: ["Average", "Median"], datasets: [{ label: targetCol, data: [stats?.mean || 0, stats?.median || 0] }] } }, insights: [{ metric: `Average ${targetCol}`, value: stats?.mean || 0 }] };
189 |     }
190 |   }
191 |   if (/sum|total/i.test(lowerQuery)) {
192 |     const numericCols = schema.numericColumns;
193 |     if (numericCols.length > 0) {
194 |       const targetCol = numericCols.find((c) => lowerQuery.includes(c.toLowerCase())) || numericCols[0];
195 |       const total = ds.rows.reduce((acc, row) => acc + (Number(row[targetCol]) || 0), 0);
196 |       return { content: `The total ${targetCol} is ${total.toLocaleString()}.`, chart: { type: "bar", data: { labels: ["Total"], datasets: [{ label: targetCol, data: [total] }] } }, insights: [{ metric: `Total ${targetCol}`, value: total }] };
197 |     }
198 |   }
199 |   if (/category|group|breakdown/i.test(lowerQuery)) {
200 |     const catCols = schema.categoricalColumns;
201 |     if (catCols.length > 0) {
202 |       const targetCol = catCols[0];
203 |       const counts = {};
204 |       ds.rows.forEach((row) => { const val = String(row[targetCol]); counts[val] = (counts[val] || 0) + 1; });
205 |       const labels = Object.keys(counts);
206 |       return { content: `Breakdown by ${targetCol}: ${labels.map((l) => `${l} (${counts[l]})`).join(", ")}.`, chart: { type: "doughnut", data: { labels, datasets: [{ label: targetCol, data: Object.values(counts) }] } }, insights: labels.slice(0, 3).map((l) => ({ metric: l, value: counts[l] })) };
207 |     }
208 |   }
209 |   return { content: `Dataset has ${ds.rowCount} rows and ${ds.columns.length} columns.`, chart: null, insights: [] };
210 | };
211 | 
212 | const generateCorrelationAnalysis = (ds) => {
213 |   const numericCols = ds.columns.filter((c) => ["number", "integer", "float", "double"].includes(c.type));
214 |   const correlations = [];
215 |   for (let i = 0; i < numericCols.length; i++) {
216 |     for (let j = i + 1; j < numericCols.length; j++) {
217 |       const values1 = ds.rows.map((r) => r[numericCols[i].name]).filter((v) => typeof v === "number");
218 |       const values2 = ds.rows.map((r) => r[numericCols[j].name]).filter((v) => typeof v === "number");
219 |       const minLen = Math.min(values1.length, values2.length);
220 |       if (minLen > 2) {
221 |         const x = values1.slice(0, minLen);
222 |         const y = values2.slice(0, minLen);
223 |         const xMean = x.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / x.length;
224 |         const yMean = y.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / y.length;
225 |         const numerator = x.reduce((acc, xi, i) => acc + (xi - xMean) * (y[i] - yMean), 0);
226 |         const denominator = Math.sqrt(x.reduce((acc, xi) => acc + Math.pow(xi - xMean, 2), 0) * y.reduce((acc, yi) => acc + Math.pow(yi - yMean, 2), 0));
227 |         const coefficient = denominator === 0 ? 0 : numerator / denominator;
228 |         let strength = Math.abs(coefficient) > 0.7 ? "strong" : Math.abs(coefficient) > 0.4 ? "moderate" : "weak";
229 |         correlations.push({ column1: numericCols[i].name, column2: numericCols[j].name, coefficient: Math.round(coefficient * 1000) / 1000, strength, interpretation: coefficient > 0.5 ? "Positive correlation" : coefficient < -0.5 ? "Negative correlation" : "Weak correlation", sampleSize: minLen });
230 |       }
231 |     }
232 |   }
233 |   return { correlations: correlations.sort((a, b) => Math.abs(b.coefficient) - Math.abs(a.coefficient)), summary: `Found ${correlations.length} pairs.`, hasGemini: false };
234 | };
235 | 
236 | const sendJson = (statusCode, payload, headers = {}) => {
237 |   return new Response(JSON.stringify(payload), {
238 |     status: statusCode,
239 |     headers: { 
240 |       "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8", 
241 |       "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*", 
242 |       "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type", 
243 |       "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET,POST,PATCH,OPTIONS",
244 |       "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
245 |       "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
246 |       "X-XSS-Protection": "1; mode=block",
247 |       ...headers 
248 |     },
249 |   });
250 | };
251 | 
252 | const readJsonBody = async (request) => {
253 |   try { const text = await request.text(); return text ? JSON.parse(text) : {}; } catch { return {}; }
254 | };
255 | 
256 | export async function GET(request) {
257 |   const startTime = Date.now();
258 |   try {
259 |     await initDB();
260 |     const url = new URL(request.url);
261 |     const pathname = url.pathname;
262 |     if (pathname === "/api/health") return sendJson(200, { status: "ok", database: "SQLite" });
263 |     if (pathname === "/api/state") {
264 |       const currentDatasetId = getCurrentDatasetId();
265 |       return sendJson(200, { dataset: currentDatasetId ? getDatasetById(currentDatasetId) : null, chatMessages: currentDatasetId ? getChatMessages(currentDatasetId) : [] });
266 |     }
267 |     const schemaMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/([^/]+)\/schema$/);
268 |     if (schemaMatch) { const ds = getDatasetById(schemaMatch[1]); return ds ? sendJson(200, { schema: buildDatasetSchema(ds) }) : sendJson(404, { error: "Not found" }); }
269 |     const aiCorrMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/datasets\/([^/]+)\/ai-correlations$/);
270 |     if (aiCorrMatch) { const ds = getDatasetById(aiCorrMatch[1]); return ds ? sendJson(200, generateCorrelationAnalysis(ds)) : sendJson(404, { error: "Not found" }); }
271 |     return sendJson(404, { error: "Route not found" });
272 |   } catch (error) {
273 |     logger.error("GET request failed", { pathname: new URL(request.url).pathname, error: error.message });
274 |     return sendJson(500, { error: "Internal server error" });
275 |   } finally {
276 |     logger.logRequest("GET", new URL(request.url).pathname, 200, Date.now() - startTime);
277 |   }
278 | }
279 | 
280 | export async function POST(request) {
281 |   const startTime = Date.now();
282 |   const pathname = new URL(request.url).pathname;
283 |   try {
284 |     await initDB();
285 |     const body = await readJsonBody(request);
286 |     
287 |     if (pathname === "/api/datasets/demo") {
288 |       const demo = generateDemoDataset();
289 |       const id = randomUUID();
290 |       db.run("INSERT INTO datasets VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)", [id, demo.name, demo.sourceType, demo.fileName, new Date().toISOString(), demo.rows.length, demo.columns.length, JSON.stringify(demo.columns), JSON.stringify(demo.rows)]);
291 |       setCurrentDatasetId(id);
292 |       
293 |       const dataset = getDatasetById(id);
294 |       console.log('[DEMO] Columns:', demo.columns.length, 'Rows:', demo.rows.length);
295 |

297 |       try {
298 |         console.log('[DEMO] Running AI analysis...');
