### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/__tests__/schema-packet-builder.test.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/__tests__/schema-packet-builder.test.js`

```js
 1 | import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
 2 | import {
 3 |   buildSchemaPacket,
 4 |   formatSchemaForPrompt,
 5 |   validateColumnsExist,
 6 |   getDataQualityScore,
 7 | } from '../schema-packet-builder.js';
 8 | 
 9 | describe('Schema Packet Builder', () => {
10 |   const mockDataset = {
11 |     name: 'Test Dataset',
12 |     rowCount: 100,
13 |     columns: [
14 |       { name: 'age', type: 'number' },
15 |       { name: 'category', type: 'string' },
16 |     ],
17 |     rows: Array.from({ length: 100 }, (_, i) => ({
18 |       age: Math.floor(Math.random() * 60) + 20,
19 |       category: ['A', 'B', 'C'][Math.floor(Math.random() * 3)],
20 |     })),
21 |   };
22 | 
23 |   it('builds valid schema packet', () => {
24 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
25 |     expect(packet).toBeDefined();
26 |     expect(packet.name).toBe('Test Dataset');
27 |     expect(packet.rowCount).toBe(100);
28 |     expect(packet.columns.length).toBe(2);
29 |   });
30 | 
31 |   it('extracts numeric statistics', () => {
32 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
33 |     const ageColumn = packet.columns.find(c => c.name === 'age');
34 |     expect(ageColumn.type).toBe('numeric');
35 |     expect(ageColumn.isValid).toBe(true);
36 |     expect(ageColumn.min).toBeLessThan(ageColumn.max);
37 |     expect(ageColumn.mean).toBeGreaterThan(0);
38 |   });
39 | 
40 |   it('extracts categorical statistics', () => {
41 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
42 |     const catColumn = packet.columns.find(c => c.name === 'category');
43 |     expect(catColumn.type).toBe('categorical');
44 |     expect(catColumn.isValid).toBe(true);
45 |     expect(Object.keys(catColumn.topValues).length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
46 |   });
47 | 
48 |   it('handles empty datasets', () => {
49 |     expect(() => {
50 |       buildSchemaPacket({ ...mockDataset, rows: [] });
51 |     }).toThrow();
52 |   });
53 | 
54 |   it('validates columns exist', () => {
55 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
56 |     expect(() => {
57 |       validateColumnsExist(['age', 'category'], packet);
58 |     }).not.toThrow();
59 |   });
60 | 
61 |   it('rejects invalid columns', () => {
62 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
63 |     expect(() => {
64 |       validateColumnsExist(['age', 'invalid_column'], packet);
65 |     }).toThrow();
66 |   });
67 | 
68 |   it('calculates data quality score', () => {
69 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
70 |     const score = getDataQualityScore(packet);
71 |     expect(score).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
72 |     expect(score).toBeLessThanOrEqual(100);
73 |   });
74 | 
75 |   it('formats schema for prompt', () => {
76 |     const packet = buildSchemaPacket(mockDataset);
77 |     const text = formatSchemaForPrompt(packet);
78 |     expect(text).toContain('Test Dataset');
79 |     expect(text).toContain('age');
80 |     expect(text).toContain('category');
81 |   });
82 | });
83 |
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/analytics-agent-planner.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/analytics-agent-planner.js`

```js
 1 | export function buildAnalyticsAgentPlan({ schemaProfile, userGoal = "" }) {
 2 |   const columns = schemaProfile.columns || [];
 3 | 
 4 |   const hasDate = columns.some((c) =>
 5 |     /date|time|timestamp|month|year/i.test(c.name)
 6 |   );
 7 | 
 8 |   const hasGeo = columns.some((c) =>
 9 |     /country|state|city|region|location|lat|lng|latitude|longitude/i.test(c.name)
10 |   );
11 | 
12 |   const hasMoney = columns.some((c) =>
13 |     /revenue|sales|salary|amount|price|profit|cost|expense|budget|margin/i.test(c.name)
14 |   );
15 | 
16 |   const hasTarget = columns.some((c) =>
17 |     /churn|fraud|target|label|status|outcome|prediction/i.test(c.name)
18 |   );
19 | 
20 |   const tasks = [
21 |     "profile_dataset",
22 |     "detect_domain",
23 |     "retrieve_schema_rag_memory",
24 |     "select_kpis",
25 |     "build_dashboard_plan",
26 |     "generate_insights",
27 |     "validate_dashboard_quality",
28 |   ];
29 | 
30 |   if (hasDate) tasks.push("time_series_analysis");
31 |   if (hasGeo) tasks.push("geo_intelligence");
32 |   if (hasMoney) tasks.push("financial_metric_analysis");
33 |   if (hasTarget) tasks.push("ml_recommendation");
34 |   if (userGoal) tasks.push("user_goal_alignment");
35 | 
36 |   return {
37 |     agentType: "pure_agentic_data_analyst",
38 |     goal: userGoal || "Automatically generate the best analytics dashboard and insights.",
39 |     tasks,
40 |     reasoningRules: [
41 |       "Prefer business KPIs over raw counts.",
42 |       "Use trend charts when date columns exist.",
43 |       "Use global map when geo columns exist.",
44 |       "Use median when numeric data likely contains outliers.",
45 |       "Use ML recommendation when target/label exists.",
46 |       "Never generate charts without analytical meaning.",
47 |     ],
48 |   };
49 | }
50 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/analytics-critic-agent.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/analytics-critic-agent.js`

```js
 1 | export function critiqueDashboard({ schemaProfile, dashboardPlan }) {
 2 |   const issues = [];
 3 |   const improvements = [];
 4 | 
 5 |   const columns = schemaProfile.columns || [];
 6 |   const kpis = dashboardPlan.kpis || [];
 7 |   const charts = dashboardPlan.charts || [];
 8 | 
 9 |   const hasMoney = columns.some((c) =>
10 |     /revenue|sales|salary|amount|profit|cost|expense/i.test(c.name)
11 |   );
12 | 
13 |   const hasDate = columns.some((c) =>
14 |     /date|time|timestamp|month|year/i.test(c.name)
15 |   );
16 | 
17 |   const hasGeo = columns.some((c) =>
18 |     /country|state|city|region|location|latitude|longitude/i.test(c.name)
19 |   );
20 | 
21 |   if (hasMoney && !kpis.some((k) => /revenue|sales|salary|profit|cost|amount/i.test(k.title))) {
22 |     issues.push("Money metric exists but no strong financial KPI was selected.");
23 |     improvements.push("Add revenue/profit/salary/cost KPI based on the detected metric.");
24 |   }
25 | 
26 |   if (hasDate && !charts.some((c) => /line|area|trend/i.test(c.type + c.title))) {
27 |     issues.push("Date column exists but no trend chart was generated.");
28 |     improvements.push("Add a time-series line chart.");
29 |   }
30 | 
31 |   if (hasGeo && !charts.some((c) => /geo|map/i.test(c.type + c.title))) {
32 |     issues.push("Geo column exists but no map chart was generated.");
33 |     improvements.push("Add global map or regional heatmap.");
34 |   }
35 | 
36 |   if (charts.some((c) => /pie|donut/i.test(c.type) && /id|email|name/i.test(c.xKey || ""))) {
37 |     issues.push("Pie/donut chart is using a high-cardinality identifier field.");
38 |     improvements.push("Replace it with a ranking bar chart.");
39 |   }
40 | 
41 |   return {
42 |     score: Math.max(0, 100 - issues.length * 20),
43 |     status: issues.length ? "needs_improvement" : "excellent",
44 |     issues,
45 |     improvements,
46 |   };
47 | }
48 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/analytics-tool-router.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/analytics-tool-router.js`

```js
 1 | export function routeAnalyticsTools(agentPlan) {
 2 |   const tools = [];
 3 | 
 4 |   for (const task of agentPlan.tasks) {
 5 |     if (task === "profile_dataset") tools.push("schema_profiler");
 6 |     if (task === "detect_domain") tools.push("domain_detector");
 7 |     if (task === "retrieve_schema_rag_memory") tools.push("schema_rag_retriever");
 8 |     if (task === "select_kpis") tools.push("kpi_engine");
 9 |     if (task === "build_dashboard_plan") tools.push("dashboard_plan_engine");
10 |     if (task === "generate_insights") tools.push("senior_analyst_brain");
11 |     if (task === "validate_dashboard_quality") tools.push("dashboard_quality_guardian");
12 |     if (task === "time_series_analysis") tools.push("time_series_agent");
13 |     if (task === "geo_intelligence") tools.push("geo_agent");
14 |     if (task === "financial_metric_analysis") tools.push("finance_agent");
15 |     if (task === "ml_recommendation") tools.push("automl_agent");
16 |   }
17 | 
18 |   return [...new Set(tools)];
19 | }
20 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/chart-agent.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/chart-agent.js`

```js
 1 | export function executeChartAgent(dashboardSpec, ontologyMapping) {
 2 |   // Ensure we have charts appropriate for the mapped semantic concepts
 3 |   const charts = [...(dashboardSpec.charts || [])];
 4 | 
 5 |   if (ontologyMapping.canonicalTerms.includes('date') && !charts.some(c => c.type === 'line')) {
 6 |     charts.push({
 7 |       id: 'agentic_trend_chart',
 8 |       title: 'Performance Trend Over Time',
 9 |       type: 'line',
10 |       priority: 'high',
11 |       reason: 'Chart Agent enforced a line chart due to semantic detection of time-series data.'
12 |     });
13 |   }
14 | 
15 |   if (ontologyMapping.canonicalTerms.includes('region') && !charts.some(c => c.type === 'geo')) {
16 |     charts.push({
17 |       id: 'agentic_geo_map',
18 |       title: 'Global Distribution Map',
19 |       type: 'geo',
20 |       priority: 'high',
21 |       reason: 'Chart Agent enforced a geo map due to semantic detection of geographic dimensions.'
22 |     });
23 |   }
24 | 
25 |   return { ...dashboardSpec, charts };
26 | }
27 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/chief-analyst-orchestrator.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/chief-analyst-orchestrator.js`

```js
 1 | import { mapSchemaToOntology } from '../ai-analyst/knowledge/ontology-mapper.js';
 2 | import { executeKpiAgent } from './kpi-agent.js';
 3 | import { executeChartAgent } from './chart-agent.js';
 4 | import { executeInsightAgent } from './insight-agent.js';
 5 | import { executeStorytellingAgent } from './storytelling-agent.js';
 6 | 
 7 | export function runChiefAnalystOrchestrator(profile, baseDashboardSpec) {
 8 |   // 1. Map Schema using Business Ontology
 9 |   const columnNames = profile.columns?.map(c => c.name) || [];
10 |   const ontologyMapping = mapSchemaToOntology(columnNames);
11 | 
12 |   // 2. Wrap legacy spec
13 |   let spec = { ...baseDashboardSpec };
14 | 
15 |   // 3. Delegate to KPI Agent
16 |   spec = executeKpiAgent(spec, ontologyMapping);
17 | 
18 |   // 4. Delegate to Chart Agent
19 |   spec = executeChartAgent(spec, ontologyMapping);
20 | 
21 |   // 5. Delegate to Insight Agent
22 |   spec = executeInsightAgent(spec, ontologyMapping);
23 | 
24 |   // 6. Delegate to Storytelling Agent
25 |   spec = executeStorytellingAgent(spec, ontologyMapping);
26 | 
27 |   return {
28 |     dashboardSpec: spec,
29 |     ontologyMapping
30 |   };
31 | }
32 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-memory.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-memory.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * @file agent-memory.js
  3 |  * @description Unified memory layer for agents — in-process with optional
  4 |  * persistence hooks for Redis / DB backends.
  5 |  *
  6 |  * Provides:
  7 |  *  - Short-term (per-request) memory via ContextMemory
  8 |  *  - Long-term (cross-request) memory via AgentMemory
  9 |  *  - Schema similarity indexing via SemanticSchemaMemory
 10 |  */
 11 | 
 12 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 13 | // ContextMemory — per-request scratch pad (lives as long as the context)
 14 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 15 | 
 16 | export class ContextMemory {
 17 |   constructor(requestId) {
 18 |     this.requestId = requestId;
 19 |     this._store    = new Map();
 20 |     this._createdAt = Date.now();
 21 |   }
 22 | 
 23 |   set(key, value) { this._store.set(key, value); return this; }
 24 |   get(key)        { return this._store.get(key); }
 25 |   has(key)        { return this._store.has(key); }
 26 |   delete(key)     { return this._store.delete(key); }
 27 |   clear()         { this._store.clear(); }
 28 | 
 29 |   /** Return all entries as a plain object. */
 30 |   dump() {
 31 |     return Object.fromEntries(this._store);
 32 |   }
 33 | 
 34 |   get size() { return this._store.size; }
 35 | }
 36 | 
 37 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 38 | // AgentMemory — cross-request, per-agent long-term memory
 39 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 40 | 
 41 | /**
 42 |  * Stores key-value memories that survive across multiple requests.
 43 |  * Backed by a JS Map (in-process). Extend with `setPersistenceBackend()`
 44 |  * to delegate reads/writes to Redis or a database.
 45 |  */
 46 | export class AgentMemory {
 47 |   /**
 48 |    * @param {string} agentId     - Owner agent's ID
 49 |    * @param {object} [opts]
 50 |    * @param {number} [opts.maxEntries=500] - LRU eviction threshold
 51 |    * @param {number} [opts.ttlMs]          - Optional TTL per entry (ms)
 52 |    */
 53 |   constructor(agentId, { maxEntries = 500, ttlMs = null } = {}) {
 54 |     this.agentId    = agentId;
 55 |     this.maxEntries = maxEntries;
 56 |     this.ttlMs      = ttlMs;
 57 | 
 58 |     /** @type {Map<string, { value: any, writtenAt: number }>} */
 59 |     this._store  = new Map();
 60 | 
 61 |     /** Optional async persistence backend */
 62 |     this._backend = null;
 63 |   }
 64 | 
 65 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 66 |   // CRUD
 67 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 68 | 
 69 |   /**
 70 |    * Store a value under a key.
 71 |    *
 72 |    * @param {string} key
 73 |    * @param {any}    value
 74 |    * @returns {Promise<void>}
 75 |    */
 76 |   async set(key, value) {
 77 |     // LRU eviction — remove oldest entry if over limit
 78 |     if (this._store.size >= this.maxEntries) {
 79 |       const oldestKey = this._store.keys().next().value;
 80 |       this._store.delete(oldestKey);
 81 |     }
 82 | 
 83 |     this._store.set(key, { value, writtenAt: Date.now() });
 84 | 
 85 |     if (this._backend?.set) {
 86 |       await this._backend.set(`${this.agentId}:${key}`, value);
 87 |     }
 88 |   }
 89 | 
 90 |   /**
 91 |    * Retrieve a value.
 92 |    *
 93 |    * @param {string} key
 94 |    * @returns {Promise<any|undefined>}
 95 |    */
 96 |   async get(key) {
 97 |     const entry = this._store.get(key);
 98 |     if (!entry) {
 99 |       // Try backend if available
100 |       if (this._backend?.get) {
101 |         return this._backend.get(`${this.agentId}:${key}`);
102 |       }
103 |       return undefined;
104 |     }
105 | 
106 |     // Check TTL
107 |     if (this.ttlMs && Date.now() - entry.writtenAt > this.ttlMs) {
108 |       this._store.delete(key);
109 |       return undefined;
110 |     }
111 | 
112 |     return entry.value;
113 |   }
114 | 
115 |   /** @returns {Promise<boolean>} */
116 |   async has(key) {
117 |     return (await this.get(key)) !== undefined;
118 |   }
119 | 
120 |   /** @returns {Promise<boolean>} */
121 |   async delete(key) {
122 |     const existed = this._store.delete(key);
123 |     if (this._backend?.delete) {
124 |       await this._backend.delete(`${this.agentId}:${key}`);
125 |     }
126 |     return existed;
127 |   }
128 | 
129 |   /** Clear all in-process entries. */
130 |   async clear() {
131 |     this._store.clear();
132 |   }
133 | 
134 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
135 |   // Persistence backend
136 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
137 | 
138 |   /**
139 |    * Attach an external persistence backend.
140 |    *
141 |    * The backend must implement:
142 |    *   async get(key): any
143 |    *   async set(key, value): void
144 |    *   async delete(key): void
145 |    *
146 |    * Example — a Redis client wrapper:
147 |    *   memory.setPersistenceBackend(redisAdapter);
148 |    *
149 |    * @param {object} backend
150 |    */
151 |   setPersistenceBackend(backend) {
152 |     this._backend = backend;
153 |   }
154 | 
155 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
156 |   // Diagnostics
157 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
158 | 
159 |   getStatus() {
160 |     return {
161 |       agentId:       this.agentId,
162 |       entryCount:    this._store.size,
163 |       maxEntries:    this.maxEntries,
164 |       ttlMs:         this.ttlMs,
165 |       hasBackend:    !!this._backend,
166 |     };
167 |   }
168 | }
169 | 
170 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
171 | // SemanticSchemaMemory — schema similarity index
172 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
173 | 
174 | /**
175 |  * Lightweight schema similarity store.
176 |  *
177 |  * Stores fingerprints of previously seen schemas so the orchestrator can
178 |  * re-use analysis plans without re-running all agents.
179 |  *
180 |  * Similarity is computed via Jaccard similarity on column name sets.
181 |  * Replace `_similarity()` with a real embedding model for production.
182 |  */
183 | export class SemanticSchemaMemory {
184 |   /**
185 |    * @param {object} [opts]
186 |    * @param {number} [opts.maxSchemas=200]     - Max schemas to remember
187 |    * @param {number} [opts.similarityThreshold=0.75]
188 |    */
189 |   constructor({ maxSchemas = 200, similarityThreshold = 0.75 } = {}) {
190 |     this.maxSchemas          = maxSchemas;
191 |     this.similarityThreshold = similarityThreshold;
192 | 
193 |     /** @type {Map<string, { schema: object, plan: any, hitCount: number }>} */
194 |     this._index = new Map();
195 |   }
196 | 
197 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
198 |   // Store / Lookup
199 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
200 | 
201 |   /**
202 |    * Store a schema profile alongside the analytics plan that was generated for it.
203 |    *
204 |    * @param {object} schemaProfile   - Normalised schema profile
205 |    * @param {any}    analyticsPlan   - The plan to cache
206 |    * @returns {string}               - Schema fingerprint key
207 |    */
208 |   store(schemaProfile, analyticsPlan) {
209 |     const key = this._fingerprint(schemaProfile);
210 | 
211 |     // LRU eviction
212 |     if (this._index.size >= this.maxSchemas) {
213 |       const lruKey = this._lruKey();
214 |       this._index.delete(lruKey);
215 |     }
216 | 
217 |     this._index.set(key, {
218 |       schema:   schemaProfile,
219 |       plan:     analyticsPlan,
220 |       hitCount: 0,
221 |       storedAt: Date.now(),
222 |     });
223 | 
224 |     return key;
225 |   }
226 | 
227 |   /**
228 |    * Find the most similar stored schema.
229 |    *
230 |    * @param {object} schemaProfile
231 |    * @returns {{ match: object, plan: any, similarity: number } | null}
232 |    */
233 |   findSimilar(schemaProfile) {
234 |     let bestKey        = null;
235 |     let bestSimilarity = 0;
236 | 
237 |     for (const [key, entry] of this._index.entries()) {
238 |       const sim = this._similarity(schemaProfile, entry.schema);
239 |       if (sim > bestSimilarity) {
240 |         bestSimilarity = sim;
241 |         bestKey        = key;
242 |       }
243 |     }
244 | 
245 |     if (bestKey && bestSimilarity >= this.similarityThreshold) {
246 |       const entry = this._index.get(bestKey);
247 |       entry.hitCount++;
248 |       return { match: entry.schema, plan: entry.plan, similarity: bestSimilarity };
249 |     }
250 | 
251 |     return null;
252 |   }
253 | 
254 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
255 |   // Internals
256 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
257 | 
258 |   /**
259 |    * Create a stable string key from a schema profile.
260 |    * Uses sorted column names to be order-independent.
261 |    */
262 |   _fingerprint(schemaProfile) {
263 |     const cols = (schemaProfile.columns ?? [])
264 |       .map(c => `${c.name}:${c.type}`)
265 |       .sort()
266 |       .join('|');
267 |     return `${schemaProfile.domain ?? 'generic'}::${cols}`;
268 |   }
269 | 
270 |   /**
271 |    * Jaccard similarity between two schema's column name sets.
272 |    * Returns a value in [0, 1].
273 |    */
274 |   _similarity(a, b) {
275 |     const setA = new Set((a.columns ?? []).map(c => c.name.toLowerCase()));
276 |     const setB = new Set((b.columns ?? []).map(c => c.name.toLowerCase()));
277 | 
278 |     const intersection = [...setA].filter(x => setB.has(x)).length;
279 |     const union        = new Set([...setA, ...setB]).size;
280 | 
281 |     return union === 0 ? 0 : intersection / union;
282 |   }
283 | 
284 |   /** Return the key with the lowest hit count (least recently used proxy). */
285 |   _lruKey() {
286 |     let minHits = Infinity;
287 |     let lruKey  = null;
288 |     for (const [key, entry] of this._index.entries()) {
289 |       if (entry.hitCount < minHits) {
290 |         minHits = entry.hitCount;
291 |         lruKey  = key;
292 |       }
293 |     }
294 |     return lruKey;
295 |   }
296 | 
297 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
298 |   // Diagnostics
299 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
300 | 
301 |   getStatus() {
302 |     return {
303 |       storedSchemas:       this._index.size,
304 |       maxSchemas:          this.maxSchemas,
305 |       similarityThreshold: this.similarityThreshold,
306 |       topSchemas: [...this._index.entries()]
307 |         .sort(([, a], [, b]) => b.hitCount - a.hitCount)
308 |         .slice(0, 5)
309 |         .map(([key, e]) => ({ key, hitCount: e.hitCount, domain: e.schema.domain })),
310 |     };
311 |   }
312 | }
313 | 
314 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
315 | // Global singletons
316 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
317 | 
318 | export const globalSchemaMemory = new SemanticSchemaMemory();
319 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-message-bus.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-message-bus.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * @file agent-message-bus.js
  3 |  * @description Lightweight in-process pub-sub message bus for inter-agent communication.
  4 |  *
  5 |  * Agents publish structured messages to named channels.
  6 |  * Other agents subscribe to receive them synchronously or asynchronously.
  7 |  *
  8 |  * This is intentionally simple — no persistence, no external broker.
  9 |  * Swap with Redis Pub/Sub or NATS for distributed deployments.
 10 |  */
 11 | 
 12 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 13 | // AgentMessage — typed message envelope
 14 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 15 | 
 16 | export class AgentMessage {
 17 |   /**
 18 |    * @param {object} opts
 19 |    * @param {string}   opts.fromAgentId   - Sender agent ID
 20 |    * @param {string}   opts.toAgentId     - Target agent ID (or '*' for broadcast)
 21 |    * @param {string}   opts.channel       - Logical channel name (e.g. 'schema.ready')
 22 |    * @param {any}      opts.payload       - The message body
 23 |    * @param {string}   [opts.requestId]   - Correlation ID for tracing
 24 |    * @param {string}   [opts.replyTo]     - Channel to send a response to (optional)
 25 |    */
 26 |   constructor({ fromAgentId, toAgentId = '*', channel, payload, requestId = null, replyTo = null }) {
 27 |     this.id          = `msg-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 7)}`;
 28 |     this.fromAgentId = fromAgentId;
 29 |     this.toAgentId   = toAgentId;
 30 |     this.channel     = channel;
 31 |     this.payload     = payload;
 32 |     this.requestId   = requestId;
 33 |     this.replyTo     = replyTo;
 34 |     this.timestamp   = Date.now();
 35 |   }
 36 | }
 37 | 
 38 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 39 | // AgentMessageBus
 40 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 41 | 
 42 | export class AgentMessageBus {
 43 |   constructor() {
 44 |     /**
 45 |      * channel → Set of subscriber callbacks
 46 |      * @type {Map<string, Set<Function>>}
 47 |      */
 48 |     this._subscribers = new Map();
 49 | 
 50 |     /** Message history — last N messages per channel (for replay / debugging) */
 51 |     this._historyLimit = 50;
 52 | 
 53 |     /** @type {Map<string, AgentMessage[]>} */
 54 |     this._history = new Map();
 55 | 
 56 |     /** Total messages published since startup */
 57 |     this._totalPublished = 0;
 58 |   }
 59 | 
 60 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 61 |   // Publish
 62 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 63 | 
 64 |   /**
 65 |    * Publish a message to a channel.
 66 |    * All subscribers on that channel are called synchronously in order.
 67 |    *
 68 |    * @param {string}   channel        - e.g. 'schema.ready', 'analytics.plan'
 69 |    * @param {any}      payload        - The message body
 70 |    * @param {object}   [opts]         - Optional message metadata
 71 |    * @param {string}   [opts.fromAgentId]
 72 |    * @param {string}   [opts.toAgentId]
 73 |    * @param {string}   [opts.requestId]
 74 |    * @param {string}   [opts.replyTo]
 75 |    * @returns {AgentMessage}          - The created message (for chaining / debugging)
 76 |    */
 77 |   publish(channel, payload, opts = {}) {
 78 |     const message = new AgentMessage({ channel, payload, ...opts });
 79 |     this._record(channel, message);
 80 |     this._totalPublished++;
 81 | 
 82 |     const subscribers = this._subscribers.get(channel) ?? new Set();
 83 |     const wildcardSubscribers = this._subscribers.get('*') ?? new Set();
 84 | 
 85 |     for (const fn of [...subscribers, ...wildcardSubscribers]) {
 86 |       try {
 87 |         fn(message);
 88 |       } catch (err) {
 89 |         console.error(`[AgentMessageBus] Subscriber error on channel "${channel}":`, err.message);
 90 |       }
 91 |     }
 92 | 
 93 |     return message;
 94 |   }
 95 | 
 96 |   /**
 97 |    * Async publish — awaits all subscriber Promises before returning.
 98 |    * Use when subscribers do async work and ordering matters.
 99 |    *
100 |    * @param {string} channel
101 |    * @param {any}    payload
102 |    * @param {object} [opts]
103 |    * @returns {Promise<void>}
104 |    */
105 |   async publishAsync(channel, payload, opts = {}) {
106 |     const message = new AgentMessage({ channel, payload, ...opts });
107 |     this._record(channel, message);
108 |     this._totalPublished++;
109 | 
110 |     const subscribers = [
111 |       ...(this._subscribers.get(channel) ?? []),
112 |       ...(this._subscribers.get('*') ?? []),
113 |     ];
114 | 
115 |     await Promise.allSettled(
116 |       subscribers.map(fn => {
117 |         try { return Promise.resolve(fn(message)); } catch (e) { return Promise.reject(e); }
118 |       })
119 |     );
120 | 
121 |     return message;
122 |   }
123 | 
124 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
125 |   // Subscribe / Unsubscribe
126 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127 | 
128 |   /**
129 |    * Subscribe to a channel.
130 |    *
131 |    * @param {string}   channel   - Channel name or '*' for all messages
132 |    * @param {Function} listener  - Called with (AgentMessage) on every publish
133 |    * @returns {Function}         - Unsubscribe function (call to remove the listener)
134 |    */
135 |   subscribe(channel, listener) {
136 |     if (!this._subscribers.has(channel)) {
137 |       this._subscribers.set(channel, new Set());
138 |     }
139 |     this._subscribers.get(channel).add(listener);
140 | 
141 |     // Return an unsubscribe handle
142 |     return () => this.unsubscribe(channel, listener);
143 |   }
144 | 
145 |   /**
146 |    * One-time subscription — listener fires once then auto-unsubscribes.
147 |    *
148 |    * @param {string}   channel
149 |    * @param {Function} listener
150 |    * @returns {Promise<AgentMessage>}  - Resolves when the message arrives
151 |    */
152 |   once(channel, listener) {
153 |     return new Promise(resolve => {
154 |       const unsubscribe = this.subscribe(channel, (msg) => {
155 |         unsubscribe();
156 |         listener?.(msg);
157 |         resolve(msg);
158 |       });
159 |     });
160 |   }
161 | 
162 |   /**
163 |    * Remove a specific listener from a channel.
164 |    *
165 |    * @param {string}   channel
166 |    * @param {Function} listener
167 |    * @returns {boolean}
168 |    */
169 |   unsubscribe(channel, listener) {
170 |     const subs = this._subscribers.get(channel);
171 |     if (!subs) return false;
172 |     return subs.delete(listener);
173 |   }
174 | 
175 |   /** Remove ALL listeners on a given channel. */
176 |   clearChannel(channel) {
177 |     return this._subscribers.delete(channel);
178 |   }
179 | 
180 |   /** Remove ALL listeners on ALL channels. */
181 |   clearAll() {
182 |     this._subscribers.clear();
183 |   }
184 | 
185 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
186 |   // History / diagnostics
187 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
188 | 
189 |   /**
190 |    * Get recent messages published to a channel (newest last).
191 |    *
192 |    * @param {string} channel
193 |    * @param {number} [limit=10]
194 |    * @returns {AgentMessage[]}
195 |    */
196 |   getHistory(channel, limit = 10) {
197 |     const history = this._history.get(channel) ?? [];
198 |     return history.slice(-limit);
199 |   }
200 | 
201 |   /** Return a snapshot of bus health for monitoring endpoints. */
202 |   getStatus() {
203 |     const channels = {};
204 |     for (const [channel, subs] of this._subscribers.entries()) {
205 |       channels[channel] = {
206 |         subscribers:    subs.size,
207 |         recentMessages: (this._history.get(channel) ?? []).length,
208 |       };
209 |     }
210 | 
211 |     return {
212 |       totalPublished:  this._totalPublished,
213 |       activeChannels:  this._subscribers.size,
214 |       channels,
215 |     };
216 |   }
217 | 
218 |   /** Internal: append a message to the channel's history, honouring the limit. */
219 |   _record(channel, message) {
220 |     if (!this._history.has(channel)) this._history.set(channel, []);
221 |     const hist = this._history.get(channel);
222 |     hist.push(message);
223 |     if (hist.length > this._historyLimit) hist.shift();
224 |   }
225 | }
226 | 
227 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
228 | // Global singleton
229 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
230 | 
231 | export const globalMessageBus = new AgentMessageBus();
232 | 
233 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234 | // Standard channel names — import these to avoid magic strings
235 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
236 | export const CHANNELS = Object.freeze({
237 |   SCHEMA_READY:         'schema.ready',
238 |   ANALYTICS_PLAN_READY: 'analytics.plan.ready',
239 |   TOOLS_SELECTED:       'tools.selected',
240 |   EXECUTION_COMPLETE:   'execution.complete',
241 |   CRITIQUE_READY:       'critique.ready',
242 |   ORCHESTRATION_DONE:   'orchestration.done',
243 |   AGENT_ERROR:          'agent.error',
244 |   PROVIDER_SELECTED:    'provider.selected',
245 | });
246 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-registry.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agent-registry.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * @file agent-registry.js
  3 |  * @description Agent Registry — manages agent lifecycle and discovery.
  4 |  *
  5 |  * Use the exported `globalRegistry` singleton in application code.
  6 |  * Import `AgentRegistry` directly only for testing or isolated scopes.
  7 |  */
  8 | 
  9 | import { AGENT_ROLES } from './agentic-framework.js';
 10 | 
 11 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 12 | // AgentRegistry
 13 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 14 | 
 15 | export class AgentRegistry {
 16 |   constructor() {
 17 |     /** @type {Map<string, import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent>} */
 18 |     this._agents = new Map();
 19 | 
 20 |     /** @type {Map<string, Function[]>} — role → list of listener callbacks */
 21 |     this._listeners = new Map();
 22 |   }
 23 | 
 24 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 25 |   // Registration
 26 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 27 | 
 28 |   /**
 29 |    * Register an agent with the registry.
 30 |    *
 31 |    * @param {import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent} agent
 32 |    * @throws {Error} if the agent is missing required fields or is already registered
 33 |    */
 34 |   register(agent) {
 35 |     if (!agent || !agent.id || !agent.name || !agent.role) {
 36 |       throw new Error('Agent must have id, name, and role before registering');
 37 |     }
 38 | 
 39 |     if (this._agents.has(agent.id)) {
 40 |       throw new Error(
 41 |         `Agent "${agent.id}" is already registered. Use replace() to overwrite.`
 42 |       );
 43 |     }
 44 | 
 45 |     this._agents.set(agent.id, agent);
 46 |     this._emit('register', agent);
 47 |     console.log(`[AgentRegistry] ✅ Registered "${agent.name}" (${agent.id}) as ${agent.role}`);
 48 |     return this; // allow chaining
 49 |   }
 50 | 
 51 |   /**
 52 |    * Replace an existing registration (useful during hot-reload / testing).
 53 |    *
 54 |    * @param {import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent} agent
 55 |    */
 56 |   replace(agent) {
 57 |     if (this._agents.has(agent.id)) {
 58 |       this._emit('unregister', this._agents.get(agent.id));
 59 |     }
 60 |     this._agents.set(agent.id, agent);
 61 |     this._emit('register', agent);
 62 |     console.log(`[AgentRegistry] 🔄 Replaced agent "${agent.id}"`);
 63 |     return this;
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   /**
 67 |    * Remove an agent from the registry.
 68 |    *
 69 |    * @param {string} agentId
 70 |    * @returns {boolean} true if the agent existed and was removed
 71 |    */
 72 |   unregister(agentId) {
 73 |     const agent = this._agents.get(agentId);
 74 |     if (!agent) return false;
 75 | 
 76 |     this._agents.delete(agentId);
 77 |     this._emit('unregister', agent);
 78 |     console.log(`[AgentRegistry] 🗑️  Unregistered "${agentId}"`);
 79 |     return true;
 80 |   }
 81 | 
 82 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 83 |   // Lookup
 84 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 85 | 
 86 |   /**
 87 |    * Get a single agent by ID.
 88 |    *
 89 |    * @param {string} agentId
 90 |    * @returns {import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent|undefined}
 91 |    */
 92 |   getAgent(agentId) {
 93 |     return this._agents.get(agentId);
 94 |   }
 95 | 
 96 |   /**
 97 |    * Get a single agent by ID, throwing if not found.
 98 |    *
 99 |    * @param {string} agentId
100 |    * @returns {import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent}
101 |    */
102 |   requireAgent(agentId) {
103 |     const agent = this._agents.get(agentId);
104 |     if (!agent) {
105 |       throw new Error(`Agent "${agentId}" is not registered. Available: ${this.listIds().join(', ')}`);
106 |     }
107 |     return agent;
108 |   }
109 | 
110 |   /**
111 |    * Return all agents that have a given role.
112 |    *
113 |    * @param {string} role  — one of AGENT_ROLES values
114 |    * @returns {import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent[]}
115 |    */
116 |   getAgentsByRole(role) {
117 |     return [...this._agents.values()].filter(a => a.role === role);
118 |   }
119 | 
120 |   /**
121 |    * Return all agents that have ALL the specified capabilities.
122 |    *
123 |    * @param {string[]} requiredCapabilities
124 |    * @returns {import('./agentic-framework.js').Agent[]}
125 |    */
126 |   findByCapabilities(requiredCapabilities) {
127 |     return [...this._agents.values()].filter(agent =>
128 |       requiredCapabilities.every(cap => agent.capabilities.includes(cap))
129 |     );
130 |   }
131 | 
132 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
133 |   // Enumeration
134 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
135 | 
136 |   /** List all registered agent IDs. */
137 |   listIds() {
138 |     return [...this._agents.keys()];
139 |   }
140 | 
141 |   /**
142 |    * Return a lightweight summary of every registered agent.
143 |    * Safe to expose via API (does not include internal state or data).
144 |    */
145 |   listAgents() {
146 |     return [...this._agents.values()].map(a => ({
147 |       id:           a.id,
148 |       name:         a.name,
149 |       role:         a.role,
150 |       capabilities: a.capabilities,
151 |     }));
152 |   }
153 | 
154 |   /** Total number of registered agents. */
155 |   get size() {
156 |     return this._agents.size;
157 |   }
158 | 
159 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
160 |   // Status & diagnostics
161 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
162 | 
163 |   /**
164 |    * Return a comprehensive status object — useful for health-check endpoints.
165 |    */
166 |   getStatus() {
167 |     const roleBreakdown = {};
168 |     for (const role of Object.values(AGENT_ROLES)) {
169 |       roleBreakdown[role] = 0;
170 |     }
171 |     for (const agent of this._agents.values()) {
172 |       if (roleBreakdown[agent.role] !== undefined) {
173 |         roleBreakdown[agent.role]++;
174 |       } else {
175 |         roleBreakdown[agent.role] = 1;
176 |       }
177 |     }
178 | 
179 |     return {
180 |       totalAgents:   this.size,
181 |       roleBreakdown,
182 |       agents:        this.listAgents(),
183 |     };
184 |   }
185 | 
186 |   /**
187 |    * Collect and return metrics from all registered agents.
188 |    */
189 |   collectMetrics() {
190 |     const metrics = {};
191 |     for (const agent of this._agents.values()) {
192 |       metrics[agent.id] = agent.getStatus?.() ?? { id: agent.id };
193 |     }
194 |     return metrics;
195 |   }
196 | 
197 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
198 |   // Event system (lightweight pub-sub for lifecycle hooks)
199 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
200 | 
201 |   /**
202 |    * Subscribe to registry events.
203 |    *
204 |    * @param {'register'|'unregister'} event
205 |    * @param {Function} listener  — called with the affected Agent
206 |    */
207 |   on(event, listener) {
208 |     if (!this._listeners.has(event)) this._listeners.set(event, []);
209 |     this._listeners.get(event).push(listener);
210 |     return this; // chainable
211 |   }
212 | 
213 |   /** Internal: fire all listeners for an event. */
214 |   _emit(event, agent) {
215 |     const listeners = this._listeners.get(event) ?? [];
216 |     for (const fn of listeners) {
217 |       try { fn(agent); } catch { /* listener errors must not crash the registry */ }
218 |     }
219 |   }
220 | }
221 | 
222 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
223 | // Global singleton
224 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
225 | 
226 | export const globalRegistry = new AgentRegistry();
227 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agentic-framework.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/core/agentic-framework.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * @file agentic-framework.js
  3 |  * @description Core Agentic Framework — base classes and interfaces for all agents.
  4 |  *
  5 |  * This module is the single foundation all agents in InsightFlow build on.
  6 |  * Every agent extends `Agent`, every pipeline step uses `ExecutionContext`,
  7 |  * and every agent response is wrapped in `AgentResult`.
  8 |  */
  9 | 
 10 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 11 | // AGENT_ROLES — canonical role identifiers
 12 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 13 | export const AGENT_ROLES = Object.freeze({
 14 |   SCHEMA_ANALYZER:    'schema_analyzer',
 15 |   ANALYTICS_PLANNER:  'analytics_planner',
 16 |   TOOL_ROUTER:        'tool_router',
 17 |   EXECUTOR:           'executor',
 18 |   CRITIC:             'critic',
 19 |   ORCHESTRATOR:       'orchestrator',
 20 |   AI_PROVIDER:        'ai_provider',
 21 |   MEMORY:             'memory',
 22 | });
 23 | 
 24 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 25 | // Agent — abstract base class
 26 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 27 | 
 28 | /**
 29 |  * Abstract base class that every InsightFlow agent must extend.
 30 |  *
 31 |  * Subclasses MUST implement the `process(context)` method.
 32 |  * All metric tracking, logging, and lifecycle hooks are inherited.
 33 |  */
 34 | export class Agent {
 35 |   /**
 36 |    * @param {string} id          Unique agent identifier (e.g. 'schema-analyzer')
 37 |    * @param {string} name        Human-readable name (e.g. 'Schema Analyzer')
 38 |    * @param {string} role        One of AGENT_ROLES values
 39 |    * @param {string[]} capabilities  What this agent can do (used by registry)
 40 |    */
 41 |   constructor(id, name, role, capabilities = []) {
 42 |     if (!id || !name || !role) {
 43 |       throw new Error('Agent requires id, name, and role');
 44 |     }
 45 | 
 46 |     this.id           = id;
 47 |     this.name         = name;
 48 |     this.role         = role;
 49 |     this.capabilities = capabilities;
 50 | 
 51 |     /** @type {Map<string, any>} — in-process key-value memory */
 52 |     this.memory  = new Map();
 53 | 
 54 |     /** Performance counters */
 55 |     this.metrics = {
 56 |       invocationCount: 0,
 57 |       totalLatencyMs:  0,
 58 |       errorCount:      0,
 59 |       lastInvokedAt:   null,
 60 |     };
 61 |   }
 62 | 
 63 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 64 |   // Core lifecycle
 65 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
 66 | 
 67 |   /**
 68 |    * Process an execution context and return an AgentResult.
 69 |    *
 70 |    * @abstract
 71 |    * @param {ExecutionContext} context
 72 |    * @returns {Promise<AgentResult>}
 73 |    */
 74 |   async process(context) {  // eslint-disable-line no-unused-vars
 75 |     throw new Error(`Agent.process() not implemented for "${this.name}" (${this.id})`);
 76 |   }
 77 | 
 78 |   /**
 79 |    * Convenience wrapper — times the process() call and records metrics.
 80 |    *
 81 |    * @param {ExecutionContext} context
 82 |    * @returns {Promise<AgentResult>}
 83 |    */
 84 |   async run(context) {
 85 |     const start = Date.now();
 86 |     this.metrics.lastInvokedAt = new Date().toISOString();
 87 | 
 88 |     try {
 89 |       const result = await this.process(context);
 90 |       const latency = Date.now() - start;
 91 |       this.recordMetric(latency, result.status);
 92 |       result.latency = latency;
 93 |       return result;
 94 |     } catch (err) {
 95 |       const latency = Date.now() - start;
 96 |       this.recordMetric(latency, 'error');
 97 | 
 98 |       return new AgentResult({
 99 |         agentId:   this.id,
100 |         agentName: this.name,
101 |         status:    'error',
102 |         output:    null,
103 |         reasoning: `Agent "${this.name}" threw an unhandled error: ${err.message}`,
104 |         latency,
105 |       });
106 |     }
107 |   }
108 | 
109 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
110 |   // Memory helpers
111 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
112 | 
113 |   /** Store a value in the agent's in-process memory. */
114 |   remember(key, value) {
115 |     this.memory.set(key, value);
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   /** Retrieve a previously stored value (or `undefined`). */
119 |   recall(key) {
120 |     return this.memory.get(key);
121 |   }
122 | 
123 |   /** Clear all in-process memory. */
124 |   forgetAll() {
125 |     this.memory.clear();
126 |   }
127 | 
128 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
129 |   // Metrics helpers
130 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
131 | 
132 |   /**
133 |    * Record a single invocation's outcome.
134 |    * Called automatically by `run()`.
135 |    *
136 |    * @param {number} latencyMs
137 |    * @param {'success'|'partial'|'error'} status
138 |    */
139 |   recordMetric(latencyMs, status = 'success') {
140 |     this.metrics.invocationCount++;
141 |     this.metrics.totalLatencyMs += latencyMs;
142 |     if (status === 'error') this.metrics.errorCount++;
143 |   }
144 | 
145 |   /** Average latency across all invocations (ms). */
146 |   get avgLatencyMs() {
147 |     if (this.metrics.invocationCount === 0) return 0;
148 |     return Math.round(this.metrics.totalLatencyMs / this.metrics.invocationCount);
149 |   }
150 | 
151 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
152 |   // Introspection
153 |   // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
154 | 
155 |   /** Serialisable status snapshot. */
156 |   getStatus() {
157 |     return {
158 |       id:           this.id,
159 |       name:         this.name,
160 |       role:         this.role,
161 |       capabilities: this.capabilities,
162 |       memorySize:   this.memory.size,
163 |       metrics: {
164 |         ...this.metrics,
165 |         avgLatencyMs: this.avgLatencyMs,
166 |         successRate: this.metrics.invocationCount > 0
167 |           ? ((this.metrics.invocationCount - this.metrics.errorCount) /
168 |               this.metrics.invocationCount * 100).toFixed(1) + '%'
169 |           : 'N/A',
170 |       },
171 |     };
172 |   }
173 | }
174 | 
175 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
176 | // ExecutionContext — immutable pipeline state
177 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
178 | 
179 | /**
180 |  * Immutable state object threaded through every agent in a pipeline run.
181 |  *
182 |  * Agents never mutate this object — they call `recordAgentStep()` to produce
183 |  * a new context with an updated `agentTrail`.
184 |  */
185 | export class ExecutionContext {
186 |   /**
187 |    * @param {object} opts
188 |    * @param {string}  opts.datasetId       - Unique identifier for the dataset
189 |    * @param {object}  opts.schema          - Normalised schema profile (see SchemaProfile)
190 |    * @param {Array}   opts.rows            - Raw data rows (array of objects)
191 |    * @param {string}  opts.goal            - User-supplied analysis goal
192 |    * @param {string}  [opts.userId]        - Authenticated user ID (optional)
193 |    * @param {string}  [opts.requestId]     - Unique request identifier for tracing
194 |    * @param {number}  [opts.timestamp]     - Unix timestamp of context creation
195 |    * @param {Array}   [opts.agentTrail]    - Ordered list of AgentStep records
196 |    * @param {object}  [opts.options]       - Additional pass-through options
197 |    */
198 |   constructor({
199 |     datasetId,
200 |     schema       = {},
201 |     rows         = [],
202 |     goal         = '',
203 |     userId       = null,
204 |     requestId    = null,
205 |     timestamp    = Date.now(),
206 |     agentTrail   = [],
207 |     options      = {},
208 |   } = {}) {
209 |     if (!datasetId) throw new Error('ExecutionContext requires datasetId');
210 | 
211 |     this.datasetId   = datasetId;
212 |     this.schema      = schema;
213 |     this.rows        = rows;
214 |     this.goal        = goal;
215 |     this.userId      = userId;
216 |     this.requestId   = requestId ?? `req-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 7)}`;
217 |     this.timestamp   = timestamp;
218 |     this.agentTrail  = agentTrail;
219 |     this.options     = options;
220 | 
221 |     // Freeze to prevent accidental mutation
222 |     Object.freeze(this);
223 |   }
224 | 
225 |   /**
226 |    * Produce a new context with the agent step appended to the trail.
227 |    * Original context is unchanged.
228 |    *
229 |    * @param {string} agentId    - ID of the agent that just ran
230 |    * @param {string} decision   - Human-readable summary of the agent's decision
231 |    * @param {any}    output     - The raw output from the agent
232 |    * @returns {ExecutionContext}
233 |    */
234 |   recordAgentStep(agentId, decision, output) {
235 |     return new ExecutionContext({
236 |       ...this,
237 |       agentTrail: [
238 |         ...this.agentTrail,
239 |         {
240 |           agentId,
241 |           decision,
242 |           output,
243 |           timestamp: Date.now(),
244 |         },
245 |       ],
246 |     });
247 |   }
248 | 
249 |   /** Number of agents that have run so far. */
250 |   get stageCount() {
251 |     return this.agentTrail.length;
252 |   }
253 | 
254 |   /** Last agent step recorded, or null. */
255 |   get lastStep() {
256 |     return this.agentTrail.length > 0
257 |       ? this.agentTrail[this.agentTrail.length - 1]
258 |       : null;
259 |   }
260 | 
261 |   /** Plain-object summary suitable for logging / API responses. */
262 |   toSummary() {
263 |     return {
264 |       requestId:   this.requestId,
265 |       datasetId:   this.datasetId,
266 |       goal:        this.goal,
267 |       stageCount:  this.stageCount,
268 |       rowCount:    this.rows.length,
269 |       timestamp:   this.timestamp,
270 |     };
271 |   }
272 | }
273 | 
274 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
275 | // AgentResult — standardised response envelope
276 | // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
277 | 
278 | /**
279 |  * Every agent returns an `AgentResult` — the standard envelope for all
280 |  * inter-agent communication.
281 |  */
282 | export class AgentResult {
283 |   /**
284 |    * @param {object} opts
285 |    * @param {string}   opts.agentId        - ID of the producing agent
286 |    * @param {string}   opts.agentName      - Name of the producing agent
287 |    * @param {'success'|'partial'|'error'} [opts.status='success']
288 |    * @param {any}      opts.output         - The actual result payload
289 |    * @param {string}   [opts.reasoning]    - Human-readable explanation
290 |    * @param {number}   [opts.latency]      - Time taken in ms (set by Agent.run)
291 |    * @param {number}   [opts.tokensUsed]   - LLM tokens consumed (if applicable)
292 |    * @param {string[]} [opts.nextAgentIds] - Suggested next agents to invoke
293 |    * @param {object}   [opts.metadata]     - Any extra key-value data
294 |    */
295 |   constructor({
296 |     agentId,
297 |     agentName,
298 |     status       = 'success',
299 |     output,
300 |     reasoning    = '',
301 |     latency      = 0,
302 |     tokensUsed   = [REDACTED_SECRET],
303 |     nextAgentIds = [],
304 |     metadata     = {},
305 |   }) {
306 |     this.agentId      = agentId;
307 |     this.agentName    = agentName;
308 |     this.status       = status;       // 'success' | 'partial' | 'error'
309 |     this.output       = output;
310 |     this.reasoning    = reasoning;
311 |     this.latency      = latency;
312 |     this.tokensUsed   = tokensUsed;
313 |     this.nextAgentIds = nextAgentIds;
314 |     this.metadata     = metadata;
315 |     this.createdAt    = new Date().toISOString();
316 |   }
317 | 
318 |   /** Whether this result represents a successful outcome. */
319 |   get isSuccess() { return this.status === 'success'; }
320 | 
321 |   /** Whether this result represents a partial outcome. */
322 |   get isPartial() { return this.status === 'partial'; }
323 | 
324 |   /** Whether this result represents a failure. */
325 |   get isError() { return this.status === 'error'; }
326 | 
327 |   /** Serialisable representation. */
328 |   toJSON() {
329 |     return {
330 |       agentId:      this.agentId,
331 |       agentName:    this.agentName,
332 |       status:       this.status,
333 |       reasoning:    this.reasoning,
334 |       latency:      this.latency,
335 |       tokensUsed:   [REDACTED_SECRET],
336 |       nextAgentIds: this.nextAgentIds,
337 |       createdAt:    this.createdAt,
338 |       // Do NOT include raw output by default — it can be very large
339 |     };
340 |   }
341 | }
342 | 
```
