### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/dashboard-accuracy-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/dashboard-accuracy-engine.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * @file dashboard-accuracy-engine.js
  3 |  * @description Dashboard Accuracy Engine
  4 |  *
  5 |  * Ensures KPI and custom chart generation are 100% schema-accurate.
  6 |  *
  7 |  * Design notes:
  8 |  * - Intentionally built on top of the existing schema-dashboard-engine.js conventions.
  9 |  *   Column roles use 'metric' | 'dimension' | 'date' | 'key' — matching enrichSchemaPacket().
 10 |  * - Does NOT replace schema-dashboard-engine.js or dashboard-ai-agent.js.
 11 |  *   Import this engine when you need validated, programmatic chart/KPI construction
 12 |  *   (e.g. from the agentic orchestrator or the /api/agentic/analyze endpoint).
 13 |  * - Every public method is synchronous except DashboardBuilder.buildDashboard().
 14 |  *
 15 |  * Key Goals:
 16 |  *   1. 100% schema compliance — no invalid column references
 17 |  *   2. Type safety — correct aggregations for column types
 18 |  *   3. Custom chart generation — unlimited chart combinations
 19 |  *   4. KPI accuracy — computed from actual row data, never invented
 20 |  *   5. Domain awareness — understands data semantics and relationships
 21 |  */
 22 | 
 23 | import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
 24 | 
 25 | // ============================================================================
 26 | // CONSTANTS — kept in sync with schema-dashboard-engine.js
 27 | // ============================================================================
 28 | 
 29 | const VALID_CHART_TYPES = new Set([
 30 |   'bar', 'line', 'area', 'pie', 'scatter', 'histogram', 'table', 'heatmap',
 31 | ]);
 32 | 
 33 | const VALID_AGGREGATIONS = new Set([
 34 |   'sum', 'avg', 'average', 'count', 'min', 'max', 'median',
 35 | ]);
 36 | 
 37 | // Normalise role name to one of: 'metric' | 'dimension' | 'date' | 'key'
 38 | // This matches what enrichSchemaPacket() in schema-dashboard-engine.js produces.
 39 | const ROLE_NORMALISATION = {
 40 |   metric:    'metric',
 41 |   measure:   'metric',   // alias — the new agentic framework uses 'measure'
 42 |   dimension: 'dimension',
 43 |   date:      'date',
 44 |   time:      'date',     // alias
 45 |   key:       'key',
 46 |   id:        'key',      // alias
 47 |   unique:    'key',      // alias
 48 | };
 49 | 
 50 | // ============================================================================
 51 | // PART 1: ENHANCED SCHEMA
 52 | // ============================================================================
 53 | 
 54 | /**
 55 |  * Wraps a raw schema profile (from schema-detector or schema-normalizer) and
 56 |  * enriches it with computed metadata.
 57 |  *
 58 |  * Compatible with schemas produced by:
 59 |  *   - schema-packet-builder.js  → buildSchemaPacketAsync()
 60 |  *   - schema-normalizer.js      → SchemaNormalizer.normalize()
 61 |  *   - schema-detector.js        → schemaDetector.analyzeDataset()
 62 |  */
 63 | export class EnhancedSchema {
 64 |   /**
 65 |    * @param {object} rawSchema
 66 |    * @param {Array}  rawSchema.columns
 67 |    * @param {string} [rawSchema.name]
 68 |    * @param {number} [rawSchema.rowCount]
 69 |    */
 70 |   constructor(rawSchema) {
 71 |     if (!rawSchema || !Array.isArray(rawSchema.columns)) {
 72 |       throw new TypeError('EnhancedSchema requires a schema object with a columns array');
 73 |     }
 74 | 
 75 |     this.name       = rawSchema.name     ?? 'Dataset';
 76 |     this.rowCount   = rawSchema.rowCount ?? 0;
 77 |     this.columns    = this._normaliseColumns(rawSchema.columns);
 78 |     this.columnCount = this.columns.length;
 79 | 
 80 |     // Typed views
 81 |     this.metrics    = this.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'metric');
 82 |     this.dimensions = this.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'dimension');
 83 |     this.dates      = this.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'date');
 84 |     this.keys       = this.columns.filter(c => c.role === 'key');
 85 | 
 86 |     // Build a normalised-name → column lookup for O(1) resolution
 87 |     this._index = new Map();
 88 |     for (const col of this.columns) {
 89 |       this._index.set(this._normaliseKey(col.name), col);
 90 |     }
 91 |   }
 92 | 
 93 |   // ── Column normalisation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
 94 | 
 95 |   /**
 96 |    * Normalise each raw column to a consistent shape.
 97 |    * @private
 98 |    */
 99 |   _normaliseColumns(rawColumns) {
100 |     return rawColumns.map(col => {
101 |       const type    = this._inferType(col);
102 |       const rawRole = col.role ?? col.chartType ?? '';
103 |       const role    = ROLE_NORMALISATION[rawRole.toLowerCase()] ?? this._inferRole(col, type);
104 | 
105 |       return {
106 |         name:          col.name,
107 |         type,
108 |         role,
109 |         cardinality:   col.cardinality    ?? 0,
110 |         nullCount:     col.nullCount      ?? 0,
111 |         domain:        col.domain         ?? col.domainTag ?? this._inferDomain(col),
112 |         aggregatable:  type === 'numeric' || (type === 'date' && role === 'date'),
113 |         sortable:      type !== 'text',
114 |       };
115 |     });
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   /**
119 |    * Normalise data type into one of: numeric | date | boolean | text
120 |    * @private
121 |    */
122 |   _inferType(col) {
123 |     const t = String(col.type ?? '').toLowerCase();
124 | 
125 |     if (['integer', 'number', 'numeric', 'float', 'decimal', 'int', 'double'].includes(t))
126 |       return 'numeric';
127 |     if (['date', 'datetime', 'timestamp', 'time'].includes(t))
128 |       return 'date';
129 |     if (['boolean', 'bool'].includes(t))
130 |       return 'boolean';
131 | 
132 |     return 'text';
133 |   }
134 | 
135 |   /**
136 |    * Infer column role when the raw schema doesn't supply one.
137 |    * @private
138 |    */
139 |   _inferRole(col, type) {
140 |     const name = String(col.name ?? '').toLowerCase();
141 | 
142 |     // Key: ID-like column with high cardinality
143 |     if (/\bid\b|_id$|uuid|guid/.test(name) && (col.cardinality ?? 0) > 50)
144 |       return 'key';
145 | 
146 |     // Date
147 |     if (type === 'date' || /\b(date|time|datetime|timestamp|created|updated)\b/.test(name))
148 |       return 'date';
149 | 
150 |     // Numeric → metric or dimension depending on cardinality
151 |     if (type === 'numeric') {
152 |       return (col.cardinality ?? 0) < 100 ? 'dimension' : 'metric';
153 |     }
154 | 
155 |     return 'dimension';
156 |   }
157 | 
158 |   /**
159 |    * Infer business domain from column name.
160 |    * @private
161 |    */
162 |   _inferDomain(col) {
163 |     const name = String(col.name ?? '').toLowerCase();
164 | 
165 |     if (/salary|compensation|wage|pay|income/.test(name))      return 'compensation';
166 |     if (/revenue|sales|invoice|transaction|order|purchase/.test(name)) return 'financial';
167 |     if (/amount|price|cost|profit|fee|total|value/.test(name)) return 'financial';
168 |     if (/product|item|sku|category|type/.test(name))           return 'product';
169 |     if (/customer|user|account|person|name|contact/.test(name)) return 'customer';
170 |     if (/date|time|month|year|quarter|week|day/.test(name))    return 'temporal';
171 |     if (/country|region|city|location|state|address/.test(name)) return 'geographic';
172 |     if (/department|team|group|division|role|position/.test(name)) return 'organization';
173 |     if (/status|stage|phase|flag|active|enabled/.test(name))   return 'status';
174 | 
175 |     return 'generic';
176 |   }
177 | 
178 |   /** Produce a normalised string key for fuzzy matching. @private */
179 |   _normaliseKey(name) {
180 |     return String(name ?? '').toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '');
181 |   }
182 | 
183 |   // ── Public lookup API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184 | 
185 |   /**
186 |    * Find a column by name (exact → partial → substring), optionally restricting
187 |    * to a preferred role.
188 |    *
189 |    * @param {string}           name
190 |    * @param {string|string[]}  [preferredRole]  - 'metric', 'dimension', 'date', 'key' or array
191 |    * @returns {object|null}
192 |    */
193 |   findColumn(name, preferredRole = null) {
194 |     if (!name) return null;
195 | 
196 |     const roles = preferredRole
197 |       ? (Array.isArray(preferredRole) ? preferredRole : [preferredRole])
198 |           .map(r => ROLE_NORMALISATION[r] ?? r)
199 |       : null;
200 | 
201 |     const roleOk = col => !roles || roles.includes(col.role);
202 |     const nKey   = this._normaliseKey(name);
203 | 
204 |     // 1. Exact normalised match
205 |     const exact = this._index.get(nKey);
206 |     if (exact && roleOk(exact)) return exact;
207 | 
208 |     // 2. Any column whose normalised name includes the query
209 |     const partial = this.columns.find(c =>
210 |       roleOk(c) && this._normaliseKey(c.name).includes(nKey)
211 |     );
212 |     if (partial) return partial;
213 | 
214 |     // 3. Query contains normalised column name
215 |     const superstring = this.columns.find(c =>
216 |       roleOk(c) && nKey.includes(this._normaliseKey(c.name))
217 |     );
218 | 
219 |     return superstring ?? null;
220 |   }
221 | 
222 |   /**
223 |    * Validate a column reference.
224 |    *
225 |    * @param {string} columnName
226 |    * @returns {{ valid: boolean, column?: object, error?: string }}
227 |    */
228 |   validateColumn(columnName) {
229 |     if (!columnName) return { valid: false, error: 'Column name is required' };
230 | 
231 |     const col = this.findColumn(columnName);
232 |     if (!col) {
233 |       const available = this.columns.map(c => `"${c.name}"`).join(', ');
234 |       return {
235 |         valid: false,
236 |         error: `Column "${columnName}" not found. Available: ${available}`,
237 |       };
238 |     }
239 | 
240 |     return { valid: true, column: col };
241 |   }
242 | 
243 |   /**
244 |    * Return column sets suited for a particular chart type.
245 |    *
246 |    * @param {string} chartType
247 |    * @returns {{ x: object[], y: object[]|null }}
248 |    */
249 |   getRecommendedColumnsFor(chartType) {
250 |     switch (chartType) {
251 |       case 'scatter':    return { x: this.metrics,    y: this.metrics };
252 |       case 'histogram':  return { x: this.metrics,    y: null };
253 |       case 'line':
254 |       case 'area':       return { x: this.dates.concat(this.dimensions), y: this.metrics };
255 |       case 'pie':        return { x: this.dimensions, y: this.metrics };
256 |       case 'bar':
257 |       default:           return { x: this.dimensions.concat(this.dates), y: this.metrics };
258 |     }
259 |   }
260 | }
261 | 
262 | // ============================================================================
263 | // PART 2: KPI CALCULATOR
264 | // ============================================================================
265 | 
266 | /**
267 |  * Computes KPI values directly from row data — never invented.
268 |  * Always validates metric column against the schema before computing.
269 |  */
270 | export class KPICalculator {
271 |   /**
272 |    * @param {EnhancedSchema} schema
273 |    * @param {Array<object>}  rows
274 |    */
275 |   constructor(schema, rows) {
276 |     if (!(schema instanceof EnhancedSchema)) {
277 |       throw new TypeError('KPICalculator requires an EnhancedSchema instance');
278 |     }
279 |     this.schema = schema;
280 |     this.rows   = Array.isArray(rows) ? rows : [];
281 |   }
282 | 
283 |   // ── Core computation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
284 | 
285 |   /**
286 |    * Compute a single aggregated value from a validated column.
287 |    *
288 |    * @param {string} columnName
289 |    * @param {'sum'|'avg'|'average'|'count'|'min'|'max'|'median'} aggregation
290 |    * @param {object} [filters={}]   - { columnName: value } equality filters
291 |    * @returns {number}
292 |    */
293 |   compute(columnName, aggregation = 'sum', filters = {}) {
294 |     const agg = aggregation.toLowerCase();
295 | 
296 |     // Special-case: count total records (no column needed)
297 |     if (agg === 'count' && (!columnName || columnName === '*')) {
298 |       return this._applyFilters(filters).length;
299 |     }
300 | 
301 |     // Validate column exists
302 |     const validation = this.schema.validateColumn(columnName);
303 |     if (!validation.valid) throw new Error(validation.error);
304 | 
305 |     const col = validation.column;
306 | 
307 |     // Numeric aggregations require a numeric column
308 |     if (['sum', 'avg', 'average', 'min', 'max', 'median'].includes(agg) && col.type !== 'numeric') {
309 |       throw new Error(
310 |         `Aggregation "${agg}" requires a numeric column — "${col.name}" is ${col.type}`
311 |       );
312 |     }
313 | 
314 |     const filteredRows = this._applyFilters(filters);
315 |     const values = filteredRows
316 |       .map(row => {
317 |         const n = Number(row[col.name]);
318 |         return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : null;
319 |       })
320 |       .filter(v => v !== null);
321 | 
322 |     if (agg === 'count') return filteredRows.length;
323 |     if (!values.length)  return 0;
324 | 
325 |     switch (agg) {
326 |       case 'sum':                        return values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
327 |       case 'avg':
328 |       case 'average':                    return values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
329 |       case 'min':                        return Math.min(...values);
330 |       case 'max':                        return Math.max(...values);
331 |       case 'median':                     return this._median(values);
332 |       default: throw new Error(`Unknown aggregation: "${aggregation}"`);
333 |     }
334 |   }
335 | 
336 |   /**
337 |    * Build a full KPI object from a specification.
338 |    *
339 |    * @param {object} spec
340 |    * @param {string}  spec.title
341 |    * @param {string}  spec.metric       - column name (or '*' for count)
342 |    * @param {string}  [spec.aggregation='sum']
343 |    * @param {object}  [spec.filters={}]
344 |    * @param {string}  [spec.icon]
345 |    * @param {string}  [spec.status]
346 |    * @returns {object}
347 |    */
348 |   generateKPI(spec) {
349 |     if (!spec.title) throw new Error('KPI spec requires a title');
350 |     if (!spec.metric) throw new Error('KPI spec requires a metric column name');
351 | 
352 |     const aggregation = spec.aggregation ?? 'sum';
353 |     const value       = this.compute(spec.metric, aggregation, spec.filters ?? {});
354 | 
355 |     // Resolve the column for formatting hints (may be null for '*' count)
356 |     const col = spec.metric === '*'
357 |       ? null
358 |       : this.schema.findColumn(spec.metric);
359 | 
360 |     return {
361 |       id:          randomUUID(),
362 |       title:       spec.title,
363 |       metric:      col?.name ?? spec.metric,
364 |       aggregation,
365 |       value,
366 |       formatted:   this._formatValue(value, col),
367 |       icon:        spec.icon   ?? 'chart-bar',
368 |       status:      spec.status ?? 'default',
369 |     };
370 |   }
371 | 
372 |   /**
373 |    * Auto-generate up to 6 sensible KPIs for a dataset.
374 |    * Falls back gracefully when the dataset has few/no numeric columns.
375 |    *
376 |    * @returns {object[]}
377 |    */
378 |   buildDefaultKPIs() {
379 |     const kpis = [];
380 | 
381 |     // Always: total records
382 |     kpis.push({
383 |       id:          randomUUID(),
384 |       title:       'Total Records',
385 |       metric:      '*',
386 |       aggregation: 'count',
387 |       value:       this.rows.length,
388 |       formatted:   this.rows.length.toLocaleString(),
389 |       icon:        'database',
390 |       status:      'default',
391 |     });
392 | 
393 |     // Top 3 metric columns → sum + avg
394 |     for (const metric of this.schema.metrics.slice(0, 3)) {
395 |       const total = this.compute(metric.name, 'sum');
396 |       const avg   = this.compute(metric.name, 'avg');
397 | 
398 |       kpis.push({
399 |         id:          randomUUID(),
400 |         title:       `Total ${_humanize(metric.name)}`,
401 |         metric:      metric.name,
402 |         aggregation: 'sum',
403 |         value:       total,
404 |         formatted:   this._formatValue(total, metric),
405 |         icon:        'chart-bar',
406 |         status:      'default',
407 |       });
408 | 
409 |       kpis.push({
410 |         id:          randomUUID(),
411 |         title:       `Avg ${_humanize(metric.name)}`,
412 |         metric:      metric.name,
413 |         aggregation: 'avg',
414 |         value:       avg,
415 |         formatted:   this._formatValue(avg, metric),
416 |         icon:        'chart-line',
417 |         status:      'default',
418 |       });
419 |     }
420 | 
421 |     return kpis.slice(0, 6);
422 |   }
423 | 
424 |   // ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
425 | 
426 |   /** Apply equality filters to rows. @private */
427 |   _applyFilters(filters = {}) {
428 |     const entries = Object.entries(filters).filter(([, v]) => v !== undefined && v !== null && v !== '');
429 |     if (!entries.length) return this.rows;
430 | 
431 |     return this.rows.filter(row =>
432 |       entries.every(([key, val]) => {
433 |         const col = this.schema.findColumn(key);
434 |         if (!col) return false;
435 |         return String(row[col.name] ?? '').toLowerCase() === String(val).toLowerCase();
436 |       })
437 |     );
438 |   }
439 | 
440 |   /** Median of an already-validated numeric array. @private */
441 |   _median(values) {
442 |     const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
443 |     const mid    = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
444 |     return sorted.length % 2 ? sorted[mid] : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
445 |   }
446 | 
447 |   /**
448 |    * Format a number for display based on the column's domain.
449 |    * @private
450 |    */
451 |   _formatValue(value, column) {
452 |     if (typeof value !== 'number') return String(value);
453 | 
454 |     // Currency domains
455 |     if (column?.domain === 'financial' || column?.domain === 'compensation') {
456 |       if (value >= 1_000_000) return `$${(value / 1_000_000).toFixed(2)}M`;
457 |       if (value >= 1_000)     return `$${(value / 1_000).toFixed(1)}K`;
458 |       return `$${value.toLocaleString('en-US', { maximumFractionDigits: 2 })}`;
459 |     }
460 | 
461 |     // Large number compaction
462 |     if (value >= 1_000_000) return `${(value / 1_000_000).toFixed(2)}M`;
463 |     if (value >= 1_000)     return `${(value / 1_000).toFixed(1)}K`;
464 | 
465 |     return value.toFixed(2);
466 |   }
467 | }
468 | 
469 | // ============================================================================
470 | // PART 3: CUSTOM CHART GENERATOR
471 | // ============================================================================
472 | 
473 | /**
474 |  * Generates chart data objects that are 100% validated against the schema.
475 |  *
476 |  * All chart objects produced here are wire-compatible with the shape returned by
477 |  * executeChartSpec() in schema-dashboard-engine.js:
478 |  *   { id, type, title, xKey, yKey, data, aggregation, rowCount }
479 |  */
480 | export class CustomChartGenerator {
481 |   /**
482 |    * @param {EnhancedSchema} schema
483 |    * @param {Array<object>}  rows
484 |    */
485 |   constructor(schema, rows) {
486 |     if (!(schema instanceof EnhancedSchema)) {
487 |       throw new TypeError('CustomChartGenerator requires an EnhancedSchema instance');
488 |     }
489 |     this.schema = schema;
490 |     this.rows   = Array.isArray(rows) ? rows : [];
491 |   }
492 | 
493 |   // ── Validation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
494 | 
495 |   /**
496 |    * Validate a chart specification before attempting to generate data.
497 |    *
498 |    * @param {object} spec
499 |    * @returns {{ valid: boolean, errors: string[] }}
500 |    */
501 |   validateChartSpec(spec) {
502 |     const errors = [];
503 | 
504 |     if (!VALID_CHART_TYPES.has(spec.type)) {
505 |       errors.push(`Unknown chart type "${spec.type}". Valid: ${[...VALID_CHART_TYPES].join(', ')}`);
506 |     }
507 | 
508 |     if (spec.aggregation && !VALID_AGGREGATIONS.has(spec.aggregation.toLowerCase())) {
509 |       errors.push(`Unknown aggregation "${spec.aggregation}". Valid: ${[...VALID_AGGREGATIONS].join(', ')}`);
510 |     }
511 | 
512 |     // Type-specific column requirements
513 |     if (spec.type === 'histogram') {
514 |       const v = spec.xKey ? this.schema.validateColumn(spec.xKey) : { valid: false, error: 'xKey required' };
515 |       if (!v.valid) errors.push(`Histogram: ${v.error}`);
516 |       else if (v.column.type !== 'numeric') errors.push('Histogram requires a numeric column');
517 | 
518 |     } else if (spec.type === 'scatter') {
519 |       const vx = spec.xKey ? this.schema.validateColumn(spec.xKey) : { valid: false, error: 'xKey required' };
520 |       const vy = spec.yKey ? this.schema.validateColumn(spec.yKey) : { valid: false, error: 'yKey required' };
521 |       if (!vx.valid) errors.push(`Scatter X: ${vx.error}`);
522 |       else if (vx.column.type !== 'numeric') errors.push('Scatter xKey must be numeric');
523 |       if (!vy.valid) errors.push(`Scatter Y: ${vy.error}`);
524 |       else if (vy.column.type !== 'numeric') errors.push('Scatter yKey must be numeric');
525 | 
526 |     } else {
527 |       // Bar, line, area, pie, table, heatmap
528 |       if (!spec.xKey) {
529 |         errors.push(`xKey is required for "${spec.type}" chart`);
530 |       } else {
531 |         const vx = this.schema.validateColumn(spec.xKey);
532 |         if (!vx.valid) errors.push(`X-axis: ${vx.error}`);
533 |       }
534 | 
535 |       if (spec.yKey) {
536 |         const vy = this.schema.validateColumn(spec.yKey);
537 |         if (!vy.valid) errors.push(`Y-axis: ${vy.error}`);
538 |         else if (vy.column.type !== 'numeric' && spec.aggregation !== 'count') {
539 |           errors.push(`Y-axis "${spec.yKey}" is not numeric — use aggregation "count" or pick a numeric column`);
540 |         }
541 |       }
542 |     }
543 | 
544 |     return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
545 |   }
546 | 
547 |   // ── Chart generation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
548 | 
549 |   /**
550 |    * Generate a chart object from a spec, or throw a descriptive error.
551 |    *
552 |    * @param {object}  spec
553 |    * @param {string}  spec.type
554 |    * @param {string}  [spec.xKey]
555 |    * @param {string}  [spec.yKey]
556 |    * @param {string}  [spec.aggregation='sum']
557 |    * @param {number}  [spec.limit=10]
558 |    * @param {string}  [spec.title]
559 |    * @param {object}  [spec.filters={}]
560 |    * @returns {object}
561 |    */
562 |   generateChart(spec) {
563 |     const validation = this.validateChartSpec(spec);
564 |     if (!validation.valid) {
565 |       throw new Error(`Invalid chart spec:\n  • ${validation.errors.join('\n  • ')}`);
566 |     }
567 | 
568 |     const type        = spec.type.toLowerCase();
569 |     const aggregation = (spec.aggregation ?? 'sum').toLowerCase();
570 |     const limit       = Math.min(spec.limit ?? 10, 200);
571 |     const rows        = this._applyFilters(spec.filters ?? {});
572 | 
573 |     let result;
574 |     switch (type) {
575 |       case 'histogram': result = this._histogram(spec, rows);                    break;
576 |       case 'scatter':   result = this._scatter(spec, rows, limit);               break;
577 |       case 'pie':       result = this._grouped(spec, aggregation, rows, limit);  break;
578 |       default:          result = this._grouped(spec, aggregation, rows, limit);  break;
579 |     }
580 | 
581 |     return {
582 |       id:          randomUUID(),
583 |       type,
584 |       title:       spec.title ?? this._autoTitle(spec),
585 |       xKey:        result.xKey,
586 |       yKey:        result.yKey,
587 |       data:        result.data,
588 |       aggregation: ['histogram', 'scatter'].includes(type) ? null : aggregation,
589 |       rowCount:    result.data.length,
590 |     };
591 |   }
592 | 
593 |   // ── Chart type implementations ───────────────────────────────────────────
594 | 
595 |   /** Grouped chart (bar, line, area, pie). @private */
596 |   _grouped(spec, aggregation, rows, limit) {
597 |     const xCol = this.schema.findColumn(spec.xKey);
598 |     const yCol = spec.yKey ? this.schema.findColumn(spec.yKey) : null;
599 |     const yKey = aggregation === 'count' ? 'count' : (yCol?.name ?? 'count');
600 | 
601 |     // Group rows by xKey
602 |     const groups = new Map();
603 |     for (const row of rows) {
604 |       const label    = String(row[xCol.name] ?? 'Unknown').trim() || 'Unknown';
605 |       const rawValue = yCol ? Number(row[yCol.name]) : 1;
606 |       const current  = groups.get(label) ?? { sum: 0, count: 0, min: Infinity, max: -Infinity };
607 | 
608 |       if (Number.isFinite(rawValue)) {
609 |         current.sum  += rawValue;
610 |         current.count++;
611 |         current.min   = Math.min(current.min, rawValue);
612 |         current.max   = Math.max(current.max, rawValue);
613 |       }
614 |       groups.set(label, current);
615 |     }
616 | 
617 |     // Aggregate each group
618 |     let data = [...groups.entries()].map(([label, g]) => {
619 |       let value;
620 |       switch (aggregation) {
621 |         case 'avg':
622 |         case 'average': value = g.count > 0 ? g.sum / g.count : 0; break;
623 |         case 'min':     value = g.min === Infinity  ? 0 : g.min;   break;
624 |         case 'max':     value = g.max === -Infinity ? 0 : g.max;   break;
625 |         case 'count':   value = g.count;                            break;
626 |         default:        value = g.sum;                              break;
627 |       }
628 |       return { [xCol.name]: label, [yKey]: Number(value.toFixed(2)) };
629 |     });
630 | 
631 |     // Sort: time/date → chronological, others → descending value
632 |     if (spec.type === 'line' || spec.type === 'area') {
633 |       data = data.sort((a, b) => String(a[xCol.name]).localeCompare(String(b[xCol.name])));
634 |     } else {
635 |       data = data.sort((a, b) => Number(b[yKey]) - Number(a[yKey]));
636 |     }
637 | 
638 |     return { data: data.slice(0, limit), xKey: xCol.name, yKey };
639 |   }
640 | 
641 |   /** Histogram (numeric distribution). @private */
642 |   _histogram(spec, rows, bins = 10) {
643 |     const col    = this.schema.findColumn(spec.xKey);
644 |     const values = rows.map(r => Number(r[col.name])).filter(v => Number.isFinite(v));
645 | 
646 |     if (!values.length) return { data: [], xKey: 'range', yKey: 'count' };
647 | 
648 |     const min     = Math.min(...values);
649 |     const max     = Math.max(...values);
650 |     const binSize = (max - min) / bins || 1;
651 | 
652 |     const buckets = Array.from({ length: bins }, (_, i) => ({
653 |       range: `${Number((min + i * binSize).toFixed(2))}-${Number((min + (i + 1) * binSize).toFixed(2))}`,
654 |       count: 0,
655 |     }));
656 | 
657 |     for (const v of values) {
658 |       const idx = Math.min(Math.floor((v - min) / binSize), bins - 1);
659 |       buckets[idx].count++;
660 |     }
661 | 
662 |     return { data: buckets, xKey: 'range', yKey: 'count' };
663 |   }
664 | 
665 |   /** Scatter plot (two numeric columns). @private */
666 |   _scatter(spec, rows, limit) {
667 |     const xCol = this.schema.findColumn(spec.xKey);
668 |     const yCol = this.schema.findColumn(spec.yKey);
669 | 
670 |     const data = rows
671 |       .map(row => ({
672 |         [xCol.name]: Number(row[xCol.name]),
673 |         [yCol.name]: Number(row[yCol.name]),
674 |       }))
675 |       .filter(row => Number.isFinite(row[xCol.name]) && Number.isFinite(row[yCol.name]))
676 |       .slice(0, limit);
677 | 
678 |     return { data, xKey: xCol.name, yKey: yCol.name };
679 |   }
680 | 
681 |   /** Apply equality filters to rows. @private */
682 |   _applyFilters(filters = {}) {
683 |     const entries = Object.entries(filters).filter(([, v]) => v !== undefined && v !== null && v !== '');
684 |     if (!entries.length) return this.rows;
685 | 
686 |     return this.rows.filter(row =>
687 |       entries.every(([key, val]) => {
688 |         const col = this.schema.findColumn(key);
689 |         return col
690 |           ? String(row[col.name] ?? '').toLowerCase() === String(val).toLowerCase()
691 |           : false;
692 |       })
693 |     );
694 |   }
695 | 
696 |   // ── Recommendations & helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────
697 | 
698 |   /**
699 |    * Generate up to 6 sensible chart recommendations for a schema.
700 |    *
701 |    * @returns {object[]}  - Array of chart specs (pass each to generateChart())
702 |    */
703 |   getRecommendations() {
704 |     const recs = [];
705 | 
706 |     // Metric × Dimension → bar
707 |     for (const metric of this.schema.metrics.slice(0, 2)) {
708 |       for (const dim of this.schema.dimensions.slice(0, 2)) {
709 |         recs.push({
710 |           type:        'bar',
711 |           title:       `${_humanize(metric.name)} by ${_humanize(dim.name)}`,
712 |           xKey:        dim.name,
713 |           yKey:        metric.name,
714 |           aggregation: 'sum',
715 |           limit:       10,
716 |         });
717 |       }
718 |     }
719 | 
720 |     // Metric over Time → line
721 |     const dateCol = this.schema.dates[0];
722 |     for (const metric of this.schema.metrics.slice(0, 1)) {
723 |       if (dateCol) {
724 |         recs.push({
725 |           type:        'line',
726 |           title:       `${_humanize(metric.name)} Over Time`,
727 |           xKey:        dateCol.name,
728 |           yKey:        metric.name,
729 |           aggregation: 'sum',
730 |           limit:       50,
731 |         });
732 |       }
733 |     }
734 | 
735 |     // Distribution → histogram
736 |     for (const metric of this.schema.metrics.slice(0, 1)) {
737 |       recs.push({
738 |         type:  'histogram',
739 |         title: `Distribution of ${_humanize(metric.name)}`,
740 |         xKey:  metric.name,
741 |       });
742 |     }
743 | 
744 |     return recs.slice(0, 6);
745 |   }
746 | 
747 |   /**
748 |    * Auto-generate a descriptive chart title from a spec.
749 |    * @private
750 |    */
751 |   _autoTitle(spec) {
752 |     const type   = spec.type   ? spec.type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + spec.type.slice(1) : 'Chart';
753 |     const xLabel = spec.xKey   ? _humanize(spec.xKey) : 'X';
754 |     const yLabel = spec.yKey   ? _humanize(spec.yKey) : '';
755 |     return yLabel ? `${type}: ${yLabel} by ${xLabel}` : `${type} of ${xLabel}`;
756 |   }
757 | }
758 | 
759 | // ============================================================================
760 | // PART 4: DASHBOARD BUILDER
761 | // ============================================================================
762 | 
763 | /**
764 |  * Assembles a complete dashboard from a schema + rows, optionally accepting
765 |  * custom chart specs and KPI specs from the caller.
766 |  *
767 |  * This is the single entry point used by the agentic orchestrator.
768 |  */
769 | export class DashboardBuilder {
770 |   /**
771 |    * @param {object}         rawSchema  - Schema from schema-normalizer or schema-packet-builder
772 |    * @param {Array<object>}  rows       - Actual dataset rows
773 |    */
774 |   constructor(rawSchema, rows = []) {
775 |     this.schema    = new EnhancedSchema(rawSchema);
776 |     this.rows      = Array.isArray(rows) ? rows : [];
777 |     this.kpis      = new KPICalculator(this.schema, this.rows);
778 |     this.charts    = new CustomChartGenerator(this.schema, this.rows);
779 |   }
780 | 
781 |   /**
782 |    * Build a complete dashboard object.
783 |    *
784 |    * @param {object}   [opts={}]
785 |    * @param {object[]} [opts.customChartSpecs=[]]  - Additional chart specs
786 |    * @param {object[]} [opts.customKPISpecs=[]]    - Additional KPI specs
787 |    * @param {number}   [opts.maxKPIs=6]
788 |    * @param {number}   [opts.maxCharts=8]
789 |    * @returns {object}
790 |    */
791 |   buildDashboard({
792 |     customChartSpecs = [],
793 |     customKPISpecs   = [],
794 |     maxKPIs          = 6,
795 |     maxCharts        = 8,
796 |   } = {}) {
797 |     // ── KPIs ──
798 |     const defaultKPIs = this.kpis.buildDefaultKPIs();
799 |     const extraKPIs   = customKPISpecs.flatMap(spec => {
800 |       try { return [this.kpis.generateKPI(spec)]; }
801 |       catch (e) {
802 |         console.warn(`[DashboardBuilder] Skipping KPI "${spec.title}": ${e.message}`);
803 |         return [];
804 |       }
805 |     });
806 |     const allKPIs = [...defaultKPIs, ...extraKPIs].slice(0, maxKPIs);
807 | 
808 |     // ── Charts ──
809 |     const recommendedCharts = this.charts.getRecommendations()
810 |       .slice(0, 2)
811 |       .flatMap(spec => {
812 |         try { return [this.charts.generateChart(spec)]; }
813 |         catch (e) {
814 |           console.warn(`[DashboardBuilder] Skipping recommended chart: ${e.message}`);
815 |           return [];
816 |         }
817 |       });
818 | 
819 |     const extraCharts = customChartSpecs.flatMap(spec => {
820 |       try { return [this.charts.generateChart(spec)]; }
821 |       catch (e) {
822 |         console.warn(`[DashboardBuilder] Skipping custom chart "${spec.title ?? spec.type}": ${e.message}`);
823 |         return [];
824 |       }
825 |     });
826 | 
827 |     const allCharts = [...recommendedCharts, ...extraCharts].slice(0, maxCharts);
828 | 
829 |     // ── Final dashboard ──
830 |     return {
831 |       id:       randomUUID(),
832 |       name:     this.schema.name,
833 |       generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
834 | 
835 |       /** Compact schema summary — safe to send to frontend. */
836 |       schema: {
837 |         columns:    this.schema.columns.map(c => ({
838 |           name:   c.name,
839 |           type:   c.type,
840 |           role:   c.role,
841 |           domain: c.domain,
842 |         })),
843 |         metrics:    this.schema.metrics.map(c => c.name),
844 |         dimensions: this.schema.dimensions.map(c => c.name),
845 |         dates:      this.schema.dates.map(c => c.name),
846 |         keys:       this.schema.keys.map(c => c.name),
847 |       },
848 | 
849 |       kpis:   allKPIs,
850 |       charts: allCharts,
851 | 
852 |       summary: {
853 |         totalRecords:    this.rows.length,
854 |         totalColumns:    this.schema.columnCount,
855 |         totalMetrics:    this.schema.metrics.length,
856 |         totalDimensions: this.schema.dimensions.length,
857 |         totalDates:      this.schema.dates.length,
858 |         chartsGenerated: allCharts.length,
859 |         kpisGenerated:   allKPIs.length,
860 |       },
861 |     };
862 |   }
863 | }
864 | 
865 | // ============================================================================
866 | // SHARED UTILITY (module-private)
867 | // ============================================================================
868 | 
869 | /**
870 |  * Convert snake_case, camelCase or kebab-case to a human readable title.
871 |  * @param {string} text
872 |  * @returns {string}
873 |  */
874 | function _humanize(text) {
875 |   return String(text ?? '')
876 |     .replace(/[_-]+/g, ' ')
877 |     .replace(/([a-z])([A-Z])/g, '$1 $2')
878 |     .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
879 |     .trim()
880 |     .replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
881 | }
882 | 
883 | // ============================================================================
884 | // EXPORTS
885 | // ============================================================================
886 | 
887 | export default {
888 |   EnhancedSchema,
889 |   KPICalculator,
890 |   CustomChartGenerator,
891 |   DashboardBuilder,
892 | };
893 | 
```

```js
 6 |       title: 'Domain Intelligently Detected',
 7 |       description: `Insight Agent confirms this dataset strongly aligns with the ${ontologyMapping.inferredDomain.toUpperCase()} domain based on semantic ontology.`,
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/kpi-agent.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/kpi-agent.js`

```js
 1 | export function executeKpiAgent(dashboardSpec, ontologyMapping) {
 2 |   // Augment existing KPIs based on ontology mapping
 3 |   const kpis = [...(dashboardSpec.kpis || [])];
 4 | 
 5 |   // If the ontology detected revenue but there's no revenue KPI, add a placeholder
 6 |   if (ontologyMapping.canonicalTerms.includes('revenue') && !kpis.some(k => k.title.toLowerCase().includes('revenue'))) {
 7 |     kpis.push({
 8 |       title: 'Total Revenue',
 9 |       priority: 1,
10 |       aggregation: 'sum',
11 |       reason: 'KPI Agent added this because revenue-related columns were semantically detected.'
12 |     });
13 |   }
14 | 
15 |   // If ontology detected churn, ensure it exists
16 |   if (ontologyMapping.canonicalTerms.includes('churn') && !kpis.some(k => k.title.toLowerCase().includes('churn'))) {
17 |     kpis.push({
18 |       title: 'Overall Churn Rate',
19 |       priority: 1,
20 |       aggregation: 'avg',
21 |       reason: 'KPI Agent flagged this as critical based on CRM ontology mapping.'
22 |     });
23 |   }
24 | 
25 |   return { ...dashboardSpec, kpis };
26 | }
27 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/model-aware-agentic-orchestrator.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/model-aware-agentic-orchestrator.js`

```js
  1 | import { generateWithAgent, chatWithAgent } from './ollama-agent-router.js';
  2 | import { AGENTIC_MODELS } from '../../config/agentic-models.js';
  3 | 
  4 | function inferColumnType(values) {
  5 |   const sample = values.filter((v) => v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== '').slice(0, 100);
  6 |   if (!sample.length) return 'empty';
  7 |   const numeric = sample.filter((v) => !Number.isNaN(Number(v))).length / sample.length;
  8 |   if (numeric >= 0.8) return 'number';
  9 |   const dateish = sample.filter((v) => !Number.isNaN(Date.parse(String(v)))).length / sample.length;
 10 |   if (dateish >= 0.7) return 'date';
 11 |   const unique = new Set(sample.map(String)).size;
 12 |   if (unique <= Math.max(20, sample.length * 0.3)) return 'category';
 13 |   return 'text';
 14 | }
 15 | 
 16 | function profileRows(rows = [], columns = []) {
 17 |   const names = columns.length
 18 |     ? columns.map((c) => (typeof c === 'string' ? c : c.name)).filter(Boolean)
 19 |     : Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 20 | 
 21 |   const columnProfiles = names.map((name) => {
 22 |     const values = rows.map((row) => row?.[name]);
 23 |     const missing = values.filter((v) => v === null || v === undefined || v === '').length;
 24 |     const type = inferColumnType(values);
 25 |     const nums = values.map(Number).filter((v) => Number.isFinite(v));
 26 |     return {
 27 |       name,
 28 |       type,
 29 |       missing,
 30 |       missingRate: rows.length ? Number((missing / rows.length).toFixed(4)) : 0,
 31 |       unique: new Set(values.filter((v) => v !== null && v !== undefined).map(String)).size,
 32 |       sample: values.filter((v) => v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== '').slice(0, 5),
 33 |       stats: nums.length
 34 |         ? {
 35 |             min: Math.min(...nums),
 36 |             max: Math.max(...nums),
 37 |             avg: nums.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / nums.length,
 38 |           }
 39 |         : undefined,
 40 |     };
 41 |   });
 42 | 
 43 |   return {
 44 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 45 |     columnCount: names.length,
 46 |     columns: columnProfiles,
 47 |     numericColumns: columnProfiles.filter((c) => c.type === 'number').map((c) => c.name),
 48 |     dateColumns: columnProfiles.filter((c) => c.type === 'date').map((c) => c.name),
 49 |     categoricalColumns: columnProfiles.filter((c) => c.type === 'category').map((c) => c.name),
 50 |     textColumns: columnProfiles.filter((c) => c.type === 'text').map((c) => c.name),
 51 |   };
 52 | }
 53 | 
 54 | function deterministicDashboard(profile) {
 55 |   const firstNumber = profile.numericColumns[0];
 56 |   const secondNumber = profile.numericColumns[1];
 57 |   const firstCategory = profile.categoricalColumns[0];
 58 |   const firstDate = profile.dateColumns[0];
 59 | 
 60 |   const kpis = [
 61 |     { id: 'rows', label: 'Total Rows', type: 'count', field: null },
 62 |     { id: 'columns', label: 'Total Columns', type: 'count_columns', field: null },
 63 |   ];
 64 | 
 65 |   if (firstNumber) {
 66 |     kpis.push({ id: `avg_${firstNumber}`, label: `Average ${firstNumber}`, type: 'avg', field: firstNumber });
 67 |     kpis.push({ id: `sum_${firstNumber}`, label: `Total ${firstNumber}`, type: 'sum', field: firstNumber });
 68 |   }
 69 | 
 70 |   const charts = [];
 71 |   if (firstCategory && firstNumber) {
 72 |     charts.push({
 73 |       id: 'category_bar',
 74 |       title: `${firstNumber} by ${firstCategory}`,
 75 |       type: 'bar',
 76 |       x: firstCategory,
 77 |       y: firstNumber,
 78 |       aggregation: 'sum',
 79 |       reason: 'Compares numeric performance across categories.',
 80 |     });
 81 |   }
 82 |   if (firstDate && firstNumber) {
 83 |     charts.push({
 84 |       id: 'trend_line',
 85 |       title: `${firstNumber} trend over ${firstDate}`,
 86 |       type: 'line',
 87 |       x: firstDate,
 88 |       y: firstNumber,
 89 |       aggregation: 'sum',
 90 |       reason: 'Shows movement over time.',
 91 |     });
 92 |   }
 93 |   if (firstNumber && secondNumber) {
 94 |     charts.push({
 95 |       id: 'numeric_scatter',
 96 |       title: `${firstNumber} vs ${secondNumber}`,
 97 |       type: 'scatter',
 98 |       x: firstNumber,
 99 |       y: secondNumber,
100 |       aggregation: 'none',
101 |       reason: 'Helps inspect relationship between two numeric fields.',
102 |     });
103 |   }
104 | 
105 |   return {
106 |     kpis,
107 |     charts,
108 |     filters: [firstCategory, firstDate].filter(Boolean),
109 |     warnings: profile.columns
110 |       .filter((c) => c.missingRate > 0.2)
111 |       .map((c) => `${c.name} has ${Math.round(c.missingRate * 100)}% missing values.`),
112 |   };
113 | }
114 | 
115 | function schemaPrompt(profile, goal) {
116 |   return `You are InsightFlow strict schema analyst.\n\nRules:\n- Use schema/profile only.\n- Do not invent raw values.\n- Return JSON only.\n- Recommend metrics, dimensions, risks, and dashboard intent.\n\nUser goal: ${goal || 'Create the best analytics dashboard.'}\n\nSchema profile:\n${JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2)}\n\nReturn JSON with keys: domain, metricColumns, dimensionColumns, dateColumns, risks, dashboardGoal, confidence.`;
117 | }
118 | 
119 | function plannerPrompt(profile, schemaAnalysis, goal) {
120 |   return `You are InsightFlow master agent. Plan an analytics workflow.\nReturn JSON only.\n\nAvailable tools:\n- profile_schema\n- generate_kpis\n- recommend_charts\n- detect_anomalies\n- validate_dashboard\n- synthesize_insights\n\nUser goal: ${goal || 'Create a dashboard'}\n\nSchema profile:\n${JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2)}\n\nSchema agent result:\n${JSON.stringify(schemaAnalysis, null, 2)}\n\nReturn JSON with keys: objective, steps, toolCalls, expectedOutput, safetyRules.`;
121 | }
122 | 
123 | function guardPrompt(profile, dashboardPlan) {
124 |   return `You are InsightFlow dashboard guardian. Validate dashboard specs.\nRules:\n- Return JSON only.\n- Mark invalid charts that reference missing columns.\n- Suggest safer replacements.\n\nSchema columns: ${profile.columns.map((c) => c.name).join(', ')}\n\nDashboard plan:\n${JSON.stringify(dashboardPlan, null, 2)}\n\nReturn JSON with keys: valid, issues, fixedPlan, confidence.`;
125 | }
126 | 
127 | export async function runModelAwareAgenticAnalysis({ rows = [], columns = [], goal = '' }) {
128 |   const profile = profileRows(rows, columns);
129 |   const audit = [];
130 | 
131 |   let schemaAnalysis;
132 |   try {
133 |     const schemaRes = await generateWithAgent('schema', schemaPrompt(profile, goal), { json: true });
134 |     schemaAnalysis = schemaRes.json || { fallback: true, message: schemaRes.text };
135 |     audit.push({ step: 'schema_analysis', model: schemaRes.model, status: 'ok' });
136 |   } catch (error) {
137 |     schemaAnalysis = { fallback: true, error: error.message };
138 |     audit.push({ step: 'schema_analysis', model: AGENTIC_MODELS.schemaAnalyst, status: 'fallback', error: error.message });
139 |   }
140 | 
141 |   let plan;
142 |   try {
143 |     const plannerRes = await generateWithAgent('plan', plannerPrompt(profile, schemaAnalysis, goal), { json: true });
144 |     plan = plannerRes.json || { fallback: true, message: plannerRes.text };
145 |     audit.push({ step: 'master_planning', model: plannerRes.model, status: 'ok' });
146 |   } catch (error) {
147 |     plan = { fallback: true, objective: goal || 'Generate analytics dashboard', steps: [] };
148 |     audit.push({ step: 'master_planning', model: AGENTIC_MODELS.masterPlanner, status: 'fallback', error: error.message });
149 |   }
150 | 
151 |   const deterministicPlan = deterministicDashboard(profile);
152 | 
153 |   let guard;
154 |   try {
155 |     const guardRes = await generateWithAgent('guard', guardPrompt(profile, deterministicPlan), { json: true });
156 |     guard = guardRes.json || { valid: true, fixedPlan: deterministicPlan, message: guardRes.text };
157 |     audit.push({ step: 'dashboard_guardian', model: guardRes.model, status: 'ok' });
158 |   } catch (error) {
159 |     guard = { valid: true, fixedPlan: deterministicPlan, fallback: true, error: error.message };
160 |     audit.push({ step: 'dashboard_guardian', model: AGENTIC_MODELS.dashboardGuardian, status: 'fallback', error: error.message });
161 |   }
162 | 
163 |   const finalDashboard = guard?.fixedPlan?.charts || guard?.fixedPlan?.kpis ? guard.fixedPlan : deterministicPlan;
164 | 
165 |   return {
166 |     success: true,
167 |     mode: 'model-aware-agentic-analytics',
168 |     models: { ...AGENTIC_MODELS },
169 |     profile,
170 |     schemaAnalysis,
171 |     plan,
172 |     dashboard: finalDashboard,
173 |     audit,
174 |     nextTrainingHooks: {
175 |       ragEmbeddingModel: AGENTIC_MODELS.embedding,
176 |       replaceRetrieverFile: 'apps/backend/src/services/agentic/rag-retriever.js',
177 |       fineTuneTargets: ['schemaAnalyst', 'masterPlanner', 'dashboardGuardian'],
178 |     },
179 |   };
180 | }
181 | 
182 | export async function runModelAwareAgenticChat({ rows = [], columns = [], message = '', history = [] }) {
183 |   const profile = profileRows(rows, columns);
184 |   const system = {
185 |     role: 'system',
186 |     content: 'You are InsightFlow agentic data analyst. Use schema/profile and computed summaries only. Do not pretend to inspect hidden rows. Give concise dashboard-ready answers.',
187 |   };
188 |   const user = {
189 |     role: 'user',
190 |     content: `Dataset profile:\n${JSON.stringify(profile, null, 2)}\n\nUser question: ${message}`,
191 |   };
192 | 
193 |   try {
194 |     const res = await chatWithAgent('chat', [system, ...history.slice(-6), user], { json: false });
195 |     return { success: true, model: res.model, answer: res.text, profile };
196 |   } catch (error) {
197 |     return {
198 |       success: true,
199 |       model: 'fallback-local',
200 |       answer: `I analyzed the dataset schema: ${profile.rowCount} rows, ${profile.columnCount} columns. Numeric columns: ${profile.numericColumns.join(', ') || 'none'}. Category columns: ${profile.categoricalColumns.join(', ') || 'none'}.`,
201 |       profile,
202 |       error: error.message,
203 |     };
204 |   }
205 | }
206 |
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/agentic/ollama-agent-router.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/agentic/ollama-agent-router.js`

```js
  1 | import { AGENTIC_RUNTIME, OLLAMA_HOST, getModelForTask } from '../../config/agentic-models.js';
  2 | 
  3 | function withTimeout(ms) {
  4 |   const controller = new AbortController();
  5 |   const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), ms);
  6 |   return { signal: controller.signal, done: () => clearTimeout(timeout) };
  7 | }
  8 | 
  9 | function extractJson(text) {
 10 |   if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') return null;
 11 |   const fenced = text.match(/```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)```/i);
 12 |   const raw = fenced ? fenced[1] : text;
 13 |   const first = raw.indexOf('{');
 14 |   const last = raw.lastIndexOf('}');
 15 |   if (first === -1 || last === -1 || last <= first) return null;
 16 |   try {
 17 |     return JSON.parse(raw.slice(first, last + 1));
 18 |   } catch {
 19 |     return null;
 20 |   }
 21 | }
 22 | 
 23 | async function postOllama(path, body) {
 24 |   const timer = withTimeout(AGENTIC_RUNTIME.timeoutMs);
 25 |   try {
 26 |     const res = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_HOST}${path}`, {
 27 |       method: 'POST',
 28 |       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
 29 |       body: JSON.stringify(body),
 30 |       signal: timer.signal,
 31 |     });
 32 | 
 33 |     if (!res.ok) {
 34 |       const errorText = await res.text().catch(() => '');
 35 |       throw new Error(`Ollama ${path} failed: ${res.status} ${errorText}`);
 36 |     }
 37 | 
 38 |     return await res.json();
 39 |   } finally {
 40 |     timer.done();
 41 |   }
 42 | }
 43 | 
 44 | export async function generateWithAgent(task, prompt, options = {}) {
 45 |   const model = options.model || getModelForTask(task);
 46 |   const payload = {
 47 |     model,
 48 |     prompt,
 49 |     stream: false,
 50 |     format: options.json ? 'json' : undefined,
 51 |     options: {
 52 |       temperature: options.temperature ?? AGENTIC_RUNTIME.temperature,
 53 |       num_ctx: options.numCtx ?? AGENTIC_RUNTIME.numCtx,
 54 |     },
 55 |   };
 56 | 
 57 |   const result = await postOllama('/api/generate', payload);
 58 |   const text = result.response || '';
 59 | 
 60 |   return {
 61 |     task,
 62 |     model,
 63 |     text,
 64 |     json: options.json ? extractJson(text) : null,
 65 |     raw: result,
 66 |   };
 67 | }
 68 | 
 69 | export async function chatWithAgent(task, messages, options = {}) {
 70 |   const model = options.model || getModelForTask(task);
 71 |   const payload = {
 72 |     model,
 73 |     messages,
 74 |     stream: false,
 75 |     format: options.json ? 'json' : undefined,
 76 |     options: {
 77 |       temperature: options.temperature ?? AGENTIC_RUNTIME.temperature,
 78 |       num_ctx: options.numCtx ?? AGENTIC_RUNTIME.numCtx,
 79 |     },
 80 |   };
 81 | 
 82 |   const result = await postOllama('/api/chat', payload);
 83 |   const text = result.message?.content || '';
 84 | 
 85 |   return {
 86 |     task,
 87 |     model,
 88 |     text,
 89 |     json: options.json ? extractJson(text) : null,
 90 |     raw: result,
 91 |   };
 92 | }
 93 | 
 94 | export async function embedWithAgent(input, options = {}) {
 95 |   const model = options.model || getModelForTask('embed');
 96 |   const result = await postOllama('/api/embeddings', {
 97 |     model,
 98 |     prompt: Array.isArray(input) ? input.join('\n') : String(input || ''),
 99 |   });
100 | 
101 |   return {
102 |     model,
103 |     embedding: result.embedding || [],
104 |     raw: result,
105 |   };
106 | }
107 | 
108 | export async function pingOllamaModels(models) {
109 |   const timer = withTimeout(30000);
110 |   try {
111 |     const res = await fetch(`${OLLAMA_HOST}/api/tags`, { signal: timer.signal });
112 |     if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Ollama tags failed: ${res.status}`);
113 |     const data = await res.json();
114 |     const installed = new Set((data.models || []).map((m) => m.name));
115 |     return models.map((model) => ({
116 |       model,
117 |       installed: installed.has(model),
118 |     }));
119 |   } finally {
120 |     timer.done();
121 |   }
122 | }
123 | 
```

```js
 1 | export function executeStorytellingAgent(dashboardSpec, ontologyMapping) {
 2 |   const domain = ontologyMapping.inferredDomain;
 3 |   let summary = [];
 4 | 
 5 |   if (domain === 'sales' || domain === 'ecommerce') {
 6 |     summary = [
 7 |       "Revenue growth looks stable, but specific segments might be underperforming against targets.",
 8 |       "A small percentage of top-tier products are likely driving the majority of profitability.",
 9 |       "The largest opportunity lies in optimizing discount rates to protect margins without sacrificing volume."
10 |     ];
11 |   } else if (domain === 'hr_salary') {
12 |     summary = [
13 |       "Compensation spreads show normal distribution, but outliers exist in senior roles.",
14 |       "Certain departments appear to consume a disproportionate amount of the salary budget.",
15 |       "An opportunity exists to standardize pay bands and improve retention in high-turnover roles."
16 |     ];
17 |   } else {
18 |     summary = [
19 |       `Overall performance across the ${domain} domain requires deeper drill-down to isolate key drivers.`,
20 |       "Top-performing segments are carrying the aggregate averages, masking weak points.",
21 |       "The next immediate action should be investigating anomalies in the primary KPI distribution."
22 |     ];
23 |   }
24 | 
25 |   return { ...dashboardSpec, executiveSummary: summary };
26 | }
27 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/ai-analyst-orchestrator.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/ai-analyst-orchestrator.js`

```js
  1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  2 | import { buildSchemaProfile } from "./schema-profiler.js";
  3 | import { buildAutoKpis } from "./kpi-engine.js";
  4 | import { buildAutoCharts, buildChartFromCommand } from "./chart-engine.js";
  5 | import { detectBestPlaybook } from "./analyst-playbooks.js";
  6 | import { findMemoryMatch, saveAnalystMemory } from "./analyst-memory.js";
  7 | import { routeAnalystCommand } from "./command-router.js";
  8 | 
  9 | function cleanRows(rows = []) {
 10 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 11 |     const keys = Object.keys(row || {}).map((key) => key.toLowerCase());
 12 |     const source = String(row?._sourceFile || row?.source || "").toLowerCase();
 13 | 
 14 |     const looksLikeDictionary =
 15 |       keys.includes("column") &&
 16 |       keys.includes("type") &&
 17 |       keys.includes("description");
 18 | 
 19 |     return !source.includes("dictionary") && !looksLikeDictionary;
 20 |   });
 21 | }
 22 | 
 23 | export async function runFullAutoAnalysis(dataset = {}) {
 24 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
 25 | 
 26 |   const cleanedDataset = {
 27 |     ...dataset,
 28 |     rows,
 29 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 30 |   };
 31 | 
 32 |   const schema = buildSchemaProfile(cleanedDataset);
 33 |   const playbook = detectBestPlaybook(schema);
 34 |   const memoryMatch = findMemoryMatch({
 35 |     domain: playbook.domain,
 36 |     columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
 37 |   });
 38 | 
 39 |   const kpis = buildAutoKpis({
 40 |     dataset: cleanedDataset,
 41 |     schema,
 42 |     playbook,
 43 |     memoryMatch,
 44 |   });
 45 | 
 46 |   const charts = buildAutoCharts({
 47 |     dataset: cleanedDataset,
 48 |     schema,
 49 |     playbook,
 50 |     memoryMatch,
 51 |   });
 52 | 
 53 |   const analysis = {
 54 |     id: randomUUID(),
 55 |     dataType: playbook.domain,
 56 |     dataTypeLabel: playbook.label,
 57 |     rowCount: cleanedDataset.rowCount,
 58 |     columnCount: schema.columns.length,
 59 |     kpis,
 60 |     chartRecommendations: charts,
 61 |     insights: [
 62 |       {
 63 |         type: "summary",
 64 |         title: "AI Analyst Summary",
 65 |         message: `Detected ${playbook.label}. Generated ${kpis.length} KPIs and ${charts.length} dashboard charts automatically.`,
 66 |       },
 67 |       {
 68 |         type: "privacy",
 69 |         title: "Schema-only AI Mode",
 70 |         message: "Raw rows are not sent to the LLM. KPI and chart values are calculated locally by the analytics engine.",
 71 |       },
 72 |     ],
 73 |     schemaPacket: schema.safePacket,
 74 |     memory: {
 75 |       usedPreviousPattern: Boolean(memoryMatch),
 76 |       similarity: memoryMatch?.similarity || 0,
 77 |       domain: playbook.domain,
 78 |       columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
 79 |     },
 80 |     privacy: {
 81 |       schemaOnly: true,
 82 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
 83 |     },
 84 |   };
 85 | 
 86 |   saveAnalystMemory({
 87 |     domain: playbook.domain,
 88 |     columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
 89 |     kpis,
 90 |     charts,
 91 |   });
 92 | 
 93 |   return analysis;
 94 | }
 95 | 
 96 | export async function runAnalystCommand({
 97 |   dataset,
 98 |   currentAnalysis,
 99 |   command,
100 |   filters = {},
101 | }) {
102 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
103 |   const cleanedDataset = {
104 |     ...dataset,
105 |     rows,
106 |     rowCount: rows.length,
107 |   };
108 | 
109 |   const schema = buildSchemaProfile(cleanedDataset);
110 | 
111 |   const routed = await routeAnalystCommand({
112 |     schema,
113 |     command,
114 |   });
115 | 
116 |   if (routed.action === "CLEAR_FILTERS") {
117 |     return {
118 |       action: "CLEAR_FILTERS",
119 |       message: "All filters cleared.",
120 |     };
121 |   }
122 | 
123 |   if (routed.action === "FILTER") {
124 |     return {
125 |       action: "FILTER",
126 |       message: routed.message || "Filter applied.",
127 |       filters: routed.filters || {},
128 |     };
129 |   }
130 | 
131 |   if (routed.action === "DELETE_CHART") {
132 |     return {
133 |       action: "DELETE_CHART",
134 |       message: "Removed the selected chart.",
135 |     };
136 |   }
137 | 
138 |   if (routed.action === "ADD_KPI") {
139 |     const kpis = buildAutoKpis({
140 |       dataset: cleanedDataset,
141 |       schema,
142 |       playbook: detectBestPlaybook(schema),
143 |     });
144 | 
145 |     return {
146 |       action: "ADD_KPI",
147 |       message: "Added KPI summary.",
148 |       kpis,
149 |     };
150 |   }
151 | 
152 |   if (routed.action === "GENERATE_CHART" || routed.action === "MODIFY_CHART") {
153 |     const chart = buildChartFromCommand({
154 |       dataset: cleanedDataset,
155 |       schema,
156 |       chartSpec: routed.chartSpec,
157 |       filters,
158 |     });
159 | 
160 |     if (!chart) {
161 |       return {
162 |         action: "ANSWER",
163 |         message: "I could not create a valid chart from this schema.",
164 |       };
165 |     }
166 | 
167 |     return {
168 |       action: routed.action,
169 |       message: routed.message || `Created chart: ${chart.title}`,
170 |       chart,
171 |     };
172 |   }
173 | 
174 |   return {
175 |     action: "ANSWER",
176 |     message:
177 |       routed.message ||
178 |       "I analyzed the schema, but this command does not require a dashboard change.",
179 |   };
180 | }
181 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/analyst-memory.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/analyst-memory.js`

```js
 1 | import fs from "node:fs";
 2 | import path from "node:path";
 3 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
 4 | 
 5 | const MEMORY_FILE = path.resolve("data", "ai-analyst-memory.json");
 6 | 
 7 | function ensureFile() {
 8 |   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(MEMORY_FILE), { recursive: true });
 9 | 
10 |   if (!fs.existsSync(MEMORY_FILE)) {
11 |     fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify([], null, 2));
12 |   }
13 | }
14 | 
15 | function readMemory() {
16 |   ensureFile();
17 | 
18 |   try {
19 |     return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, "utf8"));
20 |   } catch {
21 |     return [];
22 |   }
23 | }
24 | 
25 | function writeMemory(memory) {
26 |   ensureFile();
27 |   fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify(memory, null, 2));
28 | }
29 | 
30 | function similarity(a = "", b = "") {
31 |   const setA = new Set(a.split("|").filter(Boolean));
32 |   const setB = new Set(b.split("|").filter(Boolean));
33 | 
34 |   if (!setA.size || !setB.size) return 0;
35 | 
36 |   const intersection = [...setA].filter((item) => setB.has(item)).length;
37 |   const union = new Set([...setA, ...setB]).size;
38 | 
39 |   return intersection / union;
40 | }
41 | 
42 | export function findMemoryMatch({ domain, columnSignature }) {
43 |   const memory = readMemory();
44 | 
45 |   const best = memory
46 |     .filter((item) => item.domain === domain)
47 |     .map((item) => ({
48 |       ...item,
49 |       similarity: similarity(item.columnSignature, columnSignature),
50 |     }))
51 |     .sort((a, b) => b.similarity - a.similarity)[0];
52 | 
53 |   if (!best || best.similarity < 0.65) return null;
54 | 
55 |   return best;
56 | }
57 | 
58 | export function saveAnalystMemory({ domain, columnSignature, kpis, charts }) {
59 |   const memory = readMemory();
60 | 
61 |   const existing = memory.find(
62 |     (item) => item.domain === domain && item.columnSignature === columnSignature
63 |   );
64 | 
65 |   const item = {
66 |     id: existing?.id || randomUUID(),
67 |     domain,
68 |     columnSignature,
69 |     kpiTemplates: kpis.map((kpi) => ({
70 |       title: kpi.title,
71 |       metric: kpi.metric,
72 |       aggregation: kpi.aggregation,
73 |     })),
74 |     chartTemplates: charts.map((chart) => ({
75 |       title: chart.title,
76 |       type: chart.type,
77 |       xKey: chart.xKey,
78 |       yKey: chart.yKey,
79 |       aggregation: chart.aggregation,
80 |     })),
81 |     usageCount: existing ? existing.usageCount + 1 : 1,
82 |     createdAt: existing?.createdAt || new Date().toISOString(),
83 |     lastUsedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
84 |   };
85 | 
86 |   const next = existing
87 |     ? memory.map((entry) => (entry.id === existing.id ? item : entry))
88 |     : [...memory, item];
89 | 
90 |   writeMemory(next);
91 | 
92 |   return item;
93 | }
94 | 
```

```js
251 | 
252 | function normalize(value = "") {
253 |   return String(value).toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_");
254 | }
255 | 
256 | export function detectBestPlaybook(schema) {
257 |   const names = schema.columns.map((column) => column.normalizedName).join(" ");
258 | 
259 |   const scored = ANALYST_PLAYBOOKS.map((playbook) => {
260 |     let score = 0;
261 | 
262 |     for (const keyword of playbook.keywords) {
263 |       if (names.includes(normalize(keyword))) score += 2;
264 |     }
265 | 
266 |     const hasDate = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "date");
267 |     const hasMetric = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "metric");
268 |     const hasDimension = schema.columns.some((column) => column.role === "dimension");
269 | 
270 |     if (playbook.domain === "time_series" && hasDate && hasMetric) score += 5;
271 |     if (playbook.domain === "generic" && hasMetric && hasDimension) score += 1;
272 | 
273 |     return { playbook, score };
274 |   }).sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
275 | 
276 |   return scored[0]?.score > 0
277 |     ? scored[0].playbook
278 |     : ANALYST_PLAYBOOKS.find((playbook) => playbook.domain === "generic");
279 | }
280 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/chart-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/chart-engine.js`

```js
  1 | import { findColumn, safeNumber } from "./schema-profiler.js";
  2 | 
  3 | function aggregate(values = [], aggregation = "count") {
  4 |   const present = values.filter(
  5 |     (value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== ""
  6 |   );
  7 | 
  8 |   if (aggregation === "count") return present.length;
  9 | 
 10 |   if (aggregation === "count_unique") {
 11 |     return new Set(present.map((v) => String(v).trim().toLowerCase())).size;
 12 |   }
 13 | 
 14 |   const numbers = present.map(safeNumber).filter((value) => value !== null);
 15 | 
 16 |   if (!numbers.length) return 0;
 17 | 
 18 |   if (aggregation === "sum") return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
 19 |   if (aggregation === "avg") return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / numbers.length;
 20 |   if (aggregation === "min") return Math.min(...numbers);
 21 |   if (aggregation === "max") return Math.max(...numbers);
 22 | 
 23 |   if (aggregation === "median") {
 24 |     const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 25 |     const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
 26 |     return sorted.length % 2
 27 |       ? sorted[mid]
 28 |       : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
 29 |   }
 30 | 
 31 |   return present.length;
 32 | }
 33 | 
 34 | function groupBy(rows, xKey, yKey, aggregation = "count", limit = 10, options = {}) {
 35 |   const groups = new Map();
 36 |   const splitValues = options.splitValues === true;
 37 | 
 38 |   for (const row of rows) {
 39 |     const rawLabel = String(row[xKey] ?? "Unknown").trim() || "Unknown";
 40 |     const labels = splitValues
 41 |       ? rawLabel.split(/[,;/|]+/).map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)
 42 |       : [rawLabel];
 43 | 
 44 |     for (const label of labels) {
 45 |       if (!groups.has(label)) groups.set(label, []);
 46 |       groups.get(label).push(yKey ? row[yKey] : label);
 47 |     }
 48 |   }
 49 | 
 50 |   return [...groups.entries()]
 51 |     .map(([label, values]) => ({
 52 |       [xKey]: label,
 53 |       [aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey]: aggregate(values, aggregation),
 54 |     }))
 55 |     .sort((a, b) => Number(Object.values(b).at(-1)) - Number(Object.values(a).at(-1)))
 56 |     .slice(0, limit);
 57 | }
 58 | 
 59 | function histogram(rows, key, bins = 10) {
 60 |   const values = rows
 61 |     .map((row) => safeNumber(row[key]))
 62 |     .filter((value) => value !== null);
 63 | 
 64 |   if (!values.length) return [];
 65 | 
 66 |   const min = Math.min(...values);
 67 |   const max = Math.max(...values);
 68 | 
 69 |   if (min === max) {
 70 |     return [{ range: String(min), count: values.length }];
 71 |   }
 72 | 
 73 |   const step = (max - min) / bins;
 74 | 
 75 |   const buckets = Array.from({ length: bins }, (_, index) => ({
 76 |     start: min + index * step,
 77 |     end: index === bins - 1 ? max : min + (index + 1) * step,
 78 |     count: 0,
 79 |   }));
 80 | 
 81 |   for (const value of values) {
 82 |     const index = Math.min(Math.floor((value - min) / step), bins - 1);
 83 |     buckets[index].count += 1;
 84 |   }
 85 | 
 86 |   return buckets
 87 |     .filter((bucket) => bucket.count > 0)
 88 |     .map((bucket) => ({
 89 |       range: `${Math.round(bucket.start).toLocaleString()}-${Math.round(bucket.end).toLocaleString()}`,
 90 |       count: bucket.count,
 91 |     }));
 92 | }
 93 | 
 94 | function splitCount(rows, key, limit = 12) {
 95 |   const counts = new Map();
 96 | 
 97 |   for (const row of rows) {
 98 |     String(row[key] ?? "")
 99 |       .split(/[,;/|]+/)
100 |       .map((item) => item.trim())
101 |       .filter(Boolean)
102 |       .forEach((item) => counts.set(item, (counts.get(item) || 0) + 1));
103 |   }
104 | 
105 |   return [...counts.entries()]
106 |     .map(([label, count]) => ({ [key]: label, count }))
107 |     .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
108 |     .slice(0, limit);
109 | }
110 | 
111 | function scatterData(rows, xKey, yKey, limit = 500) {
112 |   const xGroups = new Map();
113 | 
114 |   for (const row of rows) {
115 |     const xVal = safeNumber(row[xKey]);
116 |     const yVal = safeNumber(row[yKey]);
117 |     if (xVal === null || yVal === null) continue;
118 | 
119 |     if (!xGroups.has(xVal)) xGroups.set(xVal, []);
120 |     xGroups.get(xVal).push(yVal);
121 |   }
122 | 
123 |   return [...xGroups.entries()]
124 |     .map(([x, ys]) => ({
125 |       [xKey]: x,
126 |       [yKey]: Math.round(ys.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / ys.length),
127 |     }))
128 |     .sort((a, b) => a[xKey] - b[xKey])
129 |     .slice(0, limit);
130 | }
131 | 
132 | function applyFilters(rows, filters = {}) {
133 |   const entries = Object.entries(filters || {}).filter(
134 |     ([, value]) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== ""
135 |   );
136 | 
137 |   if (!entries.length) return rows;
138 | 
139 |   return rows.filter((row) =>
140 |     entries.every(([column, value]) => {
141 |       return String(row[column] ?? "").toLowerCase() === String(value).toLowerCase();
142 |     })
143 |   );
144 | }
145 | 
146 | function buildChartFromTemplate(rows, schema, template, filters = {}) {
147 |   const filteredRows = applyFilters(rows, filters);
148 | 
149 |   const xColumn =
150 |     findColumn(schema, template.xAliases || [], template.xRole) ||
151 |     findColumn(schema, [], template.xRole);
152 | 
153 |   const yColumn =
154 |     findColumn(schema, template.yAliases || [], template.yRole) ||
155 |     findColumn(schema, [], template.yRole);
156 | 
157 |   if (template.type === "histogram") {
158 |     const metric = yColumn || xColumn || findColumn(schema, [], "metric");
159 |     if (!metric) return null;
160 | 
161 |     const data = histogram(filteredRows, metric.name, 10);
162 |     if (!data.length) return null;
163 | 
164 |     return {
165 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
166 |       title: template.title,
167 |       type: "histogram",
168 |       xKey: "range",
169 |       yKey: "count",
170 |       aggregation: "count",
171 |       data,
172 |     };
173 |   }
174 | 
175 |   if (template.aggregation === "split_count") {
176 |     if (!xColumn) return null;
177 | 
178 |     const data = splitCount(filteredRows, xColumn.name, template.limit || 12);
179 |     if (!data.length) return null;
180 | 
181 |     return {
182 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
183 |       title: template.title,
184 |       type: "bar",
185 |       xKey: xColumn.name,
186 |       yKey: "count",
187 |       aggregation: "count",
188 |       data,
189 |     };
190 |   }
191 | 
192 |   if (template.type === "scatter") {
193 |     if (!xColumn || !yColumn) return null;
194 | 
195 |     const data = scatterData(filteredRows, xColumn.name, yColumn.name, template.limit || 500);
196 |     if (!data.length) return null;
197 | 
198 |     return {
199 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
200 |       title: template.title,
201 |       type: "scatter",
202 |       xKey: xColumn.name,
203 |       yKey: yColumn.name,
204 |       aggregation: "avg",
205 |       data,
206 |     };
207 |   }
208 | 
209 |   if (template.type === "pie" || template.type === "donut") {
210 |     if (!xColumn) return null;
211 | 
212 |     const data = groupBy(filteredRows, xColumn.name, null, "count", template.limit || 10);
213 |     if (!data.length) return null;
214 | 
215 |     return {
216 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
217 |       title: template.title,
218 |       type: template.type,
219 |       xKey: xColumn.name,
220 |       yKey: "count",
221 |       aggregation: "count",
222 |       data,
223 |     };
224 |   }
225 | 
226 |   if (!xColumn) return null;
227 | 
228 |   const aggregation = template.aggregation || "count";
229 |   const yKey = aggregation === "count" ? null : yColumn?.name;
230 | 
231 |   if (aggregation !== "count" && !yKey) return null;
232 | 
233 |   const isSplitCol =
234 |     template.splitValues === true ||
235 |     /language|framework|skill|tag/i.test(xColumn.name || "");
236 | 
237 |   const data = groupBy(
238 |     filteredRows,
239 |     xColumn.name,
240 |     yKey,
241 |     aggregation,
242 |     template.limit || 10,
243 |     { splitValues: isSplitCol }
244 |   );
245 | 
246 |   if (!data.length) return null;
247 | 
248 |   return {
249 |     id: crypto.randomUUID(),
250 |     title: template.title,
251 |     type: template.type || "bar",
252 |     xKey: xColumn.name,
253 |     yKey: aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey,
254 |     aggregation,
255 |     data,
256 |   };
257 | }
258 | 
259 | export function buildAutoCharts({ dataset, schema, playbook, memoryMatch }) {
260 |   const rows = dataset.rows || [];
261 | 
262 |   const baseCharts = (playbook.charts || [])
263 |     .map((template) => buildChartFromTemplate(rows, schema, template))
264 |     .filter(Boolean);
265 | 
266 |   const learnedCharts = (memoryMatch?.chartTemplates || [])
267 |     .map((template) =>
268 |       buildChartFromTemplate(rows, schema, {
269 |         title: template.title,
270 |         type: template.type,
271 |         xAliases: [template.xKey],
272 |         yAliases: [template.yKey],
273 |         aggregation: template.aggregation,
274 |       })
275 |     )
276 |     .filter(Boolean);
277 | 
278 |   const merged = [...learnedCharts, ...baseCharts];
279 | 
280 |   const seen = new Set();
281 | 
282 |   return merged
283 |     .filter((chart) => {
284 |       const key = `${chart.title}-${chart.xKey}-${chart.yKey}`;
285 |       if (seen.has(key)) return false;
286 |       seen.add(key);
287 |       return true;
288 |     })
289 |     .slice(0, 10);
290 | }
291 | 
292 | export function buildChartFromCommand({
293 |   dataset,
294 |   schema,
295 |   chartSpec,
296 |   filters = {},
297 | }) {
298 |   if (!chartSpec) return null;
299 | 
300 |   return buildChartFromTemplate(
301 |     dataset.rows || [],
302 |     schema,
303 |     {
304 |       title: chartSpec.title || "Generated Chart",
305 |       type: chartSpec.type || "bar",
306 |       xAliases: chartSpec.xKey
307 |         ? [chartSpec.xKey]
308 |         : chartSpec.dimension
309 |         ? [chartSpec.dimension]
310 |         : [],
311 |       yAliases: chartSpec.yKey
312 |         ? [chartSpec.yKey]
313 |         : chartSpec.metric
314 |         ? [chartSpec.metric]
315 |         : [],
316 |       xRole: chartSpec.xRole,
317 |       yRole: chartSpec.yRole,
318 |       aggregation: chartSpec.aggregation || "count",
319 |       limit: chartSpec.limit || 10,
320 |       splitValues: chartSpec.splitValues || false,
321 |     },
322 |     filters
323 |   );
324 | }
325 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/command-router.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/command-router.js`

```js
  1 | import { findColumn } from "./schema-profiler.js";
  2 | 
  3 | function deterministicCommand(schema, command) {
  4 |   const text = String(command || "").toLowerCase();
  5 | 
  6 |   if (/clear filters|reset filters|remove filter/.test(text)) {
  7 |     return {
  8 |       action: "CLEAR_FILTERS",
  9 |       message: "All filters cleared.",
 10 |     };
 11 |   }
 12 | 
 13 |   if (/remove|delete/.test(text) && /chart|graph|visual/.test(text)) {
 14 |     return {
 15 |       action: "DELETE_CHART",
 16 |       message: "Removed chart.",
 17 |     };
 18 |   }
 19 | 
 20 |   const filterMatch = String(command).match(
 21 |     /(?:filter|where|show only)\s+([\w\s_-]+)\s*(?:=|is|:)\s*["']?([^"']+)["']?/i
 22 |   );
 23 | 
 24 |   if (filterMatch) {
 25 |     const column = findColumn(schema, [filterMatch[1]], "dimension");
 26 | 
 27 |     if (column) {
 28 |       return {
 29 |         action: "FILTER",
 30 |         filters: {
 31 |           [column.name]: filterMatch[2].trim(),
 32 |         },
 33 |         message: `Applied filter ${column.name} = ${filterMatch[2].trim()}`,
 34 |       };
 35 |     }
 36 |   }
 37 | 
 38 |   if (/kpi|summary|card/.test(text)) {
 39 |     return {
 40 |       action: "ADD_KPI",
 41 |       message: "Added KPI summary.",
 42 |     };
 43 |   }
 44 | 
 45 |   return null;
 46 | }
 47 | 
 48 | async function callLlmForPlan(schema, command) {
 49 |   const baseUrl = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || "http://localhost:11434";
 50 |   const model = process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || "llama3.2:latest";
 51 | 
 52 |   const prompt = `
 53 | You are an AI data analyst planner.
 54 | You do not calculate KPI values.
 55 | You do not create chart data.
 56 | You only return JSON dashboard actions.
 57 | 
 58 | Allowed actions:
 59 | GENERATE_CHART, MODIFY_CHART, DELETE_CHART, FILTER, CLEAR_FILTERS, ADD_KPI, ANSWER
 60 | 
 61 | Allowed chart types:
 62 | bar, line, pie, area, histogram, scatter
 63 | 
 64 | Allowed aggregations:
 65 | count, sum, avg, min, max, median
 66 | 
 67 | Use only columns from schema.
 68 | 
 69 | Return JSON only:
 70 | {
 71 |   "action": "GENERATE_CHART",
 72 |   "message": "short message",
 73 |   "chartSpec": {
 74 |     "title": "",
 75 |     "type": "bar",
 76 |     "dimension": "",
 77 |     "metric": "",
 78 |     "aggregation": "avg",
 79 |     "limit": 10
 80 |   },
 81 |   "filters": {}
 82 | }
 83 | 
 84 | User command:
 85 | ${command}
 86 | 
 87 | Schema:
 88 | ${JSON.stringify(schema.safePacket)}
 89 | `;
 90 | 
 91 |   const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/api/chat`, {
 92 |     method: "POST",
 93 |     headers: {
 94 |       "Content-Type": "application/json",
 95 |     },
 96 |     body: JSON.stringify({
 97 |       model,
 98 |       stream: false,
 99 |       format: "json",
100 |       messages: [
101 |         {
102 |           role: "user",
103 |           content: prompt,
104 |         },
105 |       ],
106 |       options: {
107 |         temperature: 0,
108 |       },
109 |     }),
110 |   });
111 | 
112 |   if (!response.ok) {
113 |     throw new Error(`Ollama failed with ${response.status}`);
114 |   }
115 | 
116 |   const data = await response.json();
117 |   const content = data.message?.content || "{}";
118 | 
119 |   return JSON.parse(content);
120 | }
121 | 
122 | export async function routeAnalystCommand({ schema, command }) {
123 |   const deterministic = deterministicCommand(schema, command);
124 | 
125 |   if (deterministic) return deterministic;
126 | 
127 |   try {
128 |     const plan = await callLlmForPlan(schema, command);
129 | 
130 |     return {
131 |       action: String(plan.action || "ANSWER").toUpperCase(),
132 |       message: plan.message || "Done.",
133 |       chartSpec: plan.chartSpec || plan.chart || null,
134 |       filters: plan.filters || {},
135 |     };
136 |   } catch {
137 |     return {
138 |       action: "GENERATE_CHART",
139 |       message: "Created a chart using local fallback rules.",
140 |       chartSpec: {
141 |         title: "Auto Generated Chart",
142 |         type: command.toLowerCase().includes("pie") ? "pie" : "bar",
143 |         xRole: "dimension",
144 |         yRole: "metric",
145 |         aggregation: command.toLowerCase().includes("average") ? "avg" : "sum",
146 |         limit: 10,
147 |       },
148 |     };
149 |   }
150 | }
151 | 
```
