### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/dashboard-plan-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/dashboard-plan-engine.js`

```js
  1 | import { humanize, normalizeColumnName } from "./schema-fingerprint.js";
  2 | 
  3 | const CHART_TYPES = new Set(["bar", "horizontalBar", "line", "area", "pie", "donut", "histogram", "scatter", "radar", "composed", "heatmap"]);
  4 | const AGGREGATIONS = new Set([
  5 |   "count",
  6 |   "sum",
  7 |   "avg",
  8 |   "min",
  9 |   "max",
 10 |   "median",
 11 |   "count_unique",
 12 | ]);
 13 | 
 14 | function byPriority(profile, roles, nameHints = []) {
 15 |   const columns = profile.columns || [];
 16 |   return columns.find((column) =>
 17 |     roles.includes(column.role) && nameHints.some((hint) => column.normalizedName.includes(hint))
 18 |   ) || columns.find((column) => roles.includes(column.role));
 19 | }
 20 | 
 21 | export function pickPrimaryMetric(profile) {
 22 |   return byPriority(
 23 |     profile,
 24 |     ["money_metric", "score_metric", "rate_metric", "count_metric", "continuous_metric"],
 25 |     ["salary", "revenue", "sales", "amount", "profit", "score", "stress", "anxiety", "depression", "performance"]
 26 |   );
 27 | }
 28 | 
 29 | export function pickSecondaryMetric(profile) {
 30 |   const primary = pickPrimaryMetric(profile);
 31 |   return (profile.columns || []).find((column) =>
 32 |     column.name !== primary?.name && ["continuous_metric", "score_metric", "count_metric", "rate_metric", "money_metric"].includes(column.role)
 33 |   );
 34 | }
 35 | 
 36 | export function pickPrimaryCategory(profile) {
 37 |   return byPriority(
 38 |     profile,
 39 |     ["location", "category", "target", "numeric_category"],
 40 |     ["country", "region", "education", "product", "department", "gender", "platform", "company_size", "status"]
 41 |   );
 42 | }
 43 | 
 44 | export function pickSecondaryCategory(profile) {
 45 |   const primary = pickPrimaryCategory(profile);
 46 |   return (profile.columns || []).find((column) =>
 47 |     column.name !== primary?.name && ["category", "location", "target", "numeric_category"].includes(column.role)
 48 |   );
 49 | }
 50 | 
 51 | export function pickDateColumn(profile) {
 52 |   return (profile.columns || []).find((column) => column.role === "date" || column.type === "date");
 53 | }
 54 | 
 55 | function kpi(id, title, metric, aggregation, options = {}) {
 56 |   return {
 57 |     id,
 58 |     title,
 59 |     metric,
 60 |     aggregation,
 61 |     format: options.format || (aggregation === "count" ? "number" : undefined),
 62 |     description: options.description || "",
 63 |   };
 64 | }
 65 | 
 66 | function chart(id, type, title, xKey, yKey, aggregation = "count", options = {}) {
 67 |   return sanitizeChartSpec({ id, type, title, xKey, yKey, aggregation, limit: options.limit || 10, ...options });
 68 | }
 69 | 
 70 | export function sanitizeChartSpec(spec = {}, profile) {
 71 |   const safe = {
 72 |     id: spec.id || `chart-${Date.now().toString(36)}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 7)}`,
 73 |     type: CHART_TYPES.has(spec.type) ? spec.type : "bar",
 74 |     title: spec.title || "Generated Chart",
 75 |     xKey: spec.xKey || spec.category || spec.dimension || "",
 76 |     yKey: spec.yKey || spec.metric || "count",
 77 |     aggregation: AGGREGATIONS.has(spec.aggregation) ? spec.aggregation : "count",
 78 |     limit: Number.isFinite(Number(spec.limit)) ? Math.max(1, Math.min(50, Number(spec.limit))) : 10,
 79 |     reason: spec.reason || "",
 80 |   };
 81 | 
 82 |   if (profile?.columns?.length) {
 83 |     const names = new Set(profile.columns.map((column) => column.name));
 84 |     const normalizedMap = new Map(profile.columns.map((column) => [column.normalizedName, column.name]));
 85 |     safe.xKey = names.has(safe.xKey) ? safe.xKey : normalizedMap.get(normalizeColumnName(safe.xKey)) || safe.xKey;
 86 |     safe.yKey = safe.yKey === "count" || names.has(safe.yKey) ? safe.yKey : normalizedMap.get(normalizeColumnName(safe.yKey)) || safe.yKey;
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   if (["pie", "donut"].includes(safe.type)) {
 90 |     safe.aggregation = "count";
 91 |     safe.yKey = "count";
 92 |   }
 93 | 
 94 |   if (safe.type === "histogram") {
 95 |     safe.aggregation = "count";
 96 |   }
 97 | 
 98 |   if (spec.splitValues === true) {
 99 |     safe.splitValues = true;
100 |   }
101 | 
102 |   if (Number.isFinite(Number(spec.bins))) {
103 |     safe.bins = Math.max(2, Math.min(50, Number(spec.bins)));
104 |   }
105 | 
106 |   return safe;
107 | }
108 | 
109 | export function sanitizeKpiSpec(spec = {}, profile) {
110 |   const title = spec.title || humanize(`${spec.aggregation || "count"} ${spec.metric || "records"}`);
111 |   return {
112 |     id: spec.id || `kpi-${Date.now().toString(36)}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 7)}`,
113 |     title,
114 |     metric: spec.metric || "__row_count__",
115 |     aggregation: AGGREGATIONS.has(spec.aggregation) ? spec.aggregation : "count",
116 |     format: spec.format || undefined,
117 |     description: spec.description || "",
118 |   };
119 | }
120 | 
121 | export function buildRuleDashboardPlan(profile) {
122 |   const metric = pickPrimaryMetric(profile);
123 |   const metric2 = pickSecondaryMetric(profile);
124 |   const category = pickPrimaryCategory(profile);
125 |   const category2 = pickSecondaryCategory(profile);
126 |   const date = pickDateColumn(profile);
127 | 
128 |   const kpis = [
129 |     kpi("total-records", "Total Records", "__row_count__", "count", { format: "number" }),
130 |   ];
131 | 
132 |   if (metric) {
133 |     const isMoney = metric.role === "money_metric";
134 |     kpis.push(kpi(`avg-${metric.normalizedName}`, `Average ${metric.title}`, metric.name, "avg", { format: isMoney ? "currency" : "number" }));
135 |     kpis.push(kpi(`median-${metric.normalizedName}`, `Median ${metric.title}`, metric.name, "median", { format: isMoney ? "currency" : "number" }));
136 |     kpis.push(kpi(`max-${metric.normalizedName}`, `Highest ${metric.title}`, metric.name, "max", { format: isMoney ? "currency" : "number" }));
137 |   }
138 | 
139 |   if (category) {
140 |     kpis.push(kpi(`unique-${category.normalizedName}`, `${category.title} Count`, category.name, "count_unique", { format: "number" }));
141 |   }
142 | 
143 |   const charts = [];
144 | 
145 |   if (metric && category) {
146 |     charts.push(chart(`avg-${metric.normalizedName}-by-${category.normalizedName}`, "bar", `Average ${metric.title} by ${category.title}`, category.name, metric.name, "avg"));
147 |   }
148 | 
149 |   if (metric) {
150 |     charts.push(chart(`${metric.normalizedName}-distribution`, "histogram", `${metric.title} Distribution`, metric.name, metric.name, "count", { bins: 12 }));
151 |   }
152 | 
153 |   if (category) {
154 |     charts.push(chart(`${category.normalizedName}-distribution`, "donut", `${category.title} Distribution`, category.name, "count", "count"));
155 |   }
156 | 
157 |   if (metric && metric2) {
158 |     charts.push(chart(`${metric.normalizedName}-vs-${metric2.normalizedName}`, "scatter", `${metric.title} vs ${metric2.title}`, metric2.name, metric.name, "count", { limit: 500 }));
159 |   }
160 | 
161 |   if (date && metric) {
162 |     charts.push(chart(`${metric.normalizedName}-trend`, "line", `${metric.title} Trend`, date.name, metric.name, "avg", { limit: 24 }));
163 |   }
164 | 
165 |   if (metric && category2) {
166 |     charts.push(chart(`avg-${metric.normalizedName}-by-${category2.normalizedName}`, "bar", `Average ${metric.title} by ${category2.title}`, category2.name, metric.name, "avg"));
167 |   }
168 | 
169 |   return {
170 |     source: "rules",
171 |     domain: profile.domain,
172 |     kpis: dedupeBy(kpis.map((item) => sanitizeKpiSpec(item, profile)), "title").slice(0, 8),
173 |     charts: dedupeBy(charts.map((item) => sanitizeChartSpec(item, profile)), "title").slice(0, 8),
174 |   };
175 | }
176 | 
177 | function dedupeBy(items, key) {
178 |   const seen = new Set();
179 |   return items.filter((item) => {
180 |     const value = item[key];
181 |     if (seen.has(value)) return false;
182 |     seen.add(value);
183 |     return true;
184 |   });
185 | }
186 | 
187 | function resolveColumn(profile, template = {}, propName, roleName) {
188 |   const raw = template[propName];
189 |   if (raw) {
190 |     const exact = profile.columns.find((column) => column.name === raw || column.normalizedName === normalizeColumnName(raw));
191 |     if (exact) return exact.name;
192 |   }
193 | 
194 |   const role = template[roleName];
195 |   if (role) {
196 |     const byRole = profile.columns.find((column) => column.role === role);
197 |     if (byRole) return byRole.name;
198 |   }
199 | 
200 |   return raw || "";
201 | }
202 | 
203 | export function applyTrainedTemplates(profile, memoryMatch) {
204 |   if (!memoryMatch?.entry?.dashboardPlan) return null;
205 | 
206 |   const plan = memoryMatch.entry.dashboardPlan;
207 |   const kpis = (plan.kpis || []).map((template) => sanitizeKpiSpec({
208 |     ...template,
209 |     metric: template.metric === "__row_count__" ? "__row_count__" : resolveColumn(profile, template, "metric", "metricRole"),
210 |   }, profile));
211 | 
212 |   const charts = (plan.charts || []).map((template) => sanitizeChartSpec({
213 |     ...template,
214 |     xKey: resolveColumn(profile, template, "xKey", "xRole"),
215 |     yKey: template.yKey === "count" ? "count" : resolveColumn(profile, template, "yKey", "yRole"),
216 |   }, profile));
217 | 
218 |   return {
219 |     source: "trained-memory",
220 |     matchedDataset: memoryMatch.entry.name,
221 |     matchScore: memoryMatch.score,
222 |     domain: profile.domain,
223 |     kpis: dedupeBy(kpis, "title").slice(0, 8),
224 |     charts: dedupeBy(charts, "title").slice(0, 8),
225 |   };
226 | }
227 | 
228 | export function mergePlans(profile, ...plans) {
229 |   const validPlans = plans.filter(Boolean);
230 |   const kpis = dedupeBy(validPlans.flatMap((plan) => plan.kpis || []), "title").map((item) => sanitizeKpiSpec(item, profile));
231 |   const charts = dedupeBy(validPlans.flatMap((plan) => plan.charts || []), "title").map((item) => sanitizeChartSpec(item, profile));
232 | 
233 |   return {
234 |     source: validPlans.map((plan) => plan.source).join("+"),
235 |     domain: profile.domain,
236 |     kpis: kpis.slice(0, 8),
237 |     charts: charts.slice(0, 10),
238 |   };
239 | }
240 | 
241 | export function templatePlanForStorage(plan = {}, profile) {
242 |   const columnByName = new Map((profile.columns || []).map((column) => [column.name, column]));
243 | 
244 |   return {
245 |     kpis: (plan.kpis || []).map((item) => {
246 |       const column = columnByName.get(item.metric);
247 |       return {
248 |         title: item.title,
249 |         metric: item.metric === "__row_count__" ? "__row_count__" : item.metric,
250 |         metricRole: column?.role,
251 |         aggregation: item.aggregation || "count",
252 |         format: item.format,
253 |       };
254 |     }),
255 |     charts: (plan.charts || []).map((item) => {
256 |       const x = columnByName.get(item.xKey);
257 |       const y = columnByName.get(item.yKey);
258 |       return {
259 |         title: item.title,
260 |         type: item.type,
261 |         xKey: item.xKey,
262 |         xRole: x?.role,
263 |         yKey: item.yKey || "count",
264 |         yRole: y?.role,
265 |         aggregation: item.aggregation || "count",
266 |         limit: item.limit || 10,
267 |       };
268 |     }),
269 |   };
270 | }
271 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/dashboard-quality-guardian.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/dashboard-quality-guardian.js`

```js
  1 | import {
  2 |   buildRuleDashboardPlan,
  3 |   sanitizeChartSpec,
  4 |   sanitizeKpiSpec,
  5 | } from "./dashboard-plan-engine.js";
  6 | 
  7 | const METRIC_ROLES = new Set([
  8 |   "money_metric",
  9 |   "score_metric",
 10 |   "rate_metric",
 11 |   "count_metric",
 12 |   "continuous_metric",
 13 |   "metric",
 14 | ]);
 15 | 
 16 | const CATEGORY_ROLES = new Set([
 17 |   "location",
 18 |   "category",
 19 |   "target",
 20 |   "numeric_category",
 21 |   "dimension",
 22 |   "date",
 23 | ]);
 24 | 
 25 | const CHART_TYPES = new Set([
 26 |   "bar",
 27 |   "horizontalBar",
 28 |   "line",
 29 |   "area",
 30 |   "pie",
 31 |   "donut",
 32 |   "histogram",
 33 |   "scatter",
 34 |   "radar",
 35 |   "composed",
 36 |   "heatmap",
 37 | ]);
 38 | 
 39 | const AGGREGATIONS = new Set([
 40 |   "count",
 41 |   "sum",
 42 |   "avg",
 43 |   "min",
 44 |   "max",
 45 |   "median",
 46 |   "count_unique",
 47 | ]);
 48 | 
 49 | function normalize(value = "") {
 50 |   return String(value)
 51 |     .trim()
 52 |     .toLowerCase()
 53 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
 54 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
 55 | }
 56 | 
 57 | function getColumns(profile) {
 58 |   return Array.isArray(profile?.columns) ? profile.columns : [];
 59 | }
 60 | 
 61 | function getColumn(profile, name) {
 62 |   const wanted = normalize(name);
 63 |   return getColumns(profile).find((column) => {
 64 |     const ownName = normalize(column.name);
 65 |     const normalizedName = normalize(column.normalizedName || column.name);
 66 |     return ownName === wanted || normalizedName === wanted;
 67 |   });
 68 | }
 69 | 
 70 | function findColumn(profile, aliases = [], roles = []) {
 71 |   const normalizedAliases = aliases.map(normalize).filter(Boolean);
 72 | 
 73 |   const scored = getColumns(profile)
 74 |     .map((column) => {
 75 |       const name = normalize(column.name);
 76 |       const normalizedName = normalize(column.normalizedName || column.name);
 77 |       let score = 0;
 78 | 
 79 |       for (const alias of normalizedAliases) {
 80 |         if (name === alias || normalizedName === alias) score += 30;
 81 |         else if (name.includes(alias) || normalizedName.includes(alias)) score += 14;
 82 |         else if (alias.includes(name) || alias.includes(normalizedName)) score += 6;
 83 |       }
 84 | 
 85 |       if (roles.includes(column.role)) score += 10;
 86 |       if (roles.includes("metric") && METRIC_ROLES.has(column.role)) score += 5;
 87 |       if (roles.includes("category") && CATEGORY_ROLES.has(column.role)) score += 5;
 88 | 
 89 |       return { column, score };
 90 |     })
 91 |     .filter((item) => item.score > 0)
 92 |     .sort((left, right) => right.score - left.score);
 93 | 
 94 |   return scored[0]?.column || null;
 95 | }
 96 | 
 97 | function isMetricColumn(column) {
 98 |   return Boolean(column && (METRIC_ROLES.has(column.role) || column.type === "number"));
 99 | }
100 | 
101 | function isCategoryColumn(column) {
102 |   return Boolean(
103 |     column &&
104 |       (CATEGORY_ROLES.has(column.role) ||
105 |         ["string", "category", "boolean", "date"].includes(column.type))
106 |   );
107 | }
108 | 
109 | function isDateColumn(column) {
110 |   return Boolean(column && (column.role === "date" || column.type === "date"));
111 | }
112 | 
113 | function looksMultiValueColumn(column) {
114 |   const name = normalize(column?.name || column?.normalizedName || "");
115 |   return /(language|languages|framework|frameworks|skill|skills|tag|tags|categor|tools|technolog)/.test(name);
116 | }
117 | 
118 | function uniqBy(items, keyFn) {
119 |   const seen = new Set();
120 |   const output = [];
121 | 
122 |   for (const item of items || []) {
123 |     const key = keyFn(item);
124 |     if (!key || seen.has(key)) continue;
125 |     seen.add(key);
126 |     output.push(item);
127 |   }
128 | 
129 |   return output;
130 | }
131 | 
132 | function stripUnsafeChartData(chart) {
133 |   if (!chart || typeof chart !== "object") return chart;
134 |   const { data, value, values, rows, rawRows, sampleRows, calculatedValues, ...safe } = chart;
135 |   return safe;
136 | }
137 | 
138 | function stripUnsafeKpiData(kpi) {
139 |   if (!kpi || typeof kpi !== "object") return kpi;
140 |   const { value, values, data, rows, rawRows, sampleRows, calculatedValues, ...safe } = kpi;
141 |   return safe;
142 | }
143 | 
144 | function normalizeKpiSpec(profile, spec) {
145 |   const safe = sanitizeKpiSpec(stripUnsafeKpiData(spec), profile);
146 |   if (!AGGREGATIONS.has(safe.aggregation)) safe.aggregation = "count";
147 |   return safe;
148 | }
149 | 
150 | function normalizeChartSpec(profile, spec) {
151 |   const input = stripUnsafeChartData(spec || {});
152 |   const safe = sanitizeChartSpec(input, profile);
153 | 
154 |   safe.type = CHART_TYPES.has(safe.type) ? safe.type : "bar";
155 |   safe.aggregation = AGGREGATIONS.has(safe.aggregation) ? safe.aggregation : "count";
156 |   safe.limit = Math.max(
157 |     1,
158 |     Math.min(Number(safe.limit || input.limit || 10), safe.type === "scatter" ? 1000 : 50)
159 |   );
160 | 
161 |   const x = getColumn(profile, safe.xKey);
162 |   if (input.splitValues === true || looksMultiValueColumn(x)) safe.splitValues = true;
163 | 
164 |   if (["pie", "donut"].includes(safe.type)) {
165 |     safe.yKey = "count";
166 |     safe.aggregation = "count";
167 |   }
168 | 
169 |   if (safe.type === "histogram") {
170 |     safe.yKey = safe.xKey;
171 |     safe.aggregation = "count";
172 |   }
173 | 
174 |   if (safe.type === "scatter") {
175 |     safe.aggregation = "count";
176 |     safe.limit = Math.max(safe.limit, 500);
177 |   }
178 | 
179 |   delete safe.data;
180 |   delete safe.rows;
181 |   delete safe.rawRows;
182 |   delete safe.sampleRows;
183 | 
184 |   return safe;
185 | }
186 | 
187 | export function validateChartCombination(profile, chartSpec = {}) {
188 |   const chart = normalizeChartSpec(profile, chartSpec);
189 |   const x = getColumn(profile, chart.xKey);
190 |   const y = chart.yKey === "count" ? null : getColumn(profile, chart.yKey);
191 | 
192 |   if (!CHART_TYPES.has(chart.type)) {
193 |     return { ok: false, reason: `Unsupported chart type: ${chart.type}` };
194 |   }
195 | 
196 |   if (chart.type === "histogram") {
197 |     if (!x || !isMetricColumn(x)) return { ok: false, reason: "Histogram needs one numeric metric column." };
198 |     return { ok: true, chart };
199 |   }
200 | 
201 |   if (chart.type === "scatter") {
202 |     if (!x || !y || !isMetricColumn(x) || !isMetricColumn(y)) {
203 |       return { ok: false, reason: "Scatter chart needs two numeric metric columns." };
204 |     }
205 |     return { ok: true, chart };
206 |   }
207 | 
208 |   if (["pie", "donut"].includes(chart.type)) {
209 |     if (!x || !isCategoryColumn(x)) return { ok: false, reason: "Pie/donut chart needs a category column." };
210 |     return { ok: true, chart };
211 |   }
212 | 
213 |   if (["line", "area"].includes(chart.type)) {
214 |     if (!x) return { ok: false, reason: "Line/area chart needs an X column." };
215 |     if (!isDateColumn(x) && !isCategoryColumn(x)) {
216 |       return { ok: false, reason: "Line/area chart needs date or ordered category on X." };
217 |     }
218 |     if (chart.yKey !== "count" && (!y || !isMetricColumn(y))) {
219 |       return { ok: false, reason: "Line/area chart needs numeric Y metric." };
220 |     }
221 |     return { ok: true, chart };
222 |   }
223 | 
224 |   if (["bar", "horizontalBar", "radar", "composed", "heatmap"].includes(chart.type)) {
225 |     if (!x) return { ok: false, reason: `Column ${chart.xKey} does not exist.` };
226 |     if (chart.yKey !== "count" && (!y || !isMetricColumn(y))) {
227 |       return { ok: false, reason: "This chart needs numeric metric on Y." };
228 |     }
229 |     return { ok: true, chart };
230 |   }
231 | 
232 |   return { ok: true, chart };
233 | }
234 | 
235 | function isWorkforceSalarySchema(profile) {
236 |   const names = new Set(getColumns(profile).map((column) => normalize(column.name)));
237 |   const hasSalary = [...names].some((name) => /salary|compensation|income|pay/.test(name));
238 |   const hasWorkforceHints = ["experience", "country", "education", "languages", "frameworks", "company_size"]
239 |     .some((hint) => names.has(hint));
240 |   return hasSalary && hasWorkforceHints;
241 | }
242 | 
243 | function workforceSalaryPlan(profile) {
244 |   const salary = findColumn(profile, ["salary_usd", "salary", "annual_salary", "compensation", "income"], ["money_metric", "metric"]);
245 |   const experience = findColumn(profile, ["experience", "years_experience", "years_coding", "exp"], ["continuous_metric", "metric"]);
246 |   const country = findColumn(profile, ["country", "location", "region"], ["location", "category"]);
247 |   const education = findColumn(profile, ["education", "degree", "qualification"], ["category"]);
248 |   const companySize = findColumn(profile, ["company_size", "companysize", "organization_size", "org_size"], ["category"]);
249 |   const languages = findColumn(profile, ["languages", "language", "programming_languages", "skills"], ["category"]);
250 |   const frameworks = findColumn(profile, ["frameworks", "framework", "tools", "libraries"], ["category"]);
251 | 
252 |   if (!salary) return null;
253 | 
254 |   const kpis = [
255 |     { title: "Total Records", metric: "__row_count__", aggregation: "count", format: "number" },
256 |     { title: "Average Salary", metric: salary.name, metricRole: salary.role, aggregation: "avg", format: "currency" },
257 |     { title: "Median Salary", metric: salary.name, metricRole: salary.role, aggregation: "median", format: "currency" },
258 |     { title: "Highest Salary", metric: salary.name, metricRole: salary.role, aggregation: "max", format: "currency" },
259 |   ];
260 | 
261 |   if (country) {
262 |     kpis.push({ title: "Countries", metric: country.name, metricRole: country.role, aggregation: "count_unique", format: "number" });
263 |   }
264 | 
265 |   const charts = [];
266 |   if (country) charts.push({ title: "Average Salary by Country", type: "bar", xKey: country.name, yKey: salary.name, aggregation: "avg", limit: 10 });
267 |   charts.push({ title: "Salary Distribution", type: "histogram", xKey: salary.name, yKey: salary.name, aggregation: "count", limit: 12 });
268 |   if (experience) charts.push({ title: "Salary vs Experience", type: "scatter", xKey: experience.name, yKey: salary.name, aggregation: "count", limit: 500 });
269 |   if (education) charts.push({ title: "Education Distribution", type: "donut", xKey: education.name, yKey: "count", aggregation: "count", limit: 10 });
270 |   if (companySize) charts.push({ title: "Average Salary by Company Size", type: "bar", xKey: companySize.name, yKey: salary.name, aggregation: "avg", limit: 10 });
271 |   if (languages) charts.push({ title: "Average Salary by Language", type: "bar", xKey: languages.name, yKey: salary.name, aggregation: "avg", limit: 10, splitValues: true });
272 |   if (frameworks) charts.push({ title: "Average Salary by Framework", type: "bar", xKey: frameworks.name, yKey: salary.name, aggregation: "avg", limit: 10, splitValues: true });
273 | 
274 |   return {
275 |     source: "guardian-workforce-salary",
276 |     domain: profile.domain || "workforce_salary",
277 |     kpis,
278 |     charts,
279 |   };
280 | }
281 | 
282 | function ensureRuleFallback(profile, dashboardPlan) {
283 |   const rulePlan = buildRuleDashboardPlan(profile);
284 |   return {
285 |     source: [dashboardPlan?.source, "guardian-rules"].filter(Boolean).join("+"),
286 |     domain: dashboardPlan?.domain || profile.domain,
287 |     kpis: [...(dashboardPlan?.kpis || []), ...(rulePlan.kpis || [])],
288 |     charts: [...(dashboardPlan?.charts || []), ...(rulePlan.charts || [])],
289 |     filters: [...(dashboardPlan?.filters || []), ...(rulePlan.filters || [])],
290 |   };
291 | }
292 | 
293 | export function enforceDashboardQuality(profile, dashboardPlan = {}, options = {}) {
294 |   const maxCharts = options.maxCharts || 7;
295 |   const maxKpis = options.maxKpis || 8;
296 | 
297 |   let combined = ensureRuleFallback(profile, dashboardPlan);
298 | 
299 |   if (isWorkforceSalarySchema(profile)) {
300 |     const salaryPlan = workforceSalaryPlan(profile);
301 |     if (salaryPlan) {
302 |       combined = {
303 |         source: [salaryPlan.source, combined.source].filter(Boolean).join("+"),
304 |         domain: combined.domain || salaryPlan.domain,
305 |         kpis: [...salaryPlan.kpis, ...(combined.kpis || [])],
306 |         charts: [...salaryPlan.charts, ...(combined.charts || [])],
307 |         filters: combined.filters || [],
308 |       };
309 |     }
310 |   }
311 | 
312 |   const safeKpis = uniqBy(
313 |     (combined.kpis || [])
314 |       .map((item) => normalizeKpiSpec(profile, item))
315 |       .filter((kpi) => kpi.metric === "__row_count__" || kpi.metric === "__column_count__" || Boolean(getColumn(profile, kpi.metric))),
316 |     (kpi) => normalize(`${kpi.title}-${kpi.metric}-${kpi.aggregation}`)
317 |   ).slice(0, maxKpis);
318 | 
319 |   const safeCharts = uniqBy(
320 |     (combined.charts || [])
321 |       .map((item) => normalizeChartSpec(profile, item))
322 |       .map((chart) => validateChartCombination(profile, chart))
323 |       .filter((result) => result.ok)
324 |       .map((result) => result.chart),
325 |     (chart) => normalize(`${chart.title}-${chart.type}-${chart.xKey}-${chart.yKey}-${chart.aggregation}`)
326 |   ).slice(0, maxCharts);
327 | 
328 |   return {
329 |     source: combined.source || "dashboard-quality-guardian",
330 |     domain: combined.domain || profile.domain,
331 |     schemaOnly: true,
332 |     kpis: safeKpis,
333 |     charts: safeCharts,
334 |     filters: Array.isArray(combined.filters) ? combined.filters.slice(0, 8) : [],
335 |     guardian: {
336 |       enforced: true,
337 |       chartCount: safeCharts.length,
338 |       kpiCount: safeKpis.length,
339 |       maxCharts,
340 |       maxKpis,
341 |       unsafeAiDataRemoved: true,
342 |       schemaOnly: true,
343 |       warnings: [],
344 |       fixes: ["Removed unsafe AI values/data and validated dashboard specs."],
345 |     },
346 |     qualityGuard: {
347 |       applied: true,
348 |       expectedCharts: maxCharts,
349 |       actualCharts: safeCharts.length,
350 |       schemaOnly: true,
351 |       reason: isWorkforceSalarySchema(profile)
352 |         ? "Strict workforce salary dashboard pattern enforced."
353 |         : "Generic dashboard quality rules enforced.",
354 |     },
355 |   };
356 | }
357 | 
358 | export function assessDashboardHealth(profile, dashboardPlan = {}) {
359 |   const issues = [];
360 |   const warnings = [];
361 |   const charts = Array.isArray(dashboardPlan.charts) ? dashboardPlan.charts : [];
362 |   const kpis = Array.isArray(dashboardPlan.kpis) ? dashboardPlan.kpis : [];
363 | 
364 |   if (!kpis.length) issues.push({ type: "missing_kpis", message: "Dashboard has no KPI cards." });
365 |   if (!charts.length) issues.push({ type: "missing_charts", message: "Dashboard has no charts." });
366 |   if (charts.some((chart) => Array.isArray(chart.data))) {
367 |     issues.push({ type: "unsafe_chart_data", message: "Chart data exists in dashboard plan. AI must not provide chart.data." });
368 |   }
369 |   if (kpis.some((kpi) => Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(kpi, "value"))) {
370 |     issues.push({ type: "unsafe_kpi_value", message: "KPI values exist in dashboard plan. Values must be calculated locally." });
371 |   }
372 | 
373 |   for (const chart of charts) {
374 |     const result = validateChartCombination(profile, chart);
375 |     if (!result.ok) issues.push({ type: "invalid_chart", chart: chart.title, message: result.reason });
376 |   }
377 | 
378 |   if (isWorkforceSalarySchema(profile) && charts.length < 7) {
379 |     warnings.push({ type: "incomplete_salary_dashboard", message: "Salary/workforce dataset should have up to 7 useful charts when columns exist." });
380 |   }
381 | 
382 |   const score = Math.max(0, 100 - issues.length * 20 - warnings.length * 8);
383 |   return {
384 |     status: issues.length ? "failed" : warnings.length ? "warning" : "healthy",
385 |     score,
386 |     issues,
387 |     warnings,
388 |   };
389 | }
390 | 
391 | export function getDashboardQualityIssues(profile, dashboardPlan = {}) {
392 |   return assessDashboardHealth(profile, dashboardPlan).issues;
393 | }
394 | 
395 | export function buildGuardianDashboardResponse(profile, dashboardPlan, options = {}) {
396 |   const dashboard = enforceDashboardQuality(profile, dashboardPlan, options);
397 |   return {
398 |     dashboard,
399 |     safePlan: dashboard,
400 |     qualityScore: assessDashboardHealth(profile, dashboard).score,
401 |     dashboardHealth: assessDashboardHealth(profile, dashboard),
402 |     schemaOnly: true,
403 |     warnings: dashboard.guardian?.warnings || [],
404 |     fixes: dashboard.guardian?.fixes || [],
405 |   };
406 | }
407 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/knowledge/ontology-mapper.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/knowledge/ontology-mapper.js`

```js
 1 | import fs from 'fs';
 2 | import path from 'path';
 3 | import { fileURLToPath } from 'url';
 4 | 
 5 | const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
 6 | const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);
 7 | 
 8 | const ontologyPath = path.join(__dirname, 'business-ontology.json');
 9 | const ontologyRaw = fs.readFileSync(ontologyPath, 'utf8');
10 | const ontology = JSON.parse(ontologyRaw);
11 | 
12 | /**
13 |  * Maps a given array of raw column names to canonical terms based on the business ontology.
14 |  */
15 | export function mapSchemaToOntology(schemaColumns = []) {
16 |   const mappedTerms = new Set();
17 |   const rawToCanonical = {};
18 | 
19 |   for (const col of schemaColumns) {
20 |     const rawName = col.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]/g, '');
21 |     let matchedCanonical = null;
22 | 
23 |     for (const [canonical, synonyms] of Object.entries(ontology)) {
24 |       if (rawName.includes(canonical)) {
25 |         matchedCanonical = canonical;
26 |         break;
27 |       }
28 |       for (const synonym of synonyms) {
29 |         if (rawName.includes(synonym)) {
30 |           matchedCanonical = canonical;
31 |           break;
32 |         }
33 |       }
34 |     }
35 | 
36 |     if (matchedCanonical) {
37 |       mappedTerms.add(matchedCanonical);
38 |       rawToCanonical[col] = matchedCanonical;
39 |     }
40 |   }
41 | 
42 |   // Very simple heuristic to guess domain based on canonical overlaps
43 |   const terms = Array.from(mappedTerms);
44 |   let inferredDomain = 'generic';
45 | 
46 |   if (terms.includes('salary') || (terms.includes('performance') && terms.includes('cost'))) inferredDomain = 'hr_salary';
47 |   if (terms.includes('revenue') && terms.includes('customer')) inferredDomain = 'sales';
48 |   if (terms.includes('order') && terms.includes('product')) inferredDomain = 'ecommerce';
49 |   if (terms.includes('campaign') || terms.includes('conversion')) inferredDomain = 'marketing';
50 |   if (terms.includes('churn') || terms.includes('customer')) inferredDomain = 'crm';
51 |   if (terms.includes('fraud') || terms.includes('risk')) inferredDomain = 'banking_fraud';
52 |   if (terms.includes('claim')) inferredDomain = 'insurance';
53 |   if (terms.includes('delivery') || terms.includes('stock')) inferredDomain = 'logistics_inventory';
54 | 
55 |   return {
56 |     inferredDomain,
57 |     canonicalTerms: terms,
58 |     mapping: rawToCanonical
59 |   };
60 | }
61 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/kpi-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/kpi-engine.js`

```js
  1 | import { findColumn, safeNumber } from "./schema-profiler.js";
  2 | 
  3 | function aggregate(values = [], aggregation = "count") {
  4 |   const present = values.filter(
  5 |     (value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== ""
  6 |   );
  7 | 
  8 |   if (aggregation === "count") return present.length;
  9 | 
 10 |   if (aggregation === "count_unique") {
 11 |     return new Set(present.map((v) => String(v).trim().toLowerCase())).size;
 12 |   }
 13 | 
 14 |   const numbers = present.map(safeNumber).filter((value) => value !== null);
 15 | 
 16 |   if (!numbers.length) return 0;
 17 | 
 18 |   if (aggregation === "sum") return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
 19 |   if (aggregation === "avg") return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / numbers.length;
 20 |   if (aggregation === "min") return Math.min(...numbers);
 21 |   if (aggregation === "max") return Math.max(...numbers);
 22 | 
 23 |   if (aggregation === "median") {
 24 |     const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 25 |     const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
 26 |     return sorted.length % 2
 27 |       ? sorted[mid]
 28 |       : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
 29 |   }
 30 | 
 31 |   if (aggregation === "p75") {
 32 |     const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 33 |     const index = Math.ceil(sorted.length * 0.75) - 1;
 34 |     return sorted[Math.max(0, index)];
 35 |   }
 36 | 
 37 |   if (aggregation === "p25") {
 38 |     const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 39 |     const index = Math.ceil(sorted.length * 0.25) - 1;
 40 |     return sorted[Math.max(0, index)];
 41 |   }
 42 | 
 43 |   return present.length;
 44 | }
 45 | 
 46 | function calculateQualityScore(rows, schema) {
 47 |   const totalCells = Math.max(rows.length * schema.columns.length, 1);
 48 |   const missingCells = schema.columns.reduce(
 49 |     (sum, column) => sum + column.nullCount,
 50 |     0
 51 |   );
 52 |   return ((totalCells - missingCells) / totalCells) * 100;
 53 | }
 54 | 
 55 | function formatValue(metricName, value, aggregation) {
 56 |   const name = String(metricName || "").toLowerCase();
 57 | 
 58 |   if (aggregation === "count_unique") {
 59 |     return Math.round(value).toLocaleString();
 60 |   }
 61 | 
 62 |   if (/salary|revenue|sales|amount|price|cost|profit|income|compensation/.test(name)) {
 63 |     return `$${Math.round(value).toLocaleString()}`;
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   if (/rate|percent|margin|quality|attendance/.test(name)) {
 67 |     return `${Number(value).toFixed(1)}%`;
 68 |   }
 69 | 
 70 |   if (Number.isInteger(value) || Math.abs(value) >= 1000) {
 71 |     return Math.round(value).toLocaleString();
 72 |   }
 73 |   return Number(value.toFixed ? value.toFixed(2) : value).toLocaleString();
 74 | }
 75 | 
 76 | function buildKpiFromTemplate(rows, schema, template) {
 77 |   if (template.aggregation === "count") {
 78 |     return {
 79 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
 80 |       title: template.title,
 81 |       value: rows.length.toLocaleString(),
 82 |       metric: "*",
 83 |       aggregation: "count",
 84 |     };
 85 |   }
 86 | 
 87 |   if (template.aggregation === "quality_score") {
 88 |     return {
 89 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
 90 |       title: template.title,
 91 |       value: `${calculateQualityScore(rows, schema).toFixed(1)}%`,
 92 |       metric: "*",
 93 |       aggregation: "quality_score",
 94 |     };
 95 |   }
 96 | 
 97 |   if (template.aggregation === "count_unique") {
 98 |     const col =
 99 |       findColumn(schema, template.metricAliases || [], "dimension") ||
100 |       findColumn(schema, template.metricAliases || [], "category") ||
101 |       findColumn(schema, template.metricAliases || [], "location");
102 | 
103 |     if (!col) return null;
104 | 
105 |     const uniqueCount = new Set(
106 |       rows
107 |         .map((row) => row[col.name])
108 |         .filter((v) => v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== "")
109 |         .map((v) => String(v).trim().toLowerCase())
110 |     ).size;
111 | 
112 |     return {
113 |       id: crypto.randomUUID(),
114 |       title: template.title,
115 |       value: uniqueCount.toLocaleString(),
116 |       metric: col.name,
117 |       aggregation: "count_unique",
118 |     };
119 |   }
120 | 
121 |   const metric =
122 |     findColumn(schema, template.metricAliases || [], "metric") ||
123 |     (template.metricRole ? findColumn(schema, [], template.metricRole) : null) ||
124 |     findColumn(schema, [], "metric");
125 | 
126 |   if (!metric) return null;
127 | 
128 |   const value = aggregate(
129 |     rows.map((row) => row[metric.name]),
130 |     template.aggregation
131 |   );
132 | 
133 |   return {
134 |     id: crypto.randomUUID(),
135 |     title: template.title,
136 |     value: formatValue(metric.name, value, template.aggregation),
137 |     metric: metric.name,
138 |     aggregation: template.aggregation,
139 |   };
140 | }
141 | 
142 | export function buildAutoKpis({ dataset, schema, playbook, memoryMatch }) {
143 |   const rows = dataset.rows || [];
144 | 
145 |   const baseKpis = (playbook.kpis || [])
146 |     .map((template) => buildKpiFromTemplate(rows, schema, template))
147 |     .filter(Boolean);
148 | 
149 |   const learnedKpis = (memoryMatch?.kpiTemplates || [])
150 |     .map((template) =>
151 |       buildKpiFromTemplate(rows, schema, {
152 |         title: template.title,
153 |         metricAliases: [template.metric],
154 |         aggregation: template.aggregation,
155 |       })
156 |     )
157 |     .filter(Boolean);
158 | 
159 |   const merged = [...learnedKpis, ...baseKpis];
160 | 
161 |   const seen = new Set();
162 | 
163 |   return merged
164 |     .filter((kpi) => {
165 |       if (seen.has(kpi.title)) return false;
166 |       seen.add(kpi.title);
167 |       return true;
168 |     })
169 |     .slice(0, 8);
170 | }
171 | 
```

  8 | const INSIGHTFLOW_DASHBOARD_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are InsightFlow AI.
  9 | 
 10 | You are not a chart generator. You are a Chief Data Analyst, RAG-aware Schema Engine, and production dashboard architect.
 11 | 
 12 | Think like a McKinsey analyst, Tableau expert, Power BI expert, senior data scientist, and BI consultant.
 13 | 
 14 | You convert schema-only dataset intelligence into an enterprise-grade analytics dashboard. You NEVER see raw dataset rows.
 15 | 
 16 | You only receive:
 17 | - Dataset schema and column roles
 18 | - Semantic business ontology / schema understanding
 19 | - RAG knowledge base matches
 20 | - Calculated statistics already present in schema/profile packets
 21 | 
 22 | Reasoning process:
 23 | 1. Understand the dataset domain from schema semantics, examples, roles, and RAG.
 24 | 2. Identify primary KPI, secondary KPIs, dimensions, segments, dates, geography, and relationships.
 25 | 3. Generate dashboard intent: Executive, Analyst, Story, Forecast, Geo Analysis, and AI Insights where supported by schema.
 26 | 4. Prefer the most useful KPI and chart decisions first. Every KPI and chart must have business value.
 27 | 
 28 | Mandatory dashboard intent:
 29 | - Executive summary metadata
 30 | - 4 to 6 KPI cards where schema supports them
 31 | - Minimum 4 and maximum 8 useful charts
 32 | - Top insights and AI recommendations metadata
 33 | - Story mode metadata
 34 | - Agent execution timeline metadata
 35 | 
 36 | KPI rules:
 37 | - Never hardcode KPI names by dataset type.
 38 | - Infer KPI names from schema meaning and RAG context.
 39 | - Include Total Records only as a supporting KPI; do not make it the main business KPI when a meaningful metric exists.
 40 | - Examples: salary implies average/median/highest salary; sales implies revenue/profit/margin/orders/customers; healthcare implies patients/diseases/risk/recovery where columns exist.
 41 | 
 42 | Chart rules:
 43 | - Numeric + category -> bar or horizontal bar.
 44 | - Numeric + numeric -> scatter.
 45 | - Date + numeric -> line or area.
 46 | - Category distribution -> donut or pie.
 47 | - Many categories / rankings -> horizontal bar.
 48 | - Correlation -> heatmap.
 49 | - Geographic fields -> geo analysis metadata and a ranked regional chart using supported chart types.
 50 | 
 51 | Geo rules:
 52 | - Generate geo analysis only when schema contains country, state, region, city, zipcode, latitude, or longitude semantics.
 53 | - Include geoAnalysis metadata for world map, choropleth, bubble map, geo KPI cards, regional ranking, and regional insights.
 54 | - Do not invent geographic values or percentages.
 55 | 
 56 | Executive summary rules:
 57 | - Generate overview, topTrend, biggestOpportunity, biggestRisk, businessRecommendation, and confidenceScore metadata.
 58 | - Insights must be explainable from schema meaning, RAG context, or provided statistics.
 59 | 
 60 | Critical safety:
 61 | - Never generate random charts.
 62 | - Never invent KPI values, chart.data, raw rows, sample records, or private row-derived facts.
 63 | - Use only existing schema columns.
 64 | - Return strict JSON only.`;
 65 | 
 66 | function extractJson(text = "") {
 67 |   const trimmed = String(text).trim();
 68 |   if (!trimmed) return null;
 69 | 
 70 |   try {
 71 |     return JSON.parse(trimmed);
 72 |   } catch {}
 73 | 
 74 |   const fenced = trimmed.match(/```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)```/i);
 75 |   if (fenced) {
 76 |     try { return JSON.parse(fenced[1]); } catch {}
 77 |   }
 78 | 
 79 |   const first = trimmed.indexOf("{");
 80 |   const last = trimmed.lastIndexOf("}");
 81 |   if (first >= 0 && last > first) {
 82 |     try { return JSON.parse(trimmed.slice(first, last + 1)); } catch {}
 83 |   }
 84 | 
 85 |   return null;
 86 | }
 87 | 
 88 | async function callOllama({ model, prompt, temperature = 0.1 }) {
 89 |   const controller = new AbortController();
 90 |   const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 30000);
 91 | 
 92 |   try {
 93 |     const response = await fetch(`${DEFAULT_OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/generate`, {
 94 |       method: "POST",
 95 |       headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
 96 |       body: JSON.stringify({
 97 |         model,
 98 |         prompt,
 99 |         stream: false,
100 |         options: {
101 |           temperature,
102 |           num_ctx: 8192,
103 |         },
104 |       }),
105 |       signal: controller.signal,
106 |     });
107 | 
108 |     if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`Ollama HTTP ${response.status}`);
109 |     const payload = await response.json();
110 |     return payload.response || "";
111 |   } finally {
112 |     clearTimeout(timer);
113 |   }
114 | }
115 |

122 |       name: entry.name,

125 |       schemaSignature: entry.schemaSignature,

169 | function buildDashboardPrompt(schemaProfile, memoryMatch, options = {}) {
170 |   const packet = makeSchemaOnlyPacket(schemaProfile);
171 | 
172 |   const legacyMemory = memoryMatch?.entry

177 |       }

181 |   const smartUnderstanding = options.schemaUnderstanding || null;
182 | 
183 |   return `${INSIGHTFLOW_DASHBOARD_SYSTEM_PROMPT}
184 | 
185 | YOUR JOB:
186 | Understand the dataset schema and create the best dashboard plan.
187 | 
188 | VERY IMPORTANT:
189 | You do NOT see raw data rows.
190 | You only see schema metadata, profile stats, role detection, and RAG memory.
191 | You must return dashboard specifications only.
192 | The local analytics engine will calculate real values later.
193 | 
194 | NEVER RETURN:
195 | - raw rows
196 | - chart.data
197 | - KPI values
198 | - fake numbers
199 | - sample records
200 | - SQL over private rows
201 | 
202 | ALLOWED OUTPUT:
203 | - action
204 | - message
205 | - kpis
206 | - charts
207 | - executiveSummary
208 | - geoAnalysis
209 | - insights
210 | - recommendations
211 | - storyMode
212 | - confidenceScore
213 | - schemaOnly
214 | 
215 | ALLOWED CHART TYPES:
216 | bar, horizontalBar, line, area, pie, donut, histogram, scatter, radar, composed, heatmap
217 | 
218 | ALLOWED AGGREGATIONS:
219 | count, sum, avg, min, max, median, count_unique
220 | 
221 | DASHBOARD QUALITY RULES:
222 | 1. Always include Total Records KPI, but prioritize a meaningful primary business metric when available.
223 | 2. Prefer 4 to 6 useful KPIs.
224 | 3. Prefer 4 to 8 useful charts.
225 | 4. Use bar chart for metric by category.
226 | 5. Use histogram for numeric distribution.
227 | 6. Use scatter for metric vs metric.
228 | 7. Use line chart for metric by date.
229 | 8. Use donut/pie only for category distribution.
230 | 9. Use count_unique for unique category count.
231 | 10. For language/framework/skill/tag columns, add splitValues: true.
232 | 11. Use only existing column names from the current schema.
233 | 12. If geography exists, include geoAnalysis metadata and at least one regional ranking chart using a supported chart type.
234 | 
235 | CURRENT SCHEMA PACKET:
236 | ${JSON.stringify(packet, null, 2)}
237 | 
238 | SMART SCHEMA UNDERSTANDING:
239 | ${JSON.stringify(smartUnderstanding, null, 2)}
240 | 
241 | LEGACY TRAINED MEMORY:
242 | ${JSON.stringify(legacyMemory, null, 2)}
243 | 
244 | RETRIEVED RAG MEMORIES:
245 | ${JSON.stringify(retrievedRagMemories, null, 2)}
246 | 
247 | RETURN STRICT JSON ONLY:
248 | {
249 |   "action": "GENERATE_DASHBOARD",
250 |   "message": "short explanation for the user",
251 |   "kpis": [
252 |     {
253 |       "title": "Total Records",
254 |       "metric": "__row_count__",
255 |       "aggregation": "count",
256 |       "format": "number"
257 |     }
258 |   ],
259 |   "charts": [
260 |     {
261 |       "type": "bar",
262 |       "title": "Average Salary by Country",
263 |       "xKey": "country",
264 |       "yKey": "salary_usd",
265 |       "aggregation": "avg",
266 |       "limit": 10
267 |     }
268 |   ],
269 |   "executiveSummary": {
270 |     "overview": "Schema-aware overview without invented values.",
271 |     "topTrend": "Trend to calculate from available date and metric columns, or null.",
272 |     "biggestOpportunity": "Opportunity implied by segment/metric semantics, or null.",
273 |     "biggestRisk": "Risk implied by schema/statistics, or null.",
274 |     "businessRecommendation": "Recommended analytical action.",
275 |     "confidenceScore": 0.85
276 |   },
277 |   "geoAnalysis": [],
278 |   "insights": [],
279 |   "recommendations": [],
280 |   "storyMode": {
281 |     "whatHappened": "What the dashboard should evaluate.",
282 |     "whyItHappened": "Drivers to test from schema relationships.",
283 |     "whatWillHappen": "Forecast intent if date and metric columns exist.",
284 |     "recommendedAction": "Next analytical action."
285 |   },
286 |   "confidenceScore": 0.85,
287 |   "schemaOnly": true
288 | }`;

291 | function buildCommandPrompt(query, schemaProfile, memoryMatch, currentDashboard, options = {}) {
292 |   const packet = makeSchemaOnlyPacket(schemaProfile);
293 |   const retrievedRagMemories = safeRagMatchesForPrompt(options.ragMatches);
294 | 
295 |   return `${INSIGHTFLOW_DASHBOARD_SYSTEM_PROMPT}
296 | 
297 | You are InsightFlow's schema-only dashboard command router.
298 | 
299 | Rules:
300 | - Return strict JSON only.
301 | - Never include raw data rows.
302 | - Never include chart.data.
303 | - Never include KPI values.
304 | - Use only schema column names.
305 | - RAG memories are examples only, not real values.
306 | - If user asks a normal question, return action ANSWER with a short answer.
307 | - If user asks chart/KPI/filter change, return action and spec.
308 | 
309 | Allowed actions:
310 | GENERATE_CHART, MODIFY_CHART, DELETE_CHART, GENERATE_KPI, FILTER, CLEAR_FILTERS, GENERATE_DASHBOARD, FIX_DASHBOARD, ANSWER
311 | 
312 | Current schema:
313 | ${JSON.stringify(packet, null, 2)}
314 | 
315 | Legacy trained memory:
316 | ${JSON.stringify(memoryMatch?.entry?.dashboardPlan || null, null, 2)}
317 | 
318 | Retrieved RAG memories:
319 | ${JSON.stringify(retrievedRagMemories, null, 2)}
320 | 
321 | Current dashboard specs:
322 | ${JSON.stringify(currentDashboard || {}, null, 2)}
323 | 
324 | User command:
325 | ${query}
326 | 
327 | Return example:
328 | {"action":"GENERATE_CHART","message":"Created chart.","chartSpec":{"type":"scatter","title":"Salary vs Experience","xKey":"experience","yKey":"salary_usd","aggregation":"count","limit":500},"schemaOnly":true}`;
329 | }

331 | export async function planDashboardWithOllama(schemaProfile, memoryMatch, options = {}) {
332 |   try {
333 |     const text = await callOllama({
334 |       model: DASHBOARD_MODEL,
335 |       prompt: buildDashboardPrompt(schemaProfile, memoryMatch, options),
336 |       temperature: 0.1,
337 |     });
338 |     const json = extractJson(text);
339 |     if (!json) return null;
340 | 
341 |     return {
342 |       source: `ollama:${DASHBOARD_MODEL}`,
343 |       message: json.message || "Dashboard plan generated.",
344 |       dashboardType: json.dashboardType,
345 |       kpis: (json.kpis || []).map((item) => sanitizeKpiSpec(stripUnsafeSpec(item), schemaProfile)).slice(0, 8),
346 |       charts: (json.charts || json.chartSpecs || []).map((item) => sanitizeChartSpec(stripUnsafeSpec(item), schemaProfile)).slice(0, 10),
347 |       executiveSummary: json.executiveSummary,
348 |       geoAnalysis: Array.isArray(json.geoAnalysis) ? json.geoAnalysis : [],
349 |       insights: Array.isArray(json.insights) ? json.insights : [],
350 |       recommendations: Array.isArray(json.recommendations) ? json.recommendations : [],
351 |       storyMode: json.storyMode,
352 |       confidenceScore: Number.isFinite(Number(json.confidenceScore)) ? Number(json.confidenceScore) : undefined,
353 |       schemaOnly: true,
354 |       model: DASHBOARD_MODEL,
355 |     };
356 |   } catch (error) {
357 |     return {
358 |       source: "ollama-error",
359 |       error: error.message,
360 |       kpis: [],
361 |       charts: [],
362 |       schemaOnly: true,
363 |       model: DASHBOARD_MODEL,
364 |     };
365 |   }
366 | }
367 | 
368 | export async function planCommandWithOllama({
369 |   query,
370 |   schemaProfile,
371 |   memoryMatch,
372 |   ragMatches = [],
373 |   currentDashboard,
374 | }) {
375 |   try {
376 |     const text = await callOllama({ model: DASHBOARD_MODEL, prompt: buildCommandPrompt(query, schemaProfile, memoryMatch, currentDashboard, {
377 |       ragMatches,
378 |     }) });
379 |     const json = extractJson(text);

381 |

384 |       message: json.message || "Command processed.",
385 |       chartSpec: json.chartSpec ? sanitizeChartSpec(stripUnsafeSpec(json.chartSpec), schemaProfile) : undefined,
386 |       kpiSpec: json.kpiSpec ? sanitizeKpiSpec(stripUnsafeSpec(json.kpiSpec), schemaProfile) : undefined,
387 |       filters: json.filters,
388 |       dashboardPlan: json.dashboardPlan
389 |         ? sanitizeDashboardPlan(json.dashboardPlan, schemaProfile)
390 |         : undefined,
391 |       schemaOnly: true,

393 |       model: DASHBOARD_MODEL,

398 |       message: "I used the local dashboard engine because the LLM planner was unavailable.",
399 |       schemaOnly: true,
400 |       provider: "local",
401 |       model: DASHBOARD_MODEL,
402 |       aiError: error.message,
403 |     };
404 |   }
405 | }

407 | export async function formatChatAnswerWithOllama({ query, schemaProfile, localAnswer }) {
408 |   try {
409 |     const prompt = `You are InsightFlow AI Analyst.
410 | 
411 | You are not a generic chatbot. You are a Senior Business Analyst, Data Scientist, and BI Consultant.
412 | You only know the uploaded dataset through schema, semantic mappings, RAG knowledge, dashboard metadata, generated KPIs/charts, insights, geo analysis, executive summaries, calculated metrics, and the user conversation supplied to you.
413 | 
414 | Grounding rules:
415 | - Never invent information, columns, segments, statistics, or KPI values.
416 | - Never hallucinate values.
417 | - Use only the provided schema-only packet and local computed analytics answer.
418 | - If the requested information is not available, say: "Based on the current schema and available analytics, this information cannot be determined."
419 | - Do not ask for raw rows.
420 | - Keep the response concise, analytical, and business-focused.
421 | 
422 | Reasoning Layer, internal only:
423 | 1. Identify user intent.
424 | 2. Find the relevant KPI.
425 | 3. Find related charts.
426 | 4. Find related insights.
427 | 5. Find related geo information.
428 | 6. Find related story summary.
429 | 7. Generate the answer.
430 | 8. Generate the recommendation.
431 | 9. Generate the confidence score.
432 | 
433 | Always return these sections:
434 | Summary
435 | Key Findings
436 | Business Impact
437 | Recommendation
438 | Confidence Score
439 | 
440 | Schema only:
441 | ${JSON.stringify(makeSchemaOnlyPacket(schemaProfile), null, 2)}
442 | 
443 | User question: ${query}
444 | Local computed answer: ${localAnswer}
445 | 
446 | Return only the final answer text. Do not expose the Reasoning Layer.`;
447 |     const text = await callOllama({ model: CHAT_MODEL, prompt, temperature: 0.2 });
448 |     return { answer: text.trim() || localAnswer, model: CHAT_MODEL, provider: "ollama" };
449 |   } catch (error) {
450 |     return { answer: localAnswer, model: CHAT_MODEL, provider: "local", aiError: error.message };
451 |   }
452 | }
453 |

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-fingerprint.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-fingerprint.js`

```js
  1 | import { createHash } from "node:crypto";
  2 | 
  3 | const MAX_TOP_VALUES = 15;
  4 | 
  5 | export function normalizeColumnName(value = "") {
  6 |   return String(value)
  7 |     .trim()
  8 |     .toLowerCase()
  9 |     .replace(/^[\ufeff\s]+/, "")
 10 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
 11 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
 12 | }
 13 | 
 14 | export function humanize(value = "") {
 15 |   return String(value)
 16 |     .replace(/^_+/, "")
 17 |     .replace(/[_-]+/g, " ")
 18 |     .replace(/\b\w/g, (char) => char.toUpperCase());
 19 | }
 20 | 
 21 | export function isMissing(value) {
 22 |   return value === null || value === undefined || String(value).trim() === "";
 23 | }
 24 | 
 25 | export function safeNumber(value) {
 26 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
 27 |   if (typeof value === "bigint") return Number(value);
 28 | 
 29 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "")
 30 |     .replace(/[₹$€£,%\s]/g, "")
 31 |     .replace(/,/g, "");
 32 | 
 33 |   if (!cleaned || cleaned === "-" || cleaned.toLowerCase() === "nan") return null;
 34 | 
 35 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
 36 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 37 | }
 38 | 
 39 | export function isDateLike(value) {
 40 |   if (value instanceof Date && !Number.isNaN(value.getTime())) return true;
 41 |   if (typeof value !== "string") return false;
 42 |   const text = value.trim();
 43 |   if (!text) return false;
 44 |   if (!/(\d{4}[-/]\d{1,2}[-/]\d{1,2})|(\d{1,2}[-/]\d{1,2}[-/]\d{2,4})/.test(text)) return false;
 45 |   const parsed = Date.parse(text);
 46 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed);
 47 | }
 48 | 
 49 | export function splitMultiValue(value) {
 50 |   if (isMissing(value)) return [];
 51 |   return String(value)
 52 |     .split(/[;,|]/g)
 53 |     .map((item) => item.trim())
 54 |     .filter(Boolean);
 55 | }
 56 | 
 57 | export function cleanDatasetRows(rows = []) {
 58 |   return (Array.isArray(rows) ? rows : []).filter((row) => {
 59 |     if (!row || typeof row !== "object") return false;
 60 | 
 61 |     const keys = Object.keys(row).map(normalizeColumnName);
 62 |     const source = String(row._sourceFile || row.sourceFile || row.source || row.fileName || "").toLowerCase();
 63 | 
 64 |     const looksLikeDictionary =
 65 |       keys.includes("column") &&
 66 |       keys.includes("type") &&
 67 |       (keys.includes("description") || keys.includes("desc"));
 68 | 
 69 |     return !source.includes("dictionary") && !source.includes("data_dictionary") && !looksLikeDictionary;
 70 |   });
 71 | }
 72 | 
 73 | function inferColumnType(values = []) {
 74 |   const present = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value)).slice(0, 500);
 75 |   if (!present.length) return "string";
 76 | 
 77 |   const numericCount = present.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
 78 |   const dateCount = present.filter(isDateLike).length;
 79 | 
 80 |   if (dateCount / present.length >= 0.8) return "date";
 81 |   if (numericCount / present.length >= 0.8) return "number";
 82 |   if (new Set(present.map(String)).size <= Math.max(2, present.length * 0.2)) return "category";
 83 |   return "string";
 84 | }
 85 | 
 86 | function getStats(values = []) {
 87 |   const numbers = values.map(safeNumber).filter((value) => value !== null).sort((a, b) => a - b);
 88 |   if (!numbers.length) return null;
 89 | 
 90 |   const sum = numbers.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0);
 91 |   const mid = Math.floor(numbers.length / 2);
 92 |   const median = numbers.length % 2 ? numbers[mid] : (numbers[mid - 1] + numbers[mid]) / 2;
 93 | 
 94 |   return {
 95 |     min: numbers[0],
 96 |     max: numbers[numbers.length - 1],
 97 |     mean: Math.round((sum / numbers.length) * 100) / 100,
 98 |     median: Math.round(median * 100) / 100,
 99 |   };
100 | }
101 | 
102 | function inferRole(name, type, uniqueCount, rowCount) {
103 |   const n = normalizeColumnName(name);
104 | 
105 |   if (/^(id|uuid|row_id|index|sr_no|serial|customer_code|user_id|student_id)$/.test(n) || n.endsWith("_id")) return "id";
106 |   if (type === "date" || /date|time|year|month|day|created|updated|timestamp/.test(n)) return "date";
107 |   if (/country|state|city|region|location|address|market|territory/.test(n)) return "location";
108 |   if (/salary|revenue|sales|amount|price|cost|profit|income|spend|budget|value|gmv|arr|mrr/.test(n) && !/rate|ratio|percent|margin/.test(n)) return "money_metric";
109 |   if (/score|rating|marks|grade|performance|level|index|rank|gpa/.test(n)) return "score_metric";
110 |   if (/age|experience|years|tenure|duration/.test(n)) return "continuous_metric";
111 |   if (/quantity|qty|units|orders|count|visits|clicks|sessions/.test(n)) return "count_metric";
112 |   if (/rate|ratio|percent|percentage|margin|conversion|churn/.test(n)) return "rate_metric";
113 |   if (/target|label|outcome|class|status|result/.test(n)) return "target";
114 |   if (/language|framework|skill|tag|category|product|education|gender|platform|segment|department|company_size/.test(n)) return "category";
115 | 
116 |   if (type === "number") return uniqueCount <= Math.max(20, rowCount * 0.03) ? "numeric_category" : "continuous_metric";
117 |   if (type === "category") return "category";
118 |   return "text";
119 | }
120 | 
121 | function topValues(values = []) {
122 |   const counts = new Map();
123 |   for (const value of values) {
124 |     if (isMissing(value)) continue;
125 |     const parts = splitMultiValue(value);
126 |     const items = parts.length > 1 ? parts : [String(value).trim()];
127 |     for (const item of items) counts.set(item, (counts.get(item) || 0) + 1);
128 |   }
129 |   return [...counts.entries()]
130 |     .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
131 |     .slice(0, MAX_TOP_VALUES)
132 |     .map(([value, count]) => ({ value, count }));
133 | }
134 | 
135 | export function buildSchemaProfile(dataset = {}) {
136 |   const rows = cleanDatasetRows(dataset.rows || []);
137 |   const suppliedColumns = Array.isArray(dataset.columns) ? dataset.columns : [];
138 |   const dictionaryRows = Array.isArray(dataset.dictionaryRows) ? dataset.dictionaryRows : [];
139 | 
140 |   const names = suppliedColumns.length
141 |     ? suppliedColumns.map((column) => column.name || column.Column || column.column || column)
142 |     : Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
143 | 
144 |   const dictionaryMap = new Map(
145 |     dictionaryRows.map((item) => [
146 |       normalizeColumnName(item.Column || item.column || item.name),
147 |       {
148 |         type: item.Type || item.type,
149 |         description: item.Description || item.description || item.desc,
150 |       },
151 |     ])
152 |   );
153 | 
154 |   const columns = names
155 |     .filter(Boolean)
156 |     .filter((name) => !String(name).startsWith("_"))
157 |     .map((name) => {
158 |       const normalized = normalizeColumnName(name);
159 |       const values = rows.map((row) => row[name]);
160 |       const present = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value));
161 |       const uniqueCount = new Set(present.map((value) => String(value).trim())).size;
162 |       const dictionary = dictionaryMap.get(normalized) || {};
163 |       const explicitType = suppliedColumns.find((column) => normalizeColumnName(column.name || column.Column || column) === normalized)?.type || dictionary.type;
164 |       const type = explicitType ? normalizeColumnName(explicitType).replace("integer", "number").replace("float", "number") : inferColumnType(values);
165 |       const role = inferRole(name, type, uniqueCount, rows.length);
166 | 
167 |       return {
168 |         name: String(name),
169 |         normalizedName: normalized,
170 |         title: humanize(name),
171 |         type,
172 |         role,
173 |         description: dictionary.description || "",
174 |         uniqueCount,
175 |         missingCount: values.length - present.length,
176 |         missingPct: rows.length ? Math.round(((values.length - present.length) / rows.length) * 10000) / 100 : 0,
177 |         topValues: type === "number" && !["numeric_category", "target"].includes(role) ? [] : topValues(values),
178 |         stats: type === "number" ? getStats(values) : null,
179 |       };
180 |     });
181 | 
182 |   const domain = detectDomain(columns);
183 |   const signature = getSchemaSignature(columns);
184 | 
185 |   return {
186 |     datasetName: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || "Uploaded Dataset",
187 |     rowCount: rows.length,
188 |     columnCount: columns.length,
189 |     domain,
190 |     signature,
191 |     columns,
192 |     roleCounts: columns.reduce((acc, column) => {
193 |       acc[column.role] = (acc[column.role] || 0) + 1;
194 |       return acc;
195 |     }, {}),
196 |   };
197 | }
198 | 
199 | export function detectDomain(columns = []) {
200 |   const text = columns.map((column) => `${column.normalizedName} ${column.role}`).join(" ");
201 | 
202 |   if (/salary|experience|education|framework|language|company_size|developer|employee|hr|department/.test(text)) return "workforce_salary";
203 |   if (/stress|anxiety|depression|sleep|mental|health|screen_time|social_media|addiction/.test(text)) return "health_wellness";
204 |   if (/student|marks|grade|attendance|course|school|exam|gpa|academic/.test(text)) return "education";
205 |   if (/revenue|sales|amount|product|customer|order|quantity|profit|market/.test(text)) return "sales_commerce";
206 |   if (/sentiment|review|rating|text|comment|feedback/.test(text)) return "sentiment_text";
207 |   if (/date|time|year|month/.test(text) && /revenue|sales|amount|price|temperature|traffic|demand|volume/.test(text)) return "time_series";
208 |   return "generic";
209 | }
210 | 
211 | export function getSchemaSignature(columns = []) {
212 |   const compact = columns
213 |     .map((column) => `${column.normalizedName}:${column.type}:${column.role}`)
214 |     .sort()
215 |     .join("|");
216 | 
217 |   return createHash("sha256").update(compact).digest("hex").slice(0, 24);
218 | }
219 | 
220 | export function schemaSimilarity(a, b) {
221 |   if (!a || !b) return 0;
222 |   const colsA = a.columns || [];
223 |   const colsB = b.columns || [];
224 | 
225 |   const nameA = new Set(colsA.map((column) => column.normalizedName));
226 |   const nameB = new Set(colsB.map((column) => column.normalizedName));
227 |   const roleA = new Set(colsA.map((column) => column.role));
228 |   const roleB = new Set(colsB.map((column) => column.role));
229 | 
230 |   const jaccard = (set1, set2) => {
231 |     const intersection = [...set1].filter((value) => set2.has(value)).length;
232 |     const union = new Set([...set1, ...set2]).size || 1;
233 |     return intersection / union;
234 |   };
235 | 
236 |   const nameScore = jaccard(nameA, nameB);
237 |   const roleScore = jaccard(roleA, roleB);
238 |   const domainScore = a.domain && b.domain && a.domain === b.domain ? 1 : 0;
239 | 
240 |   return Math.round((nameScore * 0.5 + roleScore * 0.35 + domainScore * 0.15) * 1000) / 1000;
241 | }
242 | 
243 | export function makeSchemaOnlyPacket(profile, options = {}) {
244 |   const includeTopValues = options.includeTopValues === true;
245 |   const includeStats = options.includeStats === true;
246 | 
247 |   return {
248 |     datasetName: profile.datasetName,
249 |     rowCount: profile.rowCount,
250 |     columnCount: profile.columnCount,
251 |     domain: profile.domain,
252 |     signature: profile.signature,
253 |     columns: profile.columns.map((column) => ({
254 |       name: column.name,
255 |       normalizedName: column.normalizedName,
256 |       type: column.type,
257 |       role: column.role,
258 |       description: column.description,
259 |       uniqueCount: column.uniqueCount,
260 |       missingPct: column.missingPct,
261 |       ...(includeTopValues
262 |         ? {
263 |             topValues: column.topValues?.slice(0, 10)?.map((item) => item.value) || [],
264 |           }
265 |         : {
266 |             topValuesCount: column.topValues?.length || 0,
267 |           }),
268 |       ...(includeStats && column.stats
269 |         ? {
270 |             stats: column.stats,
271 |           }
272 |         : {}),
273 |     })),
274 |   };
275 | }
276 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-profiler.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-profiler.js`

```js
  1 | function normalizeName(value = "") {
  2 |   return String(value)
  3 |     .toLowerCase()
  4 |     .trim()
  5 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
  6 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
  7 | }
  8 | 
  9 | export function safeNumber(value) {
 10 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
 11 | 
 12 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "")
 13 |     .replace(/[₹$,%]/g, "")
 14 |     .replace(/,/g, "")
 15 |     .trim();
 16 | 
 17 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
 18 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 19 | }
 20 | 
 21 | function isDateLike(value) {
 22 |   if (value instanceof Date) return true;
 23 |   if (typeof value !== "string") return false;
 24 |   if (!/\d/.test(value)) return false;
 25 | 
 26 |   const parsed = Date.parse(value);
 27 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed);
 28 | }
 29 | 
 30 | function inferType(values = []) {
 31 |   const sample = values
 32 |     .filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== "")
 33 |     .slice(0, 100);
 34 | 
 35 |   if (!sample.length) return "string";
 36 | 
 37 |   const numeric = sample.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
 38 |   const date = sample.filter(isDateLike).length;
 39 | 
 40 |   if (numeric / sample.length >= 0.8) return "number";
 41 |   if (date / sample.length >= 0.8) return "date";
 42 | 
 43 |   return "string";
 44 | }
 45 | 
 46 | function getStats(values = []) {
 47 |   const numbers = values.map(safeNumber).filter((value) => value !== null);
 48 | 
 49 |   if (!numbers.length) return null;
 50 | 
 51 |   const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 52 |   const sum = numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
 53 |   const avg = sum / numbers.length;
 54 | 
 55 |   return {
 56 |     min: sorted[0],
 57 |     max: sorted[sorted.length - 1],
 58 |     avg: Number(avg.toFixed(2)),
 59 |     validCount: numbers.length,
 60 |   };
 61 | }
 62 | 
 63 | export function buildSchemaProfile(dataset = {}) {
 64 |   const rows = Array.isArray(dataset.rows) ? dataset.rows : [];
 65 | 
 66 |   const columnNames =
 67 |     Array.isArray(dataset.columns) && dataset.columns.length
 68 |       ? dataset.columns.map((column) => column.name || column)
 69 |       : Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 70 | 
 71 |   const columns = columnNames
 72 |     .filter((name) => name && !String(name).startsWith("__"))
 73 |     .map((name) => {
 74 |       const values = rows.map((row) => row[name]);
 75 |       const type = inferType(values);
 76 |       const present = values.filter(
 77 |         (value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && value !== ""
 78 |       );
 79 |       const uniqueValues = [...new Set(present.map(String))];
 80 | 
 81 |       const role =
 82 |         type === "number" ? "metric" : type === "date" ? "date" : "dimension";
 83 | 
 84 |       return {
 85 |         name,
 86 |         normalizedName: normalizeName(name),
 87 |         type,
 88 |         role,
 89 |         uniqueCount: uniqueValues.length,
 90 |         nullCount: rows.length - present.length,
 91 |         topValues: role === "dimension" ? uniqueValues.slice(0, 12) : [],
 92 |         stats: type === "number" ? getStats(values) : null,
 93 |       };
 94 |     });
 95 | 
 96 |   const columnSignature = columns
 97 |     .map((column) => column.normalizedName)
 98 |     .sort()
 99 |     .join("|");
100 | 
101 |   return {
102 |     datasetName: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || "Uploaded Dataset",
103 |     rowCount: rows.length,
104 |     columnCount: columns.length,
105 |     columns,
106 |     columnSignature,
107 |     safePacket: {
108 |       datasetName: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || "Uploaded Dataset",
109 |       rowCount: rows.length,
110 |       columnCount: columns.length,
111 |       columns: columns.map((column) => ({
112 |         name: column.name,
113 |         type: column.type,
114 |         role: column.role,
115 |         uniqueCount: column.uniqueCount,
116 |         nullCount: column.nullCount,
117 |         topValues: column.topValues.slice(0, 8),
118 |         stats: column.stats,
119 |       })),
120 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
121 |     },
122 |   };
123 | }
124 | 
125 | export function findColumn(schema, aliases = [], role = null) {
126 |   const normalizedAliases = aliases.map(normalizeName);
127 | 
128 |   const candidates = schema.columns
129 |     .map((column) => {
130 |       let score = 0;
131 | 
132 |       for (const alias of normalizedAliases) {
133 |         if (column.normalizedName === alias) score += 10;
134 |         else if (column.normalizedName.includes(alias)) score += 6;
135 |         else if (alias.includes(column.normalizedName)) score += 3;
136 |       }
137 | 
138 |       if (role && column.role === role) score += 2;
139 | 
140 |       return { column, score };
141 |     })
142 |     .filter((item) => item.score > 0)
143 |     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
144 | 
145 |   if (candidates[0]) return candidates[0].column;
146 | 
147 |   if (role) {
148 |     return schema.columns.find((column) => column.role === role) || null;
149 |   }
150 | 
151 |   return null;
152 | }
153 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-rag-embeddings.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-rag-embeddings.js`

```js
  1 | import { createHash } from "node:crypto";
  2 | 
  3 | const DEFAULT_OLLAMA_BASE_URL =
  4 |   process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || "http://127.0.0.1:11434";
  5 | 
  6 | const DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL =
  7 |   process.env.RAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL || "nomic-embed-text";
  8 | 
  9 | const DEFAULT_VECTOR_SIZE = Number(process.env.RAG_FALLBACK_VECTOR_SIZE || 384);
 10 | 
 11 | function timeoutSignal(ms = 12000) {
 12 |   const controller = new AbortController();
 13 |   const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), ms);
 14 |   return {
 15 |     signal: controller.signal,
 16 |     clear: () => clearTimeout(timer),
 17 |   };
 18 | }
 19 | 
 20 | function tokenize(text = "") {
 21 |   return String(text)
 22 |     .toLowerCase()
 23 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9_]+/g, " ")
 24 |     .split(/\s+/)
 25 |     .map((token) => token.trim())
 26 |     .filter(Boolean);
 27 | }
 28 | 
 29 | export function normalizeVector(vector = []) {
 30 |   const values = Array.isArray(vector) ? vector.map(Number).filter(Number.isFinite) : [];
 31 |   const magnitude = Math.sqrt(values.reduce((sum, value) => sum + value * value, 0));
 32 | 
 33 |   if (!values.length || magnitude === 0) return [];
 34 | 
 35 |   return values.map((value) => value / magnitude);
 36 | }
 37 | 
 38 | export function cosineSimilarity(a = [], b = []) {
 39 |   const left = normalizeVector(a);
 40 |   const right = normalizeVector(b);
 41 |   const length = Math.min(left.length, right.length);
 42 | 
 43 |   if (!length) return 0;
 44 | 
 45 |   let dot = 0;
 46 |   for (let index = 0; index < length; index += 1) {
 47 |     dot += left[index] * right[index];
 48 |   }
 49 | 
 50 |   return dot;
 51 | }
 52 | 
 53 | export function deterministicTextEmbedding(text = "", dimensions = DEFAULT_VECTOR_SIZE) {
 54 |   const vector = Array.from({ length: dimensions }, () => 0);
 55 |   const tokens = tokenize(text);
 56 | 
 57 |   if (!tokens.length) return vector;
 58 | 
 59 |   tokens.forEach((token, index) => {
 60 |     const digest = createHash("sha256")
 61 |       .update(`${token}:${index}`)
 62 |       .digest();
 63 | 
 64 |     const bucket = digest.readUInt16BE(0) % dimensions;
 65 |     const sign = digest[2] % 2 === 0 ? 1 : -1;
 66 |     const weight = 1 + Math.log(1 + token.length);
 67 | 
 68 |     vector[bucket] += sign * weight;
 69 |   });
 70 | 
 71 |   return normalizeVector(vector);
 72 | }
 73 | 
 74 | async function callOllamaEmbedApi(text, model) {
 75 |   const { signal, clear } = timeoutSignal();
 76 | 
 77 |   try {
 78 |     const response = await fetch(`${DEFAULT_OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/embed`, {
 79 |       method: "POST",
 80 |       headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
 81 |       body: JSON.stringify({ model, input: text }),
 82 |       signal,
 83 |     });
 84 | 
 85 |     if (!response.ok) {
 86 |       throw new Error(`Ollama /api/embed HTTP ${response.status}`);
 87 |     }
 88 | 
 89 |     const payload = await response.json();
 90 | 
 91 |     if (Array.isArray(payload.embeddings?.[0])) {
 92 |       return payload.embeddings[0];
 93 |     }
 94 | 
 95 |     if (Array.isArray(payload.embedding)) {
 96 |       return payload.embedding;
 97 |     }
 98 | 
 99 |     throw new Error("Ollama /api/embed returned no embedding.");
100 |   } finally {
101 |     clear();
102 |   }
103 | }
104 | 
105 | async function callOllamaEmbeddingsApi(text, model) {
106 |   const { signal, clear } = timeoutSignal();
107 | 
108 |   try {
109 |     const response = await fetch(`${DEFAULT_OLLAMA_BASE_URL}/api/embeddings`, {
110 |       method: "POST",
111 |       headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
112 |       body: JSON.stringify({ model, prompt: text }),
113 |       signal,
114 |     });
115 | 
116 |     if (!response.ok) {
117 |       throw new Error(`Ollama /api/embeddings HTTP ${response.status}`);
118 |     }
119 | 
120 |     const payload = await response.json();
121 | 
122 |     if (Array.isArray(payload.embedding)) {
123 |       return payload.embedding;
124 |     }
125 | 
126 |     throw new Error("Ollama /api/embeddings returned no embedding.");
127 |   } finally {
128 |     clear();
129 |   }
130 | }
131 | 
132 | export async function embedText(text = "", options = {}) {
133 |   const model = options.model || DEFAULT_EMBEDDING_MODEL;
134 |   const cleanText = String(text || "").trim();
135 | 
136 |   if (!cleanText) {
137 |     return {
138 |       embedding: deterministicTextEmbedding("empty-schema"),
139 |       provider: "local-deterministic",
140 |       model: "deterministic-hash",
141 |       fallback: true,
142 |     };
143 |   }
144 | 
145 |   const shouldUseLocalEmbedding =
146 |     options.useOllama === false ||
147 |     process.env.DISABLE_OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS === "1" ||
148 |     (process.env.NODE_ENV === "test" && process.env.TEST_REAL_OLLAMA !== "1");
149 | 
150 |   if (shouldUseLocalEmbedding) {
151 |     return {
152 |       embedding: deterministicTextEmbedding(cleanText),
153 |       provider: "local-deterministic",
154 |       model: "deterministic-hash",
155 |       fallback: true,
156 |     };
157 |   }
158 | 
159 |   try {
160 |     let embedding;
161 | 
162 |     try {
163 |       embedding = await callOllamaEmbedApi(cleanText, model);
164 |     } catch {
165 |       embedding = await callOllamaEmbeddingsApi(cleanText, model);
166 |     }
167 | 
168 |     return {
169 |       embedding: normalizeVector(embedding),
170 |       provider: "ollama",
171 |       model,
172 |       fallback: false,
173 |     };
174 |   } catch (error) {
175 |     if (options.allowFallback === false) {
176 |       throw error;
177 |     }
178 | 
179 |     return {
180 |       embedding: deterministicTextEmbedding(cleanText),
181 |       provider: "local-deterministic",
182 |       model: "deterministic-hash",
183 |       fallback: true,
184 |       error: error.message,
185 |     };
186 |   }
187 | }
188 | 
```

  5 | function hashText(text = "") {

  7 | }
  8 | 
  9 | function safeTopValues(values = []) {
 10 |   if (!Array.isArray(values)) return [];
 11 | 
 12 |   return values.slice(0, 10).map((item) => {
 13 |     if (item && typeof item === "object") {
 14 |       return {
 15 |         value: item.value ?? item.label ?? item.name ?? String(item),
 16 |         count: Number.isFinite(Number(item.count)) ? Number(item.count) : undefined,
 17 |       };
 18 |     }
 19 | 
 20 |     return { value: item };
 21 |   });
 22 | }
 23 | 
 24 | function memoryColumns(profile) {
 25 |   return (profile.columns || []).map((column) => ({
 26 |     name: column.name,
 27 |     normalizedName: column.normalizedName,
 28 |     title: column.title,
 29 |     type: column.type,
 30 |     role: column.role,
 31 |     description: column.description || "",
 32 |     missingPct: column.missingPct ?? 0,
 33 |     uniqueCount: column.uniqueCount ?? 0,
 34 |     topValuesCount: safeTopValues(column.topValues).length,
 35 |     hasStats: Boolean(column.stats),
 36 |   }));
 37 | }
 38 | 
 39 | function stripUnsafeKpi(kpi = {}) {
 40 |   const {
 41 |     value,
 42 |     values,
 43 |     data,
 44 |     rows,
 45 |     rawRows,
 46 |     sampleRows,
 47 |     calculatedValues,
 48 |     chartData,
 49 |     ...safe
 50 |   } = kpi || {};
 51 | 
 52 |   return safe;
 53 | }
 54 | 
 55 | function stripUnsafeChart(chart = {}) {
 56 |   const {
 57 |     data,
 58 |     rows,
 59 |     rawRows,
 60 |     sampleRows,
 61 |     calculatedValues,
 62 |     value,
 63 |     values,
 64 |     chartData,
 65 |     ...safe
 66 |   } = chart || {};

 68 |   return safe;
