### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/ai/utils/token-counter.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/ai/utils/token-counter.js`

```js
  1 | // Token Counter Utilities for AI providers
  2 | 
  3 | /**
  4 |  * Estimate token count for text (rough approximation)
  5 |  * Different models use different tokenizers, this is a general approximation
  6 |  */
  7 | export function estimateTokenCount(text, model = 'gpt-4') {
  8 |   if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') return 0;
  9 | 
 10 |   // Different models have different token ratios
 11 |   const ratios = {
 12 |     'gpt-4': 0.25,        // ~4 chars per token
 13 |     'gpt-3.5-turbo': 0.25,
 14 |     'claude': 0.25,
 15 |     'gemini': 0.25,
 16 |     'llama': 0.33,        // ~3 chars per token
 17 |     'llama3.2': 0.33,
 18 |     'neural-chat': 0.33,
 19 |     'default': 0.25
 20 |   };
 21 | 
 22 |   const ratio = ratios[model] || ratios.default;
 23 |   
 24 |   // Count characters and apply ratio
 25 |   const charCount = text.length;
 26 |   const estimatedTokens = Math.ceil(charCount * ratio);
 27 | 
 28 |   // Add overhead for special tokens, formatting, etc.
 29 |   const overhead = 10;
 30 | 
 31 |   return estimatedTokens + overhead;
 32 | }
 33 | 
 34 | /**
 35 |  * Estimate tokens for messages array (chat format)
 36 |  */
 37 | export function estimateMessagesTokenCount(messages, model = 'gpt-4') {
 38 |   if (!Array.isArray(messages)) return 0;
 39 | 
 40 |   let totalTokens = 0;
 41 | 
 42 |   // Each message has overhead for role, content structure
 43 |   const messageOverhead = 4; // tokens per message
 44 | 
 45 |   for (const message of messages) {
 46 |     // Count role tokens
 47 |     if (message.role) {
 48 |       totalTokens += estimateTokenCount(message.role, model);
 49 |     }
 50 | 
 51 |     // Count content tokens
 52 |     if (message.content) {
 53 |       totalTokens += estimateTokenCount(message.content, model);
 54 |     }
 55 | 
 56 |     // Add message overhead
 57 |     totalTokens += messageOverhead;
 58 |   }
 59 | 
 60 |   // Add conversation overhead
 61 |   totalTokens += 3; // Every reply is primed with <im_start>assistant
 62 | 
 63 |   return totalTokens;
 64 | }
 65 | 
 66 | /**
 67 |  * Check if text fits within token limit
 68 |  */
 69 | export function fitsWithinLimit(text, maxTokens, model = 'gpt-4') {
 70 |   const estimatedTokens = estimateTokenCount(text, model);
 71 |   return estimatedTokens <= maxTokens;
 72 | }
 73 | 
 74 | /**
 75 |  * Truncate text to fit within token limit
 76 |  */
 77 | export function truncateToFit(text, maxTokens, model = 'gpt-4', suffix = '...') {
 78 |   if (!text) return text;
 79 | 
 80 |   const estimatedTokens = estimateTokenCount(text, model);
 81 |   
 82 |   if (estimatedTokens <= maxTokens) {
 83 |     return text;
 84 |   }
 85 | 
 86 |   // Calculate approximate character limit
 87 |   const ratios = {
 88 |     'gpt-4': 4,
 89 |     'claude': 4,
 90 |     'gemini': 4,
 91 |     'llama': 3,
 92 |     'default': 4
 93 |   };
 94 | 
 95 |   const charsPerToken = ratios[model] || ratios.default;
 96 |   const targetChars = (maxTokens - 10) * charsPerToken; // Leave room for suffix
 97 | 
 98 |   if (text.length <= targetChars) {
 99 |     return text;
100 |   }
101 | 
102 |   // Truncate and add suffix
103 |   return text.substring(0, targetChars - suffix.length) + suffix;
104 | }
105 | 
106 | /**
107 |  * Split text into chunks that fit within token limit
108 |  */
109 | export function splitIntoChunks(text, maxTokensPerChunk, model = 'gpt-4') {
110 |   if (!text) return [];
111 | 
112 |   const estimatedTokens = estimateTokenCount(text, model);
113 |   
114 |   if (estimatedTokens <= maxTokensPerChunk) {
115 |     return [text];
116 |   }
117 | 
118 |   const chunks = [];
119 |   const ratios = {
120 |     'gpt-4': 4,
121 |     'claude': 4,
122 |     'gemini': 4,
123 |     'llama': 3,
124 |     'default': 4
125 |   };
126 | 
127 |   const charsPerToken = ratios[model] || ratios.default;
128 |   const charsPerChunk = maxTokensPerChunk * charsPerToken;
129 | 
130 |   // Split by paragraphs first
131 |   const paragraphs = text.split(/\n\n+/);
132 |   let currentChunk = '';
133 | 
134 |   for (const paragraph of paragraphs) {
135 |     const testChunk = currentChunk + '\n\n' + paragraph;
136 |     
137 |     if (estimateTokenCount(testChunk, model) <= maxTokensPerChunk) {
138 |       currentChunk = testChunk;
139 |     } else {
140 |       if (currentChunk) {
141 |         chunks.push(currentChunk.trim());
142 |       }
143 |       
144 |       // If single paragraph is too large, split it
145 |       if (estimateTokenCount(paragraph, model) > maxTokensPerChunk) {
146 |         const subChunks = splitLargeParagraph(paragraph, charsPerChunk);
147 |         chunks.push(...subChunks);
148 |         currentChunk = '';
149 |       } else {
150 |         currentChunk = paragraph;
151 |       }
152 |     }
153 |   }
154 | 
155 |   if (currentChunk) {
156 |     chunks.push(currentChunk.trim());
157 |   }
158 | 
159 |   return chunks;
160 | }
161 | 
162 | /**
163 |  * Split a large paragraph into smaller chunks
164 |  */
165 | function splitLargeParagraph(paragraph, maxChars) {
166 |   const chunks = [];
167 |   const sentences = paragraph.match(/[^.!?]+[.!?]+/g) || [paragraph];
168 |   
169 |   let currentChunk = '';
170 | 
171 |   for (const sentence of sentences) {
172 |     if (currentChunk.length + sentence.length <= maxChars) {
173 |       currentChunk += sentence;
174 |     } else {
175 |       if (currentChunk) {
176 |         chunks.push(currentChunk.trim());
177 |       }
178 |       
179 |       // If single sentence is too large, just split by characters
180 |       if (sentence.length > maxChars) {
181 |         for (let i = 0; i < sentence.length; i += maxChars) {
182 |           chunks.push(sentence.substring(i, i + maxChars).trim());
183 |         }
184 |         currentChunk = '';
185 |       } else {
186 |         currentChunk = sentence;
187 |       }
188 |     }
189 |   }
190 | 
191 |   if (currentChunk) {
192 |     chunks.push(currentChunk.trim());
193 |   }
194 | 
195 |   return chunks;
196 | }
197 | 
198 | /**
199 |  * Calculate cost estimate for tokens (approximate)
200 |  */
201 | export function estimateCost(tokenCount, model = 'gpt-4') {
202 |   // Pricing per 1K tokens (as of 2024, approximate)
203 |   const pricing = {
204 |     'gpt-4': { input: 0.03, output: 0.06 },
205 |     'gpt-3.5-turbo': { input: 0.0015, output: 0.002 },
206 |     'claude-3-opus': { input: 0.015, output: 0.075 },
207 |     'claude-3-sonnet': { input: 0.003, output: 0.015 },
208 |     'gemini-1.5-pro': { input: 0.007, output: 0.021 },
209 |     'llama': { input: 0, output: 0 }, // Local, free
210 |     'default': { input: 0, output: 0 }
211 |   };
212 | 
213 |   const prices = pricing[model] || pricing.default;
214 |   
215 |   return {
216 |     input: (tokenCount * prices.input) / 1000,
217 |     output: (tokenCount * prices.output) / 1000,
218 |     total: (tokenCount * (prices.input + prices.output)) / 1000,
219 |     currency: 'USD'
220 |   };
221 | }
222 | 
223 | /**
224 |  * Get token usage statistics
225 |  */
226 | export function getTokenStats(text, model = 'gpt-4') {
227 |   const tokens = estimateTokenCount(text, model);
228 |   const cost = estimateCost(tokens, model);
229 |   
230 |   return {
231 |     text,
232 |     model,
233 |     estimatedTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET],
234 |     characterCount: text.length,
235 |     wordCount: text.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length,
236 |     estimatedCost: cost,
237 |     fitsInContext: {
238 |       '4k': tokens <= 4096,
239 |       '8k': tokens <= 8192,
240 |       '16k': tokens <= 16384,
241 |       '32k': tokens <= 32768,
242 |       '128k': tokens <= 131072
243 |     }
244 |   };
245 | }
246 | 
247 | export default {
248 |   estimateTokenCount,
249 |   estimateMessagesTokenCount,
250 |   fitsWithinLimit,
251 |   truncateToFit,
252 |   splitIntoChunks,
253 |   estimateCost,
254 |   getTokenStats
255 | };
256 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/alert-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/alert-service.js`

```js
  1 | import { toNumber } from '@insightflow/shared-analytics';
  2 | 
  3 | export class AlertService {
  4 |   constructor() {
  5 |     this.alerts = new Map();
  6 |     this.alertHistory = [];
  7 |   }
  8 | 
  9 |   createAlert(config) {
 10 |     const {
 11 |       id = `alert_${Date.now()}`,
 12 |       name,
 13 |       datasetId,
 14 |       condition,
 15 |       threshold,
 16 |       metric,
 17 |       operator = 'gt',
 18 |       enabled = true
 19 |     } = config;
 20 | 
 21 |     const alert = {
 22 |       id,
 23 |       name: name || `${metric} ${operator} ${threshold}`,
 24 |       datasetId,
 25 |       condition: condition || { metric, operator, threshold },
 26 |       enabled,
 27 |       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 28 |       triggeredCount: 0,
 29 |       lastTriggered: null
 30 |     };
 31 | 
 32 |     this.alerts.set(id, alert);
 33 |     return alert;
 34 |   }
 35 | 
 36 |   evaluateCondition(value, operator, threshold) {
 37 |     switch (operator) {
 38 |       case 'gt': return value > threshold;
 39 |       case 'gte': return value >= threshold;
 40 |       case 'lt': return value < threshold;
 41 |       case 'lte': return value <= threshold;
 42 |       case 'eq': return value === threshold;
 43 |       case 'neq': return value !== threshold;
 44 |       case 'change_gt': return value > threshold;
 45 |       case 'change_lt': return value < threshold;
 46 |       default: return false;
 47 |     }
 48 |   }
 49 | 
 50 |   async checkAlert(alert, dataset) {
 51 |     const { metric, operator, threshold } = alert.condition;
 52 |     
 53 |     if (!dataset?.rows) return { triggered: false };
 54 | 
 55 |     const values = dataset.rows.map(r => toNumber(r[metric])?.valueOf()).filter(v => v !== null);
 56 |     if (values.length === 0) return { triggered: false };
 57 | 
 58 |     const current = values[values.length - 1];
 59 |     const previous = values.length > 1 ? values[values.length - 2] : values[0];
 60 |     const change = previous ? ((current - previous) / previous) * 100 : 0;
 61 | 
 62 |     const checkValue = operator.includes('change') ? change : current;
 63 |     const triggered = this.evaluateCondition(checkValue, operator, threshold);
 64 | 
 65 |     if (triggered) {
 66 |       alert.triggeredCount++;
 67 |       alert.lastTriggered = new Date().toISOString();
 68 |       
 69 |       this.alertHistory.push({
 70 |         alertId: alert.id,
 71 |         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
 72 |         value: current,
 73 |         change,
 74 |         threshold
 75 |       });
 76 |     }
 77 | 
 78 |     return {
 79 |       triggered,
 80 |       current,
 81 |       change,
 82 |       threshold,
 83 |       message: triggered 
 84 |         ? `Alert: ${metric} ${operator} ${threshold} (current: ${current.toFixed(2)})`
 85 |         : null
 86 |     };
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   async checkAllAlerts(dataset) {
 90 |     const results = [];
 91 |     
 92 |     for (const [id, alert] of this.alerts) {
 93 |       if (!alert.enabled) continue;
 94 |       if (alert.datasetId && alert.datasetId !== dataset.id) continue;
 95 |       
 96 |       const result = await this.checkAlert(alert, dataset);
 97 |       if (result.triggered) {
 98 |         results.push({ alertId: id, ...result });
 99 |       }
100 |     }
101 | 
102 |     return results;
103 |   }
104 | 
105 |   async detectAnomalies(rows, column, options = {}) {
106 |     const { threshold = 1.5 } = options;
107 |     
108 |     const values = rows.map(r => toNumber(r[column])?.valueOf()).filter(v => v !== null);
109 |     if (values.length < 10) return { error: 'Insufficient data' };
110 | 
111 |     values.sort((a, b) => a - b);
112 |     const q1 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.25)];
113 |     const q3 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.75)];
114 |     const iqr = q3 - q1;
115 |     const lower = q1 - threshold * iqr;
116 |     const upper = q3 + threshold * iqr;
117 | 
118 |     const anomalies = [];
119 |     rows.forEach((row, index) => {
120 |       const value = toNumber(row[column])?.valueOf();
121 |       if (value !== null && (value < lower || value > upper)) {
122 |         anomalies.push({
123 |           rowIndex: index,
124 |           value,
125 |           type: value < lower ? 'below' : 'above',
126 |           deviation: value < lower ? lower - value : value - upper
127 |         });
128 |       }
129 |     });
130 | 
131 |     return {
132 |       column,
133 |       threshold,
134 |       bounds: { lower, upper },
135 |       count: anomalies.length,
136 |       percentage: ((anomalies.length / values.length) * 100).toFixed(2),
137 |       anomalies: anomalies.slice(0, 20)
138 |     };
139 |   }
140 | 
141 |   createThresholdAlerts(dataset, options = {}) {
142 |     const { sensitivity = 1.5 } = options;
143 |     const alerts = [];
144 |     const numericCols = dataset.columns?.filter(c => c.type === 'number') || [];
145 |     
146 |     for (const col of numericCols) {
147 |       const values = dataset.rows.map(r => toNumber(r[col.name])?.valueOf()).filter(v => v !== null);
148 |       if (values.length < 10) continue;
149 | 
150 |       values.sort((a, b) => a - b);
151 |       const q1 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.25)];
152 |       const q3 = values[Math.floor(values.length * 0.75)];
153 |       const mean = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
154 |       const iqr = q3 - q1;
155 |       
156 |       const highAlert = this.createAlert({
157 |         name: `${col.name} unusually high`,
158 |         datasetId: dataset.id,
159 |         metric: col.name,
160 |         operator: 'gt',
161 |         threshold: q3 + sensitivity * iqr
162 |       });
163 |       alerts.push(highAlert);
164 | 
165 |       const lowAlert = this.createAlert({
166 |         name: `${col.name} unusually low`,
167 |         datasetId: dataset.id,
168 |         metric: col.name,
169 |         operator: 'lt',
170 |         threshold: q1 - sensitivity * iqr
171 |       });
172 |       alerts.push(lowAlert);
173 |     }
174 | 
175 |     return alerts;
176 |   }
177 | 
178 |   getAlertHistory(limit = 50) {
179 |     return this.alertHistory.slice(-limit);
180 |   }
181 | 
182 |   getAlerts() {
183 |     return Array.from(this.alerts.values());
184 |   }
185 | 
186 |   deleteAlert(alertId) {
187 |     return this.alerts.delete(alertId);
188 |   }
189 | 
190 |   toggleAlert(alertId, enabled) {
191 |     const alert = this.alerts.get(alertId);
192 |     if (alert) {
193 |       alert.enabled = enabled;
194 |       return alert;
195 |     }
196 |     return null;
197 |   }
198 | }
199 | 
200 | export const alertService = new AlertService();
```

```js
 10 | function ensureMemoryFile() {

 12 | 
 13 |   if (!fs.existsSync(MEMORY_FILE)) {
 14 |     fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify([], null, 2));

 33 | 
 34 | function normalizeName(value = "") {
 35 |   return String(value)
 36 |     .toLowerCase()
 37 |     .trim()
 38 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
 39 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
 40 | }
 41 | 
 42 | function humanize(value = "") {
 43 |   return String(value)
 44 |     .replace(/[_-]+/g, " ")
 45 |     .replace(/\b\w/g, (m) => m.toUpperCase());
 46 | }
 47 | 
 48 | function isMissing(value) {
 49 |   return value === null || value === undefined || String(value).trim() === "";
 50 | }
 51 | 
 52 | function safeNumber(value) {
 53 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
 54 | 
 55 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "")
 56 |     .replace(/[$₹,%\s]/g, "")
 57 |     .replace(/,/g, "");
 58 | 
 59 |   if (!cleaned || (cleaned.includes("-") && !/^-?\d+(\.\d+)?$/.test(cleaned))) {
 60 |     return null;
 61 |   }
 62 | 
 63 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
 64 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 65 | }
 66 | 
 67 | function isDateLike(value) {
 68 |   if (value instanceof Date) return true;
 69 |   if (typeof value !== "string") return false;
 70 |   if (!/\d{4}|\d{1,2}[/-]\d{1,2}/.test(value)) return false;
 71 |   return Number.isFinite(Date.parse(value));
 72 | }
 73 | 
 74 | function inferType(values = []) {
 75 |   const present = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value)).slice(0, 100);
 76 |   if (!present.length) return "string";
 77 | 
 78 |   const numericCount = present.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length;
 79 |   const dateCount = present.filter(isDateLike).length;
 80 | 
 81 |   if (numericCount / present.length >= 0.8) return "number";
 82 |   if (dateCount / present.length >= 0.8) return "date";
 83 |   return "string";
 84 | }
 85 | 
 86 | function cleanRows(rows = []) {
 87 |   return rows.filter((row) => {
 88 |     const keys = Object.keys(row || {}).map(normalizeName);
 89 |     const source = String(row?._sourceFile || row?.source || row?.fileName || "").toLowerCase();
 90 |     const looksLikeDictionary =
 91 |       (keys.includes("column") || keys.includes("column_name") || keys.includes("field")) &&
 92 |       (keys.includes("type") || keys.includes("data_type")) &&
 93 |       (keys.includes("description") || keys.includes("definition") || keys.includes("meaning"));
 94 | 
 95 |     return !source.includes("dictionary") && !looksLikeDictionary;
 96 |   });
 97 | }
 98 | 
 99 | function inferColumns(inputColumns = [], rows = []) {
100 |   const names =
101 |     inputColumns.length > 0
102 |       ? inputColumns.map((column) => column.name || column)
103 |       : Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
104 | 
105 |   return names
106 |     .filter(Boolean)
107 |     .map((name) => {
108 |       const values = rows.map((row) => row[name]);
109 |       const declaredType = inputColumns.find((column) => (column.name || column) === name)?.type;
110 |       const type = declaredType || inferType(values);
111 |       const presentValues = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value));
112 |       const uniqueValues = [...new Set(presentValues.map(String))];
113 | 
114 |       return {
115 |         name,
116 |         normalizedName: normalizeName(name),
117 |         type,
118 |         missingCount: values.length - presentValues.length,
119 |         uniqueCount: uniqueValues.length,
120 |         topValues: type === "string" ? uniqueValues.slice(0, VALUE_LIMIT) : [],
121 |       };
122 |     });
123 | }
124 | 
125 | function columnSignature(columns = []) {
126 |   return columns
127 |     .map((column) => column.normalizedName || normalizeName(column.name))
128 |     .sort()
129 |     .join("|");
130 | }
131 | 
132 | function similarity(a = "", b = "") {
133 |   const setA = new Set(a.split("|").filter(Boolean));
134 |   const setB = new Set(b.split("|").filter(Boolean));
135 | 
136 |   if (!setA.size || !setB.size) return 0;
137 | 
138 |   const intersection = [...setA].filter((item) => setB.has(item)).length;
139 |   const union = new Set([...setA, ...setB]).size;
140 | 
141 |   return intersection / union;
142 | }
143 | 
144 | function findColumn(schema, aliases = [], preferredTypes = []) {
145 |   const normalizedAliases = aliases.map(normalizeName);
146 | 
147 |   const scored = schema.columns
148 |     .map((column) => {
149 |       const name = column.normalizedName;
150 |       let score = 0;
151 |       let aliasMatched = false;
152 | 
153 |       for (const alias of normalizedAliases) {
154 |         if (name === alias) {
155 |           score += 10;
156 |           aliasMatched = true;
157 |         } else if (name.includes(alias)) {
158 |           score += 6;
159 |           aliasMatched = true;
160 |         } else if (alias.includes(name)) {
161 |           score += 3;
162 |           aliasMatched = true;
163 |         }
164 |       }
165 | 
166 |       if (aliasMatched && preferredTypes.includes(column.type)) score += 2;
167 |       return { column, score, aliasMatched };
168 |     })
169 |     .filter((item) => item.aliasMatched && item.score > 0)
170 |     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
171 | 
172 |   return scored[0]?.column || null;
173 | }
174 | 
175 | function detectDomain(schema) {
176 |   const names = schema.columns.map((column) => column.normalizedName).join(" ");
177 | 
178 |   if (/salary|experience|education|company_size|framework|language/.test(names)) return "salary_hr_jobs";
179 |   if (/revenue|sales|profit|amount|order|product|customer|quantity|units/.test(names)) return "sales";
180 |   if (/student|class|grade|marks|score|attendance|subject|teacher/.test(names)) return "education";
181 |   if (/transaction|invoice|basket|item|support|confidence|lift/.test(names)) return "market_basket";
182 |   if (/review|comment|feedback|sentiment|rating|text/.test(names)) return "sentiment";
183 |   if (/date|month|year|time|period|forecast/.test(names) && schema.columns.some((column) => column.type === "number")) return "time_series";
184 |   if (/user|movie|item|rating|recommendation/.test(names)) return "recommender";
185 |   if (/price|cost|expense|income|balance|payment|tax|roi/.test(names)) return "finance";
186 | 
187 |   return "generic";
188 | }
189 | 
190 | function buildSafeSchemaPacket(dataset, rows, columns) {
191 |   return {
192 |     datasetId: dataset.id || null,
193 |     name: dataset.name || dataset.fileName || "Uploaded Dataset",
194 |     rowCount: rows.length,
195 |     columnCount: columns.length,
196 |     columns,
197 |     columnSignature: columnSignature(columns),
198 |     privacy: {
199 |       schemaOnly: true,
200 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
201 |     },
202 |   };
203 | }
204 | 
205 | function aggregate(values = [], aggregation = "count") {
206 |   const present = values.filter((value) => !isMissing(value));
207 |   const numbers = values.map(safeNumber).filter((number) => number !== null);
208 | 
209 |   if (aggregation === "count") return present.length;
210 |   if (aggregation === "distinct_count") return new Set(present.map(String)).size;
211 |   if (!numbers.length) return 0;
212 | 
213 |   if (aggregation === "sum") return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
214 |   if (aggregation === "avg") return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / numbers.length;
215 |   if (aggregation === "min") return Math.min(...numbers);
216 |   if (aggregation === "max") return Math.max(...numbers);
217 | 
218 |   if (aggregation === "median") {
219 |     const sorted = [...numbers].sort((a, b) => a - b);
220 |     const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
221 |     return sorted.length % 2 ? sorted[mid] : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
222 |   }
223 | 
224 |   return 0;
225 | }
226 | 
227 | function formatValue(metric, value) {
228 |   const metricName = normalizeName(metric || "");
229 | 
230 |   if (/salary|revenue|sales|profit|amount|price|cost|income|expense/.test(metricName)) {
231 |     return `$${Math.round(value).toLocaleString()}`;
232 |   }
233 | 
234 |   if (/rate|percent|margin|ratio/.test(metricName)) {
235 |     return `${Number(value).toFixed(2)}%`;
236 |   }
237 | 
238 |   if (typeof value === "number") {
239 |     return Number(value.toFixed(2)).toLocaleString();
240 |   }
241 | 
242 |   return String(value);
243 | }
244 | 
245 | function groupByAggregate(rows, xKey, yKey, aggregation = "count", limit = 10) {
246 |   const groups = new Map();
247 | 
248 |   for (const row of rows) {
249 |     const label = String(row[xKey] ?? "Unknown").trim() || "Unknown";
250 |     if (!groups.has(label)) groups.set(label, []);
251 |     groups.get(label).push(row[yKey]);
252 |   }
253 | 
254 |   const valueKey = aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yKey;
255 | 
256 |   return [...groups.entries()]
257 |     .map(([label, values]) => ({
258 |       [xKey]: label,
259 |       [valueKey]: aggregate(values, aggregation),
260 |     }))
261 |     .sort((a, b) => Number(Object.values(b).at(-1)) - Number(Object.values(a).at(-1)))
262 |     .slice(0, limit);
263 | }
264 | 
265 | function buildHistogram(rows, key, bins = 8) {
266 |   const values = rows.map((row) => safeNumber(row[key])).filter((value) => value !== null);
267 |   if (!values.length) return [];
268 | 
269 |   const min = Math.min(...values);
270 |   const max = Math.max(...values);
271 | 
272 |   if (min === max) {
273 |     return [{ range: String(min), count: values.length }];
274 |   }
275 | 
276 |   const step = (max - min) / bins;
277 |   const buckets = Array.from({ length: bins }, (_, index) => {
278 |     const start = min + index * step;
279 |     const end = index === bins - 1 ? max : start + step;
280 |     return {
281 |       range: `${Math.round(start).toLocaleString()}-${Math.round(end).toLocaleString()}`,
282 |       count: 0,
283 |     };
284 |   });
285 | 
286 |   for (const value of values) {
287 |     const index = Math.min(Math.floor((value - min) / step), bins - 1);
288 |     buckets[index].count += 1;
289 |   }
290 | 
291 |   return buckets;
292 | }
293 | 
294 | function buildExperienceBuckets(rows, experienceKey, metricKey) {
295 |   const buckets = [
296 |     { label: "0-2", min: 0, max: 2, values: [] },
297 |     { label: "2-5", min: 2, max: 5, values: [] },
298 |     { label: "5-10", min: 5, max: 10, values: [] },
299 |     { label: "10-15", min: 10, max: 15, values: [] },
300 |     { label: "15+", min: 15, max: Infinity, values: [] },
301 |   ];
302 | 
303 |   for (const row of rows) {
304 |     const experience = safeNumber(row[experienceKey]);
305 |     if (experience === null) continue;
306 | 
307 |     const bucket = buckets.find((item) => experience >= item.min && experience < item.max);
308 |     if (bucket) bucket.values.push(row[metricKey]);
309 |   }
310 | 
311 |   return buckets.map((bucket) => ({
312 |     [experienceKey]: bucket.label,
313 |     [metricKey]: aggregate(bucket.values, "avg"),
314 |   }));
315 | }
316 | 
317 | function countSplitValues(rows, key, limit = 10) {
318 |   const counts = new Map();
319 | 
320 |   for (const row of rows) {
321 |     String(row[key] ?? "")
322 |       .split(/[,;/|]+/)
323 |       .map((value) => value.trim())
324 |       .filter(Boolean)
325 |       .forEach((value) => counts.set(value, (counts.get(value) || 0) + 1));
326 |   }
327 | 
328 |   return [...counts.entries()]
329 |     .map(([label, count]) => ({ [key]: label, count }))
330 |     .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
331 |     .slice(0, limit);
332 | }

446 | 
447 | function resolveMetric(schema, aliases) {
448 |   if (!aliases) return null;
449 |   return findColumn(schema, Array.isArray(aliases) ? aliases : [aliases], ["number"]);
450 | }
451 | 
452 | function resolveDimension(schema, aliases) {
453 |   if (!aliases) return null;
454 |   return findColumn(schema, Array.isArray(aliases) ? aliases : [aliases], ["string", "date"]);
455 | }
456 | 
457 | function buildKpi(rows, schema, template) {
458 |   if (template.aggregation === "count" && !template.metric) {
459 |     return {
460 |       id: randomUUID(),
461 |       title: template.title || "Total Records",
462 |       value: rows.length.toLocaleString(),
463 |       aggregation: "count",
464 |       metric: "*",
465 |       icon: "rows",
466 |     };
467 |   }
468 | 
469 |   const preferredTypes = template.aggregation === "distinct_count" || template.aggregation === "count" ? ["string", "number"] : ["number"];
470 |   const metric = template.metric ? findColumn(schema, Array.isArray(template.metric) ? template.metric : [template.metric], preferredTypes) : null;
471 |   if (!metric) return null;
472 | 
473 |   const value = aggregate(rows.map((row) => row[metric.name]), template.aggregation);
474 | 
475 |   return {
476 |     id: randomUUID(),
477 |     title: template.title || `${humanize(template.aggregation)} ${humanize(metric.name)}`,
478 |     value: formatValue(metric.name, value),
479 |     metric: metric.name,
480 |     aggregation: template.aggregation,
481 |     icon: "chart",
482 |   };
483 | }
484 | 
485 | function buildChart(rows, schema, template) {
486 |   const xColumn = resolveDimension(schema, template.x);
487 |   const yColumn = template.aggregation === "count" ? null : resolveMetric(schema, template.y);
488 | 
489 |   if (!xColumn && template.type !== "histogram") return null;
490 |   if (template.aggregation !== "count" && !yColumn) return null;
491 | 
492 |   if (template.type === "histogram") {
493 |     const metric = resolveMetric(schema, template.x || template.y);
494 |     if (!metric) return null;
495 | 
496 |     return {
497 |       id: randomUUID(),
498 |       type: "histogram",
499 |       title: template.title || `${humanize(metric.name)} Distribution`,
500 |       xKey: "range",
501 |       yKey: "count",
502 |       aggregation: "count",
503 |       data: buildHistogram(rows, metric.name, 8),
504 |     };
505 |   }
506 | 
507 |   if (template.bucket === "experience" && xColumn && yColumn) {
508 |     return {
509 |       id: randomUUID(),
510 |       type: "bar",
511 |       title: template.title,
512 |       xKey: xColumn.name,
513 |       yKey: yColumn.name,
514 |       aggregation: "avg",
515 |       data: buildExperienceBuckets(rows, xColumn.name, yColumn.name),
516 |     };
517 |   }
518 | 
519 |   if (template.splitValues && xColumn) {
520 |     return {
521 |       id: randomUUID(),
522 |       type: template.type || "bar",
523 |       title: template.title,
524 |       xKey: xColumn.name,
525 |       yKey: "count",
526 |       aggregation: "count",
527 |       data: countSplitValues(rows, xColumn.name, template.limit || 10),
528 |     };
529 |   }
530 | 
531 |   return {
532 |     id: randomUUID(),
533 |     type: template.type || "bar",
534 |     title: template.title,
535 |     xKey: xColumn.name,
536 |     yKey: template.aggregation === "count" ? "count" : yColumn.name,
537 |     aggregation: template.aggregation || "count",
538 |     data: groupByAggregate(
539 |       rows,
540 |       xColumn.name,
541 |       yColumn?.name || xColumn.name,
542 |       template.aggregation || "count",
543 |       template.limit || 10,
544 |     ),
545 |   };
546 | }
547 | 
548 | function findSimilarMemory(domain, signature) {
549 |   const best = readMemory()
550 |     .filter((entry) => entry.domain === domain)
551 |     .map((entry) => ({
552 |       ...entry,
553 |       similarity: similarity(entry.columnSignature, signature),
554 |     }))
555 |     .sort((a, b) => b.similarity - a.similarity)[0];
556 | 
557 |   if (!best || best.similarity < 0.65) return null;
558 |   return best;
559 | }
560 | 
561 | function savePattern({ domain, schema, kpis, charts }) {
562 |   const memory = readMemory();
563 |   const signature = schema.columnSignature;
564 |   const existing = memory.find((entry) => entry.domain === domain && entry.columnSignature === signature);
565 | 
566 |   const item = {
567 |     id: existing?.id || randomUUID(),
568 |     domain,
569 |     columnSignature: signature,
570 |     schemaColumns: schema.columns.map((column) => ({ name: column.name, type: column.type })),
571 |     kpiTemplates: kpis.map((kpi) => ({
572 |       title: kpi.title,
573 |       metric: kpi.metric,
574 |       aggregation: kpi.aggregation,
575 |     })),
576 |     chartTemplates: charts.map((chart) => ({
577 |       title: chart.title,
578 |       type: chart.type,
579 |       xKey: chart.xKey,
580 |       yKey: chart.yKey,
581 |       aggregation: chart.aggregation,
582 |     })),
583 |     feedback: existing?.feedback || [],
584 |     usageCount: existing ? existing.usageCount + 1 : 1,
585 |     createdAt: existing?.createdAt || new Date().toISOString(),
586 |     lastUsedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
587 |   };
588 | 
589 |   const next = existing
590 |     ? memory.map((entry) => (entry.id === existing.id ? item : entry))
591 |     : [...memory, item];
592 | 
593 |   writeMemory(next);
594 |   return item;
595 | }
596 | 
597 | function buildInsights(rows, schema, domain, kpis, charts) {
598 |   const numericColumns = schema.columns.filter((column) => column.type === "number");
599 |   const categoricalColumns = schema.columns.filter((column) => column.type === "string");
600 | 
601 |   const insights = [
602 |     {
603 |       type: "summary",
604 |       title: "Dataset Overview",
605 |       message: `Detected ${PLAYBOOKS[domain]?.label || "General Analytics"} with ${rows.length.toLocaleString()} rows and ${schema.columns.length} columns.`,
606 |     },
607 |   ];
608 | 
609 |   if (numericColumns.length) {
610 |     const firstMetric = numericColumns[0];
611 |     const values = rows.map((row) => row[firstMetric.name]);
612 |     insights.push({
613 |       type: "metric",
614 |       title: `${humanize(firstMetric.name)} Range`,
615 |       message: `${humanize(firstMetric.name)} ranges from ${formatValue(firstMetric.name, aggregate(values, "min"))} to ${formatValue(firstMetric.name, aggregate(values, "max"))}.`,
616 |     });
617 |   }
618 | 
619 |   if (categoricalColumns.length) {
620 |     const firstDimension = categoricalColumns[0];
621 |     const values = rows.map((row) => row[firstDimension.name]).filter((value) => !isMissing(value));
622 |     const unique = new Set(values.map(String)).size;
623 |     insights.push({
624 |       type: "dimension",
625 |       title: `${humanize(firstDimension.name)} Coverage`,
626 |       message: `${humanize(firstDimension.name)} contains ${unique.toLocaleString()} unique values.`,
627 |     });
628 |   }
629 | 
630 |   insights.push({
631 |     type: "dashboard",
632 |     title: "Dashboard Generated",
633 |     message: `Generated ${kpis.length} KPI cards and ${charts.length} charts using schema-safe analytics rules.`,
634 |   });
635 | 
636 |   return insights;
637 | }
638 | 
639 | export async function analyzeDatasetWithAnalyticsBrain(dataset = {}) {
640 |   const rows = cleanRows(dataset.rows || []);
641 |   const columns = inferColumns(dataset.columns || [], rows);
642 |   const schema = buildSafeSchemaPacket(dataset, rows, columns);
643 |   const domain = detectDomain(schema);
644 |   const basePlaybook = PLAYBOOKS[domain] || PLAYBOOKS.generic;
645 |   const remembered = findSimilarMemory(domain, schema.columnSignature);
646 | 
647 |   const kpiTemplates = remembered?.kpiTemplates?.length ? remembered.kpiTemplates : basePlaybook.kpis;
648 |   const chartTemplates = remembered?.chartTemplates?.length ? remembered.chartTemplates : basePlaybook.charts;
649 | 
650 |   const kpis = kpiTemplates
651 |     .map((template) => {
652 |       if (template.metric && typeof template.metric === "string") {
653 |         return buildKpi(rows, schema, { ...template, metric: [template.metric] });
654 |       }
655 |       return buildKpi(rows, schema, template);
656 |     })
657 |     .filter(Boolean)
658 |     .slice(0, 8);
659 | 
660 |   const charts = chartTemplates
661 |     .map((template) => {
662 |       if (template.xKey || template.yKey) {
663 |         return buildChart(rows, schema, {
664 |           type: template.type,
665 |           title: template.title,
666 |           x: [template.xKey],
667 |           y: [template.yKey],
668 |           aggregation: template.aggregation || "count",
669 |         });
670 |       }
671 |       return buildChart(rows, schema, template);
672 |     })
673 |     .filter((chart) => chart && chart.data && chart.data.length)
674 |     .slice(0, 12);
675 | 
676 |   const savedPattern = savePattern({ domain, schema, kpis, charts });
677 | 
678 |   return {
679 |     success: true,
680 |     schemaOnly: true,
681 |     dataType: domain,
682 |     dataTypeLabel: basePlaybook.label,
683 |     rowCount: rows.length,
684 |     columnCount: columns.length,
685 |     kpis,
686 |     chartRecommendations: charts,
687 |     insights: buildInsights(rows, schema, domain, kpis, charts),
688 |     memory: {
689 |       usedPreviousPattern: Boolean(remembered),
690 |       similarity: remembered?.similarity || 0,
691 |       patternId: savedPattern.id,
692 |       usageCount: savedPattern.usageCount,
693 |       domain,
694 |       columnSignature: schema.columnSignature,
695 |     },
696 |     privacy: {
697 |       rawRowsSentToAI: false,
698 |       schemaOnly: true,
699 |     },
700 |   };
701 | }
702 | 
703 | export function saveAnalyticsBrainFeedback({ patternId, action, rating, note }) {
704 |   const next = readMemory().map((entry) => {
705 |     if (entry.id !== patternId) return entry;
706 | 
707 |     return {
708 |       ...entry,
709 |       feedback: [
710 |         ...(entry.feedback || []),
711 |         {
712 |           id: randomUUID(),
713 |           action,
714 |           rating,
715 |           note,
716 |           createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
717 |         },
718 |       ],
719 |       lastUsedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
720 |     };
721 |   });
722 | 
723 |   writeMemory(next);
724 |   return { success: true };
725 | }
726 | 
727 | export function getAnalyticsBrainMemory() {
728 |   return readMemory();
729 | }
730 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/analytics-memory-service.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/analytics-memory-service.js`

```js
 1 | import fs from "node:fs";
 2 | import path from "node:path";
 3 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
 4 | 
 5 | const MEMORY_FILE = path.resolve("data", "analytics-playbook-memory.json");
 6 | 
 7 | function ensureMemoryFile() {
 8 |   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(MEMORY_FILE), { recursive: true });
 9 | 
10 |   if (!fs.existsSync(MEMORY_FILE)) {
11 |     fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify([], null, 2));
12 |   }
13 | }
14 | 
15 | export function createColumnSignature(columns = []) {
16 |   return columns
17 |     .map((column) => String(column.name || column).toLowerCase().trim())
18 |     .sort()
19 |     .join("|");
20 | }
21 | 
22 | export function similarityScore(a = "", b = "") {
23 |   const setA = new Set(a.split("|").filter(Boolean));
24 |   const setB = new Set(b.split("|").filter(Boolean));
25 | 
26 |   if (!setA.size || !setB.size) return 0;
27 | 
28 |   const intersection = [...setA].filter((value) => setB.has(value)).length;
29 |   const union = new Set([...setA, ...setB]).size;
30 | 
31 |   return intersection / union;
32 | }
33 | 
34 | export function loadAnalyticsMemory() {
35 |   ensureMemoryFile();
36 | 
37 |   try {
38 |     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, "utf8"));
39 |     return Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [];
40 |   } catch {
41 |     return [];
42 |   }
43 | }
44 | 
45 | export function findSimilarPlaybook({ domain, columnSignature }) {
46 |   const candidates = loadAnalyticsMemory()
47 |     .filter((item) => item.domain === domain)
48 |     .map((item) => ({
49 |       ...item,
50 |       similarity: similarityScore(item.columnSignature, columnSignature),
51 |     }))
52 |     .sort((left, right) => right.similarity - left.similarity);
53 | 
54 |   const best = candidates[0];
55 | 
56 |   if (!best || best.similarity < 0.65) {
57 |     return null;
58 |   }
59 | 
60 |   return best;
61 | }
62 | 
63 | export function saveAnalyticsMemory({
64 |   domain,
65 |   columnSignature,
66 |   kpiTemplates,
67 |   chartTemplates,
68 | }) {
69 |   ensureMemoryFile();
70 | 
71 |   const memory = loadAnalyticsMemory();
72 |   const existing = memory.find(
73 |     (item) => item.domain === domain && item.columnSignature === columnSignature,
74 |   );
75 | 
76 |   if (existing) {
77 |     existing.kpiTemplates = kpiTemplates;
78 |     existing.chartTemplates = chartTemplates;
79 |     existing.usageCount = Number(existing.usageCount || 0) + 1;
80 |     existing.lastUsedAt = new Date().toISOString();
81 |   } else {
82 |     memory.push({
83 |       id: randomUUID(),
84 |       domain,
85 |       columnSignature,
86 |       kpiTemplates,
87 |       chartTemplates,
88 |       usageCount: 1,
89 |       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
90 |       lastUsedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
91 |     });
92 |   }
93 | 
94 |   fs.writeFileSync(MEMORY_FILE, JSON.stringify(memory, null, 2));
95 | }
96 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/services/analytics-playbook-engine.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/services/analytics-playbook-engine.js`

```js
  1 | import { randomUUID } from "node:crypto";
  2 | import { detectDomain } from "./domain-detector.js";
  3 | import {
  4 |   createColumnSignature,
  5 |   findSimilarPlaybook,
  6 |   saveAnalyticsMemory,
  7 | } from "./analytics-memory-service.js";
  8 | 
  9 | function safeNumber(value) {
 10 |   const n = Number(value);
 11 |   return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : null;
 12 | }
 13 | 
 14 | function humanize(value) {
 15 |   return String(value || "")
 16 |     .replace(/[_-]+/g, " ")
 17 |     .replace(/\b\w/g, (c) => c.toUpperCase());
 18 | }
 19 | 
 20 | function detectColumns(rows = [], dictionary = {}) {
 21 |   const first = rows[0] || {};
 22 | 
 23 |   return Object.keys(first).map((name) => {
 24 |     const values = rows.map((r) => r[name]).filter((v) => v !== null && v !== undefined && v !== "");
 25 |     const numericCount = values.filter((v) => safeNumber(v) !== null).length;
 26 |     const uniqueCount = new Set(values.map(String)).size;
 27 | 
 28 |     let type = "string";
 29 |     let role = "dimension";
 30 | 
 31 |     if (values.length && numericCount / values.length > 0.75) {
 32 |       type = "number";
 33 |       role = "metric";
 34 |     }
 35 | 
 36 |     if (/date|time|month|year|created|updated/i.test(name)) {
 37 |       type = "date";
 38 |       role = "date";
 39 |     }
 40 | 
 41 |     if (/id|uuid|email|phone|password|token|secret/i.test(name)) {
 42 |       role = "id";
 43 |     }
 44 | 
 45 |     const meta = dictionary[name] || {};
 46 | 
 47 |     return {
 48 |       name,
 49 |       type: meta.type || type,
 50 |       role,
 51 |       description: meta.description || "",
 52 |       uniqueCount,
 53 |       nullCount: rows.length - values.length,
 54 |     };
 55 |   });
 56 | }
 57 | 
 58 | function parseDictionary(metadataFiles = []) {
 59 |   const dictionary = {};
 60 | 
 61 |   for (const file of metadataFiles) {
 62 |     for (const row of file.rows || []) {
 63 |       const columnName =
 64 |         row.column ||
 65 |         row.column_name ||
 66 |         row.field ||
 67 |         row.field_name ||
 68 |         row.name ||
 69 |         row.Column ||
 70 |         row["Column Name"];
 71 | 
 72 |       if (!columnName) continue;
 73 | 
 74 |       dictionary[String(columnName)] = {
 75 |         description:
 76 |           row.description ||
 77 |           row.Description ||
 78 |           row.meaning ||
 79 |           row.Meaning ||
 80 |           "",
 81 |         type: row.type || row.Type || "",
 82 |         target:
 83 |           String(row.target || row.Target || "").toLowerCase() === "true" ||
 84 |           String(row.type || "").toLowerCase() === "target",
 85 |       };
 86 |     }
 87 |   }
 88 | 
 89 |   return dictionary;
 90 | }
 91 | 
 92 | function groupBy(rows, xKey, yKey, aggregation = "sum", limit = 10) {
 93 |   const map = new Map();
 94 | 
 95 |   for (const row of rows) {
 96 |     const label = String(row[xKey] ?? "Unknown");
 97 |     const value = aggregation === "count" ? 1 : safeNumber(row[yKey]);
 98 | 
 99 |     if (aggregation !== "count" && value === null) continue;
100 | 
101 |     const stat = map.get(label) || {
102 |       sum: 0,
103 |       count: 0,
104 |       min: Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY,
105 |       max: Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY,
106 |     };
107 | 
108 |     stat.sum += value;
109 |     stat.count += 1;
110 |     stat.min = Math.min(stat.min, value);
111 |     stat.max = Math.max(stat.max, value);
112 | 
113 |     map.set(label, stat);
114 |   }
115 | 
116 |   return [...map.entries()]
117 |     .map(([label, stat]) => {
118 |       let value = stat.sum;
119 | 
120 |       if (aggregation === "avg") value = stat.count ? stat.sum / stat.count : 0;
121 |       if (aggregation === "count") value = stat.count;
122 |       if (aggregation === "min") value = stat.min;
123 |       if (aggregation === "max") value = stat.max;
124 | 
125 |       return {
126 |         [xKey]: label,
127 |         [yKey === "count" ? "count" : yKey]: Number(value.toFixed(2)),
128 |       };
129 |     })
130 |     .sort((a, b) => {
131 |       const key = yKey === "count" ? "count" : yKey;
132 |       return Number(b[key]) - Number(a[key]);
133 |     })
134 |     .slice(0, limit);
135 | }
136 | 
137 | function columnExists(rows, key) {
138 |   return !!key && rows.length > 0 && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(rows[0], key);
139 | }
140 | 
141 | function buildChartFromTemplate(rows, template) {
142 |   if (!columnExists(rows, template.xKey)) return null;
143 | 
144 |   if (template.yKey !== "count" && !columnExists(rows, template.yKey)) {
145 |     return null;
146 |   }
147 | 
148 |   const aggregation = template.aggregation || "sum";
149 |   const yKey = aggregation === "count" ? "count" : template.yKey;
150 | 
151 |   return {
152 |     id: randomUUID(),
153 |     type: template.type || "bar",
154 |     title: template.title,
155 |     xKey: template.type === "histogram" ? "range" : template.xKey,
156 |     yKey: template.type === "histogram" ? "count" : yKey,
157 |     aggregation,
158 |     learned: true,
159 |     data:
160 |       template.type === "histogram"
161 |         ? histogram(rows, template.yKey || template.xKey, 10)
162 |         : groupBy(rows, template.xKey, template.yKey, aggregation, 10),
163 |   };
164 | }
165 | 
166 | function buildKpiFromTemplate(rows, template) {
167 |   if (template.aggregation === "count") {
168 |     return {
169 |       title: template.title || "Total Records",
170 |       value: rows.length.toLocaleString(),
171 |       metric: template.metric || "*",
172 |       aggregation: "count",
173 |       learned: true,
174 |     };
175 |   }
176 | 
177 |   if (!columnExists(rows, template.metric)) return null;
178 | 
179 |   const values = rows
180 |     .map((row) => safeNumber(row[template.metric]))
181 |     .filter((value) => value !== null);
182 | 
183 |   if (!values.length) return null;
184 | 
185 |   let value = values.reduce((sum, next) => sum + next, 0);
186 | 
187 |   if (template.aggregation === "avg") value /= values.length;
188 |   if (template.aggregation === "median") value = median(values);
189 |   if (template.aggregation === "min") value = Math.min(...values);
190 |   if (template.aggregation === "max") value = Math.max(...values);
191 | 
192 |   return {
193 |     title: template.title,
194 |     value: Number(value.toFixed(2)).toLocaleString(),
195 |     metric: template.metric,
196 |     aggregation: template.aggregation,
197 |     learned: true,
198 |   };
199 | }
200 | 
201 | function histogram(rows, key, bucketCount = 10) {
202 |   const values = rows.map((r) => safeNumber(r[key])).filter((v) => v !== null);
203 | 
204 |   if (!values.length) return [];
205 | 
206 |   const min = Math.min(...values);
207 |   const max = Math.max(...values);
208 |   const step = (max - min) / bucketCount || 1;
209 | 
210 |   const buckets = Array.from({ length: bucketCount }, (_, i) => {
211 |     const start = min + i * step;
212 |     const end = i === bucketCount - 1 ? max : start + step;
213 | 
214 |     return {
215 |       range: `${Math.round(start)}-${Math.round(end)}`,
216 |       count: 0,
217 |     };
218 |   });
219 | 
220 |   for (const value of values) {
221 |     const index = Math.min(Math.floor((value - min) / step), bucketCount - 1);
222 |     buckets[index].count += 1;
223 |   }
224 | 
225 |   return buckets;
226 | }
227 | 
228 | function median(values) {
229 |   const nums = values.filter((v) => v !== null).sort((a, b) => a - b);
230 |   if (!nums.length) return 0;
231 | 
232 |   const mid = Math.floor(nums.length / 2);
233 |   return nums.length % 2 ? nums[mid] : (nums[mid - 1] + nums[mid]) / 2;
234 | }
235 | 
236 | function topValue(rows, key) {
237 |   const map = new Map();
238 | 
239 |   for (const row of rows) {
240 |     const value = String(row[key] ?? "").trim();
241 |     if (!value) continue;
242 |     map.set(value, (map.get(value) || 0) + 1);
243 |   }
244 | 
245 |   return [...map.entries()].sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])[0] || ["N/A", 0];
246 | }
247 | 
248 | function buildSalaryKpis(rows, columns) {
249 |   const salaryKey = columns.find((c) => /salary/i.test(c.name))?.name || "salary_usd";
250 |   const values = rows.map((r) => safeNumber(r[salaryKey])).filter((v) => v !== null);
251 | 
252 |   const total = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
253 |   const avg = values.length ? total / values.length : 0;
254 | 
255 |   const [topCountry] = topValue(rows, "country");
256 |   const [topEducation] = topValue(rows, "education");
257 |   const [topExperience] = topValue(rows, "experience");
258 |   const [topLanguage] = topValue(rows, "languages");
259 |   const [topFramework] = topValue(rows, "frameworks");
260 | 
261 |   return [
262 |     { title: "Total Records", value: rows.length.toLocaleString(), metric: "*", aggregation: "count", icon: "rows" },
263 |     { title: "Average Salary", value: `$${Math.round(avg).toLocaleString()}`, metric: salaryKey, aggregation: "avg", icon: "salary" },
264 |     { title: "Median Salary", value: `$${Math.round(median(values)).toLocaleString()}`, metric: salaryKey, aggregation: "median", icon: "salary" },
265 |     { title: "Highest Salary", value: `$${Math.max(...values).toLocaleString()}`, metric: salaryKey, aggregation: "max", icon: "salary" },
266 |     { title: "Top Country", value: topCountry, metric: "country", aggregation: "top", icon: "globe" },
267 |     { title: "Top Education", value: topEducation, metric: "education", aggregation: "top", icon: "education" },
268 |     { title: "Common Experience", value: topExperience, metric: "experience", aggregation: "top", icon: "briefcase" },
269 |     { title: "Top Language", value: topLanguage, metric: "languages", aggregation: "top", icon: "code" },
270 |     { title: "Top Framework", value: topFramework, metric: "frameworks", aggregation: "top", icon: "framework" },
271 |   ];
272 | }
273 | 
274 | function chart(type, title, xKey, yKey, data, aggregation = "sum") {
275 |   return {
276 |     id: randomUUID(),
277 |     type,
278 |     title,
279 |     xKey,
280 |     yKey,
281 |     aggregation,
282 |     data,
283 |   };
284 | }
285 | 
286 | function buildSalaryCharts(rows) {
287 |   const salary = "salary_usd";
288 | 
289 |   return [
290 |     chart("bar", "Average Salary by Country", "country", salary, groupBy(rows, "country", salary, "avg", 10), "avg"),
291 |     chart("bar", "Average Salary by Experience", "experience", salary, groupBy(rows, "experience", salary, "avg", 10), "avg"),
292 |     chart("bar", "Average Salary by Education", "education", salary, groupBy(rows, "education", salary, "avg", 10), "avg"),
293 |     chart("bar", "Average Salary by Company Size", "company_size", salary, groupBy(rows, "company_size", salary, "avg", 10), "avg"),
294 |     chart("bar", "Count by Country", "country", "count", groupBy(rows, "country", "count", "count", 10), "count"),
295 |     chart("histogram", "Salary Distribution", salary, salary, histogram(rows, salary, 10), "count"),
296 |     chart("bar", "Top Languages", "languages", "count", groupBy(rows, "languages", "count", "count", 10), "count"),
297 |     chart("bar", "Top Frameworks", "frameworks", "count", groupBy(rows, "frameworks", "count", "count", 10), "count"),
298 |   ];
299 | }
300 | 
301 | function buildGenericKpis(rows, columns) {
302 |   const metric = columns.find((c) => c.role === "metric");
303 |   const dimension = columns.find((c) => c.role === "dimension");
304 | 
305 |   const kpis = [
306 |     { title: "Total Records", value: rows.length.toLocaleString(), metric: "*", aggregation: "count", icon: "rows" },
307 |     { title: "Columns", value: columns.length.toLocaleString(), metric: "*", aggregation: "count", icon: "columns" },
308 |   ];
309 | 
310 |   if (metric) {
311 |     const values = rows.map((r) => safeNumber(r[metric.name])).filter((v) => v !== null);
312 |     const total = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
313 |     const avg = values.length ? total / values.length : 0;
314 | 
315 |     kpis.push({ title: `Total ${humanize(metric.name)}`, value: total.toFixed(2), metric: metric.name, aggregation: "sum", icon: "metric" });
316 |     kpis.push({ title: `Average ${humanize(metric.name)}`, value: avg.toFixed(2), metric: metric.name, aggregation: "avg", icon: "metric" });
317 |   }
318 | 
319 |   if (dimension) {
320 |     const [top] = topValue(rows, dimension.name);
321 |     kpis.push({ title: `Top ${humanize(dimension.name)}`, value: top, metric: dimension.name, aggregation: "top", icon: "category" });
322 |   }
323 | 
324 |   return kpis;
325 | }
326 | 
327 | function buildGenericCharts(rows, columns) {
328 |   const metric = columns.find((c) => c.role === "metric");
329 |   const dimension = columns.find((c) => c.role === "dimension");
330 |   const date = columns.find((c) => c.role === "date");
331 | 
332 |   const charts = [];
333 | 
334 |   if (metric && dimension) {
335 |     charts.push(
336 |       chart(
337 |         "bar",
338 |         `${humanize(metric.name)} by ${humanize(dimension.name)}`,
339 |         dimension.name,
340 |         metric.name,
341 |         groupBy(rows, dimension.name, metric.name, "sum", 10),
342 |         "sum"
343 |       )
344 |     );
345 | 
346 |     charts.push(
347 |       chart(
348 |         "pie",
349 |         `Count by ${humanize(dimension.name)}`,
350 |         dimension.name,
351 |         "count",
352 |         groupBy(rows, dimension.name, "count", "count", 8),
353 |         "count"
354 |       )
355 |     );
356 |   }
357 | 
358 |   if (metric) {
359 |     charts.push(
360 |       chart(
361 |         "bar",
362 |         `${humanize(metric.name)} Distribution`,
363 |         "range",
364 |         "count",
365 |         histogram(rows, metric.name, 10),
366 |         "count"
367 |       )
368 |     );
369 |   }
370 | 
371 |   if (metric && date) {
372 |     charts.push(
373 |       chart(
374 |         "line",
375 |         `${humanize(metric.name)} Trend`,
376 |         date.name,
377 |         metric.name,
378 |         groupBy(rows, date.name, metric.name, "sum", 20),
379 |         "sum"
380 |       )
381 |     );
382 |   }
383 | 
384 |   return charts;
385 | }
386 | 
387 | export function buildAnalyticsPlaybook({ dataset, metadataFiles = [], testFiles = [] }) {
388 |   const dictionary = parseDictionary(metadataFiles);
389 |   const rows = dataset.rows || [];
390 |   const columns = detectColumns(rows, dictionary);
391 |   const domainInfo = detectDomain(columns, dictionary);
392 |   const columnSignature = createColumnSignature(columns);
393 |   const rememberedPlaybook = findSimilarPlaybook({
394 |     domain: domainInfo.domain,
395 |     columnSignature,
396 |   });
397 | 
398 |   let kpis = [];
399 |   let charts = [];
400 | 
401 |   if (domainInfo.domain === "salary_hr_jobs") {
402 |     kpis = buildSalaryKpis(rows, columns);
403 |     charts = buildSalaryCharts(rows);
404 |   } else {
405 |     kpis = buildGenericKpis(rows, columns);
406 |     charts = buildGenericCharts(rows, columns);
407 |   }
408 | 
409 |   if (rememberedPlaybook) {
410 |     const learnedCharts = (rememberedPlaybook.chartTemplates || [])
411 |       .map((template) => buildChartFromTemplate(rows, template))
412 |       .filter(Boolean);
413 | 
414 |     const learnedKpis = (rememberedPlaybook.kpiTemplates || [])
415 |       .map((template) => buildKpiFromTemplate(rows, template))
416 |       .filter(Boolean);
417 | 
418 |     if (learnedCharts.length) {
419 |       charts = [...learnedCharts, ...charts].slice(0, 8);
420 |     }
421 | 
422 |     if (learnedKpis.length) {
423 |       kpis = [...learnedKpis, ...kpis].slice(0, 8);
424 |     }
425 |   }
426 | 
427 |   saveAnalyticsMemory({
428 |     domain: domainInfo.domain,
429 |     columnSignature,
430 |     kpiTemplates: kpis.map((kpi) => ({
431 |       title: kpi.title,
432 |       metric: kpi.metric,
433 |       aggregation: kpi.aggregation,
434 |       dimension: kpi.dimension,
435 |     })),
436 |     chartTemplates: charts.map((chartItem) => ({
437 |       title: chartItem.title,
438 |       type: chartItem.type,
439 |       xKey: chartItem.xKey,
440 |       yKey: chartItem.yKey,
441 |       aggregation: chartItem.aggregation || "sum",
442 |     })),
443 |   });
444 | 
445 |   return {
446 |     dataType: domainInfo.domain,
447 |     dataTypeLabel: domainInfo.label,
448 |     chartRecommendations: charts,
449 |     kpis,
450 |     memory: {
451 |       usedPreviousPattern: Boolean(rememberedPlaybook),
452 |       similarity: rememberedPlaybook?.similarity || 0,
453 |       domain: domainInfo.domain,
454 |       columnSignature,
455 |     },
456 |     insights: [
457 |       {
458 |         type: "summary",
459 |         title: "Dataset Summary",
460 |         message: `Analyzed ${rows.length.toLocaleString()} rows from ${dataset.fileName || dataset.name}.`,
461 |       },
462 |       {
463 |         type: "metadata",
464 |         title: "Metadata Usage",
465 |         message: metadataFiles.length
466 |           ? `Used ${metadataFiles.map((f) => f.fileName || f.name).join(", ")} as schema metadata.`
467 |           : "No metadata dictionary found.",
468 |       },
469 |       {
470 |         type: "test_data",
471 |         title: "Test Dataset",
472 |         message: testFiles.length
473 |           ? `${testFiles.map((f) => f.fileName || f.name).join(", ")} available for prediction/scoring.`
474 |           : "No test dataset detected.",
475 |       },
476 |     ],
477 |     aiInsights: {
478 |       domain: domainInfo,
479 |       memory: {
480 |         usedPreviousPattern: Boolean(rememberedPlaybook),
481 |         similarity: rememberedPlaybook?.similarity || 0,
482 |         domain: domainInfo.domain,
483 |         columnSignature,
484 |       },
485 |       schemaPacket: {
486 |         rowCount: rows.length,
487 |         columnCount: columns.length,
488 |         columns,
489 |       },
490 |       kpis,
491 |       relatedDatasets: {
492 |         metadataFiles: metadataFiles.map((f) => f.fileName || f.name),
493 |         testFiles: testFiles.map((f) => f.fileName || f.name),
494 |       },
495 |     },
496 |   };
497 | }
498 | 
```

```js
 34 | function buildGuidedDatasetResponse(schema, chart) {
 35 |   const dataTypeLabel = schema?.dataTypeLabel || "Data";
 36 |   const primaryMetric = schema?.primaryMetric?.name || schema?.primaryMetric || "values";
 37 |   const primaryDimension = schema?.primaryDimension?.name || schema?.primaryDimension || "category";
 38 | 
 39 |   return {
 40 |     content: `This is your ${dataTypeLabel} dataset. You can ask me about trends, comparisons, or insights. For example, you could ask "Show me the top 5 ${primaryDimension} by ${primaryMetric}" or "What are the main patterns in this data?"`,
 41 |     insights: [
 42 |       `Primary metric: ${primaryMetric}`,
 43 |       `Primary dimension: ${primaryDimension}`,
 44 |       chart ? `Recommended chart: ${chart.chartType || "bar"}` : null,
 45 |     ].filter(Boolean),
 46 |   };
 47 | }

 49 | /**
 50 |  * Create schema-first chat response using Ollama llama3.2:latest
 51 |  * Enforces privacy by only sending schema metadata to AI
 52 |  */
 53 | export const createSchemaFirstChatResponse = async (dataset, query, preferences = {}) => {
 54 |   const chartCount = preferences.chartCount || "auto";
 55 |   const chartTypes = preferences.chartTypes || ["bar", "line", "pie"];
 56 |   const showTrends = preferences.showTrends !== false;
 57 |   const showCorrelations = preferences.showCorrelations !== false;
 58 | 
 59 |   const configured = await isOllamaConfigured();
 60 |   
 61 |   if (!configured) {
 62 |     console.log("[schema-first] Ollama not available, using local analytics");
 63 |     return createChatResponse(dataset, query);
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   try {
 67 |     console.log("[schema-first] Calling Ollama with schema-only approach");
 68 |     const result = await callOllamaAI(dataset, query, preferences);
 69 |     
 70 |     if (!result.success) {
 71 |       console.log("[schema-first] Ollama failed, falling back to local");
 72 |       return createChatResponse(dataset, query);
 73 |     }
 74 | 
 75 |     return {
 76 |       content: result.insights || result.content || "Analysis complete",
 77 |       sql: result.sql,
 78 |       chart: result.charts || result.chart_type,
 79 |       insights: result.insights,
 80 |       schema: result.charts,
 81 |       intent: result.intent,
 82 |       confidence: result.confidence || 0.8,
 83 |       reason: result.reasoning,
 84 |       model: result.model || "llama3.2:latest",
 85 |       usedAI: true,
 86 |       metadata: {
 87 |         columnsUsed: result.columns_used,
 88 |         chartTypes: result.charts?.map(c => c.type),
 89 |         preferences: { chartCount, chartTypes, showTrends, showCorrelations },
 90 |       },
 91 |     };
 92 |   } catch (error) {
 93 |     console.log("[schema-first] Error:", error.message);
 94 |     return createChatResponse(dataset, query);
 95 |   }
 96 | };
 97 | 
 98 | /**
 99 |  * Validate and fix AI-generated SQL to use correct values from dataset
100 |  */
101 | export function validateAndFixSQL(dataset, sql) {
102 |   if (!sql || !dataset?.rows?.length) {
103 |     return sql;
104 |   }
105 | 
106 |   try {
107 |     const whereMatch = sql.match(/WHERE\s+(\w+)\s*=\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]/gi);
108 |     if (!whereMatch) {
109 |       return sql;
110 |     }
111 | 
112 |     const columnName = sql.match(/WHERE\s+(\w+)\s*=/i)?.[1];
113 |     if (!columnName) {
114 |       return sql;
115 |     }
116 | 
117 |     const actualValues = new Set();
118 |     for (const row of dataset.rows) {
119 |       const val = row[columnName];
120 |       if (val !== null && val !== undefined && String(val).trim()) {
121 |         actualValues.add(String(val).trim().toLowerCase());
122 |       }
123 |     }
124 | 
125 |     if (actualValues.size === 0) {
126 |       return sql;
127 |     }
128 | 
129 |     let fixedSQL = sql;
130 |     for (const match of whereMatch) {
131 |       const valueMatch = match.match(/WHERE\s+(\w+)\s*=\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]/i);
132 |       if (!valueMatch) continue;
133 | 
134 |       const [, col, value] = valueMatch;
135 |       const normalizedValue = value.trim().toLowerCase();
136 | 
137 |       if (!actualValues.has(normalizedValue)) {
138 |         const matchingValue = [...actualValues].find((v) => {
139 |           if (normalizedValue === "some college" && v === "high school") return true;
140 |           if (normalizedValue === "high school" && v === "some college") return true;
141 |           if (normalizedValue === "master" && v === "masters") return true;
142 |           if (normalizedValue === "masters" && v === "master") return true;
143 |           return v.includes(normalizedValue) || normalizedValue.includes(v);
144 |         });
145 | 
146 |         if (matchingValue) {
147 |           console.log(`[analytics] 🔧 Fixing SQL value: '${value}' -> '${matchingValue}'`);
148 |           fixedSQL = fixedSQL.replace(/WHERE\s+\w+\s*=\s*['"][^'"]+['"]/i, `WHERE ${col} = '${matchingValue}'`);
149 |         }
150 |       }
151 |     }
152 | 
153 |     return fixedSQL;
154 |   } catch (error) {
155 |     console.warn("[analytics] SQL validation error:", error.message);
156 |     return sql;
157 |   }
158 | }
159 | 
160 | export const isGreetingQuery = (query) => {
161 |   const normalizedQuery = normalizeText(query);
162 | 
163 |   return /^(hello|hi|hey|hola|good morning|good afternoon|good evening)( there)?$/.test(normalizedQuery);
164 | };
165 | 
166 | const isDatasetHelpQuery = (normalizedQuery) =>
167 |   /\b(help|ask|question|questions|query|queries|prompt|prompts|analyse|analyze)\b/.test(normalizedQuery)
168 |   && /\b(dataset|data|table|file|column|columns)\b/.test(normalizedQuery);
169 | 
170 | const singularizeWord = (word) => {
171 |   if (word.length <= 3) return word;
172 |   if (word.endsWith("ies")) return `${word.slice(0, -3)}y`;
173 |   if (word.endsWith("ses")) return word.slice(0, -2);
174 |   if (word.endsWith("s")) return word.slice(0, -1);
175 |   return word;
176 | };
177 | 
178 | const normalizeComparableText = (value) =>
179 |   normalizeText(value)
180 |     .split(" ")
181 |     .filter(Boolean)
182 |     .map(singularizeWord)
183 |     .join(" ");
184 | 
185 | const includesPhrase = (source, phrase) => {
186 |   if (!source || !phrase) return false;
187 |   return (` ${source} `).includes(` ${phrase} `);
188 | };
189 | 
190 | export const isDatasetOverviewQuery = (normalizedQuery) =>
191 |   /\b(what|waht|which|describe|explain|summary|summarize|overview|about)\b/.test(normalizedQuery)
192 |   && /\b(dataset|data|table|file)\b/.test(normalizedQuery);
193 | 
194 | export const classifyChatQuery = (query) => {
195 |   const normalizedQuery = normalizeText(query);
196 | 
197 |   if (isGreetingQuery(query)) {
198 |     return "greeting";
199 |   }
200 | 
201 |   if (isDatasetOverviewQuery(normalizedQuery) || isDatasetHelpQuery(normalizedQuery)) {
202 |     return "dataset-help";
203 |   }
204 | 
205 |   return "analysis";
206 | };
207 | 
208 | const pickDimensionForQuery = (schema, queryLower) => {
209 |   const dimensions = schema.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "dimension");
210 |   return dimensions.find((column) => queryLower.includes(column.name.toLowerCase()))
211 |     ?? schema.primaryDimension
212 |     ?? schema.secondaryDimension
213 |     ?? null;
214 | };
215 | 
216 | const pickMetricForQuery = (schema, queryLower) => {
217 |   const metrics = schema.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "metric");
218 |   return metrics.find((column) => queryLower.includes(column.name.toLowerCase()))
219 |     ?? schema.primaryMetric
220 |     ?? schema.secondaryMetric
221 |     ?? null;
222 | };
223 | 
224 | const detectQueryValueFilter = (dataset, schema, normalizedQuery) => {
225 |   const dimensions = schema.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "dimension");
226 |   const comparableQuery = normalizeComparableText(normalizedQuery);
227 | 
228 |   for (const dimension of dimensions) {
229 |     const values = new Set();
230 | 
231 |     // Only analyze schema structure, not actual data values
232 |     // This prevents data leakage to AI services
233 |     if (dataset && dataset.rows) {
234 |       for (const row of dataset.rows) {
235 |         const label = normalizeDimensionLabel(dimension.name, row[dimension.name]);
236 |         if (label) {
237 |           values.add(label);
238 |         }
239 |         if (values.size >= 50) {
240 |           break;
241 |         }
242 |       }
243 |     }
244 | 
245 |     const matches = [];
246 | 
247 |     for (const value of values) {
248 |       const normalizedValue = normalizeText(value);
249 |       const comparableValue = normalizeComparableText(value);
250 |       if (!normalizedValue) {
251 |         continue;
252 |       }
253 | 
254 |       if (includesPhrase(normalizedQuery, normalizedValue) || includesPhrase(comparableQuery, comparableValue)) {
255 |         matches.push(value);
256 |       }
257 |     }
258 | 
259 |     if (matches.length > 0) {
260 |       return { dimension, values: [...new Set(matches)] };
261 |     }
262 |   }
263 | 
264 |   return null;
265 | };
266 | 
267 | const buildAnalysisPlan = (dataset, schema, query) => {
268 |   const queryLower = query.toLowerCase();
269 |   const normalizedQuery = normalizeText(query);
270 |   const dimension = pickDimensionForQuery(schema, queryLower);
271 |   const metric = pickMetricForQuery(schema, queryLower);
272 |   const valueFilter = detectQueryValueFilter(dataset, schema, normalizedQuery);
273 |   const wantsCount = /\bhow many\b|\bcount\b|\bnumber of\b|\btotal people\b|\bpeople\b/.test(normalizedQuery);
274 |   const mentionsMetric = schema.columns
275 |     .filter((column) => column.role === "metric")
276 |     .some((column) => includesPhrase(normalizedQuery, normalizeText(column.name)));
277 |   const overviewQuery = isDatasetOverviewQuery(normalizedQuery);
278 | 
279 |   if (overviewQuery) {
280 |     return {
281 |       mode: "overview",
282 |       metric,
283 |       dimension: schema.primaryDimension ?? dimension,
284 |       chartType: "bar",
285 |       intent: "overview",
286 |     };
287 |   }
288 | 
289 |   if ((queryLower.includes("trend") || queryLower.includes("monthly") || queryLower.includes("over time")) && schema.primaryDimension) {
290 |     const trendDimension = schema.columns.find((column) => column.name === schema.primaryDimension.name && (column.type === "date" || column.name === "month"))
291 |       ?? schema.columns.find((column) => column.type === "date")
292 |       ?? schema.columns.find((column) => column.name === "month")
293 |       ?? schema.primaryDimension;
294 | 
295 |     return {
296 |       mode: "metricByDimension",
297 |       metric,
298 |       dimension: trendDimension,
299 |       aggregation: metric ? metricAggregationForColumn(metric.name) : "sum",
300 |       chartType: trendDimension.type === "date" || trendDimension.name === "month" ? "area" : "line",
301 |       intent: "trend",
302 |     };
303 |   }
304 | 
305 |   if (wantsCount && valueFilter) {
306 |     return {
307 |       mode: "countMatchingValues",
308 |       dimension: valueFilter.dimension,
309 |       matchValues: valueFilter.values,
310 |       chartType: "bar",
311 |       intent: "count-filter",
312 |     };
313 |   }
314 | 
315 |   if (valueFilter && !mentionsMetric) {
316 |     return {
317 |       mode: "countMatchingValues",
318 |       dimension: valueFilter.dimension,
319 |       matchValues: valueFilter.values,
320 |       chartType: "bar",
321 |       intent: "value-filter",
322 |     };
323 |   }
324 | 
325 |   if (wantsCount && dimension) {
326 |     return {
327 |       mode: "countByDimension",
328 |       dimension,
329 |       chartType: "pie",
330 |       intent: "count",
331 |     };
332 |   }
333 | 
334 |   if (dimension && metric) {
335 |     return {
336 |       mode: "metricByDimension",
337 |       metric,
338 |       dimension,
339 |       aggregation: queryLower.includes("average") || queryLower.includes("avg")
340 |         ? "average"
341 |         : metricAggregationForColumn(metric.name),
342 |       chartType: queryLower.includes("pie") ? "pie" : "bar",
343 |       intent: "breakdown",
344 |     };
345 |   }
346 | 
347 |   if (dimension) {
348 |     return {
349 |       mode: "countByDimension",
350 |       dimension,
351 |       chartType: "pie",
352 |       intent: "count",
353 |     };
354 |   }
355 | 
356 |   return {
357 |     mode: "summary",
358 |     metric,
359 |     dimension,
360 |     chartType: "bar",
361 |     intent: "summary",
362 |   };
363 | };
364 | 
365 | const materializePlan = (dataset, plan) => {
366 |   if (plan.mode === "overview" && plan.dimension) {
367 |     const data = countRowsByDimension(dataset.rows, plan.dimension).slice(0, 8);
368 |     if (data.length === 0) {
369 |       return null;
370 |     }
371 | 
372 |     return {
373 |       type: data.length <= 6 ? "pie" : "bar",
374 |       title: `Dataset overview by ${humanize(plan.dimension.name)}`,
375 |       xKey: plan.dimension.name,
376 |       yKey: "count",
377 |       data,
378 |     };
379 |   }
380 | 
381 |   if (plan.mode === "metricByDimension" && plan.metric && plan.dimension) {
382 |     const data = groupMetricByDimension(dataset.rows, plan.dimension, plan.metric, plan.aggregation);
383 |     return {
384 |       type: plan.chartType === "pie" && data.length <= 6 ? "pie" : plan.chartType,
385 |       title: `${plan.aggregation === "average" ? "Average " : ""}${humanize(plan.metric.name)} by ${humanize(plan.dimension.name)}`,
386 |       xKey: plan.dimension.name,
387 |       yKey: plan.metric.name,
388 |       data,
389 |     };
390 |   }
391 | 
392 |   if (plan.mode === "countByDimension" && plan.dimension) {
393 |     const data = countRowsByDimension(dataset.rows, plan.dimension);
394 |     return {
395 |       type: data.length <= 6 ? "pie" : "bar",
396 |       title: `Count by ${humanize(plan.dimension.name)}`,
397 |       xKey: plan.dimension.name,
398 |       yKey: "count",
399 |       data,
400 |     };
401 |   }
402 | 
403 |   if (plan.mode === "countMatchingValues" && plan.dimension && Array.isArray(plan.matchValues) && plan.matchValues.length > 0) {
404 |     const data = countRowsMatchingValues(dataset.rows, plan.dimension, plan.matchValues);
405 |     const titleValues = plan.matchValues.join(", ");
406 |     return {
407 |       type: "bar",
408 |       title: `Count of ${titleValues} in ${humanize(plan.dimension.name)}`,
409 |       xKey: plan.dimension.name,
410 |       yKey: "count",
411 |       data,
412 |     };
413 |   }
414 | 
415 |   return null;
416 | };
417 | 
418 | const buildSqlForPlan = (plan) => {
419 |   if (plan.mode === "overview") {
420 |     return null;
421 |   }
422 | 
423 |   if (plan.mode === "metricByDimension" && plan.metric && plan.dimension) {
424 |     const aggregationSql = plan.aggregation === "average" ? "AVG" : "SUM";
425 |     return `SELECT ${plan.dimension.name}, ${aggregationSql}(${plan.metric.name}) AS ${plan.metric.name} FROM dataset_rows GROUP BY ${plan.dimension.name} ORDER BY ${plan.metric.name} DESC;`;
426 |   }
427 | 
428 |   if (plan.mode === "countByDimension" && plan.dimension) {
429 |     return `SELECT ${plan.dimension.name}, COUNT(*) AS count FROM dataset_rows GROUP BY ${plan.dimension.name} ORDER BY count DESC;`;
430 |   }
431 | 
432 |   if (plan.mode === "countMatchingValues" && plan.dimension && Array.isArray(plan.matchValues) && plan.matchValues.length > 0) {
433 |     const escapedValues = plan.matchValues.map((value) => `'${String(value).replace(/'/g, "''")}'`).join(", ");
434 |     return `SELECT ${plan.dimension.name}, COUNT(*) AS count FROM dataset_rows WHERE ${plan.dimension.name} IN (${escapedValues}) GROUP BY ${plan.dimension.name} ORDER BY count DESC;`;
435 |   }
436 | 
437 |   return "SELECT * FROM dataset_rows LIMIT 100;";
438 | };
439 | 
440 | const buildInsightsFromSchema = (schema, plan) => {
441 |   const base = [
442 |     `Schema inspection found ${schema.columnCount} columns across ${schema.rowCount} rows.`,
443 |     `Dimensions: ${schema.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "dimension").map((column) => column.name).join(", ") || "none"}.`,
444 |     `Metrics: ${schema.columns.filter((column) => column.role === "metric").map((column) => column.name).join(", ") || "none"}.`,
445 |   ];
446 | 
447 |   if (plan.mode === "metricByDimension" && plan.metric && plan.dimension) {
448 |     base.push(`This response used the schema to pair metric \`${plan.metric.name}\` with dimension \`${plan.dimension.name}\`.`);
449 |   } else if (plan.mode === "overview") {
450 |     const primaryDimension = plan.dimension?.name ? `\`${plan.dimension.name}\`` : "the primary categorical columns";
451 |     base.push(`This response summarizes the dataset structure and highlights ${primaryDimension} as the main grouping field.`);
452 |   } else if (plan.mode === "countByDimension" && plan.dimension) {
453 |     base.push(`This response used the schema to count records by \`${plan.dimension.name}\` without sending row values to any AI system.`);
454 |   } else if (plan.mode === "countMatchingValues" && plan.dimension && Array.isArray(plan.matchValues) && plan.matchValues.length > 0) {
455 |     const valuesList = plan.matchValues.map((value) => `\`${value}\``).join(", ");
456 |     base.push(`This response counted rows where \`${plan.dimension.name}\` matched ${valuesList} using only local processing.`);
457 |   }
458 | 
459 |   return base;
460 | };

462 | export const createChatResponse = async (dataset, query) => {

466 | 
467 |   if (queryType === "greeting") {
468 |     const chart = materializePlan(analyticsDataset, {
469 |       mode: "overview",
470 |       metric: schema.primaryMetric,
471 |       dimension: schema.primaryDimension ?? schema.secondaryDimension,
472 |       chartType: "bar",
473 |       intent: "overview",
474 |     });
475 |     const guided = buildGuidedDatasetResponse(schema, chart);
476 | 
477 |     return {
478 |       content: "Hello! I'm here to help you analyze your data. What would you like to explore?",
479 |       sql: null,
480 |       chart,
481 |       insights: guided.insights,

483 |       intent: "greeting",

485 |   }

489 |       mode: "overview",
490 |       metric: schema.primaryMetric,
491 |       dimension: schema.primaryDimension ?? schema.secondaryDimension,
492 |       chartType: "bar",
493 |       intent: "overview",
494 |     });
495 |     const guided = buildGuidedDatasetResponse(schema, chart);
496 | 
497 |     return {
498 |       content: guided.content,
499 |       sql: null,
500 |       chart,
501 |       insights: guided.insights,
502 |       schema,

516 |     } else if (hrPlan.intent === 'correlation') {
517 |       content = `Showing correlation between ${hrPlan.dimension?.name} and ${hrPlan.metric?.name}`;
518 |     } else if (hrPlan.intent === 'ranking') {
519 |       content = `Top rankings by ${hrPlan.metric?.name}`;
520 |     } else if (hrPlan.intent === 'distribution') {
521 |       content = `Distribution of ${hrPlan.metric?.name} by ${hrPlan.dimension?.name}`;
522 |     } else if (hrPlan.intent === 'count') {
523 |       content = `Count by ${hrPlan.dimension?.name}`;
524 |     } else if (hrPlan.intent === 'comparison') {
525 |       content = `Comparing ${hrPlan.metric?.name} across ${hrPlan.dimension?.name}`;
526 |     }
527 |     
528 |     return {
529 |       content,
530 |       chart,
531 |       insights: [
532 |         `Analysis type: ${hrPlan.intent}`,
533 |         `Chart type: ${hrPlan.chartType}`,
534 |         `Aggregation: ${hrPlan.aggregation || 'count'}`,
535 |       ],
536 |       schema: buildDatasetSchema(analyticsDataset),
537 |       aiProvider: 'hr-handler',
538 |       intent: hrPlan.intent,
539 |     };
540 |   }
541 | 
542 |   // Use smart query handler for better responses
543 |   try {
544 |     console.log('[analytics] Calling smart query handler for:', query);
545 |     const smartResponse = handleSmartQuery(dataset, query);
546 |     console.log('[analytics] Smart response:', JSON.stringify(smartResponse)?.substring(0, 100) || 'null/undefined');
547 |     if (smartResponse && typeof smartResponse === 'object' && 'content' in smartResponse && smartResponse.content) {
548 |       console.log('[analytics] Using smart query handler result');
549 |       return {
550 |         ...smartResponse,
551 |         schema: buildDatasetSchema(analyticsDataset),
552 |         aiProvider: 'smart-handler',
553 |       };

556 |     }

559 |   }

561 |   // Try to get AI-enhanced response first with Ollama + Mistral
562 |   try {
```
