### FILE: apps/backend/src/utils/helpers.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/utils/helpers.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Utility Helpers
  3 |  * Common helper functions
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | /**
  7 |  * Sleep for specified milliseconds
  8 |  * @param {number} ms - Milliseconds to sleep
  9 |  * @returns {Promise} Resolves after timeout
 10 |  */
 11 | export function sleep(ms) {
 12 |   return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
 13 | }
 14 | 
 15 | /**
 16 |  * Retry a function with exponential backoff
 17 |  * @param {Function} fn - Function to retry
 18 |  * @param {number} maxRetries - Maximum number of retries
 19 |  * @param {number} initialDelay - Initial delay in ms
 20 |  * @returns {Promise} Result of function
 21 |  */
 22 | export async function retry(fn, maxRetries = 3, initialDelay = 1000) {
 23 |   let lastError;
 24 |   
 25 |   for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
 26 |     try {
 27 |       return await fn();
 28 |     } catch (error) {
 29 |       lastError = error;
 30 |       if (attempt < maxRetries) {
 31 |         const delay = initialDelay * Math.pow(2, attempt);
 32 |         await sleep(delay);
 33 |       }
 34 |     }
 35 |   }
 36 |   
 37 |   throw lastError;
 38 | }
 39 | 
 40 | /**
 41 |  * Debounce function
 42 |  * @param {Function} fn - Function to debounce
 43 |  * @param {number} delay - Delay in ms
 44 |  * @returns {Function} Debounced function
 45 |  */
 46 | export function debounce(fn, delay) {
 47 |   let timeoutId;
 48 |   return (...args) => {
 49 |     clearTimeout(timeoutId);
 50 |     timeoutId = setTimeout(() => fn(...args), delay);
 51 |   };
 52 | }
 53 | 
 54 | /**
 55 |  * Throttle function
 56 |  * @param {Function} fn - Function to throttle
 57 |  * @param {number} limit - Limit in ms
 58 |  * @returns {Function} Throttled function
 59 |  */
 60 | export function throttle(fn, limit) {
 61 |   let inThrottle;
 62 |   return (...args) => {
 63 |     if (!inThrottle) {
 64 |       fn(...args);
 65 |       inThrottle = true;
 66 |       setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit);
 67 |     }
 68 |   };
 69 | }
 70 | 
 71 | /**
 72 |  * Deep clone an object
 73 |  * @param {any} obj - Object to clone
 74 |  * @returns {any} Cloned object
 75 |  */
 76 | export function deepClone(obj) {
 77 |   return JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj));
 78 | }
 79 | 
 80 | /**
 81 |  * Pick specific keys from object
 82 |  * @param {Object} obj - Source object
 83 |  * @param {string[]} keys - Keys to pick
 84 |  * @returns {Object} New object with picked keys
 85 |  */
 86 | export function pick(obj, keys) {
 87 |   return keys.reduce((acc, key) => {
 88 |     if (key in obj) {
 89 |       acc[key] = obj[key];
 90 |     }
 91 |     return acc;
 92 |   }, {});
 93 | }
 94 | 
 95 | /**
 96 |  * Omit specific keys from object
 97 |  * @param {Object} obj - Source object
 98 |  * @param {string[]} keys - Keys to omit
 99 |  * @returns {Object} New object without omitted keys
100 |  */
101 | export function omit(obj, keys) {
102 |   const result = { ...obj };
103 |   keys.forEach(key => delete result[key]);
104 |   return result;
105 | }
106 | 
107 | /**
108 |  * Check if object is empty
109 |  * @param {Object} obj - Object to check
110 |  * @returns {boolean} True if empty
111 |  */
112 | export function isEmpty(obj) {
113 |   if (obj == null) return true;
114 |   if (Array.isArray(obj) || typeof obj === 'string') return obj.length === 0;
115 |   return Object.keys(obj).length === 0;
116 | }
117 | 
118 | /**
119 |  * Generate random string
120 |  * @param {number} length - Length of string
121 |  * @returns {string} Random string
122 |  */
123 | export function randomString(length = 10) {
124 |   const chars = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789';
125 |   let result = '';
126 |   for (let i = 0; i < length; i++) {
127 |     result += chars.charAt(Math.floor(Math.random() * chars.length));
128 |   }
129 |   return result;
130 | }
131 | 
132 | /**
133 |  * Format bytes to human readable
134 |  * @param {number} bytes - Bytes to format
135 |  * @returns {string} Formatted string
136 |  */
137 | export function formatBytes(bytes) {
138 |   if (bytes === 0) return '0 Bytes';
139 |   const k = 1024;
140 |   const sizes = ['Bytes', 'KB', 'MB', 'GB'];
141 |   const i = Math.floor(Math.log(bytes) / Math.log(k));
142 |   return parseFloat((bytes / Math.pow(k, i)).toFixed(2)) + ' ' + sizes[i];
143 | }
144 | 
145 | /**
146 |  * Format duration in ms to human readable
147 |  * @param {number} ms - Duration in ms
148 |  * @returns {string} Formatted string
149 |  */
150 | export function formatDuration(ms) {
151 |   if (ms < 1000) return `${ms}ms`;
152 |   if (ms < 60000) return `${(ms / 1000).toFixed(1)}s`;
153 |   return `${(ms / 60000).toFixed(1)}m`;
154 | }
155 | 
156 | /**
157 |  * Parse query string to object
158 |  * @param {string} query - Query string
159 |  * @returns {Object} Parsed object
160 |  */
161 | export function parseQuery(query) {
162 |   if (!query) return {};
163 |   const params = new URLSearchParams(query);
164 |   const result = {};
165 |   for (const [key, value] of params) {
166 |     result[key] = value;
167 |   }
168 |   return result;
169 | }
170 | 
171 | /**
172 |  * Build query string from object
173 |  * @param {Object} params - Parameters object
174 |  * @returns {string} Query string
175 |  */
176 | export function buildQuery(params) {
177 |   const searchParams = new URLSearchParams();
178 |   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {
179 |     if (value !== undefined && value !== null) {
180 |       searchParams.append(key, String(value));
181 |     }
182 |   }
183 |   return searchParams.toString();
184 | }
185 | 
186 | /**
187 |  * Safe JSON parse
188 |  * @param {string} str - String to parse
189 |  * @param {any} defaultValue - Default value on error
190 |  * @returns {any} Parsed value or default
191 |  */
192 | export function safeJSONParse(str, defaultValue = null) {
193 |   try {
194 |     return JSON.parse(str);
195 |   } catch {
196 |     return defaultValue;
197 |   }
198 | }
199 | 
200 | /**
201 |  * Chunk array into smaller arrays
202 |  * @param {Array} array - Array to chunk
203 |  * @param {number} size - Chunk size
204 |  * @returns {Array[]} Chunked arrays
205 |  */
206 | export function chunk(array, size) {
207 |   const chunks = [];
208 |   for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i += size) {
209 |     chunks.push(array.slice(i, i + size));
210 |   }
211 |   return chunks;
212 | }
213 | 
214 | /**
215 |  * Unique array by key
216 |  * @param {Array} array - Array to dedupe
217 |  * @param {string} key - Key to check uniqueness
218 |  * @returns {Array} Deduplicated array
219 |  */
220 | export function uniqueBy(array, key) {
221 |   const seen = new Set();
222 |   return array.filter(item => {
223 |     const val = item[key];
224 |     if (seen.has(val)) return false;
225 |     seen.add(val);
226 |     return true;
227 |   });
228 | }
229 | 
230 | export default {
231 |   sleep,
232 |   retry,
233 |   debounce,
234 |   throttle,
235 |   deepClone,
236 |   pick,
237 |   omit,
238 |   isEmpty,
239 |   randomString,
240 |   formatBytes,
241 |   formatDuration,
242 |   parseQuery,
243 |   buildQuery,
244 |   safeJSONParse,
245 |   chunk,
246 |   uniqueBy,
247 | };
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/utils/logger.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/utils/logger.js`

```js
 1 | const LOG_LEVELS = {
 2 |   error: 0,
 3 |   warn: 1,
 4 |   info: 2,
 5 |   debug: 3,
 6 | };
 7 | 
 8 | const isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === "production";
 9 | 
10 | class Logger {
11 |   constructor() {
12 |     this.minLevel = isProduction ? "error" : "debug";
13 |   }
14 | 
15 |   shouldLog(level) {
16 |     return LOG_LEVELS[level] <= LOG_LEVELS[this.minLevel];
17 |   }
18 | 
19 |   formatEntry(level, message, context = {}) {
20 |     const entry = {
21 |       level,
22 |       message,
23 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
24 |       environment: process.env.NODE_ENV || "development",
25 |       ...context,
26 |     };
27 |     return JSON.stringify(entry);
28 |   }
29 | 
30 |   log(level, message, context = {}) {
31 |     if (!this.shouldLog(level)) return;
32 | 
33 |     const formatted = this.formatEntry(level, message, context);
34 | 
35 |     if (level === "error" || level === "warn") {
36 |       console.error(formatted);
37 |     } else {
38 |       console.log(formatted);
39 |     }
40 |   }
41 | 
42 |   error(message, context = {}) {
43 |     this.log("error", message, context);
44 |   }
45 | 
46 |   warn(message, context = {}) {
47 |     this.log("warn", message, context);
48 |   }
49 | 
50 |   info(message, context = {}) {
51 |     this.log("info", message, context);
52 |   }
53 | 
54 |   debug(message, context = {}) {
55 |     this.log("debug", message, context);
56 |   }
57 | 
58 |   logRequest(method, pathname, statusCode, duration, error = null) {
59 |     const context = {
60 |       request: { method, pathname },
61 |       response: { statusCode, duration },
62 |     };
63 | 
64 |     if (error) {
65 |       this.error(`Request failed: ${method} ${pathname}`, {
66 |         ...context,
67 |         error: { message: error.message, stack: error.stack },
68 |       });
69 |     } else if (statusCode >= 400) {
70 |       this.warn(`Request error: ${method} ${pathname}`, context);
71 |     } else {
72 |       this.info(`Request: ${method} ${pathname}`, context);
73 |     }
74 |   }
75 | }
76 | 
77 | const logger = new Logger();
78 | export default logger;
79 | export { Logger };
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/utils/response-utils.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/utils/response-utils.js`

```js
  1 | // Response utility functions
  2 | import { gzipSync } from 'node:zlib';
  3 | import { CONTENT_TYPES } from '../config/constants.js';
  4 | 
  5 | /**
  6 |  * Send JSON response
  7 |  */
  8 | export function sendJson(response, statusCode, payload) {
  9 |   const jsonString = JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2);
 10 |   const acceptsGzip = String(response.req?.headers?.['accept-encoding'] || '').includes('gzip');
 11 |   const shouldCompress = acceptsGzip && Buffer.byteLength(jsonString, 'utf8') > 1024;
 12 |   const body = shouldCompress ? gzipSync(jsonString) : jsonString;
 13 |   
 14 |   const headers = {
 15 |     'Content-Type': `${CONTENT_TYPES.JSON}; charset=utf-8`,
 16 |     'Content-Length': shouldCompress ? body.length : Buffer.byteLength(jsonString, 'utf8'),
 17 |     'Cache-Control': 'no-cache',
 18 |     'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff'
 19 |   };
 20 | 
 21 |   if (shouldCompress) {
 22 |     headers['Content-Encoding'] = 'gzip';
 23 |     headers.Vary = 'Accept-Encoding';
 24 |   }
 25 | 
 26 |   response.writeHead(statusCode, headers);
 27 |   
 28 |   response.end(body);
 29 | }
 30 | 
 31 | /**
 32 |  * Send success response
 33 |  */
 34 | export function sendSuccess(response, data = null, message = 'Success') {
 35 |   const payload = {
 36 |     success: true,
 37 |     data,
 38 |     message,
 39 |     timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
 40 |   };
 41 |   
 42 |   sendJson(response, 200, payload);
 43 | }
 44 | 
 45 | /**
 46 |  * Send created response (201)
 47 |  */
 48 | export function sendCreated(response, data = null, message = 'Created') {
 49 |   const payload = {
 50 |     success: true,
 51 |     data,
 52 |     message,
 53 |     timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
 54 |   };
 55 | 
 56 |   sendJson(response, 201, payload);
 57 | }
 58 | 
 59 | /**
 60 |  * Send error response
 61 |  */
 62 | export function sendError(response, statusCode, message, errorCode = null) {
 63 |   const payload = {
 64 |     success: false,
 65 |     error: {
 66 |       message,
 67 |       code: errorCode,
 68 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
 69 |     }
 70 |   };
 71 |   
 72 |   sendJson(response, statusCode, payload);
 73 | }
 74 | 
 75 | /**
 76 |  * Send paginated response
 77 |  */
 78 | export function sendPaginated(response, data, pagination, message = 'Success') {
 79 |   const payload = {
 80 |     success: true,
 81 |     data,
 82 |     pagination: {
 83 |       page: pagination.page || 1,
 84 |       limit: pagination.limit || 10,
 85 |       total: pagination.total || 0,
 86 |       totalPages: Math.ceil(pagination.total / (pagination.limit || 10)),
 87 |       hasNext: pagination.hasNext || false,
 88 |       hasPrev: pagination.hasPrev || false
 89 |     },
 90 |     message,
 91 |     timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
 92 |   };
 93 |   
 94 |   sendJson(response, 200, payload);
 95 | }
 96 | 
 97 | /**
 98 |  * Send file response
 99 |  */
100 | export function sendFile(response, filePath, contentType, fileName = null) {
101 |   const fs = require('fs');
102 |   const path = require('path');
103 |   
104 |   try {
105 |     const fileContent = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
106 |     const baseName = fileName || path.basename(filePath);
107 |     
108 |     response.writeHead(200, {
109 |       'Content-Type': contentType,
110 |       'Content-Length': fileContent.length,
111 |       'Content-Disposition': `attachment; filename="${baseName}"`,
112 |       'Cache-Control': 'no-cache'
113 |     });
114 |     
115 |     response.end(fileContent);
116 |   } catch (error) {
117 |     sendError(response, 500, 'File not found or cannot be read', 'FILE_ERROR');
118 |   }
119 | }
120 | 
121 | /**
122 |  * Send stream response
123 |  */
124 | export function sendStream(response, stream, contentType = CONTENT_TYPES.JSON) {
125 |   response.writeHead(200, {
126 |     'Content-Type': contentType,
127 |     'Transfer-Encoding': 'chunked',
128 |     'Cache-Control': 'no-cache'
129 |   });
130 |   
131 |   stream.pipe(response);
132 |   
133 |   stream.on('error', (error) => {
134 |     console.error('Stream error:', error);
135 |     if (!response.headersSent) {
136 |       sendError(response, 500, 'Stream error', 'STREAM_ERROR');
137 |     }
138 |   });
139 | }
140 | 
141 | /**
142 |  * Send SSE (Server-Sent Events) response
143 |  */
144 | export function sendSSE(response) {
145 |   response.writeHead(200, {
146 |     'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream',
147 |     'Cache-Control': 'no-cache',
148 |     'Connection': 'keep-alive',
149 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'
150 |   });
151 |   
152 |   return {
153 |     send: (data, event = null) => {
154 |       let message = '';
155 |       if (event) message += `event: ${event}\n`;
156 |       message += `data: ${JSON.stringify(data)}\n\n`;
157 |       response.write(message);
158 |     },
159 |     
160 |     close: () => {
161 |       response.end();
162 |     }
163 |   };
164 | }
165 | 
166 | /**
167 |  * Send redirect response
168 |  */
169 | export function sendRedirect(response, location, statusCode = 302) {
170 |   response.writeHead(statusCode, {
171 |     'Location': location,
172 |     'Content-Type': CONTENT_TYPES.TEXT
173 |   });
174 |   
175 |   response.end(`Redirecting to ${location}`);
176 | }
177 | 
178 | /**
179 |  * Send no content response
180 |  */
181 | export function sendNoContent(response, statusCode = 204) {
182 |   response.writeHead(statusCode, {
183 |     'Content-Length': '0'
184 |   });
185 |   
186 |   response.end();
187 | }
188 | 
189 | /**
190 |  * Send CORS preflight response
191 |  */
192 | export function sendPreflight(response) {
193 |   response.writeHead(204, {
194 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
195 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, PATCH',
196 |     'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type, Authorization, X-Requested-With',
197 |     'Access-Control-Max-Age': '86400'
198 |   });
199 |   
200 |   response.end();
201 | }
202 | 
203 | /**
204 |  * Send health check response
205 |  */
206 | export function sendHealth(response, status = 'healthy', details = {}) {
207 |   const payload = {
208 |     status,
209 |     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
210 |     uptime: process.uptime(),
211 |     version: process.env.npm_package_version || '1.0.0',
212 |     environment: process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development',
213 |     ...details
214 |   };
215 |   
216 |   const statusCode = status === 'healthy' ? 200 : 503;
217 |   sendJson(response, statusCode, payload);
218 | }
219 | 
220 | /**
221 |  * Send AI provider status response
222 |  */
223 | export function sendAIStatus(response, providers) {
224 |   const payload = {
225 |     success: true,
226 |     data: {
227 |       providers,
228 |       activeProvider: providers.find(p => p.available)?.name || null,
229 |       totalProviders: providers.length,
230 |       availableProviders: providers.filter(p => p.available).length,
231 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
232 |     }
233 |   };
234 |   
235 |   sendJson(response, 200, payload);
236 | }
237 | 
238 | /**
239 |  * Send validation error response
240 |  */
241 | export function sendValidationError(response, errors) {
242 |   const payload = {
243 |     success: false,
244 |     error: {
245 |       code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR',
246 |       message: 'Validation failed',
247 |       details: errors,
248 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
249 |     }
250 |   };
251 |   
252 |   sendJson(response, 400, payload);
253 | }
254 | 
255 | /**
256 |  * Send rate limit response
257 |  */
258 | export function sendRateLimit(response, retryAfter = null) {
259 |   const headers = {
260 |     'Content-Type': CONTENT_TYPES.JSON,
261 |     'X-RateLimit-Limit': '100',
262 |     'X-RateLimit-Remaining': '0',
263 |     'X-RateLimit-Reset': Math.ceil(Date.now() / 1000 + 3600).toString()
264 |   };
265 |   
266 |   if (retryAfter) {
267 |     headers['Retry-After'] = retryAfter.toString();
268 |   }
269 |   
270 |   const payload = {
271 |     success: false,
272 |     error: {
273 |       code: 'RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED',
274 |       message: 'Too many requests',
275 |       retryAfter: retryAfter || 3600,
276 |       timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
277 |     }
278 |   };
279 |   
280 |   response.writeHead(429, headers);
281 |   response.end(JSON.stringify(payload));
282 | }
283 | 
284 | export default {
285 |   sendJson,
286 |   sendSuccess,
287 |   sendCreated,
288 |   sendError,
289 |   sendPaginated,
290 |   sendFile,
291 |   sendStream,
292 |   sendSSE,
293 |   sendRedirect,
294 |   sendNoContent,
295 |   sendPreflight,
296 |   sendHealth,
297 |   sendAIStatus,
298 |   sendValidationError,
299 |   sendRateLimit
300 | };
301 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/src/utils/schema-extractor.js

Source path: `apps/backend/src/utils/schema-extractor.js`

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Schema extraction utilities for AI interactions
  3 |  * Ensures only schema information (not actual data) is sent to AI services
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | /**
  7 |  * Extract schema information for AI analysis
  8 |  */
  9 | export const extractSchemaForAI = (dataset, schema) => {
 10 |   // Classify columns properly
 11 |   const columns = (schema.columns || []).map(col => {
 12 |     const name = col.name || '';
 13 |     const type = col.type || 'string';
 14 |     
 15 |     let role = col.role || 'dimension';
 16 |     
 17 |     // Better classification for common column patterns
 18 |     if (type === 'number' || type === 'integer') {
 19 |       const lowerName = name.toLowerCase();
 20 |       if (lowerName.includes('salary') || 
 21 |           lowerName.includes('experience') ||
 22 |           lowerName.includes('revenue') ||
 23 |           lowerName.includes('amount') ||
 24 |           lowerName.includes('count') ||
 25 |           lowerName.includes('bonus') ||
 26 |           lowerName.includes('pay') ||
 27 |           lowerName.includes('cost') ||
 28 |           lowerName.includes('age')) {
 29 |         role = 'metric';
 30 |       } else if (lowerName.includes('id') || 
 31 |                  lowerName.includes('row') || 
 32 |                  lowerName.includes('index') ||
 33 |                  lowerName.includes('_id')) {
 34 |         role = 'excluded';
 35 |       }
 36 |     }
 37 |     
 38 |     return {
 39 |       name: col.name,
 40 |       type: col.type,
 41 |       role: role,
 42 |       sampleValues: col.sample ? Array.isArray(col.sample) ? col.sample.slice(0, 3) : [col.sample] : []
 43 |     };
 44 |   });
 45 | 
 46 |   return {
 47 |     dataset: {
 48 |       name: dataset.name,
 49 |       rowCount: dataset.rowCount || dataset.rows?.length || 0,
 50 |       columnCount: columns.length,
 51 |     },
 52 |     schema: {
 53 |       columns: columns,
 54 |       primaryDimension: columns.find(col => col.role === 'dimension'),
 55 |       secondaryDimension: columns.filter(col => col.role === 'dimension')[1] || null,
 56 |       primaryMetric: columns.find(col => col.role === 'metric'),
 57 |       secondaryMetric: columns.filter(col => col.role === 'metric')[1] || null,
 58 |     },
 59 |     dataSummary: {
 60 |       totalRows: dataset.rowCount || dataset.rows?.length || 0,
 61 |       totalColumns: columns.length,
 62 |       metrics: columns.filter(col => col.role === 'metric').map(c => c.name),
 63 |       dimensions: columns.filter(col => col.role === 'dimension').map(c => c.name),
 64 |       excluded: columns.filter(col => col.role === 'excluded').map(c => c.name),
 65 |     }
 66 |   };
 67 | };
 68 | 
 69 | export const sanitizeQueryContext = (context) => {
 70 |   // Remove any actual data rows from context
 71 |   const sanitized = { ...context };
 72 |   
 73 |   if (sanitized.dataset && sanitized.dataset.rows) {
 74 |     delete sanitized.dataset.rows;
 75 |   }
 76 |   
 77 |   if (sanitized.analyticsDataset && sanitized.analyticsDataset.rows) {
 78 |     delete sanitized.analyticsDataset.rows;
 79 |   }
 80 |   
 81 |   // Ensure we only have schema information
 82 |   if (sanitized.schema && sanitized.dataset) {
 83 |     sanitized.schemaOnly = extractSchemaForAI(sanitized.dataset, sanitized.schema);
 84 |   }
 85 |   
 86 |   return sanitized;
 87 | };
 88 | 
 89 | /**
 90 |  * Build schema-only prompt with better guidance for HR data
 91 |  */
 92 | export const buildSchemaOnlyPrompt = (query, schemaInfo) => {
 93 |   const { dataset, schema, dataSummary } = schemaInfo;
 94 |   
 95 |   const metrics = dataSummary.metrics || [];
 96 |   const dimensions = dataSummary.dimensions || [];
 97 |   const excluded = dataSummary.excluded || [];
 98 |   
 99 |   let prompt = `You are analyzing a dataset using SCHEMA-ONLY information. You do NOT have access to actual data values.
100 | 
101 | Dataset Information:
102 | - Name: ${dataset.name}
103 | - Total Rows: ${dataset.rowCount || 0}
104 | - Total Columns: ${dataset.columnCount || 0}
105 | 
106 | SCHEMA CLASSIFICATION:
107 | `;
108 |   
109 |   if (metrics.length > 0) {
110 |     prompt += `\nMETRIC COLUMNS (use for aggregation - SUM, AVG, COUNT, MIN, MAX):
111 | ${metrics.map(m => `  - ${m}`).join('\n')}`;
112 |   }
113 |   
114 |   if (dimensions.length > 0) {
115 |     prompt += `\nDIMENSION COLUMNS (use for grouping - GROUP BY):
116 | ${dimensions.map(d => `  - ${d}`).join('\n')}`;
117 |   }
118 |   
119 |   if (excluded.length > 0) {
120 |     prompt += `\nEXCLUDED COLUMNS (do NOT use in charts or queries):
121 | ${excluded.map(e => `  - ${e}`).join('\n')}`;
122 |   }
123 | 
124 |   prompt += `
125 | 
126 | COMMON ANALYSIS PATTERNS:
127 | - "Salary by country" → SELECT country, AVG(salary_usd) FROM data GROUP BY country
128 | - "Top experience levels" → SELECT country, MAX(experience) FROM data GROUP BY country
129 | - "Education distribution" → SELECT education, COUNT(*) FROM data GROUP BY education
130 | 
131 | CHART TYPE GUIDANCE:
132 | - bar: comparing values across categories (salary by country)
133 | - line: trends over time or sequence (if time/date column exists)
134 | - pie: proportions of a whole (education breakdown)
135 | - scatter: correlation between two numeric values (experience vs salary)
136 | 
137 | IMPORTANT RULES:
138 | 1. NEVER use excluded columns (id, row_id, index) in queries or charts
139 | 2. Only reference columns that exist in the schema above
140 | 3. Numeric columns are for metrics, string columns are for dimensions
141 | 4. Always match column names exactly (case-sensitive)
142 | 
143 | User Query: ${query}
144 | 
145 | Provide a helpful response with appropriate SQL query and chart suggestion.`;
146 | 
147 |   return prompt;
148 | };
149 | 
150 | export const validateSchemaOnlyContext = (context) => {
151 |   // Ensure no actual data is being sent to AI
152 |   const forbiddenKeys = ['rows', 'data', 'actualData', 'realData'];
153 |   const violations = [];
154 |   
155 |   const checkObject = (obj, path = '') => {
156 |     if (typeof obj !== 'object' || obj === null) return;
157 |     
158 |     for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(obj)) {
159 |       const currentPath = path ? `${path}.${key}` : key;
160 |       
161 |       if (forbiddenKeys.includes(key.toLowerCase())) {
162 |         violations.push(`Found potential data at path: ${currentPath}`);
163 |       }
164 |       
165 |       if (Array.isArray(value) && key.toLowerCase().includes('row')) {
166 |         violations.push(`Found potential row data at path: ${currentPath}`);
167 |       }
168 |       
169 |       if (typeof value === 'object' && value !== null) {
170 |         checkObject(value, currentPath);
171 |       }
172 |     }
173 |   };
174 |   
175 |   checkObject(context);
176 |   
177 |   return {
178 |     isValid: violations.length === 0,
179 |     violations,
180 |   };
181 | };
182 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/STRUCTURE_SUMMARY.md

Source path: `apps/backend/STRUCTURE_SUMMARY.md`

```md
  1 | # 🎯 InsightFlow Backend - New Optimized Folder Structure
  2 | 
  3 | ## 📊 Architecture Overview
  4 | 
  5 | The backend has been reorganized into a **domain-driven, modular architecture** that separates concerns clearly and enables easy scaling.
  6 | 
  7 | ## 🏗️ Structure
  8 | 
  9 | ```
 10 | apps/backend/
 11 | ├── src/
 12 | │   ├── config/              # Configuration Layer
 13 | │   │   ├── environment.js   # Environment variables & validation
 14 | │   │   ├── constants.js     # Application constants
 15 | │   │   └── gemini-config.js # Gemini-specific config
 16 | │   │
 17 | │   ├── core/                # Server Core
 18 | │   │   └── server.js        # HTTP server setup & lifecycle
 19 | │   │
 20 | │   ├── database/            # Data Persistence Layer
 21 | │   │   └── dataset-repository.js
 22 | │   │
 23 | │   ├── services/            # Business Logic Layer
 24 | │   │   ├── ai/              # AI & LLM Integration
 25 | │   │   │   ├── ai-manager.js           # Master orchestrator
 26 | │   │   │   ├── providers/              # AI Provider implementations
 27 | │   │   │   │   ├── ollama-provider.js  # Llama 3.2 & NeuralChat 7B
 28 | │   │   │   │   ├── gemini-provider.js  # Google Gemini
 29 | │   │   │   │   ├── openai-provider.js  # OpenAI GPT-4
 30 | │   │   │   │   └── anthropic-provider.js # Anthropic Claude
 31 | │   │   │   └── utils/                 # AI utilities
 32 | │   │   │       ├── prompt-templates.js
 33 | │   │   │       ├── response-parser.js
 34 | │   │   │       └── token-counter.js
 35 | │   │   │
 36 | │   │   ├── analytics/       # Data Analysis & Insights
 37 | │   │   │   ├── analytics-service.js
 38 | │   │   │   ├── schema-detector.js
 39 | │   │   │   ├── predictive-analytics.js
 40 | │   │   │   ├── schema-ai-service.js
 41 | │   │   │   └── schema-packet-builder.js
 42 | │   │   │
 43 | │   │   ├── data/            # Data Processing & ETL
 44 | │   │   │   ├── data-merger.js
 45 | │   │   │   ├── data-sampling-service.js
 46 | │   │   │   └── data-visualization-service.js
 47 | │   │   │
 48 | │   │   ├── export/          # Report & Export Generation
 49 | │   │   │   ├── export-service.js
 50 | │   │   │   └── report-generator.js
 51 | │   │   │
 52 | │   │   ├── query/           # Query Processing
 53 | │   │   │   ├── query-cache.js
 54 | │   │   │   └── smart-query-handler.js
 55 | │   │   │
 56 | │   │   ├── recommendation/  # Recommendation Engine
 57 | │   │   │   └── recommendation-engine.js
 58 | │   │   │
 59 | │   │   └── [other services]
 60 | │   │
 61 | │   ├── routes/              # API Route Handlers
 62 | │   │   ├── index.js         # Route aggregator
 63 | │   │   ├── ai.js            # AI endpoints
 64 | │   │   ├── health.js        # Health check
 65 | │   │   ├── datasets.js      # Dataset CRUD
 66 | │   │   ├── chat.js          # Chat endpoints
 67 | │   │   ├── analytics.js     # Analytics endpoints
 68 | │   │   └── export.js        # Export endpoints
 69 | │   │
 70 | │   ├── middleware/          # Request Processing Pipeline
 71 | │   │   ├── error-handler.js
 72 | │   │   ├── cors.js
 73 | │   │   ├── request-logger.js
 74 | │   │   ├── logger.middleware.js
 75 | │   │   ├── validation.middleware.js
 76 | │   │   └── error.middleware.js
 77 | │   │
 78 | │   ├── utils/              # Shared Utilities
 79 | │   │   ├── response-utils.js
 80 | │   │   ├── helpers.js
 81 | │   │   ├── logger.js
 82 | │   │   └── schema-extractor.js
 83 | │   │
 84 | │   ├── genai/              # GenAI Services (TypeScript)
 85 | │   │   ├── analyticsEngine.ts
 86 | │   │   ├── dashboardBuilder.ts
 87 | │   │   └── reportGenerator.ts
 88 | │   │
 89 | │   ├── types/              # Type Definitions
 90 | │   │
 91 | │   └── index.js            # Application Entry Point
 92 | │
 93 | ├── tests/                  # Test Suite
 94 | │   ├── unit/
 95 | │   └── integration/
 96 | │
 97 | ├── .env                    # Environment configuration
 98 | ├── .env.example            # Example env file
 99 | └── package.json            # Dependencies & scripts
100 | ```
101 | 
102 | ## 🎯 Key Components
103 | 
104 | ### 1. Configuration Layer (`src/config/`)
105 | 
106 | **Purpose**: Centralized configuration management
107 | 
108 | **Key Files**:
109 | - `environment.js` - Loads and validates environment variables
110 | - `constants.js` - Application-wide constants (HTTP status, error codes, etc.)
111 | - `gemini-config.js` - Gemini-specific configuration
112 | 
113 | **Usage**:
114 | ```javascript
115 | import config from './config/environment.js';
116 | 
117 | console.log(config.server.port); // 3001
118 | console.log(config.ollama.baseUrl); // http://localhost:11434
119 | ```
120 | 
121 | ### 2. Core Server (`src/core/`)
122 | 
123 | **Purpose**: HTTP server creation and lifecycle management
124 | 
125 | **Key Features**:
126 | - Server creation and configuration
127 | - Graceful shutdown handling
128 | - Error handling middleware setup
129 | - Route setup
130 | 
131 | **Usage**:
132 | ```javascript
133 | import { createHttpServer, startServer } from './core/server.js';
134 | 
135 | const server = createHttpServer();
136 | await startServer(server, 3001);
137 | ```
138 | 
139 | ### 3. AI Services (`src/services/ai/`)
140 | 
141 | **Purpose**: AI provider abstraction and orchestration
142 | 
143 | **Architecture**:
144 | ```
145 | ai/
146 | ├── ai-manager.js          # Master orchestrator
147 | ├── providers/             # Provider implementations
148 | │   ├── ollama-provider.js # Local LLM (Llama 3.2, NeuralChat 7B)
149 | │   ├── gemini-provider.js # Google Gemini
150 | │   ├── openai-provider.js # OpenAI GPT-4
151 | │   └── anthropic-provider.js # Anthropic Claude
152 | └── utils/                 # Helper utilities
153 |     ├── prompt-templates.js
154 |     ├── response-parser.js
155 |     └── token-counter.js
156 | ```
157 | 
158 | **Key Features**:
159 | - **Automatic Provider Selection**: Chooses best available provider
160 | - **Fallback Chain**: Ollama → Gemini → OpenAI → Anthropic
161 | - **Health Monitoring**: Real-time provider status
162 | - **Unified Interface**: Same API across all providers
163 | 
164 | **Usage**:
165 | ```javascript
166 | import { aiManager } from './services/ai/ai-manager.js';
167 | 
168 | // Generate response
169 | const response = await aiManager.generateResponse('Analyze this data...');
170 | 
171 | // Chat
172 | const chatResponse = await aiManager.chat([
173 |   { role: 'user', content: 'Hello!' }
174 | ]);
175 | 
176 | // Get health status
177 | const health = await aiManager.health();
178 | ```
179 | 
180 | ### 4. Analytics Services (`src/services/analytics/`)
181 | 
182 | **Purpose**: Data analysis and insights generation
183 | 
184 | **Key Services**:
185 | - `analytics-service.js` - Statistical analysis
186 | - `schema-detector.js` - Smart schema detection
187 | - `predictive-analytics.js` - Forecasting and predictions
188 | - `schema-ai-service.js` - AI-powered schema analysis
189 | 
190 | ### 5. Data Services (`src/services/data/`)
191 | 
192 | **Purpose**: Data processing and transformation
193 | 
194 | **Key Services**:
195 | - `data-merger.js` - Multi-dataset merging
196 | - `data-sampling-service.js` - Data sampling strategies
197 | - `data-visualization-service.js` - Visualization helpers
198 | 
199 | ### 6. Export Services (`src/services/export/`)
200 | 
201 | **Purpose**: Report and export generation
202 | 
203 | **Key Services**:
204 | - `export-service.js` - Export orchestration
205 | - `report-generator.js` - PDF/Excel/Markdown report generation
206 | 
207 | ### 7. Routes (`src/routes/`)
208 | 
209 | **Purpose**: API endpoint handlers
210 | 
211 | **Key Routes**:
212 | - `ai.js` - `/api/ai/*` endpoints
213 | - `health.js` - `/api/health` endpoint
214 | - `datasets.js` - `/api/datasets/*` endpoints
215 | - `chat.js` - `/api/datasets/:id/chat` endpoint
216 | - `analytics.js` - `/api/datasets/:id/analyze` endpoint
217 | - `export.js` - `/api/datasets/:id/export` endpoint
218 | 
219 | ### 8. Middleware (`src/middleware/`)
220 | 
221 | **Purpose**: Request/response processing pipeline
222 | 
223 | **Key Middleware**:
224 | - `error-handler.js` - Global error handling
225 | - `cors.js` - CORS configuration
226 | - `request-logger.js` - Request logging
227 | - `validation.middleware.js` - Input validation
228 | 
229 | ## 🔄 AI Provider Architecture
230 | 
231 | ### Priority Order
232 | 1. **Ollama** (Primary) - Local inference, no API keys, privacy-focused
233 | 2. **Gemini** (Fallback 1) - Google's LLM, good performance
234 | 3. **OpenAI** (Fallback 2) - GPT-4, industry standard
235 | 4. **Anthropic** (Fallback 3) - Claude, excellent reasoning
236 | 
237 | ### Fallback Flow
238 | ```
239 | Request → Ollama
240 |            ↓ (failed)
241 |          Gemini
242 |            ↓ (failed)
243 |          OpenAI
244 |            ↓ (failed)
245 |          Anthropic
246 |            ↓ (failed)
247 |          Error: All providers failed
248 | ```
249 | 
250 | ### Provider Interface
251 | All providers implement the same interface:
252 | 
253 | ```javascript
254 | class AIProvider {
255 |   async initialize() { /* ... */ }
256 |   async generate(prompt, options) { /* ... */ }
257 |   async chat(messages, options) { /* ... */ }
258 |   async health() { /* ... */ }
259 |   async isAvailable() { /* ... */ }
260 |   getCapabilities() { /* ... */ }
261 | }
262 | ```
263 | 
264 | ## 📡 API Endpoints
265 | 
266 | ### Health & Status
267 | ```
268 | GET  /                           # Root endpoint
269 | GET  /api/health                 # Health check
270 | GET  /api/ai/status              # AI provider status
271 | GET  /api/ai/models              # Available models
272 | POST /api/ai/test                # Test AI generation
273 | ```
274 | 
275 | ### Dataset Operations
276 | ```
277 | POST /api/datasets/import         # Import dataset
278 | GET  /api/datasets/:id            # Get dataset info
279 | POST /api/datasets/:id/chat       # Chat with dataset
280 | GET  /api/datasets/:id/analyze    # AI analysis
281 | GET  /api/datasets/:id/schema     # Get schema
282 | GET  /api/datasets/:id/auto-charts # Auto-generate charts
283 | ```
284 | 
285 | ### Analytics
286 | ```
287 | GET  /api/analytics/:id/correlations  # Find correlations
288 | POST /api/analytics/:id/predict       # Predictive analysis
289 | ```
290 | 
291 | ### Export
292 | ```
293 | GET  /api/export/:id/pdf          # Export as PDF
294 | GET  /api/export/:id/excel        # Export as Excel
295 | GET  /api/export/:id/markdown     # Export as Markdown
296 | ```
297 | 
298 | ## ⚙️ Configuration
299 | 
300 | ### Environment Variables
301 | 
302 | ```env
303 | # Server
304 | PORT=3001
305 | NODE_ENV=development
306 | HOST=localhost
307 | 
308 | # Ollama (Primary - Local LLM)
309 | OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
310 | OLLAMA_ENABLED=true
311 | OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2
312 | OLLAMA_CHAT_MODEL=neural-chat:7b
313 | OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS=120000
314 | OLLAMA_MAX_TOKENS=[REDACTED_SECRET]
315 | OLLAMA_TEMPERATURE=0.7
316 | 
317 | # Google Gemini (Fallback 1)
318 | GOOGLE_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
319 | 
320 | # OpenAI (Fallback 2)
321 | OPENAI_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
322 | 
323 | # Anthropic (Fallback 3)
324 | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
325 | 
326 | # AI Settings
327 | AI_PROVIDER_PRIORITY=ollama,gemini,openai,anthropic
328 | ENABLE_AI_FALLBACK=true
329 | LOCAL_AI_ONLY=false
330 | AI_TIMEOUT_MS=120000
331 | AI_MAX_TOKENS=[REDACTED_SECRET]
332 | AI_TEMPERATURE=0.7
333 | 
334 | # Database
335 | DATABASE_PATH=./data/insightflow.db
336 | DATA_DIR=./data
337 | 
338 | # CORS
339 | CORS_ORIGIN=*
340 | CORS_CREDENTIALS=false
341 | 
342 | # Logging
343 | LOG_LEVEL=info
344 | VERBOSE_LOGGING=true
345 | 
346 | # Features
347 | LOCAL_NLP_ENABLED=true
348 | AUTO_CHART_GENERATION=true
349 | CORRELATION_ANALYSIS=true
350 | OUTLIER_DETECTION=true
351 | CHAT_HISTORY_ENABLED=true
352 | SMARTCHART_ENABLED=true
353 | ```
354 | 
355 | ## 🚀 Getting Started
356 | 
357 | ### 1. Install Dependencies
358 | ```bash
359 | cd apps/backend
360 | npm install
361 | ```
362 | 
363 | ### 2. Configure Environment
364 | ```bash
365 | cp .env.example .env
366 | # Edit .env with your settings
367 | ```
368 | 
369 | ### 3. Start Ollama (in separate terminal)
370 | ```bash
371 | ollama serve
372 | ollama pull llama3.2
373 | ollama pull neural-chat:7b
374 | ```
375 | 
376 | ### 4. Start Backend
377 | ```bash
378 | npm run dev
379 | ```
380 | 
381 | ### 5. Test
382 | ```bash
383 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/health
384 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/ai/status
385 | ```
386 | 
387 | ## 📈 Benefits
388 | 
389 | | Aspect | Benefit |
390 | |--------|---------|
391 | | **Scalability** | Easy to add new providers or services |
392 | | **Maintainability** | Clear file hierarchy, easy to find code |
393 | | **Testability** | Modular structure enables unit testing |
394 | | **Performance** | Better tree-shaking in production builds |
395 | | **Debugging** | Clear stack traces and error context |
396 | | **Onboarding** | New developers can understand structure quickly |
397 | | **Flexibility** | Easy to swap implementations (e.g., AI providers) |
398 | | **Encapsulation** | Services are self-contained |
399 | 
400 | ## 🎓 Adding New Features
401 | 
402 | ### Add a New AI Provider
403 | 
404 | 1. Create `src/services/ai/providers/newprovider-provider.js`
405 | 2. Implement the provider interface
406 | 3. Add to `ai-manager.js` provider list
407 | 4. Update `.env` with config
408 | 5. Done! Automatic fallback support
409 | 
410 | ### Add a New Service
411 | 
412 | 1. Create in appropriate `src/services/` subdirectory
413 | 2. Follow existing patterns
414 | 3. Export as default or named export
415 | 4. Import in routes as needed
416 | 
417 | ### Add a New Route
418 | 
419 | 1. Create handler in `src/routes/`
420 | 2. Import in `src/routes/index.js`
421 | 3. Add to route setup function
422 | 
423 | ## ✅ Status
424 | 
425 | - ✅ Configuration Layer: **COMPLETE**
426 | - ✅ Core Server: **COMPLETE**
427 | - ✅ AI Services: **COMPLETE**
428 | - ✅ Routes: **COMPLETE**
429 | - ✅ Middleware: **COMPLETE**
430 | - ✅ Utilities: **COMPLETE**
431 | - 🟡 Service Migration: **IN PROGRESS**
432 | 
433 | ## 📝 Next Steps
434 | 
435 | 1. Move remaining services to proper directories
436 | 2. Update import statements in moved files
437 | 3. Remove old/duplicate files
438 | 4. Add unit tests
439 | 5. Add integration tests
440 | 6. Set up CI/CD pipeline
441 | 
442 | ## 📞 Support
443 | 
444 | If you encounter issues:
445 | 
446 | 1. Check `MIGRATION_GUIDE.md` for detailed instructions
447 | 2. Verify your `.env` configuration
448 | 3. Check logs for specific error messages
449 | 4. Test individual components in isolation
450 | 
451 | ---
452 | 
453 | **Last Updated**: 2026-05-09
454 | **Version**: 2.0.0
455 | **Status**: Production Ready (after migration completion)
456 | 
```

### FILE: apps/backend/test-gemini.js

Source path: `apps/backend/test-gemini.js`

```js
 1 | import path from "node:path";
 2 | import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
 3 | import dotenv from "dotenv";
 4 | import { GoogleGenerativeAI } from "@google/generative-ai";
 5 | 
 6 | const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
 7 | const __dirname = path.dirname(__filename);
 8 | dotenv.config({ path: path.resolve(__dirname, "../../.env") });
 9 | 
10 | const apiKey = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY;
11 | 
12 | if (!apiKey) {
13 |   console.error("❌ GEMINI_API_KEY not set");
14 |   process.exit(1);
15 | }
16 | 
17 | console.log(`✅ API Key found: ${apiKey.slice(0, 10)}...`);
18 | 
19 | const genAI = new GoogleGenerativeAI(apiKey);
20 | const model = genAI.getGenerativeModel({
21 |   model: "gemini-flash-latest",
22 |   generationConfig: {
23 |     maxOutputTokens: [REDACTED_SECRET],
24 |   },
25 | });
26 | 
27 | try {
28 |   console.log("[test] Calling Gemini API...");
29 |   const response = await model.generateContent("What is 2+2?");
30 |   console.log("[test] ✅ Response received:");
31 |   console.log(response.response.text());
32 | } catch (error) {
33 |   console.error("[test] ❌ Error:", error.message);
34 |   process.exit(1);
35 | }
36 | 
```

### FILE: apps/frontend/README.md

Source path: `apps/frontend/README.md`

```md
 1 | # Frontend Structure
 2 | 
 3 | Path: `apps/frontend`
 4 | 
 5 | Local development runs on port `5173`. API requests should use relative `/api/*`
 6 | paths so Vite can proxy them to the backend on port `3001`.
 7 | 
 8 | ## Key Folders
 9 | 
10 | ```text
11 | public/                       Static assets
12 | src/
13 |   app/                        App bootstrap, router, providers
14 |   features/                   Dashboard, chat, data flows
15 |   shared/components/          Shared UI primitives and navigation
16 |   shared/hooks/               Reusable hooks
17 |   shared/layout/              App shell layout
18 |   shared/lib/                 Shared utility helpers
19 |   test/                       Vitest tests and setup
20 | ```
21 | 
22 | ## Placement Rules
23 | 
24 | - Put route wiring and providers in `src/app`.
25 | - Put domain-specific screens and logic in `src/features`.
26 | - Put reusable primitives and helpers in `src/shared`.
27 | 
```

---

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/__tests__/ChatInterfaceCommands.test.ts

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/__tests__/ChatInterfaceCommands.test.ts`

```ts
 1 | import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
 2 | import { localCommand } from "@/features/chat/components/ChatInterface";
 3 | 
 4 | const rows = [
 5 |   { country: "India", salary_usd: 50000, experience: 2 },
 6 |   { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000, experience: 5 },
 7 |   { country: "India", salary_usd: 65000, experience: 3 },
 8 |   { country: "Canada", salary_usd: 120000, experience: 8 },
 9 | ];
10 | 
11 | describe("ChatInterface local command options", () => {
12 |   it("turns a bare numeric threshold into a useful metric answer", () => {
13 |     const command = localCommand("75000", rows);
14 | 
15 |     expect(command).toMatchObject({
16 |       action: "ANSWER",
17 |       schemaOnly: true,
18 |     });
19 |     expect(command?.message).toContain("2 rows");
20 |     expect(command?.message).toContain("Salary Usd");
21 |     expect(command?.message).toContain("75,000");
22 |   });
23 | 
24 |   it("maps generic chart options to a schema-aware chart", () => {
25 |     const command = localCommand("Generate Chart", rows);
26 | 
27 |     expect(command).toMatchObject({
28 |       action: "GENERATE_CHART",
29 |       chartSpec: {
30 |         type: "bar",
31 |         xKey: "country",
32 |         yKey: "salary_usd",
33 |         aggregation: "avg",
34 |       },
35 |     });
36 |   });
37 | 
38 |   it("answers data quality option prompts locally", () => {
39 |     const command = localCommand("Explain data quality", rows);
40 | 
41 |     expect(command).toMatchObject({
42 |       action: "ANSWER",
43 |       schemaOnly: true,
44 |     });
45 |     expect(command?.message).toContain("Data quality");
46 |     expect(command?.message).toContain("Completeness");
47 |   });
48 | });
49 | 
```

 2 | import { describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
 3 | import UploadPage from "@/features/data/pages/UploadPage";
 4 | 
 5 | const uploadFiles = vi.fn(async () => undefined);
 6 | const navigate = vi.fn();
 7 | 
 8 | vi.mock("react-router-dom", async () => {
 9 |   const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import("react-router-dom")>("react-router-dom");
10 |   return { ...actual, useNavigate: () => navigate };
11 | });
12 |

 1 | import { fireEvent, render, screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react";
 2 | import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
 3 | import SchemaDashboardChat from "@/features/dashboard/components/SchemaDashboardChat";
 4 | import { api } from "@/features/data/api/dataApi";
 5 | 
 6 | vi.mock("@/features/data/api/dataApi", () => ({
 7 |   api: {
 8 |     sendDashboardCommand: vi.fn(async () => ({
 9 |       action: "GENERATE_CHART",
10 |       message: "Chart generated",
11 |       schemaOnly: true,
12 |       provider: "local-rules",
13 |       chartSpec: {
14 |         type: "bar",
15 |         title: "Average Salary by Country",
16 |         xKey: "country",
17 |         yKey: "salary_usd",
18 |         aggregation: "avg",
19 |       },
20 |     })),
21 |   },
22 | }));
23 | 
24 | const dataset = {
25 |   rows: [
26 |     { country: "India", salary_usd: 50000, experience: 2 },
27 |     { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000, experience: 5 },
28 |   ],
29 |   columns: ["country", "salary_usd", "experience"],
30 | };
31 | 
32 | describe("SchemaDashboardChat", () => {
33 |   beforeEach(() => {
34 |     vi.clearAllMocks();
35 |   });
36 | 
37 |   it("renders schema-only/provider badges and dynamic suggestions", () => {
38 |     render(<SchemaDashboardChat dataset={dataset} onCommand={vi.fn()} />);
39 | 
40 |     expect(screen.getByText("Schema-only")).toBeInTheDocument();
41 |     expect(screen.getByText(/Provider:/)).toBeInTheDocument();
42 |     expect(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /Show average .* by .*/i })).toBeInTheDocument();
43 |   });
44 | 
45 |   it("sends suggestion and manual input commands", async () => {
46 |     const onCommand = vi.fn();
47 |     const onSend = vi.fn();
48 |     render(<SchemaDashboardChat dataset={dataset} onCommand={onCommand} onSend={onSend} />);
49 | 
50 |     fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /Show average .* by .*/i }));
51 |     await waitFor(() => expect(onCommand).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1));
52 |     expect(onSend).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.stringMatching(/salary/i));
53 |     expect(screen.getByText(/Chart generated/)).toBeInTheDocument();
54 |     expect(screen.getByText("Provider: local-rules")).toBeInTheDocument();
55 | 
56 |     fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText(/Ask:/), {
57 |       target: { value: "add KPI for highest salary_usd" },
58 |     });
59 |     fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /send dashboard command/i }));
60 |     await waitFor(() => expect(onCommand).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2));
61 |   });
62 | 
63 |   it("shows a loading state while command is in flight", async () => {
64 |     vi.mocked(api.sendDashboardCommand).mockImplementationOnce(async () => {
65 |       await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 50));
66 |       return {
67 |         action: "ANSWER",
68 |         message: "Chart generated",
69 |         schemaOnly: true,
70 |         provider: "local-rules",
71 |       } as any;
72 |     });
73 |     render(<SchemaDashboardChat dataset={dataset} onCommand={vi.fn()} />);
74 | 
75 |     fireEvent.change(screen.getByPlaceholderText(/Ask:/), {
76 |       target: { value: "show chart" },
77 |     });
78 |     fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: /send dashboard command/i }));
79 | 
80 |     expect(document.querySelector(".animate-spin")).toBeTruthy();
81 |     await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText(/Chart generated/)).toBeInTheDocument());
82 |   });
83 | });

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/__tests__/schemaLocalAnalytics.test.ts

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/__tests__/schemaLocalAnalytics.test.ts`

```ts
 1 | import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
 2 | import {
 3 |   applyDashboardFilters,
 4 |   calculateChart,
 5 |   calculateKpi,
 6 | } from "@/features/dashboard/utils/schemaLocalAnalytics";
 7 | 
 8 | const rows = [
 9 |   { country: "India", salary_usd: 50000, experience: 2 },
10 |   { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000, experience: 5 },
11 |   { country: "India", salary_usd: 65000, experience: 3 },
12 |   { country: "", salary_usd: null, experience: "" },
13 | ];
14 | 
15 | describe("schema local analytics", () => {
16 |   it("calculates KPI aggregations locally", () => {
17 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Rows", metric: "__row_count__", aggregation: "count" }).value).toBe(4);
18 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Countries", metric: "country", aggregation: "count_unique" }).value).toBe(2);
19 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Salary Sum", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "sum" }).value).toBe(205000);
20 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Salary Avg", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg" }).value).toBeCloseTo(68333.33, 1);
21 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Min", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "min" }).value).toBe(50000);
22 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Max", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "max" }).value).toBe(90000);
23 |     expect(calculateKpi(rows, { title: "Median", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "median" }).value).toBe(65000);
24 |   });
25 | 
26 |   it("groups, histograms, scatters, and recomputes after filters", () => {
27 |     const indiaRows = applyDashboardFilters(rows, [{ key: "country", value: "India" }]);
28 |     const indiaAverage = calculateKpi(indiaRows, { title: "India Avg", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg" });
29 |     const countryDistribution = calculateChart(rows, {
30 |       title: "Countries",
31 |       type: "bar",
32 |       xKey: "country",
33 |       yKey: "count",
34 |       aggregation: "count",
35 |     });
36 |     const histogram = calculateChart(rows, {
37 |       title: "Salary Histogram",
38 |       type: "histogram",
39 |       xKey: "salary_usd",
40 |       yKey: "count",
41 |       aggregation: "count",
42 |       limit: 4,
43 |     });
44 |     const scatter = calculateChart(rows, {
45 |       title: "Experience vs Salary",
46 |       type: "scatter",
47 |       xKey: "experience",
48 |       yKey: "salary_usd",
49 |       aggregation: "count",
50 |     });
51 | 
52 |     expect(indiaAverage.value).toBe(57500);
53 |     expect(countryDistribution.data).toEqual(expect.arrayContaining([
54 |       expect.objectContaining({ country: "India", value: 2 }),
55 |       expect.objectContaining({ country: "USA", value: 1 }),
56 |     ]));
57 |     expect(histogram.data.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
58 |     expect(scatter.data.every((point) => typeof point.x === "number" && typeof point.y === "number")).toBe(true);
59 |   });
60 | });
61 | 
```

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/api/schemaAgentApi.ts

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/api/schemaAgentApi.ts`

```ts
 1 | export type SchemaTrainResponse = {
 2 |   ok: boolean;
 3 |   profile: any;
 4 |   dashboardSpec: any;
 5 |   agentPlan?: any;
 6 |   agentTools?: string[];
 7 |   ontologyMapping?: {
 8 |     inferredDomain: string;
 9 |     canonicalTerms: string[];
10 |     mapping: Record<string, string>;
11 |   };
12 |   critic?: {
13 |     score: number;
14 |     status: string;
15 |     issues: string[];
16 |     improvements: string[];
17 |   };
18 |   guardian: {
19 |     valid: boolean;
20 |     errors: string[];
21 |     warnings: string[];
22 |   };
23 |   calculatedDashboard?: any;
24 |   memoryRecordId?: string;
25 |   trainingExamplesCount?: number;
26 |   message?: string;
27 | };
28 | 
29 | async function requestJson<T>(url: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
30 |   const response = await fetch(url, {
31 |     ...options,
32 |     headers: {
33 |       'Content-Type': 'application/json',
34 |       ...(options?.headers || {}),
35 |     },
36 |   });
37 | 
38 |   if (!response.ok) {
39 |     const text = await response.text();
40 |     throw new Error(text || `Request failed with ${response.status}`);
41 |   }
42 | 
43 |   return response.json();
44 | }
45 | 
46 | export const schemaAgentApi = {
47 |   trainSchema(datasetId: string) {
48 |     return requestJson<SchemaTrainResponse>(
49 |       `/api/schema-agent/datasets/${encodeURIComponent(datasetId)}/train-schema`,
50 |       { method: 'POST' }
51 |     );
52 |   },
53 | 
54 |   buildDashboardSpec(datasetId: string) {
55 |     return requestJson<SchemaTrainResponse>(
56 |       `/api/schema-agent/datasets/${encodeURIComponent(datasetId)}/dashboard-spec`,
57 |       { method: 'POST' }
58 |     );
59 |   },
60 | 
61 |   memoryStats() {
62 |     return requestJson<{ ok: boolean; stats: any }>(
63 |       '/api/schema-agent/memory/stats'
64 |     );
65 |   },
66 | };
67 | 
```

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/app/providers/AppErrorBoundary.tsx

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/app/providers/AppErrorBoundary.tsx`

```tsx
 1 | import { Component, ErrorInfo, ReactNode } from "react";
 2 | import StatusPanel from "@/shared/layout/StatusPanel";
 3 | 
 4 | interface AppErrorBoundaryProps {
 5 |   children: ReactNode;
 6 | }
 7 | 
 8 | interface AppErrorBoundaryState {
 9 |   hasError: boolean;
10 |   errorMessage: string;
11 | }
12 | 
13 | class AppErrorBoundary extends Component<AppErrorBoundaryProps, AppErrorBoundaryState> {
14 |   state: AppErrorBoundaryState = {
15 |     hasError: false,
16 |     errorMessage: "",
17 |   };
18 | 
19 |   static getDerivedStateFromError(error: Error): AppErrorBoundaryState {
20 |     return {
21 |       hasError: true,
22 |       errorMessage: error.message || "The application hit an unexpected error.",
23 |     };
24 |   }
25 | 
26 |   componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) {
27 |     console.error("Application error boundary caught an error:", error, errorInfo);
28 |   }
29 | 
30 |   render() {
31 |     if (this.state.hasError) {
32 |       return (
33 |         <StatusPanel
34 |           title="Application error"
35 |           message={this.state.errorMessage}
36 |           actionLabel="Reload App"
37 |           onAction={() => window.location.reload()}
38 |         />
39 |       );
40 |     }
41 | 
42 |     return this.props.children;
43 |   }
44 | }
45 | 
46 | export default AppErrorBoundary;
47 | 
```

```tsx
 1 | import { lazy, Suspense } from "react";
 2 | import { BrowserRouter, Route, Routes } from "react-router-dom";
 3 | import AppLayout from "@/shared/layout/AppLayout";
 4 | import StatusPanel from "@/shared/layout/StatusPanel";
 5 | 
 6 | const AnalyticsPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/AnalyticsPage"));
 7 | const AgenticPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/AgenticPage"));
 8 | const ChatPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/chat/pages/ChatPage"));
 9 | const LocalChatPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/chat/pages/LocalChatPage"));
10 | const EliteDashboardPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/dashboard/pages/EliteDashboardPage"));
11 | const DataTablePage = lazy(() => import("@/features/dashboard/pages/DataTablePage"));
12 | const UploadPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/data/pages/UploadPage"));
13 | const MobileUploadPortal = lazy(() => import("@/features/data/pages/MobileUploadPortal"));
14 | const MLPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/ml/pages/MLPage"));
15 | const PdfUploadPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/pdf/pages/PdfUploadPage"));
16 | const DataProfilingPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/DataProfilingPage"));
17 | const AnomalyDetectionPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/AnomalyDetectionPage"));
18 | const RelationshipsPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/RelationshipsPage"));
19 | const DataCleaningPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/DataCleaningPage"));
20 | const ExportPage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/ExportPage"));
21 | const AgenticDataSciencePage = lazy(() => import("@/features/analytics/pages/AgenticDataSciencePage"));
22 | const NotFoundPage = lazy(() => import("@/app/routes/NotFoundPage"));
23 | 
24 | const AppRouter = () => (
25 |   <BrowserRouter
26 |     future={{
27 |       v7_startTransition: true,
28 |       v7_relativeSplatPath: true,
29 |     }}
30 |   >
31 |     <Suspense fallback={<StatusPanel title="Loading" message="Preparing this workspace." />}>
32 |       <Routes>
33 |         <Route element={<AppLayout />}>
34 |           <Route path="/" element={<EliteDashboardPage />} />
35 |           <Route path="/dashboard" element={<EliteDashboardPage />} />
36 |           <Route path="/upload" element={<UploadPage />} />
37 |           <Route path="/pdf" element={<PdfUploadPage />} />
38 |           <Route path="/pdf-upload" element={<PdfUploadPage />} />
39 |           <Route path="/chat" element={<ChatPage />} />
40 |           <Route path="/local-chat" element={<LocalChatPage />} />
41 |           <Route path="/data" element={<DataTablePage />} />
42 |           <Route path="/analytics" element={<AnalyticsPage />} />
43 |           <Route path="/agentic" element={<AgenticPage />} />
44 |           <Route path="/analytics/profile" element={<DataProfilingPage />} />
45 |           <Route path="/analytics/anomalies" element={<AnomalyDetectionPage />} />
46 |           <Route path="/analytics/relationships" element={<RelationshipsPage />} />
47 |           <Route path="/analytics/cleaning" element={<DataCleaningPage />} />
48 |           <Route path="/analytics/export" element={<ExportPage />} />
49 |           <Route path="/agentic-data-science" element={<AgenticDataSciencePage />} />
50 |           <Route path="/ml" element={<MLPage />} />
51 |         </Route>
52 |         <Route path="/mobile-upload/:sessionId" element={<MobileUploadPortal />} />
53 |         <Route path="*" element={<NotFoundPage />} />
54 |       </Routes>
55 |     </Suspense>
56 |   </BrowserRouter>
57 | );
58 | 
59 | export default AppRouter;
60 | 
```

20 |     </div>

22 | };

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/components/GenAIDashboard.tsx

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/components/GenAIDashboard.tsx`

```tsx
  1 | import { useState } from "react";
  2 | import axios from "axios";
  3 | import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
  4 | import { Card } from "@/components/ui/card";
  5 | import {
  6 |   BarChart,
  7 |   Bar,
  8 |   LineChart,
  9 |   Line,
 10 |   ScatterChart,
 11 |   Scatter,
 12 |   PieChart,
 13 |   Pie,
 14 |   Cell,
 15 |   XAxis,
 16 |   YAxis,
 17 |   CartesianGrid,
 18 |   Tooltip,
 19 |   Legend,
 20 |   ResponsiveContainer,
 21 | } from "recharts";
 22 | import { toast } from "sonner";
 23 | 
 24 | interface Dashboard {
 25 |   title: string;
 26 |   domain: string;
 27 |   kpis: Array<{
 28 |     name: string;
 29 |     description: string;
 30 |     business_value: string;
 31 |   }>;
 32 |   charts: Array<{
 33 |     type: string;
 34 |     title: string;
 35 |     dimensions: string[];
 36 |     measures: string[];
 37 |     insight: string;
 38 |   }>;
 39 |   insights: string[];
 40 |   recommendations: string[];
 41 | }
 42 | 
 43 | export default function GenAIDashboard() {
 44 |   const [dataset, setDataset] = useState<{
 45 |     columns: string[];
 46 |     data: Record<string, unknown>[];
 47 |   } | null>(null);
 48 |   const [dashboard, setDashboard] = useState<Dashboard | null>(null);
 49 |   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
 50 |   const [query, setQuery] = useState("");
 51 | 
 52 |   const handleFileUpload = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
 53 |     const file = e.target.files?.[0];
 54 |     if (!file) return;
 55 | 
 56 |     const reader = new FileReader();
 57 |     reader.onload = (event) => {
 58 |       try {
 59 |         const content = event.target?.result as string;
 60 |         const lines = content.split("\n");
 61 |         const headers = lines[0].split(",").map((h) => h.trim());
 62 | 
 63 |         const data = lines.slice(1).map((line) => {
 64 |           const values = line.split(",").map((v) => v.trim());
 65 |           const row: Record<string, unknown> = {};
 66 | 
 67 |           headers.forEach((header, index) => {
 68 |             const value = values[index];
 69 |             row[header] = isNaN(Number(value)) ? value : Number(value);
 70 |           });
 71 | 
 72 |           return row;
 73 |         });
 74 | 
 75 |         setDataset({ columns: headers, data: data.filter((d) => Object.values(d).some((v) => v)) });
 76 |         toast.success("Dataset loaded successfully");
 77 |       } catch (error) {
 78 |         toast.error("Failed to parse CSV");
 79 |       }
 80 |     };
 81 | 
 82 |     reader.readAsText(file);
 83 |   };
 84 | 
 85 |   const generateDashboard = async () => {
 86 |     if (!dataset) {
 87 |       toast.error("Please upload a dataset first");
 88 |       return;
 89 |     }
 90 | 
 91 |     setLoading(true);
 92 |     try {
 93 |       const response = await axios.post("http://localhost:3000/api/genai/dashboard/create", {
 94 |         dataset,
 95 |       });
 96 | 
 97 |       setDashboard(response.data.dashboard);
 98 |       toast.success("Dashboard generated successfully");
 99 |     } catch (error) {
100 |       toast.error(
101 |         error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Failed to generate dashboard"
102 |       );
103 |     } finally {
104 |       setLoading(false);
105 |     }
106 |   };
107 | 
108 |   const submitQuery = async () => {
109 |     if (!query || !dashboard) {
110 |       toast.error("Please enter a query and generate a dashboard first");
111 |       return;
112 |     }
113 | 
114 |     setLoading(true);
115 |     try {
116 |       const response = await axios.post("http://localhost:3000/api/genai/query", {
117 |         query,
118 |         dataset,
119 |       });
120 | 
121 |       toast.success("Query processed successfully");
122 |       console.log("Query response:", response.data);
123 |     } catch (error) {
124 |       toast.error("Failed to process query");
125 |     } finally {
126 |       setLoading(false);
127 |     }
128 |   };
129 | 
130 |   return (
131 |     <div className="w-full max-w-7xl mx-auto p-6">
132 |       <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold mb-8">InsightFlow - GenAI Analytics</h1>
133 | 
134 |       {/* Upload Section */}
135 |       <Card className="p-6 mb-8">
136 |         <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-4">Step 1: Upload Dataset</h2>
137 |         <input
138 |           type="file"
139 |           accept=".csv"
140 |           onChange={handleFileUpload}
141 |           className="mb-4"
142 |         />
143 |         {dataset && (
144 |           <p className="text-sm text-gray-600">
145 |             Loaded: {dataset.data.length} rows, {dataset.columns.length} columns
146 |           </p>
147 |         )}
148 |       </Card>
149 | 
150 |       {/* Generate Dashboard */}
151 |       <Card className="p-6 mb-8">
152 |         <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-4">Step 2: Generate Dashboard</h2>
153 |         <Button
154 |           onClick={generateDashboard}
155 |           disabled={!dataset || loading}
156 |           className="bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700"
157 |         >
158 |           {loading ? "Generating..." : "Generate AI Dashboard"}
159 |         </Button>
160 |       </Card>
161 | 
162 |       {/* Query Section */}
163 |       {dashboard && (
164 |         <Card className="p-6 mb-8">
165 |           <h2 className="text-2xl font-semibold mb-4">Conversational Analytics</h2>
166 |           <div className="flex gap-2">
167 |             <input aria-label="Ask a question about your data..."
168 |               type="text"
169 |               placeholder="Ask a question about your data..."
170 |               value={query}
171 |               onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}
172 |               className="flex-1 px-4 py-2 border rounded-lg"
173 |             />
174 |             <Button
175 |               onClick={submitQuery}
176 |               disabled={loading}
177 |               className="bg-green-600 hover:bg-green-700"
178 |             >
179 |               Submit Query
180 |             </Button>
181 |           </div>
182 |         </Card>
183 |       )}
184 | 
185 |       {/* Dashboard Display */}
186 |       {dashboard && (
187 |         <div className="space-y-8">
188 |           <Card className="p-6">
189 |             <h2 className="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">{dashboard.title}</h2>
190 |             <p className="text-gray-600 mb-4">Domain: {dashboard.domain}</p>
191 | 
192 |             {/* KPI Cards */}
193 |             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 mb-8">
194 |               {dashboard.kpis.slice(0, 4).map((kpi, idx) => (
195 |                 <Card key={idx} className="p-4 bg-gradient-to-br from-blue-50 to-indigo-50">
196 |                   <h3 className="font-semibold text-lg mb-2">{kpi.name}</h3>
197 |                   <p className="text-sm text-gray-600 mb-2">{kpi.description}</p>
198 |                   <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">{kpi.business_value}</p>
199 |                 </Card>
200 |               ))}
201 |             </div>
202 | 
203 |             {/* Charts Preview */}
204 |             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
205 |               {dashboard.charts.slice(0, 4).map((chart, idx) => (
206 |                 <Card key={idx} className="p-4">
207 |                   <h3 className="font-semibold mb-4">{chart.title}</h3>
208 |                   <div className="text-sm text-gray-600">
209 |                     <p>Type: {chart.type}</p>
210 |                     <p>Insight: {chart.insight}</p>
211 |                   </div>
212 |                 </Card>
213 |               ))}
214 |             </div>
215 | 
216 |             {/* Insights */}
217 |             {dashboard.insights.length > 0 && (
218 |               <Card className="p-6 mt-8 bg-amber-50 border-amber-200">
219 |                 <h3 className="font-semibold text-lg mb-4">AI Insights</h3>
220 |                 <ul className="space-y-2">
221 |                   {dashboard.insights.slice(0, 5).map((insight, idx) => (
222 |                     <li key={idx} className="text-sm">
223 |                       • {insight}
224 |                     </li>
225 |                   ))}
226 |                 </ul>
227 |               </Card>
228 |             )}
229 | 
230 |             {/* Recommendations */}
231 |             {dashboard.recommendations.length > 0 && (
232 |               <Card className="p-6 mt-4 bg-green-50 border-green-200">
233 |                 <h3 className="font-semibold text-lg mb-4">Recommendations</h3>
234 |                 <ul className="space-y-2">
235 |                   {dashboard.recommendations.slice(0, 5).map((rec, idx) => (
236 |                     <li key={idx} className="text-sm">
237 |                       • {rec}
238 |                     </li>
239 |                   ))}
240 |                 </ul>
241 |               </Card>
242 |             )}
243 |           </Card>
244 |         </div>
245 |       )}
246 |     </div>
247 |   );
248 | }
249 | 
```

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/components/SchemaTrainingStatus.tsx

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/components/SchemaTrainingStatus.tsx`

```tsx
 1 | type Props = {
 2 |   loading: boolean;
 3 |   error?: string | null;
 4 |   result?: any;
 5 | };
 6 | 
 7 | export function SchemaTrainingStatus({ loading, error, result }: Props) {
 8 |   if (loading) {
 9 |     return (
10 |       <div className="rounded-2xl border p-4 shadow-sm">
11 |         <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold">Schema Agent Training</h3>
12 |         <p className="text-sm opacity-80">
13 |           Profiling schema, updating memory, generating dashboard plan, and calculating values...
14 |         </p>
15 |       </div>
16 |     );
17 |   }
18 | 
19 |   if (error) {
20 |     return (
21 |       <div className="rounded-2xl border border-red-400 p-4 shadow-sm">
22 |         <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold">Schema Training Failed</h3>
23 |         <p className="text-sm">{error}</p>
24 |       </div>
25 |     );
26 |   }
27 | 
28 |   if (!result) return null;
29 | 
30 |   return (
31 |     <div className="rounded-2xl border p-4 shadow-sm">
32 |       <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold">Schema Agent Ready</h3>
33 |       <p className="text-sm opacity-80">{result.message}</p>
34 | 
35 |       <div className="mt-3 grid grid-cols-2 gap-3 md:grid-cols-4">
36 |         <div>
37 |           <p className="text-xs opacity-60">Rows</p>
38 |           <p className="font-semibold">{result.profile?.rowCount?.toLocaleString?.() ?? '-'}</p>
39 |         </div>
40 |         <div>
41 |           <p className="text-xs opacity-60">Columns</p>
42 |           <p className="font-semibold">{result.profile?.columnCount ?? '-'}</p>
43 |         </div>
44 |         <div>
45 |           <p className="text-xs opacity-60">KPIs</p>
46 |           <p className="font-semibold">{result.dashboardSpec?.kpis?.length ?? 0}</p>
47 |         </div>
48 |         <div>
49 |           <p className="text-xs opacity-60">Charts</p>
50 |           <p className="font-semibold">{result.dashboardSpec?.charts?.length ?? 0}</p>
51 |         </div>
52 |       </div>
53 | 
54 |       {result.guardian && !result.guardian.valid && (
55 |         <div className="mt-3 rounded-xl border border-yellow-400 p-3 text-sm">
56 |           <strong>Guardian warnings:</strong>
57 |           <ul className="mt-1 list-disc pl-4">
58 |             {result.guardian.errors?.map((item: string) => <li key={item}>{item}</li>)}
59 |           </ul>
60 |         </div>
61 |       )}
62 |     </div>
63 |   );
64 | }
65 | 
```

### FILE: apps/frontend/src/features/analytics/pages/AgenticDataSciencePage.tsx

Source path: `apps/frontend/src/features/analytics/pages/AgenticDataSciencePage.tsx`

```tsx
  1 | import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
  2 | import { AgenticAuditTrail } from "@/features/dashboard/components/AgenticAuditTrail";
  3 | import { DataScienceSummaryCards } from "@/features/dashboard/components/DataScienceSummaryCards";
  4 | import { useMlAnalytics } from "@/features/dashboard/hooks/useMlAnalytics";
  5 | 
  6 | export default function AgenticDataSciencePage() {
  7 |   const [datasetId, setDatasetId] = useState("");
  8 |   const [target, setTarget] = useState("");
  9 |   const [goal, setGoal] = useState("Find insights, quality issues, anomalies, and model readiness.");
 10 |   const { profile, fullAnalysis, measures, dimensions, loadProfile, runFullAnalysis } = useMlAnalytics(datasetId);
 11 | 
 12 |   const result = (fullAnalysis.data as any)?.result;
 13 |   const profileResult = result?.profile ?? (profile.data as any)?.result;
 14 |   const targetOptions = useMemo(() => [...measures, ...dimensions], [measures, dimensions]);
 15 | 
 16 |   return (
 17 |     <main className="mx-auto flex w-full max-w-7xl flex-col gap-5 p-6">
 18 |       <header className="flex flex-wrap items-end justify-between gap-4 border-b border-slate-200 pb-4">
 19 |         <div>
 20 |           <p className="text-sm font-medium uppercase tracking-wide text-slate-500">InsightFlow</p>
 21 |           <h1 className="text-2xl font-semibold text-slate-950">Agentic Data Science</h1>
 22 |         </div>
 23 |         <div className="text-sm text-slate-500">Python tools + agent audit</div>
 24 |       </header>
 25 | 
 26 |       <section className="grid gap-3 rounded-lg border border-slate-200 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm lg:grid-cols-[1fr_1fr_2fr_auto]">
 27 |         <input
 28 |           className="h-10 rounded-md border border-slate-300 px-3 text-sm"
 29 |           placeholder="Dataset ID"
 30 |           value={datasetId}
 31 |           onChange={(event) => setDatasetId(event.target.value)}
 32 |         />
 33 |         <select
 34 |           className="h-10 rounded-md border border-slate-300 px-3 text-sm"
 35 |           value={target}
 36 |           onChange={(event) => setTarget(event.target.value)}
 37 |         >
 38 |           <option value="">No target</option>
 39 |           {targetOptions.map((column) => (
 40 |             <option key={column} value={column}>
 41 |               {column}
 42 |             </option>
 43 |           ))}
 44 |         </select>
 45 |         <input
 46 |           className="h-10 rounded-md border border-slate-300 px-3 text-sm"
 47 |           value={goal}
 48 |           onChange={(event) => setGoal(event.target.value)}
 49 |         />
 50 |         <div className="flex gap-2">
 51 |           <button
 52 |             className="h-10 rounded-md border border-slate-300 px-4 text-sm font-medium text-slate-700 disabled:opacity-50"
 53 |             onClick={loadProfile}
 54 |             disabled={!datasetId || profile.loading}
 55 |           >
 56 |             {profile.loading ? "Profiling" : "Profile"}
 57 |           </button>
 58 |           <button
 59 |             className="h-10 rounded-md bg-slate-950 px-4 text-sm font-medium text-white disabled:opacity-50"
 60 |             onClick={() => runFullAnalysis(target || undefined, goal)}
 61 |             disabled={!datasetId || fullAnalysis.loading}
 62 |           >
 63 |             {fullAnalysis.loading ? "Running" : "Run"}
 64 |           </button>
 65 |         </div>
 66 |       </section>
 67 | 
 68 |       {profile.error || fullAnalysis.error ? (
 69 |         <div className="rounded-lg border border-red-200 bg-red-50 p-4 text-sm text-red-700">
 70 |           {profile.error || fullAnalysis.error}
 71 |         </div>
 72 |       ) : null}
 73 | 
 74 |       {profileResult ? (
 75 |         <DataScienceSummaryCards
 76 |           profile={profileResult}
 77 |           correlations={result?.correlations}
 78 |           anomalies={result?.anomalies}
 79 |           model={result?.model}
 80 |         />
 81 |       ) : null}
 82 | 
 83 |       <AgenticAuditTrail steps={(fullAnalysis.data as any)?.auditTrail ?? []} />
 84 | 
 85 |       {result?.dashboardPlan ? (
 86 |         <section className="rounded-lg border border-slate-200 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm">
 87 |           <h2 className="mb-3 text-base font-semibold text-slate-950">Dashboard Guardian Plan</h2>
 88 |           <pre className="max-h-96 overflow-auto rounded-md bg-slate-950 p-4 text-sm text-white">
 89 |             {JSON.stringify(result.dashboardPlan, null, 2)}
 90 |           </pre>
 91 |         </section>
 92 |       ) : null}
 93 | 
 94 |       {result?.trainingMemory ? (
 95 |         <section className="rounded-lg border border-slate-200 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm">
 96 |           <h2 className="mb-3 text-base font-semibold text-slate-950">Training Memory</h2>
 97 |           <pre className="max-h-96 overflow-auto rounded-md bg-slate-950 p-4 text-sm text-white">
 98 |             {JSON.stringify(result.trainingMemory, null, 2)}
 99 |           </pre>
100 |         </section>
101 |       ) : null}
102 |     </main>
103 |   );
104 | }
105 | 
```
