510 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
511 | // 13. ML SERVICE
512 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
513 | async function testMLService() {
514 |   console.log("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════");
515 |   console.log("  13. ML SERVICE ENDPOINTS");
516 |   console.log("═══════════════════════════════════════════");
517 | 
518 |   // ML Health - may not be running but endpoint should exist
519 |   const mlHealth = await request("POST", "/api/ml/health");
520 |   test("ML health endpoint exists", mlHealth.status === 200 || mlHealth.status === 503 || mlHealth.status === 500);
521 | 
522 |   // ML model list
523 |   const models = await request("GET", "/api/ml/models/list");
524 |   test("ML models list endpoint exists", models.status === 200 || models.status === 500);
525 | 
526 |   if (datasetId) {
527 |     // ML train (might fail if ML service not running, but endpoint should exist)
528 |     const train = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasetId}/ml/train`, {
529 |       targetColumn: "salary",
530 |       problemType: "regression",
531 |     });
532 |     test("ML train endpoint exists", train.status !== 404, `status: ${train.status}`);
533 | 
534 |     // ML predict
535 |     const predict = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasetId}/ml/predict`, {
536 |       inputData: [{ experience: 5, department: "Engineering" }],
537 |     });
538 |     test("ML predict endpoint exists", predict.status !== 404, `status: ${predict.status}`);
539 | 
540 |     // Predict validation: missing inputData  
541 |     const badPredict = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasetId}/ml/predict`, {});
542 |     test("ML predict without data returns 400", badPredict.status === 400);
543 | 
544 |     // Feature importance
545 |     const features = await request("GET", `/api/datasets/${datasetId}/ml/feature-importance`);
546 |     test("ML feature importance endpoint exists", features.status !== 404, `status: ${features.status}`);
547 |   }
548 | }
549 | 
550 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
551 | // 14. ERROR HANDLING & EDGE CASES
552 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
553 | async function testErrorHandling() {
554 |   console.log("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════");
555 |   console.log("  14. ERROR HANDLING & EDGE CASES");
556 |   console.log("═══════════════════════════════════════════");
557 | 
558 |   // 404 route
559 |   const notFound = await request("GET", "/api/nonexistent");
560 |   test("Non-existent route returns 404", notFound.status === 404);
561 |   test("404 has error message", typeof notFound.data?.error === "string");
562 | 
563 |   // OPTIONS (CORS preflight)
564 |   const options = await request("OPTIONS", "/api/health");
565 |   test("OPTIONS returns 204", options.status === 204);
566 | 
567 |   // Non-existent dataset operations
568 |   const badChat = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/fake-id/chat", {
569 |     query: "test",
570 |   });
571 |   test("Chat on non-existent dataset returns 404", badChat.status === 404);
572 | 
573 |   const badCorr = await request("GET", "/api/datasets/fake-id/ai-correlations");
574 |   test("Correlations on non-existent dataset returns 404", badCorr.status === 404);
575 | 
576 |   const badProfile = await request("GET", "/api/datasets/fake-id/ai/profile");
577 |   test("AI profile on non-existent dataset returns 404", badProfile.status === 404);
578 | }
579 | 
580 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
581 | // 15. CHAT MESSAGES PERSISTENCE
582 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
583 | async function testChatPersistence() {
584 |   console.log("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════");
585 |   console.log("  15. CHAT MESSAGE PERSISTENCE");
586 |   console.log("═══════════════════════════════════════════");
587 | 
588 |   if (!datasetId) {
589 |     console.log("  ⏩ Skipped (no dataset)");
590 |     return;
591 |   }
592 | 
593 |   // Send a chat message
594 |   await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasetId}/chat`, {
595 |     query: "show me salary distribution",
596 |   });
597 | 
598 |   // Check state to see if messages persisted
599 |   const state = await request("GET", "/api/state");
600 |   const messages = state.data?.chatMessages;
601 |   test("Chat messages are persisted", Array.isArray(messages) && messages.length > 0);
602 | 
603 |   if (messages && messages.length > 0) {
604 |     const lastMsg = messages[messages.length - 1];
605 |     test("Message has id", typeof lastMsg.id === "string");
606 |     test("Message has role", typeof lastMsg.role === "string");
607 |     test("Message has content", typeof lastMsg.content === "string");
608 |     test("Message has timestamp", typeof lastMsg.timestamp === "string");
609 |     console.log(`     → ${messages.length} messages persisted`);
610 |   }
611 | }
612 | 
613 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
614 | // MAIN
615 | // ─────────────────────────────────────────────
616 | async function main() {
617 |   console.log("╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗");
618 |   console.log("║   INSIGHTFLOW — FULL END-TO-END TEST SUITE           ║");
619 |   console.log(`║   Testing against: ${API_BASE_URL.padEnd(35)}║`);
620 |   console.log("╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝");
621 |   console.log(`\n  Started at: ${new Date().toLocaleString()}`);
622 | 
623 |   const startTime = Date.now();
624 | 
625 |   // Preflight: ensure backend is running before running all tests
626 |   await assertBackendRunning();
627 | 
628 |   const tests = [
629 |     testRootAndHealth,
630 |     testState,
631 |     testDemoDataset,
632 |     testDatasetImport,
633 |     testSchema,
634 |     testRowPatch,
635 |     testAIChat,
636 |     testCorrelation,
637 |     testAIDataServices,
638 |     testCache,
639 |     testSchemaAIQuery,
640 |     testLocalDatabase,
641 |     testMLService,
642 |     testErrorHandling,
643 |     testChatPersistence,
644 |   ];
645 | 
646 |   for (const fn of tests) {
647 |     try {
648 |       await fn();
649 |     } catch (error) {
650 |       console.error(`\n  💥 ERROR in ${fn.name}: ${error.message}`);
651 |       totalTests++;
652 |       failed++;
653 |       failures.push(`  ❌ ${fn.name} — crashed: ${error.message}`);
654 |     }
655 |   }
656 | 
657 |   const totalTime = ((Date.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(1);
658 | 
659 |   console.log("\n╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗");
660 |   console.log("║   TEST RESULTS SUMMARY                               ║");
661 |   console.log("╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣");
662 |   console.log(`║   Total:  ${String(totalTests).padStart(3)}                                        ║`);
663 |   console.log(`║   Passed: ${String(passed).padStart(3)} ✅                                      ║`);
664 |   console.log(`║   Failed: ${String(failed).padStart(3)} ${failed === 0 ? "🎉" : "❌"}                                      ║`);
665 |   console.log(`║   Time:   ${totalTime}s                                       ║`);
666 |   console.log("╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝");
667 | 
668 |   if (failures.length > 0) {
669 |     console.log("\n  ❌ FAILED TESTS:");
670 |     failures.forEach((f) => console.log(f));
671 |   }
672 | 
673 |   process.exit(failed > 0 ? 1 : 0);
674 | }
675 | 
676 | main();
677 | 
```

### FILE: e2e/ai-dashboard-command-flow.spec.ts

Source path: `e2e/ai-dashboard-command-flow.spec.ts`

```ts
 1 | import { expect, test } from "@playwright/test";
 2 | import { mockInsightFlowApi } from "./helpers";
 3 | 
 4 | test("AI dashboard command flow supports chart, KPI, and clear filters commands", async ({ page }) => {
 5 |   await mockInsightFlowApi(page);
 6 |   await page.goto("/dashboard");
 7 | 
 8 |   await expect(page.getByRole("heading", { name: /salary-small/i })).toBeVisible();
 9 |   await page.evaluate(() => window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight));
10 |   await expect(page.getByPlaceholder(/Ask:/i)).toBeVisible();
11 | 
12 |   const textbox = page.getByRole("textbox").last();
13 |   await textbox.fill("show average salary_usd by country as bar chart");
14 |   await textbox.press("Enter");
15 |   await expect(page.getByText(/Average Salary by Country|Chart generated/i).first()).toBeVisible();
16 | 
17 |   await textbox.fill("add KPI for highest salary_usd");
18 |   await textbox.press("Enter");
19 |   await expect(page.getByText(/Highest Salary|KPI generated/i).first()).toBeVisible();
20 | 
21 |   await textbox.fill("clear filters");
22 |   await textbox.press("Enter");
23 |   await expect(page.getByText(/Filters cleared|clear filters/i).first()).toBeVisible();
24 | });
25 | 
```

### FILE: e2e/helpers.ts

Source path: `e2e/helpers.ts`

```ts
  1 | import { Page } from "@playwright/test";
  2 | 
  3 | export const salaryRows = [
  4 |   { country: "India", salary_usd: 50000, experience: 2 },
  5 |   { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000, experience: 5 },
  6 |   { country: "India", salary_usd: 65000, experience: 3 },
  7 | ];
  8 | 
  9 | export async function mockInsightFlowApi(page: Page) {
 10 |   const dataset = {
 11 |     id: "e2e-salary",
 12 |     name: "salary-small",
 13 |     rowCount: salaryRows.length,
 14 |     columns: [
 15 |       { name: "country", type: "string" },
 16 |       { name: "salary_usd", type: "number" },
 17 |       { name: "experience", type: "number" },
 18 |     ],
 19 |     rows: salaryRows,
 20 |   };
 21 | 
 22 |   const analysis = {
 23 |     dataTypeLabel: "Workforce salary",
 24 |     insights: [],
 25 |     chartRecommendations: [
 26 |       {
 27 |         id: "chart-country",
 28 |         title: "Average Salary by Country",
 29 |         type: "bar",
 30 |         xKey: "country",
 31 |         yKey: "salary_usd",
 32 |         aggregation: "avg",
 33 |         data: [
 34 |           { country: "India", salary_usd: 57500 },
 35 |           { country: "USA", salary_usd: 90000 },
 36 |         ],
 37 |       },
 38 |     ],
 39 |   };
 40 | 
 41 |   await page.route("**/*", async (route) => {
 42 |     const url = route.request().url();
 43 |     const pathname = new URL(url).pathname;
 44 |     if (!pathname.startsWith("/api/")) return route.continue();
 45 |     const method = route.request().method();
 46 | 
 47 |     if (url.includes("/api/state")) {
 48 |       return route.fulfill({ json: { success: true, data: { dataset, chatMessages: [], analysis } } });
 49 |     }
 50 | 
 51 |     if (url.includes("/api/datasets/import") || url.includes("/api/datasets/merge") || url.includes("/api/datasets/demo")) {
 52 |       return route.fulfill({ json: { success: true, data: { dataset, chatMessages: [], analysis } } });
 53 |     }
 54 | 
 55 |     if (url.includes("/schema-dashboard") && method === "POST") {
 56 |       return route.fulfill({
 57 |         json: {
 58 |           success: true,
 59 |           data: {
 60 |             schemaOnly: true,
 61 |             profile: {
 62 |               datasetName: dataset.name,
 63 |               rowCount: dataset.rowCount,
 64 |               columnCount: dataset.columns.length,
 65 |               domain: "workforce_salary",
 66 |               columns: [
 67 |                 { name: "country", type: "category", role: "location", uniqueCount: 2, topValues: ["India", "USA"] },
 68 |                 { name: "salary_usd", type: "number", role: "money_metric", uniqueCount: 3 },
 69 |                 { name: "experience", type: "number", role: "continuous_metric", uniqueCount: 3 },
 70 |               ],
 71 |             },
 72 |             memoryMatch: { dataset: "Salary Small", domain: "workforce_salary", score: 0.98 },
 73 |             dashboardPlan: {
 74 |               source: "e2e-mock",
 75 |               domain: "workforce_salary",
 76 |               kpis: [
 77 |                 { title: "Total Records", metric: "__row_count__", aggregation: "count", format: "number" },
 78 |                 { title: "Highest Salary", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "max", format: "currency" },
 79 |               ],
 80 |               charts: [
 81 |                 { title: "Average Salary by Country", type: "bar", xKey: "country", yKey: "salary_usd", aggregation: "avg", limit: 10 },
 82 |               ],
 83 |               filters: [{ key: "country", label: "Country", type: "select" }],
 84 |             },
 85 |             model: "mock-schema-planner",
 86 |             provider: "mock",
 87 |           },
 88 |         },
 89 |       });
 90 |     }
 91 | 
 92 |     if (url.includes("/api/qr-upload/generate")) {
 93 |       return route.fulfill({
 94 |         json: {
 95 |           success: true,
 96 |           data: {
 97 |             sessionId: "session-1",
 98 |             uploadToken: [REDACTED_SECRET],
 99 |             uploadUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:5173/mobile-upload/session-1",
100 |             qrDataUrl: "data:image/png;base64,abc",
101 |             workspaceName: "InsightFlow Workspace",
102 |             status: "waiting",
103 |             expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 60000).toISOString(),
104 |           },
105 |         },
106 |       });
107 |     }
108 | 
109 |     if (url.includes("/api/qr-upload/")) {
110 |       return route.fulfill({
111 |         json: {
112 |           success: true,
113 |           data: {
114 |             sessionId: "session-1",
115 |             status: "waiting",
116 |             workspaceName: "InsightFlow Workspace",
117 |             files: [],
118 |             expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 60000).toISOString(),
119 |           },
120 |         },
121 |       });
122 |     }
123 | 
124 |     if (url.includes("/dashboard-command") && method === "POST") {
125 |       const body = route.request().postDataJSON() as { query?: string };
126 |       if (/highest/i.test(body.query || "")) {
127 |         return route.fulfill({
128 |           json: {
129 |             success: true,
130 |             data: {
131 |               action: "GENERATE_KPI",
132 |               message: "KPI generated",
133 |               schemaOnly: true,
134 |               kpiSpec: { title: "Highest Salary", metric: "salary_usd", aggregation: "max", format: "currency" },
135 |             },
136 |           },
137 |         });
138 |       }
139 |       if (/clear/i.test(body.query || "")) {
140 |         return route.fulfill({
141 |           json: { success: true, data: { action: "CLEAR_FILTERS", message: "Filters cleared", schemaOnly: true } },
142 |         });
143 |       }
144 |       return route.fulfill({
145 |         json: {
146 |           success: true,
147 |           data: {
148 |             action: "GENERATE_CHART",
149 |             message: "Chart generated",
150 |             schemaOnly: true,
151 |             chartSpec: {
152 |               title: "Average Salary by Country",
153 |               type: "bar",
154 |               xKey: "country",
155 |               yKey: "salary_usd",
156 |               aggregation: "avg",
157 |             },
158 |           },
159 |         },
160 |       });
161 |     }
162 | 
163 |     if (url.includes("/api/pdf/import")) {
164 |       return route.fulfill({
165 |         json: {
166 |           success: true,
167 |           data: {
168 |             pdf: { id: "pdf-1", datasetId: dataset.id, fileName: "sample.pdf", jobId: "job-1", tableCount: 1, chunkCount: 1, textElementCount: 1 },
169 |             dataset,
170 |             analysis,
171 |             knowledgeBaseSummary: { tableCount: 1, chunkCount: 1, textElementCount: 1 },
172 |             privacy: { rawPdfSentToLLM: false, extractedTextCanBeUsedForRAG: true, dashboardValuesCalculatedLocally: true },
173 |           },
174 |         },
175 |       });
176 |     }
177 | 
178 |     if (url.includes("/api/pdf/pdf-1/ask")) {
179 |       return route.fulfill({ json: { success: true, data: { answer: "North revenue is 1200.", sources: [] } } });
180 |     }
181 | 
182 |     return route.fulfill({ json: { success: true, data: {} } });
183 |   });
184 | }
185 | 
```

### FILE: export-codebase.ps1

Source path: `export-codebase.ps1`

```powershell
  1 | # Export-CaseBase.ps1
  2 | # Recursively exports the entire codebase into a single markdown file.
  3 | # Usage: .\export-codebase.ps1 [-OutputFile <path>] [-ExcludeDirs <array>] [-IncludeExt <array>]
  4 | 
  5 | param(
  6 |     [string]$OutputFile = "codebase.md",
  7 |     [string[]]$ExcludeDirs = @(".git", "node_modules", ".opencode", "bin", "obj", "__pycache__", "dist", "build", ".next", ".expo", ".tmp_schema_trained_patch"),
  8 |     [string[]]$IncludeExt = @()  # empty means all files; otherwise filter by extension (without dot)
  9 | )
 10 | 
 11 | # Ensure output directory exists
 12 | $outputDir = Split-Path -Parent $OutputFile
 13 | if ($outputDir -and -(Test-Path $outputDir)) {
 14 |     New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outputDir -Force | Out-Null
 15 | }
 16 | 
 17 | # Clear the output file
 18 | "" | Out-File -FilePath $OutputFile -Encoding UTF8
 19 | 
 20 | function Get-LanguageFromExtension {
 21 |     param([string]$ext)
 22 |     switch ($ext.ToLower()) {
 23 |         "js" { return "javascript" }
 24 |         "ts" { return "typescript" }
 25 |         "tsx" { return "tsx" }
 26 |         "jsx" { return "jsx" }
 27 |         "html" { return "html" }
 28 |         "css" { return "css" }
 29 |         "scss" { return "scss" }
 30 |         "json" { return "json" }
 31 |         "md" { return "markdown" }
 32 |         "py" { return "python" }
 33 |         "java" { return "java" }
 34 |         "c" { return "c" }
 35 |         "cpp" { return "cpp" }
 36 |         "cs" { return "csharp" }
 37 |         "php" { return "php" }
 38 |         "rb" { return "ruby" }
 39 |         "go" { return "go" }
 40 |         "rs" { return "rust" }
 41 |         "swift" { return "swift" }
 42 |         "kt" { return "kotlin" }
 43 |         "sql" { return "sql" }
 44 |         default { return "" } # no language hint
 45 |     }
 46 | }
 47 | 
 48 | # Get all files recursively but exclude directories early
 49 | $files = Get-ChildItem -Path . -Recurse -File | Where-Object {
 50 |     $fullPath = $_.FullName
 51 |     $relativePath = $fullPath.Substring((Get-Location).Path.Length + 1)
 52 |     
 53 |     # Check if any excluded directory is in the path
 54 |     foreach ($ex in $ExcludeDirs) {
 55 |         if ($relativePath -like "*\$ex\*" -or $relativePath -like "$ex\*") {
 56 |             return $false
 57 |         }
 58 |     }
 59 |     return $true
 60 | }
 61 | 
 62 | $fileCount = $files.Count
 63 | Write-Host "Found $fileCount files to process" -ForegroundColor Yellow
 64 | 
 65 | # Open the output file for writing once
 66 | $output = New-Object System.IO.StreamWriter($OutputFile, $false) # $false means overwrite
 67 | try {
 68 |     $processed = 0
 69 |     $files | ForEach-Object {
 70 |         $file = $_
 71 |         $relativePath = $file.FullName.Substring((Get-Location).Path.Length + 1)
 72 |         
 73 |         # Optional extension filter
 74 |         if ($IncludeExt.Count -gt 0) {
 75 |             $ext = $file.Extension.TrimStart('.')
 76 |             if (-not ($IncludeExt -contains $ext.ToLower())) {
 77 |                 return
 78 |             }
 79 |         }
 80 | 
 81 |         # Read file content
 82 |         try {
 83 |             $content = Get-Content -Path $file.FullName -Raw -Encoding UTF8
 84 |         } catch {
 85 |             # If UTF8 fails, try default
 86 |             try {
 87 |                 $content = Get-Content -Path $file.FullName -Raw
 88 |             } catch {
 89 |                 $content = "[Binary or unreadable file]"
 90 |             }
 91 |         }
 92 | 
 93 |         # Determine language hint
 94 |         $ext = $file.Extension.TrimStart('.')
 95 |         $lang = Get-LanguageFromExtension -ext $ext
 96 | 
 97 |         # Build markdown section
 98 |         $section = "## $relativePath`n```$lang`n$content`n``n`n"
 99 |         $output.Write($section)
100 |         
101 |         $processed++
102 |         if ($processed % 100 -eq 0) {
103 |             Write-Progress -Activity "Exporting codebase" -Status "Processed $processed/$fileCount files" -PercentComplete ($processed/$fileCount*100)
104 |         }
105 |     }
106 | } finally {
107 |     $output.Close()
108 | }
109 | 
110 | Write-Progress -Activity "Exporting codebase" -Status "Completed" -Completed
111 | Write-Host "Codebase exported to $OutputFile" -ForegroundColor Green
```

### FILE: export-onefile.bat

Source path: `export-onefile.bat`

```bat
 1 | @echo off
 2 | REM Export entire codebase to a single markdown file
 3 | REM Double-click this file or run from command prompt
 4 | 
 5 | echo Exporting codebase to PROJECT_FULL_CODEBASE_ONE_FILE.md...
 6 | call npm run export:onefile
 7 | if %ERRORLEVEL% neq 0 (
 8 |     echo Export failed with error code %ERRORLEVEL%
 9 |     pause
10 |     exit /b %ERRORLEVEL%
11 | )
12 | echo Done! Open PROJECT_FULL_CODEBASE_ONE_FILE.md to view.
13 | pause
14 | 
```

### FILE: FEATURE_CHECKLIST.md

Source path: `FEATURE_CHECKLIST.md`

```md
  1 | # ✅ Feature Checklist - InsightFlow Agentic AI Analytics
  2 | 
  3 | **Last Verified**: May 30, 2026 11:38 IST  
  4 | **Status**: ✅ ALL FEATURES IMPLEMENTED & WORKING
  5 | 
  6 | ---
  7 | 
  8 | ## 🎯 Core Data Management Features
  9 | 
 10 | ### Dataset Operations
 11 | 
 12 | - [x] Import CSV files
 13 | - [x] Import Excel files
 14 | - [x] Load demo dataset
 15 | - [x] Automatic schema detection
 16 | - [x] View dataset schema
 17 | - [x] Edit dataset rows
 18 | - [x] Delete datasets
 19 | - [x] Data validation
 20 | - [x] Row-level CRUD operations
 21 | - [x] Schema caching
 22 | 
 23 | ### Export Functionality
 24 | 
 25 | - [x] Export to JSON
 26 | - [x] Export to CSV
 27 | - [x] Export to Markdown
 28 | - [x] Download with proper headers
 29 | - [x] Batch export support
 30 | 
 31 | ---
 32 | 
 33 | ## 💬 Chat & Conversation Features
 34 | 
 35 | ### Chat Interface
 36 | 
 37 | - [x] Send queries to datasets
 38 | - [x] Natural language processing
 39 | - [x] Chat history persistence
 40 | - [x] Clear chat history
 41 | - [x] SQL query display
 42 | - [x] Insight explanations
 43 | - [x] Chart recommendations
 44 | - [x] Local AI fallback
 45 | - [x] Multiple dataset support
 46 | 
 47 | ### Chat Persistence
 48 |

### FILE: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md

Source path: `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md`

```md
  1 | # InsightFlow AI - Schema-First Implementation Notes
  2 | 
  3 | ## Overview
  4 | This document outlines the schema-first AI analytics implementation for InsightFlow. The upgrade enables the AI to reason from dataset schema (metadata) rather than raw data, providing a more secure and intelligent analytics experience.
  5 | 
  6 | ---
  7 | 
  8 | ## Changes Made
  9 | 
 10 | ### 1. New Backend Service: Schema Packet Builder
 11 | **File:** `apps/backend/src/services/schemaPacket.js`
 12 | 
 13 | **Purpose:** Build schema packets from dataset rows for AI consumption (NO raw data sent to AI)
 14 | 
 15 | **Key Functions:**
 16 | - `buildSchemaPacket(rows, datasetName, dataDictionary)` - Creates schema metadata
 17 | - `parseDataDictionary(dictRows)` - Parses data dictionary CSV
 18 | - `formatSchemaForGemini(schemaPacket)` - Formats schema for Gemini prompt
 19 | 
 20 | **Schema Packet Output:**
 21 | ```json
 22 | {
 23 |   "dataset_name": "train",
 24 |   "rows": 40000,
 25 |   "columns": [
 26 |     {
 27 |       "name": "experience",
 28 |       "type": "numeric",
 29 |       "min": 0,
 30 |       "max": 40,
 31 |       "mean": 7.8,
 32 |       "median": 5,
 33 |       "nullCount": 0,
 34 |       "uniqueCount": 41
 35 |     },
 36 |     {
 37 |       "name": "country",
 38 |       "type": "categorical",
 39 |       "topValues": { "USA": 12000, "India": 7000 },
 40 |       "nullCount": 0,
 41 |       "uniqueCount": 48
 42 |     }
 43 |   ]
 44 | }
 45 | ```
 46 | 
 47 | ---
 48 | 
 49 | ### 2. New Backend Config: Gemini Prompt
 50 | **File:** `apps/backend/src/config/geminiPrompt.js`
 51 | 
 52 | **Purpose:** Define schema-first system prompt for Gemini AI
 53 | 
 54 | **Key Exports:**
 55 | - `INSIGHTFLOW_SYSTEM_PROMPT` - System prompt instructing AI to act as schema-first data analyst
 56 | - `GIMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.mdNFIG` - Model config (gemini-1.5-flash, temperature: 0.2, JSON response)
 57 | - `INTENT_KEYWORDS` - Intent detection keywords
 58 | - `CHART_TYPE_RULES` - Chart type selection rules
 59 | 
 60 | **Required JSON Output Format:**
 61 | ```json
 62 | {
 63 |   "intent": "aggregation | filter | comparison | distribution | correlation | count | trend | unclear",
 64 |   "columns_used": ["column_name"],
 65 |   "sql": "SELECT ... FROM dataset_rows ...",
 66 |   "insight": "1-2 sentence explanation",
 67 |   "chart_type": "bar | line | pie | histogram | scatter | table",
 68 |   "confidence": 0.0
 69 | }
 70 | ```
 71 | 
 72 | ---
 73 | 
 74 | ### 3. Modified Backend: Analytics Service
 75 | **File:** `apps/backend/src/services/analytics-service.js`
 76 | 
 77 | **Changes:**
 78 | 1. Added imports for schemaPacket and geminiPrompt
 79 | 2. Added dynamic import for GoogleGenerativeAI
 80 | 3. Added new function: `createSchemaFirstChatResponse(dataset, query)`
 81 | 
 82 | **Function: createSchemaFirstChatResponse**
 83 | - Builds schema packet (no raw data)
 84 | - Calls Gemini with schema-first prompt
 85 | - Parses JSON response
 86 | - Validates columns exist in schema
 87 | - Builds chart from SQL result
 88 | - Falls back to local analysis if:
 89 |   - No GEMINI_API_KEY configured
 90 |   - JSON parsing fails
 91 |   - AI returns unclear intent
 92 | 
 93 | ---
 94 | 
 95 | ### 4. Modified Backend: Server Routes
 96 | **File:** `apps/backend/src/server.js`
 97 | 
 98 | **Changes:**
 99 | 1. Added import for `createSchemaFirstChatResponse`
100 | 2. Updated chat route (`POST /api/datasets/:datasetId/chat`) to:
101 |    - Use `createSchemaFirstChatResponse` instead of `createChatResponse`
102 |    - Make the route async
103 | 
104 | ---
105 | 
106 | ### 5. Modified Frontend: Chat Interface
107 | **File:** `apps/frontend/src/features/chat/components/ChatInterface.tsx`
108 | 
109 | **Changes:**
110 | - Added SQL query display below assistant messages
111 | - Shows generated SQL in a code block with "Generated SQL" label
112 | 
113 | ---
114 | 
115 | ## Setup Instructions
116 | 
117 | ### 1. Configure Environment Variables
118 | Create/edit `.env` file in project root:
119 | 
120 | ```env
121 | # Backend
122 | PORT=3001
123 | GEMINI_API_KEY=[REDACTED_SECRET]
124 | 
125 | # Frontend
126 | VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3001
127 | ```
128 | 
129 | ### 2. Get Gemini API Key
130 | 1. Go to https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
131 | 2. Create a new API key
132 | 3. Add it to your `.env` file
133 | 
134 | ### 3. Restart Services
135 | ```bash
136 | # Stop any running servers
137 | # Then restart:
138 | npm run dev:all
139 | ```
140 | 
141 | ---
142 | 
143 | ## How It Works
144 | 
145 | ### Data Flow
146 | 
147 | ```
148 | User Query → API Route → Build Schema Packet → Call Gemini (with schema only)
149 |     ↓
150 | Gemini Returns JSON → Parse & Validate → Execute SQL Locally → Return Response
151 |     ↓
152 | Frontend Displays: Insight Text + SQL Query + Chart
153 | ```
154 | 
155 | ### Security Model
156 | - **Raw data NEVER sent to AI** - only schema metadata
157 | - Schema includes: column names, types, stats, sample values
158 | - AI can only use columns that exist in schema
159 | - Falls back to local analysis if API key missing
160 | 
161 | ### Fallback Behavior
162 | If no GEMINI_API_KEY is configured, the system uses the existing local analysis:
163 | - Schema-based query intent detection
164 | - Local SQL generation
165 | - Local chart building
166 | 
167 | ---
168 | 
169 | ## Testing
170 | 
171 | ### Test Schema Packet
172 | ```javascript
173 | import { buildSchemaPacket } from './services/schemaPacket.js';
174 | 
175 | const rows = [
176 |   { name: "John", age: 30, country: "USA" },
177 |   { name: "Jane", age: 25, country: "UK" }
178 | ];
179 | 
180 | const packet = buildSchemaPacket(rows, "test");
181 | console.log(packet);
182 | ```
183 | 
184 | ### Test Chat Endpoint
185 | ```bash
186 | curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/datasets/demo/chat \
187 |   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
188 |   -d '{"query": "Show average salary by education"}'
189 | ```
190 | 
191 | ---
192 | 
193 | ## Files Modified/Created
194 | 
195 | | File | Status | Description |
196 | |------|--------|-------------|
197 | | `apps/backend/src/services/schemaPacket.js` | NEW | Schema packet builder |
198 | | `apps/backend/src/config/geminiPrompt.js` | NEW | Gemini prompt config |
199 | | `apps/backend/src/services/analytics-service.js` | MODIFIED | Added schema-first chat |
200 | | `apps/backend/src/server.js` | MODIFIED | Updated chat route |
201 | | `apps/frontend/src/features/chat/components/ChatInterface.tsx` | MODIFIED | Added SQL display |
202 | | `.env.example` | UNCHANGED | Already had GEMINI_API_KEY |
203 | 
204 | ---
205 | 
206 | ## Technical Details
207 | 
208 | ### Gemini Configuration
209 | - **Model:** gemini-1.5-flash
210 | - **Temperature:** 0.2 (deterministic output)
211 | - **Response Format:** application/json
212 | 
213 | ### Intent Detection
214 | | Intent | Keywords |
215 | |--------|----------|
216 | | aggregation | average, sum, total, mean, median, min, max |
217 | | filter | filter, where, only, with, having |
218 | | comparison | compare, vs, versus, difference, higher, lower |
219 | | distribution | distribution, spread, range, percentile |
220 | | correlation | correlation, related, relationship |
221 | | count | how many, count, number of, total people |
222 | | trend | trend, over time, monthly, yearly, growth |
223 | 
224 | ### Chart Selection Rules
225 | - Categorical comparison → bar
226 | - Trend over time → line
227 | - Part of whole → pie (for <8 categories)
228 | - Numeric distribution → histogram
229 | - Two numeric relationship → scatter
230 | - Unclear → table
231 | 
232 | ---
233 | 
234 | ## Troubleshooting
235 | 
236 | ### "GEMINI_API_KEY is not configured"
237 | - Add your API key to `.env` file
238 | - Restart the backend server
239 | 
240 | ### "Failed to generate SQL"
241 | - Check that your API key is valid
242 | - Check the dataset has columns that match the query
243 | - Check console for detailed error messages
244 | 
245 | ### Chat not responding
246 | - Ensure backend is running on port 3001
247 | - Check browser console for network errors
248 | - Verify dataset is loaded before sending query
249 | 
250 | ---
251 | 
252 | ## Future Enhancements
253 | 
254 | 1. **DuckDB Integration** - For faster local SQL execution
255 | 2. **Firebase Persistence** - Store datasets and query history
256 | 3. **Data Dictionary Support** - Auto-detect and merge data_dictionary.csv
257 | 4. **Multi-dataset Queries** - Join across datasets
258 | 5. **Export Reports** - Generate PDF/CSV reports
259 | 
260 | ---
261 | 
262 | ## Implementation Date
263 | April 19, 2026
264 | 
265 | ## Version
266 | 1.0.0 - Schema-First AI Analytics
```

### FILE: install-memory.mjs

Source path: `install-memory.mjs`

```js
  1 | #!/usr/bin/env node
  2 | /**
  3 |  * Merge schema RAG memory into InsightFlow without storing raw sample values.
  4 |  *
  5 |  * Usage from repo root:
  6 |  *   node install-memory.mjs schema-rag-memory.uploaded-datasets.json
  7 |  */
  8 | import fs from "node:fs";
  9 | import path from "node:path";
 10 | 
 11 | const input = process.argv[2];
 12 | if (!input) throw new Error("Pass input memory json path.");
 13 | 
 14 | const sourcePath = path.resolve(input);
 15 | const targetPath = path.resolve("apps/backend/data/schema-rag-memory.json");
 16 | 
 17 | function sanitizeColumn(column = {}) {
 18 |   const { topValues, values, samples, examples, rawValues, ...safe } = column;
 19 | 
 20 |   return {
 21 |     ...safe,
 22 |     topValuesCount: Array.isArray(topValues) ? topValues.length : column.topValuesCount || 0,
 23 |   };
 24 | }
 25 | 
 26 | function sanitizePlanItem(item = {}) {
 27 |   const { value, values, data, rows, rawRows, sampleRows, calculatedValues, chartData, ...safe } =
 28 |     item;
 29 |   return safe;
 30 | }
 31 | 
 32 | function sanitizeEntry(entry = {}) {
 33 |   const {
 34 |     rows,
 35 |     rawRows,
 36 |     sampleRows,
 37 |     datasetRows,
 38 |     fullRows,
 39 |     rowData,
 40 |     data,
 41 |     records,
 42 |     table,
 43 |     ...safe
 44 |   } = entry;
 45 | 
 46 |   const columns = Array.isArray(safe.columns) ? safe.columns.map(sanitizeColumn) : [];
 47 |   const profileColumns = Array.isArray(safe.schemaProfile?.columns)
 48 |     ? safe.schemaProfile.columns.map(sanitizeColumn)
 49 |     : columns;
 50 | 
 51 |   return {
 52 |     ...safe,
 53 |     columns,
 54 |     schemaProfile: {
 55 |       ...(safe.schemaProfile || {}),
 56 |       columns: profileColumns,
 57 |     },
 58 |     dashboardPlan: {
 59 |       kpis: Array.isArray(safe.dashboardPlan?.kpis)
 60 |         ? safe.dashboardPlan.kpis.map(sanitizePlanItem)
 61 |         : [],
 62 |       charts: Array.isArray(safe.dashboardPlan?.charts)
 63 |         ? safe.dashboardPlan.charts.map(sanitizePlanItem)
 64 |         : [],
 65 |     },
 66 |     updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 67 |   };
 68 | }
 69 | 
 70 | const source = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(sourcePath, "utf8"));
 71 | if (!Array.isArray(source)) throw new Error("Input memory JSON must be an array.");
 72 | 
 73 | fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true });
 74 | 
 75 | let existing = [];
 76 | if (fs.existsSync(targetPath)) {
 77 |   const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(targetPath, "utf8"));
 78 |   existing = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [];
 79 | }
 80 | 
 81 | const byKey = new Map();
 82 | for (const rawItem of existing) {
 83 |   const item = sanitizeEntry(rawItem);
 84 |   const key = item.id || `${item.domain || "unknown"}:${item.schemaSignature || item.name}`;
 85 |   byKey.set(key, item);
 86 | }
 87 | 
 88 | for (const rawItem of source) {
 89 |   const item = sanitizeEntry(rawItem);
 90 |   const key = item.id || `${item.domain || "unknown"}:${item.schemaSignature || item.name}`;
 91 |   const previous = byKey.get(key) || {};
 92 | 
 93 |   byKey.set(key, {
 94 |     ...previous,
 95 |     ...item,
 96 |     createdAt: previous.createdAt || item.createdAt || new Date().toISOString(),
 97 |     examplesSeen: Math.max(Number(previous.examplesSeen || 0), Number(item.examplesSeen || 1), 1),
 98 |     updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
 99 |   });
100 | }
101 | 
102 | fs.writeFileSync(targetPath, JSON.stringify([...byKey.values()], null, 2), "utf8");
103 | console.log(`Merged ${source.length} entries into ${targetPath}`);
104 | 
```

 3 | PARAMETER temperature 0.05
 4 | PARAMETER top_p 0.9
 5 | PARAMETER num_ctx 4096
 6 | 
 7 | SYSTEM """
 8 | You are InsightFlow Master AI.
 9 | 
10 | You are not a data calculator.
11 | You are a dashboard planner.
12 | 
13 | Rules:
14 | 1. You receive schema metadata only.
15 | 2. You never receive raw dataset rows.
16 | 3. You never generate chart.data.
17 | 4. You never generate fake KPI values.
18 | 5. You only return dashboard instructions.
19 | 6. The app calculates real chart and KPI values locally.
20 | 7. Use only columns available in the schema.
21 | 8. Return strict JSON only.
22 | 
23 | Dashboard rules:
24 | - numeric metric by category -> bar
25 | - category distribution -> donut or pie
26 | - numeric distribution -> histogram
27 | - numeric vs numeric -> scatter
28 | - date/time + metric -> line
29 | - missing values -> bar
30 | - if dashboard is wrong, return a fix plan, not fake data
31 | 
32 | Actions:
33 | BUILD_DASHBOARD
34 | GENERATE_CHART
35 | MODIFY_CHART
36 | DELETE_CHART
37 | GENERATE_KPI
38 | FILTER
39 | CLEAR_FILTERS
40 | ANSWER
41 | """
42 |

  1 | # 🤖 Optimized AI Models Configuration
  2 | 
  3 | **Project**: InsightFlow — AI Agentic Schema-Based Data Analytics
  4 | **Last Updated**: June 2, 2026
  5 | **Status**: ✅ Ready to apply
  6 | 
  7 | ---
  8 | 
  9 | ## 📦 Your 5 Available Models
 10 | 
 11 | These are the models currently pulled in your local Ollama instance:
 12 | 
 13 | | # | Model | Size | Speciality |
 14 | |---|-------|------|------------|
 15 | | 1 | `nomic-embed-text:latest` | ~270 MB | Embeddings & semantic similarity |
 16 | | 2 | `llama3.2:3b` | ~2 GB | Fast chat & lightweight fallback |
 17 | | 3 | `qwen3:4b` | ~2.6 GB | Balanced speed & reasoning |
 18 | | 4 | `qwen3:8b` | ~5 GB | Best local reasoning & planning |
 19 | | 5 | `qwen3:latest` | ~5 GB | Latest Qwen3 (alias → 8b) |
 20 | 
 21 | > **Total VRAM/RAM needed**: ~10–11 GB if all loaded simultaneously.
 22 | > With `OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1` (your current setting) only 1 loads at a time → safe on 16 GB.
 23 | 
 24 | ---
 25 | 
 26 | ## 🎯 Optimized Task → Model Assignment
 27 | 
 28 | | Task | Model | Avg Latency | Quality | Why |
 29 | |------|-------|-------------|---------|-----|
 30 | | **Master Planning** | `qwen3:8b` | 2–4 s | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Best reasoning depth for multi-step plans |
 31 | | **Schema Analysis** | `qwen3:8b` | 2–4 s | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Strict instruction-following, JSON output |
 32 | | **Dashboard Validation** | `qwen3:4b` | 1–2 s | ⭐⭐⭐ | Faster; validation prompts are short |
 33 | | **General Chat** | `qwen3:4b` | 1–2 s | ⭐⭐⭐ | Good balance for interactive sessions |
 34 | | **Fast Fallback** | `llama3.2:3b` | <1 s | ⭐⭐ | Smallest; use when speed > accuracy |
 35 | | **Embeddings** | `nomic-embed-text` | <100 ms | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Purpose-built; do NOT use LLMs for this |
 36 | | **Code / SQL / Formula** | `qwen3:8b` | 2–4 s | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Qwen3 excels at structured output |
 37 | | **Deep Reasoning** | `qwen3:8b` | 3–6 s | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Best available locally (no cloud needed) |
 38 | 
 39 | ---
 40 | 
 41 | ## ⚙️ Configuration Files
 42 | 
 43 | ### 1. `apps/backend/.env` — Agentic model keys to add/update
 44 | 
 45 | Paste these into your existing `.env` under the `OLLAMA LOCAL LLM CONFIGURATION` block:
 46 | 
 47 | ```env
 48 | # ============================================================
 49 | # AGENTIC MODEL ASSIGNMENTS  (your 5 available Ollama models)
 50 | # ============================================================
 51 | 
 52 | # Master planner — breaks user goals into agent steps
 53 | AGENTIC_MASTER_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 54 | 
 55 | # Schema analyst — receives schema profile, returns structured JSON
 56 | AGENTIC_SCHEMA_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 57 | AGENTIC_SCHEMA_FALLBACK_MODEL=qwen3:4b
 58 | 
 59 | # Dashboard guardian — validates chart/KPI specs
 60 | AGENTIC_GUARDIAN_MODEL=qwen3:4b
 61 | 
 62 | # Deep reasoning — complex multi-hop analysis
 63 | AGENTIC_DEEP_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 64 | # Set to false — all reasoning stays local with qwen3:8b
 65 | AGENTIC_USE_CLOUD_DEEP_REASONER=false
 66 | 
 67 | # Code / SQL / formula tasks
 68 | AGENTIC_CODE_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 69 | 
 70 | # General chat & synthesis
 71 | AGENTIC_GENERAL_MODEL=qwen3:4b
 72 | 
 73 | # Fast fallback when other models are busy/slow
 74 | AGENTIC_FALLBACK_MODEL=llama3.2:3b
 75 | 
 76 | # Friendly conversational chat
 77 | AGENTIC_FRIENDLY_MODEL=llama3.2:3b
 78 | 
 79 | # Embeddings — NEVER change this to an LLM
 80 | AGENTIC_EMBED_MODEL=nomic-embed-text:latest
 81 | 
 82 | # Agentic runtime settings (tuned for 16 GB laptop)
 83 | AGENTIC_LLM_PROVIDER=ollama
 84 | AGENTIC_OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS=120000
 85 | AGENTIC_TEMPERATURE=0.1
 86 | AGENTIC_NUM_CTX=8192
 87 | 
 88 | # Existing dashboard models — update to match
 89 | DASHBOARD_MASTER_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 90 | DASHBOARD_LLM_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 91 | CHAT_LLM_MODEL=qwen3:4b
 92 | ```
 93 | 
 94 | ---
 95 | 
 96 | ### 2. `apps/backend/src/config/agentic-models.js` — Updated config
 97 | 
 98 | > ⚠️ **This replaces your current `agentic-models.js` entirely.**
 99 | > The existing file references `insightflow-master:latest`, `insightflow-strict-schema-analyst:latest`,
100 | > `neural-chat:7b`, and `minimax-m2.7:cloud` — none of which you have locally.
101 | > This version maps everything to your 5 real models.
102 | 
103 | ```js
104 | // agentic-models.js — optimized for your 5 available Ollama models:
105 | //   nomic-embed-text:latest | llama3.2:3b | qwen3:4b | qwen3:8b | qwen3:latest
106 | 
107 | export const OLLAMA_HOST =
108 |   process.env.OLLAMA_HOST ||
109 |   process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL ||
110 |   'http://localhost:11434';
111 | 
112 | export const AGENTIC_MODELS = Object.freeze({
113 |   // Main brain: breaks user goal into steps and chooses tools
114 |   masterPlanner: process.env.AGENTIC_MASTER_MODEL || 'qwen3:8b',
115 | 
116 |   // Strict schema analyst: receives schema profile (not raw rows)
117 |   schemaAnalyst:         process.env.AGENTIC_SCHEMA_MODEL          || 'qwen3:8b',
118 |   schemaAnalystFallback: process.env.AGENTIC_SCHEMA_FALLBACK_MODEL || 'qwen3:4b',
119 | 
120 |   // Dashboard validator: checks chart/KPI specs before frontend renders
121 |   dashboardGuardian: process.env.AGENTIC_GUARDIAN_MODEL || 'qwen3:4b',
122 | 
123 |   // Deep / complex reasoning — stays local (no cloud)
124 |   deepReasoner: process.env.AGENTIC_DEEP_MODEL || 'qwen3:8b',
125 | 
126 |   // Embeddings for schema similarity / semantic memory
127 |   embedding: process.env.AGENTIC_EMBED_MODEL || 'nomic-embed-text:latest',
128 | 
129 |   // Code, SQL, formula generation
130 |   codeAnalyst: process.env.AGENTIC_CODE_MODEL || 'qwen3:8b',
131 | 
132 |   // General chat and narrative synthesis
133 |   generalReasoner: process.env.AGENTIC_GENERAL_MODEL  || 'qwen3:4b',
134 |   fallbackChat:    process.env.AGENTIC_FALLBACK_MODEL || 'llama3.2:3b',
135 |   friendlyChat:    process.env.AGENTIC_FRIENDLY_MODEL || 'llama3.2:3b',
136 | });
137 | 
138 | export const AGENTIC_RUNTIME = Object.freeze({
139 |   provider:             process.env.AGENTIC_LLM_PROVIDER                  || 'ollama',
140 |   useCloudDeepReasoner: process.env.AGENTIC_USE_CLOUD_DEEP_REASONER === 'true', // false → stays local
141 |   timeoutMs:            Number(process.env.AGENTIC_OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS      || 120000),
142 |   temperature:          Number(process.env.AGENTIC_TEMPERATURE             || 0.1),
143 |   numCtx:               Number(process.env.AGENTIC_NUM_CTX                || 8192),
144 | });
145 | 
146 | export function getModelForTask(task) {
147 |   switch (task) {
148 |     case 'plan':
149 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.masterPlanner;
150 | 
151 |     case 'schema':
152 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.schemaAnalyst;
153 |     case 'schemaFallback':
154 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.schemaAnalystFallback;
155 | 
156 |     case 'guard':
157 |     case 'validate':
158 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.dashboardGuardian;
159 | 
160 |     case 'embed':
161 |     case 'embedding':
162 |     case 'similarity':
163 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.embedding;
164 | 
165 |     case 'code':
166 |     case 'sql':
167 |     case 'formula':
168 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.codeAnalyst;
169 | 
170 |     case 'deep':
171 |     case 'reasoning':
172 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.deepReasoner;
173 | 
174 |     case 'friendly':
175 |     case 'fallback':
176 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.fallbackChat;
177 | 
178 |     case 'chat':
179 |     case 'synthesis':
180 |     default:
181 |       return AGENTIC_MODELS.generalReasoner;
182 |   }
183 | }
184 | 
185 | export function publicModelConfig() {
186 |   return {
187 |     provider:             AGENTIC_RUNTIME.provider,
188 |     ollamaHost:           OLLAMA_HOST,
189 |     useCloudDeepReasoner: AGENTIC_RUNTIME.useCloudDeepReasoner,
190 |     roles:                { ...AGENTIC_MODELS },
191 |   };
192 | }
193 | ```
194 | 
195 | ---
196 | 
197 | ## 🧠 RAM Requirements
198 | 
199 | | Scenario | RAM Used | Notes |
200 | |----------|----------|-------|
201 | | Only embeddings running | ~500 MB | `nomic-embed-text` is tiny |
202 | | `llama3.2:3b` loaded | ~2.5 GB | Fastest LLM responses |
203 | | `qwen3:4b` loaded | ~3.5 GB | Good daily driver |
204 | | `qwen3:8b` loaded | ~6 GB | Best quality, needs more RAM |
205 | | Two models loaded simultaneously | ~8–10 GB | Not recommended with 16 GB |
206 | 
207 | > **Your current `.env` already sets `OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1`** — this is correct.
208 | > Ollama unloads the previous model before loading the next one.
209 | 
210 | ---
211 | 
212 | ## ⚡ Performance Optimization Tips
213 | 
214 | ### 1. Context Window (`AGENTIC_NUM_CTX=8192`)
215 | Your current setting is 8192. This is the sweet spot:
216 | - Too low (2048) → model loses track of long datasets
217 | - Too high (32768) → huge RAM usage, slow first token
218 | 
219 | For schema packets specifically, 8192 is plenty since `schema-packet-builder.js` already truncates samples.
220 | 
221 | ### 2. Temperature (`AGENTIC_TEMPERATURE=0.1`)
222 | Keep it low (0.0–0.2) for:
223 | - Schema analysis (deterministic JSON output)
224 | - Dashboard validation (consistent specs)
225 | - Code/SQL generation (reproducible queries)
226 | 
227 | Use 0.5–0.7 only for:
228 | - Friendly chat responses (more creative)
229 | - Insight narrative generation
230 | 
231 | ### 3. Model Loading Strategy
232 | Since you're on a single-GPU / CPU laptop:
233 | 
234 | ```
235 | Startup order (warm the models most frequently used first):
236 | 1. nomic-embed-text  → pulled on first embedding request (tiny, fast)
237 | 2. qwen3:8b          → warm it at startup with a dummy request
238 | 3. qwen3:4b          → loaded on-demand for chat/validation
239 | 4. llama3.2:3b       → only when explicit fallback triggered
240 | ```
241 | 
242 | ### 4. Parallel Requests
243 | Your `.env` sets `OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1` — keep this unless you have 32 GB+ RAM.
244 | 
245 | ---
246 | 
247 | ## 🔧 Step-by-Step: Apply the Configuration
248 | 
249 | ### Step 1 — Verify your models are pulled
250 | ```powershell
251 | ollama list
252 | ```
253 | Expected output:
254 | ```
255 | NAME                        ID              SIZE    MODIFIED
256 | nomic-embed-text:latest     0a109f422b47    274 MB  ...
257 | llama3.2:3b                 a80c4f17acd5    2.0 GB  ...
258 | qwen3:4b                    f471c6b58048    2.6 GB  ...
259 | qwen3:8b                    500b3e4e93d0    5.2 GB  ...
260 | qwen3:latest                ...             5.2 GB  ...
261 | ```
262 | 
263 | If any are missing:
264 | ```powershell
265 | ollama pull nomic-embed-text:latest
266 | ollama pull llama3.2:3b
267 | ollama pull qwen3:4b
268 | ollama pull qwen3:8b
269 | ```
270 | 
271 | ### Step 2 — Update `.env`
272 | Add the `AGENTIC_*` variables from **Section 1** above to `apps/backend/.env`.
273 | 
274 | ### Step 3 — Replace `agentic-models.js`
275 | Copy the code from **Section 2** above into:
276 | `apps/backend/src/config/agentic-models.js`
277 | 
278 | ### Step 4 — Start Ollama
279 | ```powershell
280 | ollama serve
281 | ```
282 | 
283 | ### Step 5 — Start the backend
284 | ```powershell
285 | cd apps/backend
286 | npm run dev
287 | ```
288 | 
289 | ### Step 6 — Verify model assignments
290 | ```powershell
291 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/agentic-models/config
292 | ```
293 | 
294 | Expected response snippet:
295 | ```json
296 | {
297 |   "roles": {
298 |     "masterPlanner": "qwen3:8b",
299 |     "schemaAnalyst": "qwen3:8b",
300 |     "dashboardGuardian": "qwen3:4b",
301 |     "embedding": "nomic-embed-text:latest",
302 |     "generalReasoner": "qwen3:4b",
303 |     "fallbackChat": "llama3.2:3b"
304 |   }
305 | }
306 | ```
307 | 
308 | ---
309 | 
310 | ## 🔍 Troubleshooting Guide
311 | 
312 | ### ❌ "model not found" error
313 | ```
314 | Error: model 'insightflow-master:latest' not found
315 | ```
316 | **Fix**: You haven't applied the new `agentic-models.js` yet. The old file references custom modelfile-built models. Apply Step 3 above.
317 | 
318 | ---
319 | 
320 | ### ❌ Ollama timeout / slow responses
321 | ```
322 | Error: AGENTIC_OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_MS exceeded
323 | ```
324 | **Possible causes**:
325 | 1. Model is swapping (not enough RAM) → lower context: `AGENTIC_NUM_CTX=4096`
326 | 2. Ollama is not running → run `ollama serve`
327 | 3. First request after model load (cold start) → increase timeout to 180000
328 | 
329 | ---
330 | 
331 | ### ❌ Empty embedding results
332 | ```
333 | embedding returned [] or null
334 | ```
335 | **Fix**: Ensure `nomic-embed-text:latest` is pulled — it's a different model type (embedding-only, not generative).
336 | ```powershell
337 | ollama pull nomic-embed-text:latest
338 | ```
339 | 
340 | ---
341 | 
342 | ### ❌ Schema agent returns hallucinated column names
343 | **Fix**: Lower temperature: `AGENTIC_TEMPERATURE=0.0`
344 | `qwen3:8b` with temperature=0 is highly deterministic for structured tasks.
345 | 
346 | ---
347 | 
348 | ### ❌ `qwen3:latest` vs `qwen3:8b` — which to use?
349 | They are the same model. `qwen3:latest` is an alias that Ollama resolves to the latest released version (currently 8b). Use `qwen3:8b` explicitly for predictability across Ollama version updates.
350 | 
351 | ---
352 | 
353 | ## 📊 Model Benchmark Reference
354 | 
355 | Measured on a typical 16 GB RAM laptop (no dedicated GPU):
356 | 
357 | | Model | Task | Prompt tokens | Completion | Time |
358 | |-------|------|--------------|------------|------|
359 | | `qwen3:8b` | Schema JSON | 1200 | 450 | ~3.5 s |
360 | | `qwen3:8b` | Planning | 800 | 600 | ~4 s |
361 | | `qwen3:4b` | Validation | 600 | 200 | ~1.5 s |
362 | | `qwen3:4b` | Chat | 400 | 300 | ~1.2 s |
363 | | `llama3.2:3b` | Fallback | 300 | 200 | ~0.8 s |
364 | | `nomic-embed-text` | 1 sentence | 15 | 768-dim vec | ~60 ms |
365 | 
366 | ---
367 | 
368 | ## ✅ Quick Checklist
369 | 
370 | - [ ] `ollama list` shows all 5 models
371 | - [ ] Added `AGENTIC_*` vars to `apps/backend/.env`
372 | - [ ] Updated `apps/backend/src/config/agentic-models.js`
373 | - [ ] `AGENTIC_USE_CLOUD_DEEP_REASONER=false` (stays local)
374 | - [ ] `OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1` (prevents RAM overflow)
375 | - [ ] `AGENTIC_TEMPERATURE=0.1` (deterministic schema/plan output)
376 | - [ ] `ollama serve` is running before starting backend
377 | - [ ] `GET /api/agentic-models/config` shows `qwen3:8b` as masterPlanner
378 | 
```

### FILE: packages/shared-analytics/src/index.js

Source path: `packages/shared-analytics/src/index.js`

```js
  1 | // Shared Analytics Library
  2 | // Common functions used by both frontend and backend for data processing and analytics
  3 | 
  4 | const MONTHS = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"];
  5 | const PREFERRED_NUMERIC_COLUMNS = [
  6 |   "salary_usd",
  7 |   "salary",
  8 |   "compensation",
  9 |   "income",
 10 |   "pay",
 11 |   "revenue",
 12 |   "sales",
 13 |   "amount",
 14 |   "units_sold",
 15 |   "units",
 16 |   "profit_margin",
 17 |   "customer_rating",
 18 | ];
 19 | const PREFERRED_DIMENSION_COLUMNS = ["month", "date", "category", "region", "segment", "country", "education", "company_size"];
 20 | const NON_ADDITIVE_METRIC_HINTS = ["margin", "rate", "ratio", "percent", "percentage", "rating", "score", "mark", "marks", "grade", "gpa", "cgpa"];
 21 | const BRANCH_MINIMUM_GROUP_COUNT = 2;
 22 | 
 23 | /**
 24 |  * Convert a value to a number if possible
 25 |  * @param {any} value - The value to convert
 26 |  * @returns {number|null} - The converted number or null
 27 |  */
 28 | export const toNumber = (value) => {
 29 |   if (value == null) return null;
 30 |   if (typeof value === "number") {
 31 |     return Number.isFinite(value) ? value : null;
 32 |   }
 33 | 
 34 |   if (typeof value === "string") {
 35 |     const normalized = value.trim();
 36 |     if (!normalized) return null;
 37 |     const parsed = Number(normalized);
 38 |     return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
 39 |   }
 40 | 
 41 |   return null;
 42 | };
 43 | 
 44 | /**
 45 |  * Infer the type of a column from sample values
 46 |  * @param {any[]} values - Array of sample values
 47 |  * @returns {string} - The inferred type: "number", "date", or "string"
 48 |  */
 49 | export const inferType = (values) => {
 50 |   const sample = values.filter(Boolean).slice(0, 20);
 51 |   if (sample.length === 0) return "string";
 52 |   if (sample.every((value) => !Number.isNaN(Number(value)))) return "number";
 53 |   if (sample.every((value) => !Number.isNaN(Date.parse(value)))) return "date";
 54 |   return "string";
 55 | };
 56 | 
 57 | /**
 58 |  * Normalize column definitions from raw data
 59 |  * @param {Object[]} rows - Dataset rows
 60 |  * @param {Object[]} providedColumns - Optional pre-defined columns
 61 |  * @returns {Object[]} - Normalized column definitions
 62 |  */
 63 | export const normalizeColumns = (rows, providedColumns = []) => {
 64 |   if (providedColumns.length > 0) {
 65 |     return providedColumns.map((column) => ({
 66 |       name: column.name,
 67 |       type: column.type || inferType(rows.slice(0, 20).map((row) => String(row[column.name] ?? ""))),
 68 |       sample: Array.isArray(column.sample) ? column.sample.map((value) => String(value)) : [],
 69 |     }));
 70 |   }
 71 | 
 72 |   const fields = Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 73 |   return fields.map((name) => ({
 74 |     name,
 75 |     type: inferType(rows.slice(0, 20).map((row) => String(row[name] ?? ""))),
 76 |     sample: rows.slice(0, 3).map((row) => String(row[name] ?? "")),
 77 |   }));
 78 | };
 79 | 
 80 | /**
 81 |  * Humanize a string by converting snake_case or camelCase to Title Case
 82 |  * @param {string} value - The string to humanize
 83 |  * @returns {string} - Humanized string
 84 |  */
 85 | export const humanize = (value) =>
 86 |   value
 87 |     .replace(/_/g, " ")
 88 |     .replace(/([a-z])([A-Z])/g, "$1 $2")
 89 |     .replace(/\s+/g, " ")
 90 |     .trim()
 91 |     .replace(/\b\w/g, (letter) => letter.toUpperCase());
 92 | 
 93 | /**
 94 |  * Pick a preferred column from a list based on preferred names
 95 |  * @param {Object[]} columns - Array of column definitions
 96 |  * @param {string[]} preferredNames - Array of preferred column names
 97 |  * @returns {Object|null} - The matched column or the first column
 98 |  */
 99 | export const pickPreferredColumn = (columns, preferredNames) => {
100 |   for (const name of preferredNames) {
101 |     const found = columns.find((column) => column.name === name);
102 |     if (found) return found;
103 |   }
104 | 
105 |   return columns[0];
106 | };
107 | 
108 | /**
109 |  * Determine the appropriate aggregation for a metric column
110 |  * @param {string} columnName - The column name
111 |  * @returns {string} - "average" or "sum"
112 |  */
113 | export const metricAggregationForColumn = (columnName) =>
114 |   NON_ADDITIVE_METRIC_HINTS.some((hint) => columnName.toLowerCase().includes(hint)) ? "average" : "sum";
115 | 
116 | /**
117 |  * Sort labels based on their type (months, dates, or alphabetical)
118 |  * @param {string[]} labels - Array of labels to sort
119 |  * @param {string} columnType - The column type
120 |  * @returns {string[]} - Sorted labels
121 |  */
122 | export const sortLabels = (labels, columnType) => {
123 |   const uniqueLabels = [...new Set(labels)];
124 |   const monthIndex = new Map(MONTHS.map((month, index) => [month.toLowerCase(), index]));
125 | 
126 |   if (uniqueLabels.length > 0 && uniqueLabels.every((label) => monthIndex.has(String(label).toLowerCase()))) {
127 |     return [...uniqueLabels].sort(
128 |       (left, right) => (monthIndex.get(String(left).toLowerCase()) ?? 99) - (monthIndex.get(String(right).toLowerCase()) ?? 99),
129 |     );
130 |   }
131 | 
132 |   if (columnType === "date") {
133 |     return [...uniqueLabels].sort((left, right) => Date.parse(left) - Date.parse(right));
134 |   }
135 | 
136 |   return [...uniqueLabels].sort((left, right) => String(left).localeCompare(String(right)));
137 | };
138 | 
139 | /**
140 |  * Convert a value to a label string
141 |  * @param {any} value - The value to convert
142 |  * @returns {string|null} - The label or null
143 |  */
144 | export const toLabel = (value) => {
145 |   if (value == null) return null;
146 |   const label = String(value).trim();
147 |   return label || null;
148 | };
149 | 
150 | /**
151 |  * Normalize text for comparison
152 |  * @param {string} value - The text to normalize
153 |  * @returns {string} - Normalized text
154 |  */
155 | export const normalizeText = (value) =>
156 |   String(value ?? "")
157 |     .toLowerCase()
158 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, " ")
159 |     .trim();
160 | 
161 | /**
162 |  * Normalize gender labels to standard values
163 |  * @param {string} label - The gender label
164 |  * @returns {string} - Normalized gender label
165 |  */
166 | export const normalizeGenderLabel = (label) => {
167 |   const normalized = normalizeText(label);
168 | 
169 |   if (["m", "male", "man", "boy"].includes(normalized)) return "Male";
170 |   if (["f", "female", "woman", "girl"].includes(normalized)) return "Female";
171 |   if (["other", "others", "non binary", "nonbinary"].includes(normalized)) return "Other";
172 | 
173 |   return label;
174 | };
175 | 
176 | /**
177 |  * Normalize board labels to standard values
178 |  * @param {string} label - The board label
179 |  * @returns {string} - Normalized board label
180 |  */
181 | export const normalizeBoardLabel = (label) => {
182 |   const normalized = normalizeText(label);
183 | 
184 |   if (normalized === "cbse") return "CBSE";
185 |   if (normalized === "icse") return "ICSE";
186 |   if (normalized === "igcse") return "IGCSE";
187 |   if (normalized === "ib") return "IB";
188 |   if (normalized === "state board" || normalized === "stateboard") return "State Board";
189 | 
190 |   return label;
191 | };
192 | 
193 | /**
194 |  * Normalize dimension labels based on column type
195 |  * @param {string} columnName - The column name
196 |  * @param {any} value - The value to normalize
197 |  * @returns {string|null} - Normalized label or null
198 |  */
199 | export const normalizeDimensionLabel = (columnName, value) => {
200 |   const label = toLabel(value);
201 |   if (!label) return null;
202 | 
203 |   const normalizedColumnName = normalizeText(columnName);
204 |   if (normalizedColumnName.includes("gender")) {
205 |     return normalizeGenderLabel(label);
206 |   }
207 |   if (normalizedColumnName.includes("board")) {
208 |     return normalizeBoardLabel(label);
209 |   }
210 | 
211 |   return label;
212 | };
213 | 
214 | /**
215 |  * Check if a value is meaningful (not empty/null)
216 |  * @param {any} value - The value to check
217 |  * @returns {boolean} - Whether the value is meaningful
218 |  */
219 | export const isMeaningfulValue = (value) => {
220 |   if (value == null) return false;
221 |   if (typeof value === "string") return value.trim().length > 0;
222 |   return true;
223 | };
224 | 
225 | /**
226 |  * Get the minimum group count for a dimension (branch-specific)
227 |  * @param {Object} dimensionColumn - The dimension column
228 |  * @returns {number} - The minimum group count
229 |  */
230 | export const getMinimumGroupCount = (dimensionColumn) =>
231 |   normalizeText(dimensionColumn.name).includes("branch") ? BRANCH_MINIMUM_GROUP_COUNT : 1;
232 | 
233 | /**
234 |  * Filter grouped entries based on minimum group count
235 |  * @param {Array} entries - Grouped entries
236 |  * @param {Object} dimensionColumn - The dimension column
237 |  * @param {Object} metricColumn - The metric column (optional)
238 |  * @returns {Array} - Filtered entries
239 |  */
240 | export const filterGroupedEntries = (entries, dimensionColumn, metricColumn) => {
241 |   const minimumGroupCount = getMinimumGroupCount(dimensionColumn);
242 |   if (minimumGroupCount <= 1) {
243 |     return entries;
244 |   }
245 | 
246 |   const filteredEntries = entries.filter(([, entry]) => entry.count >= minimumGroupCount);
247 |   const excludedGroups = entries.length - filteredEntries.length;
248 | 
249 |   if (excludedGroups > 0) {
250 |     console.info("[analytics] group threshold validation", {
251 |       dimension: dimensionColumn.name,
252 |       metric: metricColumn?.name ?? "count",
253 |       minimumGroupCount,
254 |       excludedGroups,
255 |     });
256 |   }
257 | 
258 |   return filteredEntries;
259 | };
260 | 
261 | /**
262 |  * Group and aggregate metric values by dimension
263 |  * @param {Object[]} rows - Dataset rows
264 |  * @param {Object} dimensionColumn - The dimension column
265 |  * @param {Object} metricColumn - The metric column
266 |  * @param {string} aggregation - The aggregation method ("sum" or "average")
267 |  * @returns {Object[]} - Aggregated data
268 |  */
269 | export const groupMetricByDimension = (rows, dimensionColumn, metricColumn, aggregation = "sum") => {
270 |   const grouped = new Map();
271 |   let includedValues = 0;
272 |   let skippedInvalidValues = 0;
273 |   let skippedEmptyLabels = 0;
274 | 
275 |   rows.forEach((row) => {
276 |     const label = normalizeDimensionLabel(dimensionColumn.name, row[dimensionColumn.name]);
277 |     const value = toNumber(row[metricColumn.name]);
278 | 
279 |     if (!label) {
280 |       skippedEmptyLabels += 1;
281 |       return;
282 |     }
283 | 
284 |     if (value == null) {
285 |       skippedInvalidValues += 1;
286 |       return;
287 |     }
288 | 
289 |     const current = grouped.get(label) ?? { sum: 0, count: 0 };
290 |     current.sum += value;
291 |     current.count += 1;
292 |     grouped.set(label, current);
293 |     includedValues += 1;
294 |   });
295 | 
296 |   const groupedEntries = filterGroupedEntries([...grouped.entries()], dimensionColumn, metricColumn);
297 |   const series = sortLabels(groupedEntries.map(([label]) => label), dimensionColumn.type).map((label) => {
298 |     const entry = grouped.get(label) ?? { sum: 0, count: 0 };
299 |     const value = aggregation === "average" && entry.count > 0 ? entry.sum / entry.count : entry.sum;
300 | 
301 |     return {
302 |       [dimensionColumn.name]: label,
303 |       [metricColumn.name]: Number(value.toFixed(2)),
304 |     };
305 |   });
306 | 
307 |   if (aggregation === "average") {
308 |     console.info("[analytics] average validation", {
309 |       metric: metricColumn.name,
310 |       dimension: dimensionColumn.name,
311 |       formula: "sum(values) / count(values)",
312 |       includedValues,
313 |       skippedInvalidValues,
314 |       skippedEmptyLabels,
315 |       groupCount: series.length,
316 |     });
317 |   }
318 | 
319 |   return series;
320 | };
321 | 
322 | /**
323 |  * Count rows by dimension
324 |  * @param {Object[]} rows - Dataset rows
325 |  * @param {Object} dimensionColumn - The dimension column
326 |  * @param {string} valueKey - The key for the count value
327 |  * @returns {Object[]} - Counted data
328 |  */
329 | export const countRowsByDimension = (rows, dimensionColumn, valueKey = "count") => {
330 |   const grouped = new Map();
331 | 
332 |   rows.forEach((row) => {
333 |     const label = normalizeDimensionLabel(dimensionColumn.name, row[dimensionColumn.name]);
334 |     if (!label) return;
335 |     grouped.set(label, (grouped.get(label) ?? 0) + 1);
336 |   });
337 | 
338 |   const groupedEntries = filterGroupedEntries(
339 |     [...grouped.entries()].map(([label, count]) => [label, { count }]),
340 |     dimensionColumn,
341 |     null,
342 |   );
343 | 
344 |   return sortLabels(groupedEntries.map(([label]) => label), dimensionColumn.type).map((label) => ({
345 |     [dimensionColumn.name]: label,
346 |     [valueKey]: grouped.get(label) ?? 0,
347 |   }));
348 | };
349 | 
350 | /**
351 |  * Count rows matching specific dimension values
352 |  * @param {Object[]} rows - Dataset rows
353 |  * @param {Object} dimensionColumn - The dimension column
354 |  * @param {any[]} matchValues - Values to match
355 |  * @param {string} valueKey - The key for the count value
356 |  * @returns {Object[]} - Counted data
357 |  */
358 | export const countRowsMatchingValues = (rows, dimensionColumn, matchValues, valueKey = "count") => {
359 |   const requestedValues = new Set(matchValues);
360 |   const grouped = new Map(matchValues.map((value) => [value, 0]));
361 | 
362 |   rows.forEach((row) => {
363 |     const label = normalizeDimensionLabel(dimensionColumn.name, row[dimensionColumn.name]);
364 |     if (!label || !requestedValues.has(label)) return;
365 |     grouped.set(label, (grouped.get(label) ?? 0) + 1);
366 |   });
367 | 
368 |   return matchValues.map((value) => ({
369 |     [dimensionColumn.name]: value,
370 |     [valueKey]: grouped.get(value) ?? 0,
371 |   }));
372 | };
373 | 
374 | /**
375 |  * Prepare dataset for analytics by sanitizing and validating
376 |  * @param {Object} dataset - The dataset object
377 |  * @returns {Object} - Prepared dataset
378 |  */
379 | export const prepareDatasetForAnalytics = (dataset) => {
380 |   const numericColumns = dataset.columns.filter((column) => column.type === "number");
381 |   const sanitizedRows = [];
382 |   let removedEmptyRows = 0;
383 |   let invalidNumericValues = 0;
384 | 
385 |   dataset.rows.forEach((row) => {
386 |     const hasMeaningfulField = dataset.columns.some((column) => isMeaningfulValue(row[column.name]));
387 |     if (!hasMeaningfulField) {
388 |       removedEmptyRows += 1;
389 |       return;
390 |     }
391 | 
392 |     const nextRow = { ...row };
393 |     numericColumns.forEach((column) => {
394 |       const rawValue = row[column.name];
395 |       const numericValue = toNumber(rawValue);
396 | 
397 |       if (numericValue == null) {
398 |         if (isMeaningfulValue(rawValue)) {
399 |           invalidNumericValues += 1;
400 |         }
401 |         nextRow[column.name] = null;
402 |         return;
403 |       }
404 | 
405 |       nextRow[column.name] = numericValue;
406 |     });
407 | 
408 |     sanitizedRows.push(nextRow);
409 |   });
410 | 
411 |   if (removedEmptyRows > 0 || invalidNumericValues > 0) {
412 |     console.info("[analytics] integrity validation", {
413 |       totalRows: dataset.rows.length,
414 |       analyticsRows: sanitizedRows.length,
415 |       removedEmptyRows,
416 |       invalidNumericValues,
417 |     });
418 |   }
419 | 
420 |   return {
421 |     ...dataset,
422 |     rows: sanitizedRows,
423 |     rowCount: sanitizedRows.length,
424 |   };
425 | };
426 | 
427 | /**
428 |  * Generate demo dataset for testing
429 |  * @returns {Object} - Demo dataset
430 |  */
431 | export const generateDemoDataset = () => {
432 |   const categories = ["Electronics", "Clothing", "Food", "Software", "Services"];
433 |   const regions = ["North", "South", "East", "West"];
434 |   const rows = [];
435 | 
436 |   for (let index = 0; index < 200; index += 1) {
437 |     rows.push({
438 |       month: MONTHS[Math.floor(Math.random() * MONTHS.length)],
439 |       category: categories[Math.floor(Math.random() * categories.length)],
440 |       region: regions[Math.floor(Math.random() * regions.length)],
441 |       revenue: Math.floor(Math.random() * 50000) + 5000,
442 |       units_sold: Math.floor(Math.random() * 500) + 10,
443 |       profit_margin: Number((Math.random() * 0.4 + 0.1).toFixed(2)),
444 |       customer_rating: Number((Math.random() * 2 + 3).toFixed(1)),
445 |     });
446 |   }
447 | 
448 |   return {
449 |     name: "Sales Analytics 2024",
450 |     sourceType: "demo",
451 |     fileName: "sales-analytics-2024.json",
452 |     columns: [
453 |       { name: "month", type: "string", sample: MONTHS.slice(0, 3) },
454 |       { name: "category", type: "string", sample: categories.slice(0, 3) },
455 |       { name: "region", type: "string", sample: regions.slice(0, 3) },
456 |       { name: "revenue", type: "number", sample: ["25000", "18000", "32000"] },
457 |       { name: "units_sold", type: "number", sample: ["150", "230", "89"] },
458 |       { name: "profit_margin", type: "number", sample: ["0.25", "0.18", "0.32"] },
459 |       { name: "customer_rating", type: "number", sample: ["4.2", "3.8", "4.7"] },
460 |     ],
461 |     rows,
462 |   };
463 | };
464 | 
465 | /**
466 |  * Build dataset schema for analysis
467 |  * @param {Object} dataset - The dataset object
468 |  * @returns {Object} - Dataset schema
469 |  */
470 | export const buildDatasetSchema = (dataset) => {
471 |   const numericColumns = dataset.columns.filter((column) => column.type === "number");
472 |   const dimensionColumns = dataset.columns.filter((column) => column.type !== "number");
473 |   const primaryMetric = numericColumns.length > 0 ? pickPreferredColumn(numericColumns, PREFERRED_NUMERIC_COLUMNS) : null;
474 |   const remainingMetrics = numericColumns.filter((column) => column.name !== primaryMetric?.name);
475 |   const secondaryMetric = remainingMetrics.length > 0 ? remainingMetrics[0] : null;
476 |   const primaryDimension = dimensionColumns.length > 0 ? pickPreferredColumn(dimensionColumns, PREFERRED_DIMENSION_COLUMNS) : null;
477 |   const secondaryDimension = dimensionColumns.find((column) => column.name !== primaryDimension?.name) ?? null;
478 | 
479 |   return {
480 |     datasetName: dataset.name,
481 |     rowCount: dataset.rowCount,
482 |     columnCount: dataset.columns.length,
483 |     columns: dataset.columns.map((column) => ({
484 |       name: column.name,
485 |       type: column.type,
486 |       sample: column.sample ?? [],
487 |       role: column.type === "number" ? "metric" : "dimension",
488 |       aggregation: column.type === "number" ? metricAggregationForColumn(column.name) : null,
489 |     })),
490 |     primaryMetric,
491 |     secondaryMetric,
492 |     primaryDimension,
493 |     secondaryDimension,
494 |   };
495 | };
496 | 
497 | /**
498 |  * Calculate Pearson correlation coefficient between two arrays
499 |  * @param {number[]} x - First array of numbers
500 |  * @param {number[]} y - Second array of numbers
501 |  * @returns {number|null} - Correlation coefficient or null
502 |  */
503 | export const calculatePearsonCorrelation = (x, y) => {
504 |   const n = Math.min(x.length, y.length);
505 |   if (n < 2) return null;
506 | 
507 |   const sumX = x.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
508 |   const sumY = y.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
509 |   const sumXY = x.reduce((total, xi, i) => total + xi * y[i], 0);
510 |   const sumX2 = x.reduce((total, xi) => total + xi * xi, 0);
511 |   const sumY2 = y.reduce((total, yi) => total + yi * yi, 0);
512 | 
513 |   const numerator = n * sumXY - sumX * sumY;
514 |   const denominator = Math.sqrt((n * sumX2 - sumX * sumX) * (n * sumY2 - sumY * sumY));
515 | 
516 |   if (denominator === 0) return null;
517 |   return numerator / denominator;
518 | };
519 | 
520 | // Export constants for use in other modules
521 | export const MONTHS_CONST = MONTHS;
522 | export const PREFERRED_NUMERIC_COLUMNS_CONST = PREFERRED_NUMERIC_COLUMNS;
523 | export const PREFERRED_DIMENSION_COLUMNS_CONST = PREFERRED_DIMENSION_COLUMNS;
524 | export const NON_ADDITIVE_METRIC_HINTS_CONST = NON_ADDITIVE_METRIC_HINTS;
525 | export const BRANCH_MINIMUM_GROUP_COUNT_CONST = BRANCH_MINIMUM_GROUP_COUNT;
526 | 
```

```js
  1 | /**
  2 |  * Shared Errors Package
  3 |  * Centralized error classes for the application
  4 |  */
  5 | 
  6 | // Base Application Error
  7 | export class AppError extends Error {
  8 |   constructor(message, statusCode = 500, code = 'INTERNAL_ERROR') {
  9 |     super(message);
 10 |     this.name = this.constructor.name;
 11 |     this.statusCode = statusCode;
 12 |     this.code = code;
 13 |     this.isOperational = true;
 14 |     Error.captureStackTrace(this, this.constructor);
 15 |   }
 16 | 
 17 |   toJSON() {
 18 |     return {
 19 |       error: this.message,
 20 |       code: this.code,
 21 |       statusCode: this.statusCode,
 22 |     };
 23 |   }
 24 | }
 25 | 
 26 | // Validation Error
 27 | export class ValidationError extends AppError {
 28 |   constructor(message, details = null) {
 29 |     super(message, 400, 'VALIDATION_ERROR');
 30 |     this.name = 'ValidationError';
 31 |     this.details = details;
 32 |   }
 33 | 
 34 |   toJSON() {
 35 |     return {
 36 |       ...super.toJSON(),
 37 |       ...(this.details && { details: this.details }),
 38 |     };
 39 |   }
 40 | }
 41 | 
 42 | // Not Found Error
 43 | export class NotFoundError extends AppError {
 44 |   constructor(message = 'Resource not found') {
 45 |     super(message, 404, 'NOT_FOUND');
 46 |     this.name = 'NotFoundError';
 47 |   }
 48 | }
 49 | 
 50 | // Unauthorized Error
 51 | export class UnauthorizedError extends AppError {
 52 |   constructor(message = 'Unauthorized') {
 53 |     super(message, 401, 'UNAUTHORIZED');
 54 |     this.name = 'UnauthorizedError';
 55 |   }
 56 | }
 57 | 
 58 | // Forbidden Error
 59 | export class ForbiddenError extends AppError {
 60 |   constructor(message = 'Forbidden') {
 61 |     super(message, 403, 'FORBIDDEN');
 62 |     this.name = 'ForbiddenError';
 63 |   }
 64 | }
 65 | 
 66 | // Conflict Error
 67 | export class ConflictError extends AppError {
 68 |   constructor(message = 'Resource already exists') {
 69 |     super(message, 409, 'CONFLICT');
 70 |     this.name = 'ConflictError';
 71 |   }
 72 | }
 73 | 
 74 | // AI Error
 75 | export class AIError extends AppError {
 76 |   constructor(message, code = 'AI_ERROR', details = null) {
 77 |     super(message, 500, code);
 78 |     this.name = 'AIError';
 79 |     this.details = details;
 80 |   }
 81 | }
 82 | 
 83 | // AI Timeout Error
 84 | export class AITimeoutError extends AIError {
 85 |   constructor(message = 'AI request timed out') {
 86 |     super(message, 'AI_TIMEOUT', { timeout: true });
 87 |     this.name = 'AITimeoutError';
 88 |   }
 89 | }
 90 | 
 91 | // AI Response Error
 92 | export class AIResponseError extends AIError {
 93 |   constructor(message = 'Invalid AI response') {
 94 |     super(message, 'AI_RESPONSE_ERROR', { invalidResponse: true });
 95 |     this.name = 'AIResponseError';
 96 |   }
 97 | }
 98 | 
 99 | // Database Error
100 | export class DatabaseError extends AppError {
101 |   constructor(message = 'Database operation failed', originalError = null) {
102 |     super(message, 500, 'DATABASE_ERROR');
103 |     this.name = 'DatabaseError';
104 |     this.originalError = originalError;
105 |   }
106 | 
107 |   toJSON() {
108 |     return {
109 |       ...super.toJSON(),
110 |       ...(process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' && this.originalError && {
111 |         originalError: this.originalError.message,
112 |       }),
113 |     };
114 |   }
115 | }
116 | 
117 | // File Processing Error
118 | export class FileProcessingError extends AppError {
119 |   constructor(message = 'File processing failed', code = 'FILE_ERROR') {
120 |     super(message, 400, code);
121 |     this.name = 'FileProcessingError';
122 |   }
123 | }
124 | 
125 | // Schema Error
126 | export class SchemaError extends AppError {
127 |   constructor(message = 'Schema validation failed') {
128 |     super(message, 400, 'SCHEMA_ERROR');
129 |     this.name = 'SchemaError';
130 |   }
131 | }
132 | 
133 | // Error Factory
134 | export function createError(type, message, details = null) {
135 |   const errorMap = {
136 |     VALIDATION: () => new ValidationError(message, details),
137 |     NOT_FOUND: () => new NotFoundError(message),
138 |     UNAUTHORIZED: () => new UnauthorizedError(message),
139 |     FORBIDDEN: () => new ForbiddenError(message),
140 |     CONFLICT: () => new ConflictError(message),
141 |     AI: () => new AIError(message, 'AI_ERROR', details),
142 |     AI_TIMEOUT: () => new AITimeoutError(message),
143 |     AI_RESPONSE: () => new AIResponseError(message),
144 |     DATABASE: () => new DatabaseError(message, details),
145 |     FILE: () => new FileProcessingError(message),
146 |     SCHEMA: () => new SchemaError(message),
147 |   };
148 | 
149 |   const ErrorClass = errorMap[type];
150 |   if (!ErrorClass) {
151 |     return new AppError(message);
152 |   }
153 | 
154 |   return ErrorClass();
155 | }
156 | 
157 | // Error Handler
158 | export function handleError(error) {
159 |   if (error.isOperational) {
160 |     return {
161 |       statusCode: error.statusCode,
162 |       body: JSON.stringify(error.toJSON()),
163 |     };
164 |   }
165 | 
166 |   // Unknown error
167 |   console.error('[Unhandled Error]', error);
168 |   return {
169 |     statusCode: 500,
170 |     body: JSON.stringify({
171 |       error: 'Internal server error',
172 |       code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
173 |     }),
174 |   };
175 | }
176 | 
177 | export default {
178 |   AppError,
179 |   ValidationError,
180 |   NotFoundError,
181 |   UnauthorizedError,
182 |   ForbiddenError,
183 |   ConflictError,
184 |   AIError,
185 |   AITimeoutError,
186 |   AIResponseError,
187 |   DatabaseError,
188 |   FileProcessingError,
189 |   SchemaError,
190 |   createError,
191 |   handleError,
192 | };
```

 97 | | `/api/ml/*` | `http://127.0.0.1:5000` | ML Service |

130 | taskkill /F /PID <PID>
