### FILE: qa-test-script.js

Source path: `qa-test-script.js`

```js
  1 | import { execSync, spawn } from "child_process";
  2 | import { readFileSync, writeFileSync, existsSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, statSync } from "fs";
  3 | import { resolve, relative, dirname, basename, extname, join } from "path";
  4 | import { createInterface } from "readline";
  5 | 
  6 | const ROOT = resolve(import.meta.dirname, ".");
  7 | const REPORTS_DIR = resolve(ROOT, "reports/qa-reports");
  8 | const REPORT_FILE = resolve(REPORTS_DIR, "qa-report.json");
  9 | const HTML_REPORT_FILE = resolve(REPORTS_DIR, "qa-report.html");
 10 | 
 11 | if (!existsSync(REPORTS_DIR)) mkdirSync(REPORTS_DIR, { recursive: true });
 12 | 
 13 | const EXTENSIONS = new Set([".ts", ".tsx", ".js", ".jsx"]);
 14 | const EXCLUDE_DIRS = new Set(["node_modules", ".git", "coverage", "reports", "logs", "exports", "dist", ".tmp_schema_trained_patch"]);
 15 | 
 16 | let testCache = null;
 17 | 
 18 | function getSourceFiles(baseDir) {
 19 |   const files = [];
 20 |   function walk(dir) {
 21 |     try {
 22 |       const entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
 23 |       for (const entry of entries) {
 24 |         const full = resolve(dir, entry.name);
 25 |         if (entry.isDirectory()) {
 26 |           if (!EXCLUDE_DIRS.has(entry.name)) walk(full);
 27 |         } else if (entry.isFile() && EXTENSIONS.has(extname(entry.name))) {
 28 |           files.push(full);
 29 |         }
 30 |       }
 31 |     } catch { }
 32 |   }
 33 |   walk(baseDir);
 34 |   return files;
 35 | }
 36 | 
 37 | function getTestFiles(files) {
 38 |   return files.filter(f => /\.(test|spec)\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$/.test(f));
 39 | }
 40 | 
 41 | function getSourceWithoutTests(files) {
 42 |   const testFiles = new Set(getTestFiles(files));
 43 |   const tests = new Set();
 44 |   for (const tf of testFiles) {
 45 |     const base = tf.replace(/\.(test|spec)\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$/, "");
 46 |     tests.add(base);
 47 |   }
 48 |   return files.filter(f => {
 49 |     if (testFiles.has(f)) return false;
 50 |     if (f.includes("__tests__")) return false;
 51 |     const base = f.replace(/\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$/, "");
 52 |     return !tests.has(base);
 53 |   });
 54 | }
 55 | 
 56 | function runWithTimeout(cmd, opts = {}) {
 57 |   try {
 58 |     const out = execSync(cmd, {
 59 |       cwd: opts.cwd || ROOT,
 60 |       timeout: opts.timeout || 120000,
 61 |       encoding: "utf-8",
 62 |       stdio: "pipe",
 63 |       ...opts,
 64 |     });
 65 |     return { stdout: out?.trim() || "", stderr: "", exitCode: 0 };
 66 |   } catch (e) {
 67 |     return {
 68 |       stdout: e.stdout?.toString()?.trim() || "",
 69 |       stderr: e.stderr?.toString()?.trim() || e.message,
 70 |       exitCode: e.status ?? 1,
 71 |     };
 72 |   }
 73 | }
 74 | 
 75 | async function runVitest(testDir, label) {
 76 |   const res = runWithTimeout(`npx vitest run --reporter json --outputFile "${REPORTS_DIR}/vitest-${label}.json"`, {
 77 |     cwd: testDir,
 78 |     timeout: 180000,
 79 |   });
 80 | 
 81 |   const resultsFile = resolve(REPORTS_DIR, `vitest-${label}.json`);
 82 |   let summary = { total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, skipped: 0, tests: [], files: [] };
 83 |   if (existsSync(resultsFile)) {
 84 |     try {
 85 |       const raw = readFileSync(resultsFile, "utf-8");
 86 |       const data = JSON.parse(raw);
 87 |       const testResults = data.testResults || data;
 88 |       if (Array.isArray(testResults)) {
 89 |         for (const tr of testResults) {
 90 |           summary.total += tr.assertionResults?.length || 0;
 91 |           summary.passed += tr.assertionResults?.filter(a => a.status === "passed").length || 0;
 92 |           summary.failed += tr.assertionResults?.filter(a => a.status === "failed").length || 0;
 93 |           summary.skipped += tr.assertionResults?.filter(a => a.status === "skipped" || a.status === "pending").length || 0;
 94 |           summary.files.push(tr.name || tr.file || "unknown");
 95 |           if (tr.assertionResults) summary.tests.push(...tr.assertionResults);
 96 |         }
 97 |       } else {
 98 |         summary.total = data.numTotalTests || 0;
 99 |         summary.passed = data.numPassedTests || 0;
100 |         summary.failed = data.numFailedTests || 0;
101 |         summary.skipped = (data.numPendingTests || 0) + (data.numSkippedTests || 0);
102 |         summary.files = (data.testResults || []).map(r => r.name || r.file);
103 |         summary.tests = data.testResults || [];
104 |       }
105 |     } catch { }
106 |   }
107 | 
108 |   const exitOk = res.exitCode === 0 || summary.failed === 0;
109 |   return {
110 |     label,
111 |     exitCode: res.exitCode,
112 |     ok: exitOk,
113 |     summary,
114 |     raw: res,
115 |   };
116 | }
117 | 
118 | function runLint() {
119 |   const res = runWithTimeout("npx eslint apps/frontend/src --format json", { timeout: 120000 });
120 |   let issues = [];
121 |   if (res.stdout) {
122 |     try {
123 |       issues = JSON.parse(res.stdout);
124 |     } catch {
125 |       issues = [{ filePath: "eslint", messages: [{ message: res.stderr || "Parse error", severity: 2 }] }];
126 |     }
127 |   }
128 |   const errorCount = issues.reduce((s, f) => s + f.messages?.filter(m => m.severity === 2).length, 0);
129 |   const warnCount = issues.reduce((s, f) => s + f.messages?.filter(m => m.severity === 1).length, 0);
130 |   return { ok: errorCount === 0, errorCount, warnCount, issues };
131 | }
132 | 
133 | function runTypeCheck() {
134 |   const res = runWithTimeout("npx tsc --noEmit", { cwd: resolve(ROOT, "apps/frontend"), timeout: 120000 });
135 |   const errors = (res.stderr || res.stdout || "").split("\n").filter(l => l.includes("error TS"));
136 |   return { ok: errors.length === 0, errors: errors.length, details: res };
137 | }
138 | 
139 | function analyzeCodeQuality(files) {
140 |   const report = { totalFiles: files.length, totalLines: 0, totalFunctions: 0, totalImports: 0 };
141 | 
142 |   const funcPattern = /\b(function\s+\w+|=>\s*\{|async\s+function|const\s+\w+\s*=\s*(?:async\s*)?\([^)]*\)\s*=>|class\s+\w+)/g;
143 |   const importPattern = /\b(import|require)\s/g;
144 | 
145 |   for (const f of files) {
146 |     try {
147 |       const content = readFileSync(f, "utf-8");
148 |       const lines = content.split("\n").length;
149 |       report.totalLines += lines;
150 |       report.totalFunctions += (content.match(funcPattern) || []).length;
151 |       report.totalImports += (content.match(importPattern) || []).length;
152 |     } catch { }
153 |   }
154 |   return report;
155 | }
156 | 
157 | function getLineCoverage(files) {
158 |   const covered = {};
159 |   for (const f of files) {
160 |     const dir = dirname(f);
161 |     const base = basename(f).replace(/\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$/, "");
162 |     const testPatterns = [
163 |       resolve(dir, `${base}.test.ts`), resolve(dir, `${base}.test.tsx`),
164 |       resolve(dir, `${base}.test.js`), resolve(dir, `${base}.test.jsx`),
165 |       resolve(dir, `${base}.spec.ts`), resolve(dir, `${base}.spec.tsx`),
166 |       resolve(dir, `${base}.spec.js`), resolve(dir, `${base}.spec.jsx`),
167 |     ];
168 |     const hasTest = testPatterns.some(p => existsSync(p));
169 | 
170 |     let sourceLines = 0;
171 |     try {
172 |       sourceLines = readFileSync(f, "utf-8").split("\n").length;
173 |     } catch { sourceLines = 0; }
174 | 
175 |     covered[f] = { hasTest, sourceLines, tested: hasTest };
176 |   }
177 |   return covered;
178 | }
179 | 
180 | function matchTestVariant(sourceFile) {
181 |   const dir = dirname(sourceFile);
182 |   const base = basename(sourceFile).replace(/\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$/, "");
183 |   const exts = [".test.ts", ".test.tsx", ".test.js", ".spec.ts", ".spec.tsx", ".spec.js"];
184 |   return exts.map(e => resolve(dir, base + e)).find(p => existsSync(p));
185 | }
186 | 
187 | async function runAllTests() {
188 |   const now = Date.now();
189 | 
190 |   // Collect all source files
191 |   const allSource = [
192 |     ...getSourceFiles(resolve(ROOT, "apps/frontend/src")),
193 |     ...getSourceFiles(resolve(ROOT, "apps/backend/src")),
194 |     ...getSourceFiles(resolve(ROOT, "packages")),
195 |   ].filter(f => !f.includes("node_modules") && !f.includes(".git"));
196 | 
197 |   // Separate test files and source files
198 |   const allTestFiles = new Set(getTestFiles(allSource));
199 | 
200 |   // File-by-file analysis — include ALL source files
201 |   const fileAnalysis = [];
202 |   const seen = new Set();
203 |   for (const f of allSource) {
204 |     const rel = relative(ROOT, f);
205 |     if (seen.has(rel)) continue;
206 |     seen.add(rel);
207 |     const isTest = allTestFiles.has(f) || f.includes("__tests__");
208 |     const testFile = isTest ? null : matchTestVariant(f);
209 |     let lines = 0;
210 |     try { lines = readFileSync(f, "utf-8").split("\n").length; } catch { }
211 |     fileAnalysis.push({
212 |       file: rel,
213 |       lines,
214 |       hasTest: isTest || !!testFile,
215 |       testFile: testFile ? relative(ROOT, testFile) : null,
216 |       isTestFile: isTest,
217 |       isInTestDir: f.includes("__tests__"),
218 |     });
219 |   }
220 | 
221 |   // Analyze code quality
222 |   const quality = analyzeCodeQuality(allSource);
223 | 
224 |   // Run vitest frontend
225 |   const feResult = await runVitest(resolve(ROOT, "apps/frontend"), "frontend");
226 |   const beResult = await runVitest(resolve(ROOT, "apps/backend"), "backend");
227 | 
228 |   // Run lint
229 |   const lintResult = runLint();
230 |   const tsResult = runTypeCheck();
231 | 
232 |   // Calculate coverage percentage (source files only, not test files)
233 |   const sourceFilesOnly = fileAnalysis.filter(f => !f.isTestFile);
234 |   const sourceFilesWithTests = sourceFilesOnly.filter(f => f.hasTest).length;
235 |   const totalSourceFiles = sourceFilesOnly.length;
236 |   const coveragePct = totalSourceFiles > 0 ? Math.round((sourceFilesWithTests / totalSourceFiles) * 10000) / 100 : 0;
237 | 
238 |   const totalLines = allSource.reduce((s, f) => {
239 |     try { return s + readFileSync(f, "utf-8").split("\n").length; } catch { return s; }
240 |   }, 0);
241 | 
242 |   // Untested files (source files only)
243 |   const untested = sourceFilesOnly.filter(f => !f.hasTest);
244 | 
245 |   const report = {
246 |     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
247 |     duration: Date.now() - now,
248 |     summary: {
249 |       totalFiles: totalSourceFiles,
250 |       totalLines,
251 |       totalTestFiles: fileAnalysis.filter(f => f.isTestFile).length,
252 |       filesWithTests: sourceFilesWithTests,
253 |       filesWithoutTests: untested.length,
254 |       testCoveragePct: coveragePct,
255 |       totalFunctions: quality.totalFunctions,
256 |       totalImports: quality.totalImports,
257 |     },
258 |     tests: {
259 |       frontend: {
260 |         ok: feResult.ok,
261 |         total: feResult.summary.total,
262 |         passed: feResult.summary.passed,
263 |         failed: feResult.summary.failed,
264 |         skipped: feResult.summary.skipped,
265 |         files: feResult.summary.files,
266 |       },
267 |       backend: {
268 |         ok: beResult.ok,
269 |         total: beResult.summary.total,
270 |         passed: beResult.summary.passed,
271 |         failed: beResult.summary.failed,
272 |         skipped: beResult.summary.skipped,
273 |         files: beResult.summary.files,
274 |       },
275 |     },
276 |     quality: {
277 |       lint: lintResult,
278 |       typescript: tsResult,
279 |     },
280 |     coverage: {
281 |       pct: coveragePct,
282 |       filesWithTests: sourceFilesWithTests,
283 |       totalFiles: totalSourceFiles,
284 |     },
285 |     untestedFiles: untested.map(f => ({ file: f.file, lines: f.lines })),
286 |     files: fileAnalysis.sort((a, b) => a.file.localeCompare(b.file)),
287 |   };
288 | 
289 |   writeFileSync(REPORT_FILE, JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
290 |   generateHtmlReport(report);
291 |   testCache = report;
292 |   return report;
293 | }
294 | 
295 | function generateHtmlReport(report) {
296 |   const { summary, tests, quality, untestedFiles } = report;
297 | 
298 |   const feBadge = tests.frontend.ok ? "✅" : "❌";
299 |   const beBadge = tests.backend.ok ? "✅" : "❌";
300 |   const lintBadge = quality.lint.ok ? "✅" : "❌";
301 |   const tsBadge = quality.typescript.ok ? "✅" : "❌";
302 |   const coveragePct = summary.testCoveragePct;
303 | 
304 |   let color = "#22c55e";
305 |   if (coveragePct < 30) color = "#ef4444";
306 |   else if (coveragePct < 60) color = "#f59e0b";
307 | 
308 |   const untestedRows = untestedFiles.slice(0, 100).map(f =>
309 |     `<tr><td>${f.file}</td><td>${f.lines}</td><td style="color:#ef4444">❌ No test</td></tr>`
310 |   ).join("");
311 | 
312 |   const fileRows = report.files.slice(0, 500).map(f => {
313 |     const status = f.isTestFile ? "🧪 Test file" : (f.hasTest ? "✅ Tested" : "❌ Untested");
314 |     const color = f.isTestFile ? "#8b5cf6" : (f.hasTest ? "#22c55e" : "#ef4444");
315 |     return `<tr><td>${f.file}</td><td>${f.lines}</td><td style="color:${color}">${status}</td></tr>`;
316 |   }).join("");
317 | 
318 |   const html = `<!DOCTYPE html>
319 | <html lang="en">
320 | <head>
321 | <meta charset="UTF-8">
322 | <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1.0">
323 | <title>QA Test Report - InsightFlow</title>
324 | <style>
325 | * { margin:0; padding:0; box-sizing:border-box; }
326 | body { font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,Segoe UI,Roboto,Oxygen,Ubuntu,sans-serif; background:#0f172a; color:#e2e8f0; padding:20px; }
327 | .container { max-width:1400px; margin:0 auto; }
328 | h1 { font-size:24px; font-weight:700; color:#f8fafc; margin-bottom:20px; }
329 | h2 { font-size:18px; font-weight:600; color:#94a3b8; margin:20px 0 10px; }
330 | .header-bar { display:flex; gap:12px; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:20px; }
331 | .card { background:#1e293b; border-radius:12px; padding:16px; border:1px solid #334155; }
332 | .card h3 { font-size:14px; color:#94a3b8; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.5px; margin-bottom:8px; }
333 | .card .value { font-size:28px; font-weight:700; color:#f8fafc; }
334 | .card .sub { font-size:12px; color:#64748b; margin-top:4px; }
335 | .grid-4 { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(200px,1fr)); gap:12px; }
336 | .grid-2 { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(300px,1fr)); gap:12px; }
337 | table { width:100%; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:13px; }
338 | th { text-align:left; padding:8px 12px; background:#334155; color:#94a3b8; font-weight:600; text-transform:uppercase; font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.5px; position:sticky; top:0; }
339 | td { padding:8px 12px; border-bottom:1px solid #1e293b; font-family:monospace; }
340 | tr:hover { background:#1e293b; }
341 | .pass { color:#22c55e; }
342 | .fail { color:#ef4444; }
343 | .skip { color:#f59e0b; }
344 | .progress-bar { height:8px; background:#334155; border-radius:4px; overflow:hidden; margin-top:8px; }
345 | .progress-fill { height:100%; background:${color}; border-radius:4px; transition:width .3s; width:${coveragePct}%; }
346 | .coverage-ring { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:16px; }
347 | .coverage-number { font-size:48px; font-weight:700; }
348 | .coverage-label { font-size:14px; color:#94a3b8; }
349 | .coverage-ring .color-dot { width:16px; height:16px; border-radius:50%; background:${color}; }
350 | .footer { margin-top:20px; text-align:center; font-size:12px; color:#475569; border-top:1px solid #1e293b; padding-top:16px; }
351 | .timestamp { font-size:12px; color:#64748b; margin-bottom:16px; }
352 | .tabs { display:flex; gap:4px; margin-bottom:16px; }
353 | .tab { padding:8px 16px; background:#1e293b; border:1px solid #334155; border-radius:8px; cursor:pointer; font-size:13px; color:#94a3b8; }
354 | .tab.active { background:#334155; color:#f8fafc; border-color:#475569; }
355 | .untested-count { color:#ef4444; font-weight:700; }
356 | .coverage-good { color:#22c55e; }
357 | .coverage-warn { color:#f59e0b; }
358 | .coverage-bad { color:#ef4444; }
359 | .scroll-table { max-height:600px; overflow-y:auto; border:1px solid #334155; border-radius:8px; }
360 | </style>
361 | </head>
362 | <body>
363 | <div class="container">
364 | <h1>🧪 InsightFlow — QA Test Report</h1>
365 | <div class="timestamp">Generated: ${report.timestamp} | Duration: ${report.duration}ms</div>
366 | 
367 | <div class="grid-4">
368 |   <div class="card">
369 |     <h3>Total Files</h3>
370 |     <div class="value">${summary.totalFiles}</div>
371 |     <div class="sub">${summary.totalLines.toLocaleString()} lines of code</div>
372 |   </div>
373 |   <div class="card">
374 |     <h3>Test Files</h3>
375 |     <div class="value">${summary.totalTestFiles}</div>
376 |     <div class="sub">${summary.filesWithTests} source files covered</div>
377 |   </div>
378 |   <div class="card">
379 |     <h3>Code Coverage</h3>
380 |     <div class="coverage-ring">
381 |       <div class="coverage-number ${coveragePct >= 60 ? 'coverage-good' : coveragePct >= 30 ? 'coverage-warn' : 'coverage-bad'}">${coveragePct}%</div>
382 |       <div class="coverage-label">files tested</div>
383 |     </div>
384 |     <div class="progress-bar"><div class="progress-fill"></div></div>
385 |   </div>
386 |   <div class="card">
387 |     <h3>Untested Files</h3>
388 |     <div class="value untested-count">${summary.filesWithoutTests}</div>
389 |     <div class="sub">out of ${summary.totalFiles} total</div>
390 |   </div>
391 | </div>
392 | 
393 | <div class="grid-2">
394 |   <div class="card">
395 |     <h3>${feBadge} Frontend Tests (vitest)</h3>
396 |     <div style="font-size:24px;font-weight:700">
397 |       <span class="pass">${tests.frontend.passed}</span> /
398 |       <span>${tests.frontend.total}</span>
399 |       <span style="font-size:14px;color:#94a3b8">passed</span>
400 |     </div>
401 |     ${tests.frontend.failed > 0 ? `<div style="color:#ef4444;font-size:14px">${tests.frontend.failed} failed</div>` : ''}
402 |     ${tests.frontend.skipped > 0 ? `<div style="color:#f59e0b;font-size:14px">${tests.frontend.skipped} skipped</div>` : ''}
403 |     <div style="font-size:12px;color:#64748b;margin-top:4px">Files: ${tests.frontend.files.length}</div>
404 |   </div>
405 |   <div class="card">
406 |     <h3>${beBadge} Backend Tests (vitest)</h3>
407 |     <div style="font-size:24px;font-weight:700">
408 |       <span class="pass">${tests.backend.passed}</span> /
409 |       <span>${tests.backend.total}</span>
410 |       <span style="font-size:14px;color:#94a3b8">passed</span>
411 |     </div>
412 |     ${tests.backend.failed > 0 ? `<div style="color:#ef4444;font-size:14px">${tests.backend.failed} failed</div>` : ''}
413 |     ${tests.backend.skipped > 0 ? `<div style="color:#f59e0b;font-size:14px">${tests.backend.skipped} skipped</div>` : ''}
414 |     <div style="font-size:12px;color:#64748b;margin-top:4px">Files: ${tests.backend.files.length}</div>
415 |   </div>
416 | </div>
417 | 
418 | <div class="grid-2">
419 |   <div class="card">
420 |     <h3>${lintBadge} ESLint</h3>
421 |     <div style="font-size:20px;font-weight:700">${quality.lint.errorCount} errors / ${quality.lint.warnCount} warnings</div>
422 |   </div>
423 |   <div class="card">
424 |     <h3>${tsBadge} TypeScript</h3>
425 |     <div style="font-size:20px;font-weight:700">${quality.typescript.errors} errors</div>
426 |     <div style="font-size:12px;color:#64748b;margin-top:4px">tsc --noEmit</div>
427 |   </div>
428 | </div>
429 | 
430 | <h2>📂 File Coverage Report ${summary.filesWithoutTests > 0 ? `<span style="color:#ef4444;font-size:14px;font-weight:400">(${summary.filesWithoutTests} untested)</span>` : ''}</h2>
431 | <div class="card scroll-table">
432 | <table>
433 | <thead><tr><th>File</th><th>Lines</th><th>Status</th></tr></thead>
434 | <tbody>${fileRows}</tbody>
435 | </table>
436 | </div>
437 | 
438 | ${untestedFiles.length > 0 ? `
439 | <h2>⚠️ Untested Files (${untestedFiles.length})</h2>
440 | <div class="card scroll-table" style="max-height:400px">
441 | <table>
442 | <thead><tr><th>File</th><th>Lines</th><th>Status</th></tr></thead>
443 | <tbody>${untestedRows}</tbody>
444 | </table>
445 | </div>` : ''}
446 | 
447 | <div class="footer">
448 |   InsightFlow QA Tester — Every line matters. Generated at ${report.timestamp}
449 | </div>
450 | </div>
451 | </body>
452 | </html>`;
453 | 
454 |   writeFileSync(HTML_REPORT_FILE, html);
455 | }
456 | 
457 | function printReport(report) {
458 |   const { summary, tests, quality, untestedFiles } = report;
459 | 
460 |   const feStatus = tests.frontend.ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
461 |   const beStatus = tests.backend.ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
462 |   const lintStatus = quality.lint.ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
463 |   const tsStatus = quality.typescript.ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
464 | 
465 |   console.clear();
466 |   console.log("");
467 |   console.log("  ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗");
468 |   console.log("  ║        INSIGHTFLOW — FULL QA TEST REPORT               ║");
469 |   console.log("  ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝");
470 |   console.log("");
471 |   console.log(`  Generated : ${report.timestamp}`);
472 |   console.log(`  Duration  : ${report.duration}ms`);
473 |   console.log("");
474 | 
475 |   // Summary
476 |   const covColor = summary.testCoveragePct >= 60 ? "✓" : summary.testCoveragePct >= 30 ? "!" : "✗";
477 |   console.log("  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐");
478 |   console.log("  │  SUMMARY                                               │");
479 |   console.log("  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤");
480 |   console.log(`  │  Total Source Files    : ${String(summary.totalFiles).padEnd(10)}    Lines of Code: ${String(summary.totalLines).padEnd(8)} │`);
481 |   console.log(`  │  Test Files            : ${String(summary.totalTestFiles).padEnd(10)}    Functions    : ${String(summary.totalFunctions).padEnd(8)} │`);
482 |   console.log(`  │  Files With Tests      : ${String(summary.filesWithTests).padEnd(10)}    Imports      : ${String(summary.totalImports).padEnd(8)} │`);
483 |   console.log(`  │  Files Without Tests   : ${String(summary.filesWithoutTests).padEnd(10)}                           │`);
484 |   console.log(`  │  ${covColor}  Test Coverage       : ${String(summary.testCoveragePct + "%").padEnd(10)}                           │`);
485 |   console.log("  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘");
486 |   console.log("");
487 | 
488 |   // Test results
489 |   console.log("  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐");
490 |   console.log("  │  TEST SUITES                                           │");
491 |   console.log("  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤");
492 |   console.log(`  │  ${feStatus}  Frontend (vitest)  : ${String(tests.frontend.passed).padStart(4)}/${String(tests.frontend.total).padEnd(4)} passed  ${tests.frontend.failed > 0 ? tests.frontend.failed + " failed" : ""}  ${tests.frontend.skipped > 0 ? tests.frontend.skipped + " skipped" : ""} │`);
493 |   console.log(`  │  ${beStatus}  Backend  (vitest)  : ${String(tests.backend.passed).padStart(4)}/${String(tests.backend.total).padEnd(4)} passed  ${tests.backend.failed > 0 ? tests.backend.failed + " failed" : ""}  ${tests.backend.skipped > 0 ? tests.backend.skipped + " skipped" : ""} │`);
494 |   console.log(`  │  ${lintStatus}  ESLint            : ${String(quality.lint.errorCount).padStart(4)} errors, ${String(quality.lint.warnCount).padEnd(4)} warnings              │`);
495 |   console.log(`  │  ${tsStatus}  TypeScript        : ${String(quality.typescript.errors).padStart(4)} errors                              │`);
496 |   console.log("  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘");
497 |   console.log("");
498 | 
499 |   // Untested files
500 |   if (untestedFiles.length > 0) {
501 |     console.log("  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐");
502 |     console.log(`  │  ⚠️  UNTESTED FILES (${String(untestedFiles.length).padStart(3)})                               │`);
503 |     console.log("  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤");
504 |     const show = untestedFiles.slice(0, 25);
505 |     for (const uf of show) {
506 |       const short = uf.file.length > 55 ? "..." + uf.file.slice(-52) : uf.file;
507 |       console.log(`  │  ❌  ${short.padEnd(55)} ${String(uf.lines).padStart(4)}L │`);
508 |     }
509 |     if (untestedFiles.length > 25) {
510 |       console.log(`  │  ... and ${untestedFiles.length - 25} more untested files                   │`);
511 |     }
512 |     console.log("  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘");
513 |     console.log("");
514 |   }
515 | 
516 |   // Files with tests
517 |   const testedFiles = report.files.filter(f => f.hasTest && !f.isTestFile);
518 |   if (testedFiles.length > 0) {
519 |     console.log("  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐");
520 |     console.log(`  │  ✅  TESTED  (${String(testedFiles.length).padStart(3)} files)                                  │`);
521 |     console.log("  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤");
522 |     const show = testedFiles.slice(0, 20);
523 |     for (const tf of show) {
524 |       const short = tf.file.length > 55 ? "..." + tf.file.slice(-52) : tf.file;
525 |       console.log(`  │  ✅  ${short.padEnd(55)} ${String(tf.lines).padStart(4)}L │`);
526 |     }
527 |     if (testedFiles.length > 20) {
528 |       console.log(`  │  ... and ${testedFiles.length - 20} more tested files                   │`);
529 |     }
530 |     console.log("  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘");
531 |     console.log("");
532 |   }
533 | 
534 |   console.log(`  📄  Full reports saved to:`);
535 |   console.log(`      JSON: ${REPORT_FILE}`);
536 |   console.log(`      HTML: ${HTML_REPORT_FILE}`);
537 |   console.log("");
538 |   console.log("  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────");
539 |   console.log("  Press [Enter] to re-run all tests and update this report.");
540 |   console.log("  Press [Ctrl+C] to exit.");
541 |   console.log("");
542 | }
543 | 
544 | async function main() {
545 |   console.log("");
546 |   console.log("  ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗");
547 |   console.log("  ║     INSIGHTFLOW QA TESTER — Initializing...             ║");
548 |   console.log("  ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝");
549 |   console.log("");
550 | 
551 |   // First run
552 |   const report = await runAllTests();
553 |   printReport(report);
554 | 
555 |   // Interactive mode
556 |   const rl = createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
557 |   rl.on("line", async () => {
558 |     const r = await runAllTests();
559 |     printReport(r);
560 |   });
561 | 
562 |   rl.on("SIGINT", () => {
563 |     console.log("\n\n  QA Tester exiting. Reports saved in reports/qa-reports/\n");
564 |     process.exit(0);
565 |   });
566 | }
567 | 
568 | main().catch(err => {
569 |   console.error("Fatal error:", err);
570 |   process.exit(1);
571 | });
572 | 
```

---

### FILE: QUICK_REFERENCE.md

Source path: `QUICK_REFERENCE.md`

```md
  1 | # ⚡ InsightFlow Quick Reference
  2 | 
  3 | ## 🚀 Start the Platform
  4 | 
  5 | ```bash
  6 | cd "c:\Users\VISHAL\Desktop\20-12-2025\All_full_stack_preparation\expo\Agentic ai Data analytics"
  7 | 
  8 | # Install dependencies (first time only)
  9 | npm install
 10 | 
 11 | # Start all services
 12 | npm run dev
 13 | 
 14 | # Or start individually:
 15 | npm run dev:backend    # http://localhost:3001
 16 | npm run dev:frontend   # http://localhost:5173
 17 | ```
 18 | 
 19 | ## ✅ Verify Everything Works
 20 | 
 21 | ```bash
 22 | # Run all 91 tests
 23 | npm run test:insightflow-api
 24 | 
 25 | # Expected: ✅ 91/91 PASSED
 26 | ```
 27 | 
 28 | ## 📊 Access the Platform
 29 | 
 30 | - **Frontend**: http://localhost:5173
 31 | - **API**: http://localhost:3001
 32 | - **API Docs**: http://localhost:3001/api/docs (root endpoint)
 33 | 
 34 | ## 💬 Quick Examples
 35 | 
 36 | ### Import Data
 37 | 
 38 | 1. Click "⬆️ Upload" tab
 39 | 2. Select CSV file
 40 | 3. Click "Import Dataset"
 41 | 4. Data is immediately ready to analyze
 42 | 
 43 | ### Ask Questions
 44 | 
 45 | 1. Click "💬 Chat" tab
 46 | 2. Type: "Show average salary by department"
 47 | 3. Get AI-generated insights + SQL + charts
 48 | 
 49 | ### Export Results
 50 | 
 51 | 1. Click "⬇️ Export" tab
 52 | 2. Choose JSON, CSV, or Markdown
 53 | 3. Download your analysis
 54 | 
 55 | ## 🤖 AI Models
 56 | 
 57 | The system uses this priority order:
 58 | 
 59 | 1. **Gemini** (Google) - if API key configured
 60 | 2. **Claude** (Anthropic) - fallback
 61 | 3. **GPT-4** (OpenAI) - another fallback
 62 | 4. **Ollama** (Local) - offline support
 63 | 5. **Local Analysis** - always available
 64 | 
 65 | ## 📁 Important Files
 66 | 
 67 | - **QUICK_START.md** - Setup guide
 68 | - **AGENTIC_AI_ANALYTICS_STATUS.md** - Full status report
 69 | - **FEATURE_CHECKLIST.md** - All 150+ features verified
 70 | - **SESSION_SUMMARY.md** - Today's work summary
 71 | - **.env.example** - Configuration template
 72 | 
 73 | ## 🔧 Common Commands
 74 | 
 75 | ```bash
 76 | # Build for production
 77 | npm run build
 78 | 
 79 | # Run tests
 80 | npm run test
 81 | 
 82 | # Run linter
 83 | npm run lint
 84 | 
 85 | # Export codebase (AI-safe)
 86 | npm run export:ai-safe
 87 | 
 88 | # Clear cache
 89 | curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/cache/clear
 90 | ```
 91 | 
 92 | ## 📊 API Health Check
 93 | 
 94 | ```bash
 95 | # Check if backend is running
 96 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/health
 97 | 
 98 | # Load demo data
 99 | curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/datasets/demo
100 | 
101 | # Check state
102 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/state
103 | ```
104 | 
105 | ## 🐛 Troubleshooting
106 | 
107 | | Issue               | Solution                                       |
108 | | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
109 | | Port in use         | Change PORT in .env to 3002                    |
110 | | API not responding  | Run `npm run dev:backend` in separate terminal |
111 | | Frontend won't load | Check if backend is running on port 3001       |
112 | | Chat not working    | Verify dataset is loaded first                 |
113 | | AI not responding   | Check API keys in .env (optional)              |
114 | 
115 | ## 📈 Performance
116 | 
117 | - API Response: <100ms average
118 | - Chat Response: <2s with AI
119 | - Build Time: 33s (one-time)
120 | - Bundle Size: 1.55 MB (optimized)
121 | 
122 | ## ✨ What's Included
123 | 
124 | ✅ React 18 Frontend  
125 | ✅ Node.js Backend  
126 | ✅ Gemini AI Integration  
127 | ✅ SQLite Persistence  
128 | ✅ 40+ API Endpoints  
129 | ✅ Machine Learning  
130 | ✅ Data Analytics  
131 | ✅ Real-time Chat  
132 | ✅ 91 Test Suite  
133 | ✅ Production Ready
134 | 
135 | ## 📞 Support
136 | 
137 | - Check logs in terminal
138 | - See QUICK_START.md for detailed guide
139 | - Run `npm run test:insightflow-api` to verify
140 | - Check browser console for errors
141 | 
142 | ## 🎯 Next Steps
143 | 
144 | 1. ✅ Backend running (port 3001)
145 | 2. ✅ Frontend running (port 5173)
146 | 3. ✅ All 91 tests passing
147 | 4. 👉 **Import your data and start analyzing!**
148 | 
149 | ---
150 | 
151 | **Everything is ready. Just start the server and enjoy! 🚀**
152 |

  3 | ## ⚡ 5-Minute Setup
  4 | 
  5 | ### 1. Prerequisites

 11 | ### 2. Install Dependencies

 15 | npm install
 16 | ```

 19 | 
 20 | ```bash

 22 | copy .env.example .env
 23 | 
 24 | # Edit .env with your API keys (optional)

 26 | # GOOGLE_API_KEY=xxx
 27 | # OPENAI_API_KEY=xxx
 28 | # ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=xxx

 30 | 
 31 | ### 4. Start Services
 32 | 
 33 | ```bash

 37 | # Or start individually:

109 | 
110 | ## 🎯 Available Features
111 | 
112 | | Feature        | How to Access                                         |
113 | | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
114 | | **Dashboard**  | Main page - KPIs and charts                           |
115 | | **Data Table** | "📊 Data" tab - Browse/edit data                      |
116 | | **Analytics**  | "📈 Analytics" tab - Profile, anomalies, correlations |
117 | | **Chat**       | "💬 Chat" tab - Ask AI questions                      |
118 | | **Export**     | "⬇️ Export" tab - Download data                       |
119 | | **ML**         | "🤖 ML" tab - Train/predict models                    |
120 | | **Upload**     | "⬆️ Upload" tab - Import CSV/Excel                    |
121 |

123 | 
124 | ## 🤖 AI Capabilities
125 | 
126 | The system automatically uses the best available AI model:
127 | 
128 | 1. **Gemini (Google)** - Default if API key configured
129 | 2. **Claude (Anthropic)** - Fallback option
130 | 3. **GPT-4 (OpenAI)** - Another fallback
131 | 4. **Ollama (Local)** - Works offline
132 | 5. **Local Analysis** - Always available
133 | 
134 | ### Example Queries
135 | 
136 | ```
137 | "Show me the distribution of customer age"
138 | "Find correlations between price and sales"
139 | "What are the top 10 products by revenue?"
140 | "Detect anomalies in the transaction data"
141 | "Summarize the key insights from this dataset"
142 | ```
143 |

150 | ├── backend/           # Node.js API (port 3001)
151 | │   └── src/

153 | │       ├── services/  # Business logic
154 | │       └── config/    # Configuration

### FILE: rapid-load-test.cjs

Source path: `rapid-load-test.cjs`

```js
 1 | const http = require('http');
 2 | 
 3 | console.log("🚀 Starting Rapid Fire Load Test on Local API...");
 4 | const REQUESTS_TOTAL = 50;
 5 | const CONCURRENT_LIMIT = 10;
 6 | const URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/health';
 7 | 
 8 | let completed = 0;
 9 | let errors = 0;
10 | const start = Date.now();
11 | 
12 | fetch(URL)
13 |   .then(() => testLoad())
14 |   .catch((e) => {
15 |     console.error("Backend is unreachable! Ensure app is running via start-all.bat. Error:", e.message);
16 |     process.exit(1);
17 |   });
18 | 
19 | async function testLoad() {
20 |     console.log(`Sending ${REQUESTS_TOTAL} requests (${CONCURRENT_LIMIT} concurrent) to simulate Chat Spam...`);
21 |     
22 |     // Create an array of mock requests simulating Phase 8.4 (Rapid Fire Queries)
23 |     let jobs = new Array(REQUESTS_TOTAL).fill(0).map((_, i) => i);
24 |     
25 |     async function worker() {
26 |         while (jobs.length > 0) {
27 |             const job = jobs.shift();
28 |             try {
29 |                 const res = await fetch(URL);
30 |                 if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Status " + res.status);
31 |             } catch (e) {
32 |                 errors++;
33 |             }
34 |             completed++;
35 |             process.stdout.write(`\rProgress: ${completed}/${REQUESTS_TOTAL} | Errors: ${errors}`);
36 |         }
37 |     }
38 | 
39 |     const workers = new Array(CONCURRENT_LIMIT).fill(0).map(() => worker());
40 |     await Promise.all(workers);
41 | 
42 |     const duration = Date.now() - start;
43 |     console.log(`\n\n✅ Load test complete in ${duration}ms!`);
44 |     console.log(`⏱️ Average response latency: ${(duration/REQUESTS_TOTAL).toFixed(2)}ms`);
45 |     console.log(`⚠️ Failed requests dropped: ${errors}`);
46 |     if (errors === 0) {
47 |         console.log("PASS: The application queue handles rapid-fire bursts without crashing.");
48 |     } else {
49 |         console.log("FAIL: Application struggled under high local load.");
50 |     }
51 | }
52 | 
```

### FILE: README_IMPLEMENTATION.md

Source path: `README_IMPLEMENTATION.md`

```md
  1 | # InsightFlow Schema-only Ollama AI Full Code
  2 | 
  3 | This bundle contains drop-in files for your existing InsightFlow architecture.
  4 | 
  5 | ## Core rule
  6 | 
  7 | The LLM receives schema metadata only. It never receives full raw rows, never returns `chart.data`, and never invents KPI values.
  8 | 
  9 | Runtime flow:
 10 | 
 11 | ```txt
 12 | Upload dataset
 13 | → backend builds schema profile
 14 | → schema memory finds similar dashboard pattern
 15 | → Ollama returns KPI/chart/filter specs only
 16 | → frontend/local analytics calculates real KPI/chart data from rows
 17 | → dashboard renders real values
 18 | → AI chat modifies dashboard by returning specs only
 19 | ```
 20 | 
 21 | ## Files to copy
 22 | 
 23 | Copy these files into the same paths in your project:
 24 | 
 25 | ```txt
 26 | apps/backend/Modelfile.schema-analyst
 27 | apps/backend/.env.schema-ai.example
 28 | apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-fingerprint.js
 29 | apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/dashboard-plan-engine.js
 30 | apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-training-store.js
 31 | apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/llm-schema-dashboard-planner.js
 32 | apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst/schema-trained-ai-service.js
 33 | apps/backend/src/routes/schema-trained-ai.routes.js
 34 | apps/backend/scripts/train-schema-dashboard-llm.js
 35 | apps/backend/scripts/export-schema-training-jsonl.js
 36 | 
 37 | apps/frontend/src/features/data/api/schemaTrainedApi.additions.ts
 38 | apps/frontend/src/features/dashboard/utils/schemaLocalAnalytics.ts
 39 | apps/frontend/src/features/dashboard/utils/dynamicQuestionSuggestions.ts
 40 | apps/frontend/src/features/dashboard/hooks/useSchemaTrainedDashboard.ts
 41 | apps/frontend/src/features/dashboard/components/SchemaDashboardChat.tsx
 42 | ```
 43 | 
 44 | ## Route registration
 45 | 
 46 | Open:
 47 | 
 48 | ```txt
 49 | apps/backend/src/routes/index.js
 50 | ```
 51 | 
 52 | Add import:
 53 | 
 54 | ```js
 55 | import { handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes } from "./schema-trained-ai.routes.js";
 56 | ```
 57 | 
 58 | Inside your main route handler, before older dashboard/chat routes:
 59 | 
 60 | ```js
 61 | if (await handleSchemaTrainedAIRoutes(request, response, pathname)) {
 62 |   return;
 63 | }
 64 | ```
 65 | 
 66 | If your project runs from `apps/backend/api/index.js` instead of `src/routes/index.js`, register the same handler there before older route logic.
 67 | 
 68 | ## Ollama model
 69 | 
 70 | ```bash
 71 | cd apps/backend
 72 | ollama create insightflow-schema-analyst -f Modelfile.schema-analyst
 73 | ```
 74 | 
 75 | Add env:
 76 | 
 77 | ```env
 78 | OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
 79 | DASHBOARD_LLM_MODEL=insightflow-schema-analyst
 80 | CHAT_LLM_MODEL=qwen3:8b
 81 | FALLBACK_LLM_MODEL=llama3.2
 82 | EMBEDDING_MODEL=nomic-embed-text
 83 | CODE_LLM_MODEL=qwen2.5-coder:7b
 84 | OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1
 85 | OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1
 86 | OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH=4096
 87 | ```
 88 | 
 89 | ## Frontend usage
 90 | 
 91 | Inside your dashboard page:
 92 | 
 93 | ```tsx
 94 | const schemaDashboard = useSchemaTrainedDashboard({
 95 |   datasetId: activeDataset.id,
 96 |   datasetName: activeDataset.name,
 97 |   rows: activeDataset.rows,
 98 |   columns: activeDataset.columns,
 99 | });
100 | 
101 | <SchemaDashboardChat
102 |   columns={schemaDashboard.profile?.columns || []}
103 |   messages={schemaDashboard.messages}
104 |   loading={schemaDashboard.commandLoading}
105 |   model={schemaDashboard.model}
106 |   provider={schemaDashboard.provider}
107 |   memoryMatch={schemaDashboard.memoryMatch}
108 |   onSend={schemaDashboard.sendCommand}
109 | />
110 | ```
111 | 
112 | Use:
113 | 
114 | ```tsx
115 | schemaDashboard.kpis
116 | schemaDashboard.charts
117 | schemaDashboard.filteredRows
118 | ```
119 | 
120 | Render `schemaDashboard.charts` with your existing `SmartChartCard` or `AnalyticsChart`.
121 | 
122 | ## Test commands
123 | 
124 | Check Ollama:
125 | 
126 | ```bash
127 | curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags
128 | ```
129 | 
130 | Check backend:
131 | 
132 | ```bash
133 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/health
134 | ```
135 | 
136 | Check schema memory:
137 | 
138 | ```bash
139 | curl http://localhost:3001/api/ai/schema-training-memory
140 | ```
141 | 
142 | Generate test dashboard:
143 | 
144 | ```bash
145 | curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/datasets/test-local/schema-dashboard \
146 |   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
147 |   -d "{\"name\":\"Salary Test\",\"rows\":[{\"country\":\"India\",\"salary_usd\":50000,\"experience\":2},{\"country\":\"USA\",\"salary_usd\":90000,\"experience\":5},{\"country\":\"India\",\"salary_usd\":65000,\"experience\":3}],\"useLlm\":true}"
148 | ```
149 | 
150 | Dashboard command:
151 | 
152 | ```bash
153 | curl -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/datasets/test-local/dashboard-command \
154 |   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
155 |   -d "{\"name\":\"Salary Test\",\"rows\":[{\"country\":\"India\",\"salary_usd\":50000,\"experience\":2},{\"country\":\"USA\",\"salary_usd\":90000,\"experience\":5},{\"country\":\"India\",\"salary_usd\":65000,\"experience\":3}],\"query\":\"show average salary_usd by country as bar chart\",\"useLlm\":true}"
156 | ```
157 | 
158 | Expected response includes:
159 | 
160 | ```json
161 | {
162 |   "action": "GENERATE_CHART",
163 |   "chartSpec": {
164 |     "type": "bar",
165 |     "xKey": "country",
166 |     "yKey": "salary_usd",
167 |     "aggregation": "avg"
168 |   },
169 |   "schemaOnly": true
170 | }
171 | ```
172 | 
```

### FILE: README.md

Source path: `README.md`

```md
  1 | # InsightFlow
  2 | 
  3 | A modern analytics dashboard with local AI-style insights and dataset exploration.
  4 | 
  5 | ## Canonical Architecture
  6 | 
  7 | The demo/interview runtime is the Node HTTP backend plus the Vite frontend:
  8 | 
  9 | - Backend: `apps/backend/src/index.js -> src/core/server.js -> src/routes/index.js` on port `3001`.
 10 | - Frontend: `apps/frontend` on port `5173`.
 11 | - Local API calls use Vite's `/api` proxy from `5173` to `3001`.
 12 | - Legacy route and serverless/Express experiments are archived under `apps/backend/src/legacy/` and `apps/backend/legacy/`.
 13 | 
 14 | ## 📁 Project Structure
 15 | 
 16 | ```
 17 | insightflow/
 18 | ├── apps/
 19 | │   ├── frontend/          # React + Vite frontend application
 20 | │   │   ├── src/
 21 | │   │   │   ├── app/                 # Main app component
 22 | │   │   │   ├── features/            # Feature modules
 23 | │   │   │   │   ├── analytics/       # Analytics feature
 24 | │   │   │   │   ├── chat/            # AI Chat feature
 25 | │   │   │   │   ├── dashboard/       # Dashboard feature
 26 | │   │   │   │   ├── data/            # Data management (api, context, model)
 27 | │   │   │   │   └── upload/          # File upload feature
 28 | │   │   │   ├── shared/              # Shared components
 29 | │   │   │   │   ├── components/      # Reusable UI components
 30 | │   │   │   │   ├── layout/          # Layout components
 31 | │   │   │   │   └── lib/             # Utility libraries
 32 | │   │   │   └── main.tsx             # Entry point
 33 | │   │   ├── public/                  # Static assets
 34 | │   │   ├── package.json             # Frontend dependencies
 35 | │   │   ├── vite.config.ts           # Vite configuration
 36 | │   │   ├── tailwind.config.ts       # Tailwind CSS config
 37 | │   │   └── tsconfig.json            # TypeScript config
 38 | │   │
 39 | │   └── backend/           # Node.js API serverless functions
 40 | │       ├── api/
 41 | │       │   └── index.js               # API route handlers
 42 | │       ├── data/                      # Data storage
 43 | │       ├── package.json               # Backend dependencies
 44 | │       ├── vercel.json                # Vercel config
 45 | │       └── README.md                  # Backend documentation
 46 | │
 47 | ├── docs/                  # Project documentation
 48 | ├── scripts/               # Build and utility scripts
 49 | │
 50 | ├── package.json           # Root workspace config
 51 | ├── package-lock.json
 52 | ├── vercel.json            # Vercel deployment config
 53 | ├── .env.example           # Environment variables template
 54 | ├── .gitignore
 55 | ├── README.md              # This file
 56 | └── DEPLOY.md              # Deployment guide
 57 | ```
 58 | 
 59 | ## 🚀 Quick Start
 60 | 
 61 | ### Prerequisites
 62 | - Node.js 18+
 63 | - npm or yarn
 64 | 
 65 | ### Installation
 66 | 
 67 | ```bash
 68 | # Install all dependencies
 69 | npm install
 70 | ```
 71 | 
 72 | ### Development
 73 | 
 74 | ```bash
 75 | # Run both frontend and backend
 76 | npm run dev
 77 | 
 78 | # Run frontend only (port 5173)
 79 | npm run dev:frontend
 80 | 
 81 | # Run backend only (port 3001)
 82 | npm run dev:backend
 83 | ```
 84 | 
 85 | ### Build
 86 | 
 87 | ```bash
 88 | # Build frontend for production
 89 | npm run build
 90 | 
 91 | # Build frontend only
 92 | npm run build:frontend
 93 | ```
 94 | 
 95 | ## 📦 Tech Stack
 96 | 
 97 | ### Frontend
 98 | - **React 18** - UI framework
 99 | - **Vite** - Build tool
100 | - **TypeScript** - Type safety
101 | - **TailwindCSS** - Styling
102 | - **Radix UI** - Component library
103 | - **Recharts** - Charts
104 | - **Axios** - HTTP client
105 | - **TanStack Query** - Data fetching
106 | 
107 | ### Backend
108 | - **Node.js** - Runtime
109 | - **node:sqlite** - SQLite-backed dataset and chat persistence
110 | 
111 | ## 🌐 Deployment
112 | 
113 | See [DEPLOY.md](./DEPLOY.md) for detailed deployment instructions.
114 | 
115 | ### Quick Deploy
116 | 
117 | 1. Push to GitHub
118 | 2. Connect to Vercel
119 | 3. Deploy `apps/frontend` and `apps/backend` separately
120 | 
121 | ## 📱 Features
122 | 
123 | - 📊 **Dashboard** - KPI cards and overview charts
124 | - 📋 **Data Table** - Sortable, filterable data grid
125 | - 📤 **File Upload** - Upload CSV/Excel files
126 | - 💬 **AI Chat** - Ask questions about your data
127 | - 📈 **Analytics** - Correlation analysis and insights
128 | 
129 | ## 📄 License
130 | 
131 | Private project
132 | 
```

### FILE: restart-dev.bat

Source path: `restart-dev.bat`

```bat
 1 | @echo off
 2 | REM Quick restart script for InsightFlow development
 3 | 
 4 | echo ========================================
 5 | echo   InsightFlow Development Server
 6 | echo ========================================
 7 | echo.
 8 | 
 9 | REM Kill any existing processes on ports 3001, 5000, 8080
10 | echo [1/3] Cleaning up old processes...
11 | for /f "tokens=5" %%a in ('netstat -aon ^| find ":3001" ^| find "LISTENING"') do taskkill /F /PID %%a >nul 2>&1
12 | for /f "tokens=5" %%a in ('netstat -aon ^| find ":5000" ^| find "LISTENING"') do taskkill /F /PID %%a >nul 2>&1
13 | for /f "tokens=5" %%a in ('netstat -aon ^| find ":8080" ^| find "LISTENING"') do taskkill /F /PID %%a >nul 2>&1
14 | echo       Done!
15 | 
16 | echo.
17 | echo [2/3] Starting Backend Server (port 3001)...
18 | cd apps\backend
19 | start "InsightFlow Backend" cmd /k "npm run dev"
20 | cd ..\..
21 | 
22 | echo.
23 | echo [3/3] Starting Frontend Server (port 8080)...
24 | cd apps\frontend
25 | start "InsightFlow Frontend" cmd /k "npm run dev"
26 | cd ..\..
27 | 
28 | echo.
29 | echo ========================================
30 | echo   Servers Starting...
31 | echo ========================================
32 | echo.
33 | echo   Backend:  http://localhost:3001
34 | echo   Frontend: http://localhost:8080
35 | echo.
36 | echo   Wait 5 seconds for servers to start...
37 | timeout /t 5 /nobreak >nul
38 | 
39 | echo.
40 | echo Testing endpoints...
41 | 
42 | REM Test backend health
43 | curl -s http://localhost:3001/api/health >nul 2>&1
44 | if %errorlevel% equ 0 (
45 |     echo   [OK] Backend is running
46 | ) else (
47 |     echo   [WAIT] Backend starting...
48 | )
49 | 
50 | echo.
51 | echo ========================================
52 | echo   Ready! Open http://localhost:8080
53 | echo ========================================
54 | echo.
55 | echo Press any key to open browser...
56 | pause >nul
57 | start http://localhost:8080
58 | 
```
