### FILE: scripts/agentic-salary-stress-audit.mjs

Source path: `scripts/agentic-salary-stress-audit.mjs`

```js
   1 | #!/usr/bin/env node
   2 | 
   3 | /**
   4 |  * Agentic AI Analytics Salary Stress Audit
   5 |  *
   6 |  * Evidence-only harness for the real salary CSV files. It exercises public
   7 |  * backend APIs and writes PASS/FAIL reports without adding missing features.
   8 |  *
   9 |  * Run:
  10 |  *   node scripts/agentic-salary-stress-audit.mjs
  11 |  *
  12 |  * Optional:
  13 |  *   BACKEND_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 node scripts/agentic-salary-stress-audit.mjs
  14 |  */
  15 | 
  16 | import fs from "node:fs";
  17 | import path from "node:path";
  18 | import process from "node:process";
  19 | import Papa from "papaparse";
  20 | 
  21 | const ROOT = process.cwd();
  22 | const BASE_URL = process.env.BACKEND_URL || "http://127.0.0.1:3001";
  23 | const REPORT_JSON = path.join(ROOT, "reports", "agentic-salary-stress-audit.json");
  24 | const REPORT_MD = path.join(ROOT, "reports", "agentic-salary-stress-audit.md");
  25 | 
  26 | const CSV_PATHS = {
  27 |   train:
  28 |     process.env.SALARY_TRAIN_CSV ||
  29 |     "c:\\Users\\VISHAL\\OneDrive\\Documents\\Downloads\\SD_salary\\train.csv",
  30 |   test:
  31 |     process.env.SALARY_TEST_CSV ||
  32 |     "c:\\Users\\VISHAL\\OneDrive\\Documents\\Downloads\\SD_salary\\test.csv",
  33 |   dictionary:
  34 |     process.env.SALARY_DICTIONARY_CSV ||
  35 |     "c:\\Users\\VISHAL\\OneDrive\\Documents\\Downloads\\SD_salary\\data_dictionary.csv",
  36 | };
  37 | 
  38 | const EXPECTED_AGENT_STEPS = [
  39 |   "schema_analysis",
  40 |   "master_planning",
  41 |   "analytics_engine",
  42 |   "dashboard_guardian",
  43 |   "final_explanation",
  44 | ];
  45 | 
  46 | const phases = [];
  47 | const endpointEvidence = [];
  48 | const generatedFiles = [];
  49 | const datasets = {};
  50 | let parsedFiles = {};
  51 | let deterministic = {};
  52 | let schemaDashboard = null;
  53 | 
  54 | function nowIso() {
  55 |   return new Date().toISOString();
  56 | }
  57 | 
  58 | function durationMs(started) {
  59 |   return Number(process.hrtime.bigint() / 1_000_000n) - started;
  60 | }
  61 | 
  62 | function startTimer() {
  63 |   return Number(process.hrtime.bigint() / 1_000_000n);
  64 | }
  65 | 
  66 | function normalizeName(value = "") {
  67 |   return String(value)
  68 |     .trim()
  69 |     .toLowerCase()
  70 |     .replace(/^[\ufeff\s]+/, "")
  71 |     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_")
  72 |     .replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
  73 | }
  74 | 
  75 | function safeNumber(value) {
  76 |   if (typeof value === "number" && Number.isFinite(value)) return value;
  77 |   const cleaned = String(value ?? "")
  78 |     .replace(/[,$₹€£%\s]/g, "")
  79 |     .trim();
  80 |   if (!cleaned || cleaned === "-") return null;
  81 |   const parsed = Number(cleaned);
  82 |   return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : null;
  83 | }
  84 | 
  85 | function parseCsvFile(filePath) {
  86 |   if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
  87 |     throw new Error(`CSV file not found: ${filePath}`);
  88 |   }
  89 | 
  90 |   const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, "utf8");
  91 |   const parsed = Papa.parse(raw, {
  92 |     header: true,
  93 |     skipEmptyLines: true,
  94 |     dynamicTyping: false,
  95 |   });
  96 | 
  97 |   if (parsed.errors.length) {
  98 |     throw new Error(
  99 |       `Failed to parse ${filePath}: ${parsed.errors
 100 |         .slice(0, 3)
 101 |         .map((error) => error.message)
 102 |         .join("; ")}`
 103 |     );
 104 |   }
 105 | 
 106 |   return parsed.data.map((row) =>
 107 |     Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(row).map(([key, value]) => [key.trim(), value]))
 108 |   );
 109 | }
 110 | 
 111 | function dictionaryMap(rows) {
 112 |   return new Map(
 113 |     rows.map((row) => [
 114 |       normalizeName(row.Column || row.column || row.name),
 115 |       {
 116 |         type: row.Type || row.type || "",
 117 |         description: row.Description || row.description || "",
 118 |       },
 119 |     ])
 120 |   );
 121 | }
 122 | 
 123 | function inferType(values = [], explicitType = "") {
 124 |   const explicit = normalizeName(explicitType);
 125 |   if (explicit === "number" || explicit === "numeric" || explicit === "target") return "number";
 126 |   if (explicit === "date" || explicit === "datetime") return "date";
 127 |   if (explicit === "string" || explicit === "text" || explicit === "category") return "string";
 128 | 
 129 |   const present = values.filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && String(value).trim() !== "");
 130 |   if (!present.length) return "string";
 131 | 
 132 |   const sample = present.slice(0, 500);
 133 |   const numeric = sample.filter((value) => safeNumber(value) !== null).length / sample.length;
 134 |   const date = sample.filter((value) => Number.isFinite(Date.parse(String(value)))).length / sample.length;
 135 | 
 136 |   if (numeric >= 0.8) return "number";
 137 |   if (date >= 0.8) return "date";
 138 |   return "string";
 139 | }
 140 | 
 141 | function inferColumns(rows, dict = new Map()) {
 142 |   const names = Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 143 |   return names.map((name) => {
 144 |     const info = dict.get(normalizeName(name)) || {};
 145 |     const values = rows.map((row) => row[name]);
 146 |     const type = inferType(values, info.type);
 147 | 
 148 |     return {
 149 |       name,
 150 |       type,
 151 |       inferredType: type,
 152 |       role: normalizeName(info.type) === "target" ? "target" : type === "number" ? "metric" : type === "date" ? "date" : "dimension",
 153 |       description: info.description || "",
 154 |       sample: values.filter((value) => value !== null && value !== undefined && String(value).trim() !== "").slice(0, 5),
 155 |     };
 156 |   });
 157 | }
 158 | 
 159 | function datasetPayload({ name, fileName, rows, dictRows = [] }) {
 160 |   const dict = dictionaryMap(dictRows);
 161 |   return {
 162 |     name,
 163 |     fileName,
 164 |     sourceType: "stress-audit",
 165 |     columns: inferColumns(rows, dict),
 166 |     rows,
 167 |     dictionaryRows: dictRows,
 168 |   };
 169 | }
 170 | 
 171 | function summarizeJson(value, depth = 0) {
 172 |   if (value === null || value === undefined) return value;
 173 |   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
 174 |     if (depth > 1) return { type: "array", length: value.length };
 175 |     return {
 176 |       type: "array",
 177 |       length: value.length,
 178 |       sample: value.slice(0, 3).map((item) => summarizeJson(item, depth + 1)),
 179 |     };
 180 |   }
 181 |   if (typeof value === "object") {
 182 |     const output = {};
 183 |     for (const [key, item] of Object.entries(value).slice(0, 30)) {
 184 |       if (["rows", "data", "rawRows", "sampleRows"].includes(key) && Array.isArray(item)) {
 185 |         output[key] = { type: "array", length: item.length };
 186 |       } else {
 187 |         output[key] = depth > 2 ? typeof item : summarizeJson(item, depth + 1);
 188 |       }
 189 |     }
 190 |     return output;
 191 |   }
 192 |   if (typeof value === "string" && value.length > 300) return `${value.slice(0, 300)}...`;
 193 |   return value;
 194 | }
 195 | 
 196 | async function request(method, route, body) {
 197 |   const started = startTimer();
 198 |   const entry = {
 199 |     method,
 200 |     route,
 201 |     status: 0,
 202 |     ok: false,
 203 |     ms: 0,
 204 |   };
 205 | 
 206 |   try {
 207 |     const response = await fetch(`${BASE_URL}${route}`, {
 208 |       method,
 209 |       headers: {
 210 |         Accept: "application/json",
 211 |         ...(body === undefined ? {} : { "Content-Type": "application/json" }),
 212 |       },
 213 |       body: body === undefined ? undefined : JSON.stringify(body),
 214 |     });
 215 | 
 216 |     const text = await response.text();
 217 |     let json = null;
 218 |     try {
 219 |       json = text ? JSON.parse(text) : null;
 220 |     } catch {
 221 |       json = null;
 222 |     }
 223 | 
 224 |     entry.status = response.status;
 225 |     entry.ok = response.ok;
 226 |     entry.ms = durationMs(started);
 227 |     entry.summary = summarizeJson(json ?? text);
 228 |     endpointEvidence.push(entry);
 229 |     return { ...entry, json, text };
 230 |   } catch (error) {
 231 |     entry.ms = durationMs(started);
 232 |     entry.error = error.message;
 233 |     endpointEvidence.push(entry);
 234 |     return { ...entry, json: null, text: "" };
 235 |   }
 236 | }
 237 | 
 238 | function addPhase(id, title, status, checks, evidence = {}, improvements = []) {
 239 |   phases.push({
 240 |     id,
 241 |     title,
 242 |     status,
 243 |     checks,
 244 |     evidence,
 245 |     improvements,
 246 |   });
 247 | }
 248 | 
 249 | function metricStats(rows, column) {
 250 |   const values = rows.map((row) => safeNumber(row[column])).filter((value) => value !== null);
 251 |   const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
 252 |   const sum = values.reduce((total, value) => total + value, 0);
 253 |   const avg = values.length ? sum / values.length : 0;
 254 |   return {
 255 |     count: values.length,
 256 |     sum,
 257 |     avg,
 258 |     min: sorted[0] ?? null,
 259 |     max: sorted.at(-1) ?? null,
 260 |     median: percentile(sorted, 0.5),
 261 |     outliersIqr: countIqrOutliers(sorted),
 262 |   };
 263 | }
 264 | 
 265 | function percentile(sorted, p) {
 266 |   if (!sorted.length) return null;
 267 |   const index = (sorted.length - 1) * p;
 268 |   const lower = Math.floor(index);
 269 |   const upper = Math.ceil(index);
 270 |   if (lower === upper) return sorted[lower];
 271 |   return sorted[lower] + (sorted[upper] - sorted[lower]) * (index - lower);
 272 | }
 273 | 
 274 | function countIqrOutliers(sorted) {
 275 |   if (sorted.length < 4) return 0;
 276 |   const q1 = percentile(sorted, 0.25);
 277 |   const q3 = percentile(sorted, 0.75);
 278 |   const iqr = q3 - q1;
 279 |   const lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr;
 280 |   const upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr;
 281 |   return sorted.filter((value) => value < lower || value > upper).length;
 282 | }
 283 | 
 284 | function correlation(rows, leftKey, rightKey) {
 285 |   const pairs = rows
 286 |     .map((row) => [safeNumber(row[leftKey]), safeNumber(row[rightKey])])
 287 |     .filter(([left, right]) => left !== null && right !== null);
 288 | 
 289 |   if (pairs.length < 2) return null;
 290 | 
 291 |   const meanLeft = pairs.reduce((sum, [left]) => sum + left, 0) / pairs.length;
 292 |   const meanRight = pairs.reduce((sum, [, right]) => sum + right, 0) / pairs.length;
 293 | 
 294 |   let numerator = 0;
 295 |   let leftDenominator = 0;
 296 |   let rightDenominator = 0;
 297 | 
 298 |   for (const [left, right] of pairs) {
 299 |     const leftDelta = left - meanLeft;
 300 |     const rightDelta = right - meanRight;
 301 |     numerator += leftDelta * rightDelta;
 302 |     leftDenominator += leftDelta ** 2;
 303 |     rightDenominator += rightDelta ** 2;
 304 |   }
 305 | 
 306 |   const denominator = Math.sqrt(leftDenominator * rightDenominator);
 307 |   return denominator ? numerator / denominator : null;
 308 | }
 309 | 
 310 | function duplicateCount(rows) {
 311 |   const seen = new Set();
 312 |   let duplicates = 0;
 313 |   for (const row of rows) {
 314 |     const key = JSON.stringify(row);
 315 |     if (seen.has(key)) duplicates += 1;
 316 |     else seen.add(key);
 317 |   }
 318 |   return duplicates;
 319 | }
 320 | 
 321 | function missingValues(rows) {
 322 |   const counts = {};
 323 |   const names = Object.keys(rows[0] || {});
 324 |   for (const name of names) counts[name] = 0;
 325 |   for (const row of rows) {
 326 |     for (const name of names) {
 327 |       if (row[name] === null || row[name] === undefined || String(row[name]).trim() === "") {
 328 |         counts[name] += 1;
 329 |       }
 330 |     }
 331 |   }
 332 |   return counts;
 333 | }
 334 | 
 335 | function buildDeterministicAnalytics(rows) {
 336 |   return {
 337 |     rowCount: rows.length,
 338 |     columnCount: Object.keys(rows[0] || {}).length,
 339 |     missingValues: missingValues(rows),
 340 |     duplicateRows: duplicateCount(rows),
 341 |     experience: metricStats(rows, "experience"),
 342 |     salary_usd: metricStats(rows, "salary_usd"),
 343 |     correlation_experience_salary_usd: correlation(rows, "experience", "salary_usd"),
 344 |   };
 345 | }
 346 | 
 347 | function extractDataset(response) {
 348 |   return response.json?.data?.dataset || response.json?.dataset || response.json?.data || null;
 349 | }
 350 | 
 351 | function extractKpis(response) {
 352 |   const analysis = response.json?.data?.analysis || response.json?.analysis || response.json?.data;
 353 |   return analysis?.kpis || analysis?.dashboard?.kpis || analysis?.dashboardPlan?.kpis || [];
 354 | }
 355 | 
 356 | function parseKpiNumber(value) {
 357 |   const num = safeNumber(String(value ?? "").replace(/[^\d.,$₹€£%-]/g, ""));
 358 |   return num;
 359 | }
 360 | 
 361 | function closeEnough(actual, expected, tolerance = 0.01) {
 362 |   if (actual === null || expected === null || actual === undefined || expected === undefined) return false;
 363 |   const allowed = Math.max(tolerance, Math.abs(expected) * 0.005);
 364 |   return Math.abs(actual - expected) <= allowed;
 365 | }
 366 | 
 367 | function validateKnownKpis(kpis, expected) {
 368 |   const checks = [];
 369 |   for (const kpi of kpis || []) {
 370 |     const title = String(kpi.title || kpi.label || kpi.id || "").toLowerCase();
 371 |     const value = parseKpiNumber(kpi.value);
 372 | 
 373 |     if (/total.*record|row/.test(title)) {
 374 |       checks.push({
 375 |         kpi: kpi.title || kpi.label,
 376 |         expected: expected.rowCount,
 377 |         actual: value,
 378 |         pass: closeEnough(value, expected.rowCount, 1),
 379 |       });
 380 |     }
 381 |     if (/average.*salary|avg.*salary/.test(title)) {
 382 |       checks.push({
 383 |         kpi: kpi.title || kpi.label,
 384 |         expected: expected.salary_usd.avg,
 385 |         actual: value,
 386 |         pass: closeEnough(value, expected.salary_usd.avg, 1),
 387 |       });
 388 |     }
 389 |     if (/highest.*salary|max.*salary/.test(title)) {
 390 |       checks.push({
 391 |         kpi: kpi.title || kpi.label,
 392 |         expected: expected.salary_usd.max,
 393 |         actual: value,
 394 |         pass: closeEnough(value, expected.salary_usd.max, 1),
 395 |       });
 396 |     }
 397 |   }
 398 |   return checks;
 399 | }
 400 | 
 401 | function hasField(object, field) {
 402 |   return Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(object || {}, field);
 403 | }
 404 | 
 405 | function statusFromChecks(checks, required = null) {
 406 |   const failures = checks.filter((check) => !check.pass);
 407 |   if (required && !required.every((name) => checks.some((check) => check.name === name && check.pass))) {
 408 |     return "FAIL";
 409 |   }
 410 |   return failures.length ? "FAIL" : "PASS";
 411 | }
 412 | 
 413 | async function ensureBackendReachable() {
 414 |   const health = await request("GET", "/api/health");
 415 |   if (!health.ok) {
 416 |     throw new Error(
 417 |       `Backend is unreachable at ${BASE_URL}. Start it with "npm run dev:backend" before running this audit.`
 418 |     );
 419 |   }
 420 | }
 421 | 
 422 | async function loadFiles() {
 423 |   parsedFiles = {
 424 |     train: parseCsvFile(CSV_PATHS.train),
 425 |     test: parseCsvFile(CSV_PATHS.test),
 426 |     dictionary: parseCsvFile(CSV_PATHS.dictionary),
 427 |   };
 428 |   deterministic = buildDeterministicAnalytics(parsedFiles.train);
 429 | }
 430 | 
 431 | async function phase1DatasetRegistration() {
 432 |   const initial = await request("GET", "/api/datasets");
 433 |   const initialCount = initial.json?.data?.count ?? initial.json?.data?.datasets?.length ?? 0;
 434 | 
 435 |   const payloads = {
 436 |     train: datasetPayload({
 437 |       name: "salary_train_stress_audit",
 438 |       fileName: "train.csv",
 439 |       rows: parsedFiles.train,
 440 |       dictRows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 441 |     }),
 442 |     test: datasetPayload({
 443 |       name: "salary_test_stress_audit",
 444 |       fileName: "test.csv",
 445 |       rows: parsedFiles.test,
 446 |       dictRows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 447 |     }),
 448 |     dictionary: datasetPayload({
 449 |       name: "salary_data_dictionary_stress_audit",
 450 |       fileName: "data_dictionary.csv",
 451 |       rows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 452 |     }),
 453 |   };
 454 | 
 455 |   const imports = {};
 456 |   for (const [key, payload] of Object.entries(payloads)) {
 457 |     imports[key] = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/import", payload);
 458 |     datasets[key] = extractDataset(imports[key]);
 459 |   }
 460 | 
 461 |   const ids = Object.values(datasets).map((dataset) => dataset?.id).filter(Boolean);
 462 |   const retrieves = {};
 463 |   for (const [key, dataset] of Object.entries(datasets)) {
 464 |     retrieves[key] = dataset?.id ? await request("GET", `/api/datasets/${dataset.id}`) : null;
 465 |   }
 466 |   const final = await request("GET", "/api/datasets");
 467 |   const finalCount = final.json?.data?.count ?? final.json?.data?.datasets?.length ?? 0;
 468 | 
 469 |   const checks = [
 470 |     { name: "Backend dataset list is readable before upload", pass: initial.ok, evidence: { status: initial.status } },
 471 |     { name: "All three CSVs import successfully", pass: Object.values(imports).every((res) => res.status === 201), evidence: Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(imports).map(([k, v]) => [k, v.status])) },
 472 |     { name: "Each upload returns a datasetId", pass: ids.length === 3, evidence: { ids } },
 473 |     { name: "Dataset IDs are unique", pass: new Set(ids).size === 3, evidence: { ids } },
 474 |     { name: "Dataset count increased", pass: finalCount >= initialCount + 3, evidence: { initialCount, finalCount } },
 475 |     { name: "Datasets can be retrieved after upload", pass: Object.values(retrieves).every((res) => res?.ok), evidence: Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(retrieves).map(([k, v]) => [k, v?.status ?? 0])) },
 476 |     {
 477 |       name: "Stored metadata includes row and column counts",
 478 |       pass:
 479 |         datasets.train?.rowCount === parsedFiles.train.length &&
 480 |         datasets.test?.rowCount === parsedFiles.test.length &&
 481 |         datasets.dictionary?.rowCount === parsedFiles.dictionary.length &&
 482 |         datasets.train?.columns?.length === Object.keys(parsedFiles.train[0]).length,
 483 |       evidence: {
 484 |         train: { rowCount: datasets.train?.rowCount, columnCount: datasets.train?.columns?.length },
 485 |         test: { rowCount: datasets.test?.rowCount, columnCount: datasets.test?.columns?.length },
 486 |         dictionary: { rowCount: datasets.dictionary?.rowCount, columnCount: datasets.dictionary?.columns?.length },
 487 |       },
 488 |     },
 489 |     {
 490 |       name: "Import response includes generated schema profile/dashboard analysis",
 491 |       pass: Boolean(imports.train.json?.data?.analysis?.schemaPacket || imports.train.json?.data?.analysis?.columnCount),
 492 |       evidence: {
 493 |         hasSchemaPacket: Boolean(imports.train.json?.data?.analysis?.schemaPacket),
 494 |         analysisKeys: Object.keys(imports.train.json?.data?.analysis || {}),
 495 |       },
 496 |     },
 497 |   ];
 498 | 
 499 |   addPhase("phase1", "Dataset Registration", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 500 |     trainDatasetId: datasets.train?.id,
 501 |     testDatasetId: datasets.test?.id,
 502 |     dictionaryDatasetId: datasets.dictionary?.id,
 503 |   });
 504 | 
 505 |   datasets.trainImportResponse = imports.train;
 506 | }
 507 | 
 508 | async function phase2SchemaUnderstanding() {
 509 |   const dataset = {
 510 |     ...datasetPayload({
 511 |       name: "salary_train_with_dictionary",
 512 |       fileName: "train.csv",
 513 |       rows: parsedFiles.train,
 514 |       dictRows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 515 |     }),
 516 |     id: datasets.train?.id,
 517 |   };
 518 | 
 519 |   const understand = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/schema-understand`, { dataset });
 520 |   const schemaDashboardRes = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/schema-dashboard`, {
 521 |     dataset,
 522 |     useLlm: false,
 523 |     useRagEmbedding: false,
 524 |   });
 525 |   schemaDashboard = schemaDashboardRes.json?.data || null;
 526 | 
 527 |   const profile = schemaDashboard?.profile;
 528 |   const columns = profile?.columns || [];
 529 |   const salary = columns.find((column) => normalizeName(column.name) === "salary_usd");
 530 |   const dimensions = columns.filter((column) => ["category", "location", "text", "dimension"].includes(column.role));
 531 |   const measures = columns.filter((column) => /metric/.test(column.role) || column.type === "number");
 532 | 
 533 |   const checks = [
 534 |     { name: "Schema understanding endpoint executes", pass: understand.ok, evidence: { status: understand.status } },
 535 |     { name: "Schema dashboard/profile endpoint executes", pass: schemaDashboardRes.ok, evidence: { status: schemaDashboardRes.status } },
 536 |     { name: "Columns are inventoried", pass: columns.length === 7, evidence: { count: columns.length, names: columns.map((c) => c.name) } },
 537 |     { name: "Columns are not all treated as strings", pass: columns.some((c) => c.type === "number") && columns.some((c) => c.type !== "number"), evidence: columns.map((c) => ({ name: c.name, type: c.type, role: c.role })) },
 538 |     { name: "Measures are detected", pass: measures.length >= 2, evidence: measures.map((c) => c.name) },
 539 |     { name: "Dimensions are detected", pass: dimensions.length >= 3, evidence: dimensions.map((c) => c.name) },
 540 |     { name: "Target/salary column is recognized as analytic metric", pass: Boolean(salary && /metric|target/.test(salary.role)), evidence: salary },
 541 |     { name: "Data dictionary descriptions are used", pass: columns.some((c) => c.description), evidence: columns.map((c) => ({ name: c.name, description: c.description })).filter((c) => c.description) },
 542 |   ];
 543 | 
 544 |   addPhase("phase2", "Schema Understanding", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 545 |     outputShape: {
 546 |       datasetId: datasets.train.id,
 547 |       dimensions: dimensions.map((c) => c.name),
 548 |       measures: measures.map((c) => c.name),
 549 |       targets: salary ? [salary.name] : [],
 550 |       qualityScore: schemaDashboard?.quality?.score ?? null,
 551 |     },
 552 |   });
 553 | }
 554 | 
 555 | async function phase3MultiDatasetReasoning() {
 556 |   const trainPayload = datasetPayload({
 557 |     name: "train.csv",
 558 |     fileName: "train.csv",
 559 |     rows: parsedFiles.train,
 560 |     dictRows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 561 |   });
 562 |   const testPayload = datasetPayload({
 563 |     name: "test.csv",
 564 |     fileName: "test.csv",
 565 |     rows: parsedFiles.test,
 566 |     dictRows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 567 |   });
 568 |   const dictPayload = datasetPayload({
 569 |     name: "data_dictionary.csv",
 570 |     fileName: "data_dictionary.csv",
 571 |     rows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 572 |   });
 573 | 
 574 |   const merge = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/merge", {
 575 |     datasets: [trainPayload, testPayload, dictPayload],
 576 |   });
 577 | 
 578 |   const trainColumns = trainPayload.columns.map((column) => normalizeName(column.name));
 579 |   const testColumns = testPayload.columns.map((column) => normalizeName(column.name));
 580 |   const shared = trainColumns.filter((column) => testColumns.includes(column));
 581 |   const schemaDifferences = [
 582 |     ...trainColumns.filter((column) => !testColumns.includes(column)).map((column) => ({ onlyIn: "train", column })),
 583 |     ...testColumns.filter((column) => !trainColumns.includes(column)).map((column) => ({ onlyIn: "test", column })),
 584 |   ];
 585 |   const expectedMergePlan = {
 586 |     mergeable: schemaDifferences.length === 0,
 587 |     confidence: schemaDifferences.length === 0 ? 0.98 : shared.length / Math.max(trainColumns.length, testColumns.length),
 588 |     joinKeys: [],
 589 |     appendCompatible: schemaDifferences.length === 0,
 590 |     schemaDifferences,
 591 |   };
 592 |   const related = merge.json?.data?.relatedDatasets;
 593 |   const outputHasMergePlan =
 594 |     hasField(merge.json?.data, "mergeable") ||
 595 |     hasField(merge.json?.data, "joinKeys") ||
 596 |     hasField(merge.json?.data, "schemaDifferences");
 597 | 
 598 |   const checks = [
 599 |     { name: "train.csv and test.csv share the same schema", pass: expectedMergePlan.mergeable, evidence: expectedMergePlan },
 600 |     { name: "Multi-file endpoint executes", pass: merge.ok, evidence: { status: merge.status } },
 601 |     { name: "Endpoint classifies primary/test/dictionary roles", pass: Boolean(related?.primaryDataset && related?.testFiles?.length && related?.metadataFiles?.length), evidence: related },
 602 |     {
 603 |       name: "Endpoint returns explicit merge plan shape",
 604 |       pass: outputHasMergePlan,
 605 |       evidence: {
 606 |         expectedKeys: ["mergeable", "confidence", "joinKeys", "schemaDifferences"],
 607 |         actualKeys: Object.keys(merge.json?.data || {}),
 608 |       },
 609 |     },
 610 |   ];
 611 | 
 612 |   addPhase("phase3", "Multi-Dataset Reasoning", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 613 |     expectedMergePlan,
 614 |     endpointResult: summarizeJson(merge.json?.data),
 615 |   }, outputHasMergePlan ? [] : ["Add a real merge-plan response with mergeable, confidence, joinKeys, and schemaDifferences."]);
 616 | }
 617 | 
 618 | async function phase4DatasetUpdate() {
 619 |   const firstRowId = datasets.train?.rows?.[0]?.__rowId;
 620 |   const originalValue = datasets.train?.rows?.[0]?.salary_usd;
 621 |   const patchedValue = String(Number(safeNumber(originalValue) || 0) + 1);
 622 |   const patch = firstRowId
 623 |     ? await request("PATCH", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/rows/${firstRowId}`, {
 624 |         column: "salary_usd",
 625 |         value: patchedValue,
 626 |       })
 627 |     : { ok: false, status: 0, json: null };
 628 | 
 629 |   const restore = firstRowId
 630 |     ? await request("PATCH", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/rows/${firstRowId}`, {
 631 |         column: "salary_usd",
 632 |         value: originalValue,
 633 |       })
 634 |     : { ok: false, status: 0, json: null };
 635 | 
 636 |   const replaceProbe = await request("PUT", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}`, {
 637 |     rows: parsedFiles.train.slice(0, 3),
 638 |     columns: inferColumns(parsedFiles.train.slice(0, 3), dictionaryMap(parsedFiles.dictionary)),
 639 |   });
 640 |   const appendProbe = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/rows`, {
 641 |     rows: parsedFiles.train.slice(0, 1),
 642 |   });
 643 |   const versionsProbe = await request("GET", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/versions`);
 644 | 
 645 |   const checks = [
 646 |     { name: "Existing row-level PATCH update works", pass: patch.ok && restore.ok, evidence: { patchStatus: patch.status, restoreStatus: restore.status } },
 647 |     { name: "Dataset-level replace endpoint exists", pass: replaceProbe.ok, evidence: { status: replaceProbe.status } },
 648 |     { name: "Dataset-level append endpoint exists", pass: appendProbe.ok, evidence: { status: appendProbe.status } },
 649 |     { name: "Dataset version history endpoint exists", pass: versionsProbe.ok, evidence: { status: versionsProbe.status } },
 650 |     { name: "Schema/KPI/dashboard refresh metadata returned for update", pass: false, evidence: { expected: ["rowsAdded", "rowsRemoved", "schemaChanged", "dashboardRegenerated"] } },
 651 |   ];
 652 | 
 653 |   addPhase("phase4", "Dataset Update Test", "FAIL", checks, {
 654 |     outputShape: {
 655 |       rowsAdded: null,
 656 |       rowsRemoved: null,
 657 |       schemaChanged: null,
 658 |       dashboardRegenerated: null,
 659 |     },
 660 |   }, [
 661 |     "Add dataset-level append/replace APIs.",
 662 |     "Persist dataset versions and expose version history.",
 663 |     "Return rowsAdded, rowsRemoved, schemaChanged, and dashboardRegenerated after updates.",
 664 |   ]);
 665 | }
 666 | 
 667 | async function phase5AgentOrchestration() {
 668 |   const agentic = await request("POST", `/api/agentic-models/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/analyze`, {
 669 |     goal: "Audit salary analytics dashboard for schema-aware deterministic insights.",
 670 |   });
 671 | 
 672 |   const audit = agentic.json?.data?.audit || [];
 673 |   const steps = audit.map((item) => item.step || item.agent);
 674 |   const models = new Set(audit.map((item) => item.model).filter(Boolean));
 675 |   const expectedMissing = EXPECTED_AGENT_STEPS.filter((step) => !steps.includes(step));
 676 | 
 677 |   const checks = [
 678 |     { name: "Agentic analysis endpoint executes", pass: agentic.ok, evidence: { status: agentic.status } },
 679 |     { name: "Audit trail exists", pass: audit.length > 0, evidence: audit },
 680 |     { name: "Schema Agent executes first", pass: steps[0] === "schema_analysis", evidence: { steps } },
 681 |     { name: "Master Agent executes after schema", pass: steps.includes("master_planning") && steps.indexOf("master_planning") > steps.indexOf("schema_analysis"), evidence: { steps } },
 682 |     { name: "Dashboard Guardian executes", pass: steps.includes("dashboard_guardian"), evidence: { steps } },
 683 |     { name: "Full expected agent chain is present", pass: expectedMissing.length === 0, evidence: { expected: EXPECTED_AGENT_STEPS, actual: steps, missing: expectedMissing } },
 684 |     { name: "More than one configured model appears in audit", pass: models.size > 1, evidence: { models: [...models] } },
 685 |   ];
 686 | 
 687 |   addPhase("phase5", "Agent Orchestration Test", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 688 |     audit,
 689 |   }, expectedMissing.length ? ["Extend orchestration audit to include analytics_engine and final_explanation steps."] : []);
 690 | }
 691 | 
 692 | async function phase6DeterministicAnalytics() {
 693 |   const kpis = extractKpis(datasets.trainImportResponse);
 694 |   const kpiChecks = validateKnownKpis(kpis, deterministic);
 695 |   const allKnownPass = kpiChecks.length > 0 && kpiChecks.every((check) => check.pass);
 696 | 
 697 |   const checks = [
 698 |     { name: "Harness computed independent row count", pass: deterministic.rowCount === 40000, evidence: { rowCount: deterministic.rowCount } },
 699 |     { name: "Harness computed sum/avg/min/max", pass: deterministic.salary_usd.count === 40000 && deterministic.salary_usd.max !== null, evidence: { salary_usd: deterministic.salary_usd, experience: deterministic.experience } },
 700 |     { name: "Harness computed correlation", pass: deterministic.correlation_experience_salary_usd !== null, evidence: { correlation: deterministic.correlation_experience_salary_usd } },
 701 |     { name: "Harness computed missing values and outliers", pass: hasField(deterministic.missingValues, "salary_usd") && deterministic.salary_usd.outliersIqr >= 0, evidence: { missingValues: deterministic.missingValues, outliers: { salary_usd: deterministic.salary_usd.outliersIqr, experience: deterministic.experience.outliersIqr } } },
 702 |     { name: "Returned KPI values match deterministic calculations when present", pass: allKnownPass, evidence: kpiChecks },
 703 |     { name: "No accepted KPI value is only LLM text", pass: allKnownPass, evidence: { checkedKpiCount: kpiChecks.length } },
 704 |   ];
 705 | 
 706 |   addPhase("phase6", "Deterministic Analytics Test", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 707 |     deterministic,
 708 |   });
 709 | }
 710 | 
 711 | async function phase7DashboardGuardian() {
 712 |   const invalidDashboard = {
 713 |     kpis: [
 714 |       { title: "Fake KPI", metric: "not_a_real_salary_column", aggregation: "avg", value: "999999" },
 715 |     ],
 716 |     charts: [
 717 |       { title: "Fake Chart", type: "bar", xKey: "fake_country", yKey: "fake_salary", aggregation: "sum", data: [{ fake_country: "Nowhere", fake_salary: 1 }] },
 718 |     ],
 719 |   };
 720 | 
 721 |   const guardian = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${datasets.train.id}/dashboard-validate-fix`, {
 722 |     currentDashboard: invalidDashboard,
 723 |   });
 724 |   const data = guardian.json?.data || {};
 725 |   const serialized = JSON.stringify(data).toLowerCase();
 726 |   const fakeRemoved = !serialized.includes("fake_country") && !serialized.includes("fake_salary") && !serialized.includes("not_a_real_salary_column");
 727 | 
 728 |   const checks = [
 729 |     { name: "Dashboard guardian endpoint executes", pass: guardian.ok, evidence: { status: guardian.status } },
 730 |     { name: "Guardian reports issues, warnings, or corrections", pass: Boolean(data.issues?.length || data.warnings?.length || data.corrections?.length || data.correctedDashboard || data.observations?.length), evidence: summarizeJson(data) },
 731 |     { name: "Guardian removes or corrects non-existent chart/KPI columns", pass: fakeRemoved, evidence: summarizeJson(data) },
 732 |   ];
 733 | 
 734 |   addPhase("phase7", "Dashboard Guardian Test", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 735 |     expectedShape: { valid: guardian.ok && fakeRemoved, warnings: data.warnings || data.observations || [], corrections: data.corrections || data.issues || [] },
 736 |   });
 737 | }
 738 | 
 739 | async function phase8RagReadiness() {
 740 |   const acceptedDashboardPlan = schemaDashboard?.dashboard || schemaDashboard?.dashboardPlan || {};
 741 |   const trainRag = await request("POST", "/api/ai/schema-rag/train", {
 742 |     dataset: {
 743 |       ...datasetPayload({
 744 |         name: "salary_train_stress_audit",
 745 |         fileName: "train.csv",
 746 |         rows: parsedFiles.train,
 747 |         dictRows: parsedFiles.dictionary,
 748 |       }),
 749 |       id: datasets.train.id,
 750 |     },
 751 |     acceptedDashboardPlan,
 752 |     rating: "good",
 753 |     notes: "Stress-audit generated salary dashboard memory.",
 754 |     source: "agentic-salary-stress-audit",
 755 |     useOllama: false,
 756 |   });
 757 |   const memory = await request("GET", "/api/ai/schema-rag-memory");
 758 | 
 759 |   const stats = memory.json?.data?.stats || {};
 760 |   const memoryItems = memory.json?.data?.memory || [];
 761 |   const trainedEntry = trainRag.json?.data?.entry;
 762 |   const embeddingMeta = trainRag.json?.data?.embedding || {};
 763 | 
 764 |   const expectedFiles = [
 765 |     "schema-memory.json",
 766 |     "rag-memory.json",
 767 |     "dataset-fingerprints.json",
 768 |     "analytics-playbooks.json",
 769 |   ];
 770 |   const actualMemoryPath = stats.memoryPath || "apps/backend/data/schema-rag-memory.json";
 771 | 
 772 |   const checks = [
 773 |     { name: "RAG training endpoint executes", pass: trainRag.ok, evidence: { status: trainRag.status, embedding: embeddingMeta } },
 774 |     { name: "RAG memory endpoint executes", pass: memory.ok, evidence: { status: memory.status, stats } },
 775 |     { name: "Memory chunks/summaries are generated", pass: Boolean(trainedEntry?.schemaText && trainedEntry?.schemaProfile?.columns?.length), evidence: summarizeJson(trainedEntry) },
 776 |     { name: "Embeddings can be generated", pass: Boolean(embeddingMeta.provider && embeddingMeta.model), evidence: embeddingMeta },
 777 |     { name: "Reusable memory records exist", pass: Number(stats.count || memoryItems.length || 0) > 0, evidence: { count: stats.count, actualMemoryPath } },
 778 |     { name: "Required named memory export files exist", pass: expectedFiles.every((file) => fs.existsSync(path.join(ROOT, "reports", file)) || fs.existsSync(path.join(ROOT, "data", file)) || fs.existsSync(path.join(ROOT, "apps", "backend", "data", file))), evidence: { expectedFiles, actualMemoryPath } },
 779 |   ];
 780 | 
 781 |   addPhase("phase8", "RAG Training Readiness", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 782 |     expectedOutputs: expectedFiles,
 783 |     actualMemoryPath,
 784 |   }, [
 785 |     "Add explicit export jobs for schema-memory.json, rag-memory.json, dataset-fingerprints.json, and analytics-playbooks.json.",
 786 |   ]);
 787 | }
 788 | 
 789 | function makeTrainingExamples(count = 50) {
 790 |   const columns = inferColumns(parsedFiles.train, dictionaryMap(parsedFiles.dictionary));
 791 |   const schema = {
 792 |     datasetName: "salary_train_stress_audit",
 793 |     rowCount: parsedFiles.train.length,
 794 |     columnCount: columns.length,
 795 |     columns: columns.map(({ name, type, role, description }) => ({ name, type, role, description })),
 796 |   };
 797 |   const intents = [
 798 |     "Create a salary analytics dashboard.",
 799 |     "Compare salary by country.",
 800 |     "Find salary distribution by experience.",
 801 |     "Build a dashboard for developer compensation.",
 802 |     "Validate a workforce salary dashboard.",
 803 |   ];
 804 | 
 805 |   return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, index) => ({
 806 |     instruction: intents[index % intents.length],
 807 |     schema,
 808 |     dashboardPlan: schemaDashboard?.dashboard || schemaDashboard?.dashboardPlan || {},
 809 |     expectedOutput: {
 810 |       schemaOnly: true,
 811 |       domain: "workforce_salary",
 812 |       requiredColumns: ["experience", "country", "education", "languages", "frameworks", "company_size", "salary_usd"],
 813 |     },
 814 |   }));
 815 | }
 816 | 
 817 | async function phase9LlmTrainingReadiness() {
 818 |   const examples = makeTrainingExamples(50);
 819 |   const trainMemory = await request("POST", "/api/ai/schema-training/train-memory", {
 820 |     datasets: examples.map((example, index) => ({
 821 |       name: `salary-training-example-${index + 1}`,
 822 |       rows: parsedFiles.train.slice(index, index + 200),
 823 |       columns: inferColumns(parsedFiles.train.slice(index, index + 200), dictionaryMap(parsedFiles.dictionary)),
 824 |       dashboardPlan: example.dashboardPlan,
 825 |       rating: "good",
 826 |     })),
 827 |   });
 828 |   const memory = await request("GET", "/api/ai/schema-training-memory");
 829 |   const stats = memory.json?.data?.stats || {};
 830 |   const memoryItems = memory.json?.data?.memory || [];
 831 |   const has50ApiRecords = Number(stats.count || memoryItems.length || 0) >= 50;
 832 | 
 833 |   const checks = [
 834 |     { name: "Harness can generate at least 50 schema-aware fine-tuning examples", pass: examples.length >= 50, evidence: { count: examples.length, sample: examples[0] } },
 835 |     { name: "Training examples use instruction/schema/dashboardPlan/expectedOutput shape", pass: examples.every((item) => item.instruction && item.schema?.columns?.length && item.dashboardPlan && item.expectedOutput), evidence: { sampleKeys: Object.keys(examples[0]) } },
 836 |     { name: "Schema training endpoint executes", pass: trainMemory.ok, evidence: { status: trainMemory.status } },
 837 |     { name: "Schema training memory endpoint executes", pass: memory.ok, evidence: { status: memory.status, stats } },
 838 |     { name: "Persisted training memory has at least 50 examples", pass: has50ApiRecords, evidence: { count: stats.count || memoryItems.length } },
 839 |     { name: "Training data is not generic chatbot text", pass: examples.every((item) => item.schema.columns.some((column) => column.name === "salary_usd")), evidence: { checkedExamples: examples.length } },
 840 |   ];
 841 | 
 842 |   addPhase("phase9", "LLM Training Readiness", statusFromChecks(checks), checks, {
 843 |     generatedExamples: examples.length,
 844 |     persistedMemoryCount: stats.count || memoryItems.length || 0,
 845 |   }, has50ApiRecords ? [] : ["Add/export a JSONL fine-tuning dataset with at least 50 distinct schema-aware records."]);
 846 | }
 847 | 
 848 | function verdictValue(names) {
 849 |   return names.every((name) => phases.find((phase) => phase.title === name)?.status === "PASS") ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
 850 | }
 851 | 
 852 | function calculateScore(verdicts) {
 853 |   const weights = {
 854 |     "Multi-Dataset Support": 15,
 855 |     "Dataset Update Support": 12,
 856 |     "Agentic Architecture": 15,
 857 |     "Dashboard Guardian": 12,
 858 |     "RAG Readiness": 12,
 859 |     "LLM Training Readiness": 10,
 860 |     "Deterministic Analytics": 14,
 861 |     "Production Readiness": 10,
 862 |   };
 863 | 
 864 |   return Object.entries(weights).reduce((score, [name, weight]) => score + (verdicts[name] === "PASS" ? weight : 0), 0);
 865 | }
 866 | 
 867 | function buildVerdicts() {
 868 |   const verdicts = {
 869 |     "Multi-Dataset Support": verdictValue(["Dataset Registration", "Multi-Dataset Reasoning"]),
 870 |     "Dataset Update Support": verdictValue(["Dataset Update Test"]),
 871 |     "Agentic Architecture": verdictValue(["Schema Understanding", "Agent Orchestration Test"]),
 872 |     "Dashboard Guardian": verdictValue(["Dashboard Guardian Test"]),
 873 |     "RAG Readiness": verdictValue(["RAG Training Readiness"]),
 874 |     "LLM Training Readiness": verdictValue(["LLM Training Readiness"]),
 875 |     "Deterministic Analytics": verdictValue(["Deterministic Analytics Test"]),
 876 |   };
 877 | 
 878 |   verdicts["Production Readiness"] =
 879 |     verdicts["Multi-Dataset Support"] === "PASS" &&
 880 |     verdicts["Dataset Update Support"] === "PASS" &&
 881 |     verdicts["Agentic Architecture"] === "PASS" &&
 882 |     verdicts["Dashboard Guardian"] === "PASS" &&
 883 |     verdicts["Deterministic Analytics"] === "PASS"
 884 |       ? "PASS"
 885 |       : "FAIL";
 886 | 
 887 |   verdicts["Architecture Score"] = calculateScore(verdicts);
 888 |   return verdicts;
 889 | }
 890 | 
 891 | function buildLetteredFinalVerdict(verdicts, improvements) {
 892 |   return {
 893 |     "A. Multi-Dataset Support": verdicts["Multi-Dataset Support"],
 894 |     "B. Dataset Update Support": verdicts["Dataset Update Support"],
 895 |     "C. Agentic Architecture": verdicts["Agentic Architecture"],
 896 |     "D. Dashboard Guardian": verdicts["Dashboard Guardian"],
 897 |     "E. RAG Readiness": verdicts["RAG Readiness"],
 898 |     "F. LLM Training Readiness": verdicts["LLM Training Readiness"],
 899 |     "G. Deterministic Analytics": verdicts["Deterministic Analytics"],
 900 |     "H. Production Readiness": verdicts["Production Readiness"],
 901 |     "I. Architecture Score": `${verdicts["Architecture Score"]}/100`,
 902 |     "J. What must be built next": improvements,
 903 |   };
 904 | }
 905 | 
 906 | function nextBuildItems() {
 907 |   const items = new Set();
 908 |   for (const phase of phases) {
 909 |     for (const item of phase.improvements || []) items.add(item);
 910 |   }
 911 |   if (phases.find((phase) => phase.title === "Dataset Update Test")?.status === "FAIL") {
 912 |     items.add("apps/backend/src/routes/datasets.js: add append, replace, and version-history routes.");
 913 |     items.add("apps/backend/src/database/dataset-repository.js: persist dataset versions, schema fingerprints, and update metadata.");
 914 |   }
 915 |   if (phases.find((phase) => phase.title === "Multi-Dataset Reasoning")?.status === "FAIL") {
 916 |     items.add("apps/backend/src/services/data-merger.js: expose evidence-based merge plan instead of only normalizing rows.");
 917 |   }
 918 |   if (phases.find((phase) => phase.title === "RAG Training Readiness")?.status === "FAIL") {
 919 |     items.add("apps/backend/src/services/ai-analyst: add explicit memory/fingerprint/playbook export service.");
 920 |   }
 921 |   if (phases.find((phase) => phase.title === "LLM Training Readiness")?.status === "FAIL") {
 922 |     items.add("apps/backend/scripts/export-schema-training-jsonl.js: ensure at least 50 schema-aware fine-tuning records are generated/exported.");
 923 |   }
 924 |   return [...items];
 925 | }
 926 | 
 927 | function writeJsonReport(report) {
 928 |   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(REPORT_JSON), { recursive: true });
 929 |   fs.writeFileSync(REPORT_JSON, JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
 930 |   generatedFiles.push(REPORT_JSON);
 931 | }
 932 | 
 933 | function statusIcon(status) {
 934 |   return status === "PASS" ? "PASS" : "FAIL";
 935 | }
 936 | 
 937 | function markdownReport(report) {
 938 |   const verdictRows = Object.entries(report.finalVerdict)
 939 |     .map(([key, value]) => `| ${key} | ${value} |`)
 940 |     .join("\n");
 941 | 
 942 |   const letteredRows = Object.entries(report.finalVerdictLettered)
 943 |     .filter(([, value]) => !Array.isArray(value))
 944 |     .map(([key, value]) => `| ${key} | ${value} |`)
 945 |     .join("\n");
 946 | 
 947 |   const phaseSections = report.phases
 948 |     .map((phase) => {
 949 |       const checks = phase.checks
 950 |         .map((check) => `- ${check.pass ? "PASS" : "FAIL"}: ${check.name}`)
 951 |         .join("\n");
 952 |       const improvements = phase.improvements?.length
 953 |         ? `\n\nImprovements:\n${phase.improvements.map((item) => `- ${item}`).join("\n")}`
 954 |         : "";
 955 |       return `## ${phase.title}: ${statusIcon(phase.status)}\n\n${checks}${improvements}`;
 956 |     })
 957 |     .join("\n\n");
 958 | 
 959 |   return `# Agentic AI Analytics Salary Stress Audit
 960 | 
 961 | Generated: ${report.generatedAt}
 962 | 
 963 | Backend: ${report.backendUrl}
 964 | 
 965 | ## Dataset Inputs
 966 | 
 967 | | File | Path | Rows | Columns |
 968 | | --- | --- | ---: | ---: |
 969 | | train.csv | ${CSV_PATHS.train} | ${report.datasetInputs.train.rowCount} | ${report.datasetInputs.train.columnCount} |
 970 | | test.csv | ${CSV_PATHS.test} | ${report.datasetInputs.test.rowCount} | ${report.datasetInputs.test.columnCount} |
 971 | | data_dictionary.csv | ${CSV_PATHS.dictionary} | ${report.datasetInputs.dictionary.rowCount} | ${report.datasetInputs.dictionary.columnCount} |
 972 | 
 973 | ## Final Verdict
 974 | 
 975 | | Category | Result |
 976 | | --- | --- |
 977 | ${verdictRows}
 978 | 
 979 | ## Prompt Verdict A-J
 980 | 
 981 | | Item | Result |
 982 | | --- | --- |
 983 | ${letteredRows}
 984 | 
 985 | J. What must be built next:
 986 | 
 987 | ${report.whatMustBeBuiltNext.map((item) => `- ${item}`).join("\n")}
 988 | 
 989 | ## Deterministic Analytics Baseline
 990 | 
 991 | - Train rows: ${report.deterministic.rowCount}
 992 | - Train columns: ${report.deterministic.columnCount}
 993 | - Salary average: ${report.deterministic.salary_usd.avg}
 994 | - Salary min/max: ${report.deterministic.salary_usd.min} / ${report.deterministic.salary_usd.max}
 995 | - Experience/salary correlation: ${report.deterministic.correlation_experience_salary_usd}
 996 | - Duplicate rows: ${report.deterministic.duplicateRows}
 997 | 
 998 | ${phaseSections}
 999 | 
1000 | ## What Must Be Built Next
1001 | 
1002 | ${report.whatMustBeBuiltNext.map((item) => `- ${item}`).join("\n")}
1003 | 
1004 | ## Endpoint Evidence
1005 | 
1006 | Full request evidence is available in \`reports/agentic-salary-stress-audit.json\`.
1007 | `;
1008 | }
1009 | 
1010 | function writeMarkdownReport(report) {
1011 |   fs.writeFileSync(REPORT_MD, markdownReport(report));
1012 |   generatedFiles.push(REPORT_MD);
1013 | }
1014 | 
1015 | async function run() {
1016 |   console.log("Agentic AI Analytics Salary Stress Audit");
1017 |   console.log(`Backend: ${BASE_URL}`);
1018 | 
1019 |   await loadFiles();
1020 |   await ensureBackendReachable();
1021 | 
1022 |   console.log("Running phase 1/9: dataset registration");
1023 |   await phase1DatasetRegistration();
1024 |   console.log("Running phase 2/9: schema understanding");
1025 |   await phase2SchemaUnderstanding();
1026 |   console.log("Running phase 3/9: multi-dataset reasoning");
1027 |   await phase3MultiDatasetReasoning();
1028 |   console.log("Running phase 4/9: dataset update");
1029 |   await phase4DatasetUpdate();
1030 |   console.log("Running phase 5/9: agent orchestration");
1031 |   await phase5AgentOrchestration();
1032 |   console.log("Running phase 6/9: deterministic analytics");
1033 |   await phase6DeterministicAnalytics();
1034 |   console.log("Running phase 7/9: dashboard guardian");
1035 |   await phase7DashboardGuardian();
1036 |   console.log("Running phase 8/9: RAG readiness");
1037 |   await phase8RagReadiness();
1038 |   console.log("Running phase 9/9: LLM training readiness");
1039 |   await phase9LlmTrainingReadiness();
1040 | 
1041 |   const finalVerdict = buildVerdicts();
1042 |   const whatMustBeBuiltNext = nextBuildItems();
1043 |   const report = {
1044 |     generatedAt: nowIso(),
1045 |     backendUrl: BASE_URL,
1046 |     csvPaths: CSV_PATHS,
1047 |     datasetInputs: {
1048 |       train: { rowCount: parsedFiles.train.length, columnCount: Object.keys(parsedFiles.train[0] || {}).length },
1049 |       test: { rowCount: parsedFiles.test.length, columnCount: Object.keys(parsedFiles.test[0] || {}).length },
1050 |       dictionary: { rowCount: parsedFiles.dictionary.length, columnCount: Object.keys(parsedFiles.dictionary[0] || {}).length },
1051 |     },
1052 |     deterministic,
1053 |     phases,
1054 |     finalVerdict,
1055 |     finalVerdictLettered: buildLetteredFinalVerdict(finalVerdict, whatMustBeBuiltNext),
1056 |     whatMustBeBuiltNext,
1057 |     endpointEvidence,
1058 |   };
1059 | 
1060 |   writeJsonReport(report);
1061 |   writeMarkdownReport(report);
1062 | 
1063 |   console.log("");
1064 |   console.log("Final Verdict");
1065 |   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(finalVerdict)) {
1066 |     console.log(`${key}: ${value}`);
1067 |   }
1068 |   console.log("");
1069 |   console.log(`Wrote ${path.relative(ROOT, REPORT_JSON)}`);
1070 |   console.log(`Wrote ${path.relative(ROOT, REPORT_MD)}`);
1071 | }
1072 | 
1073 | run().catch((error) => {
1074 |   const failedReport = {
1075 |     generatedAt: nowIso(),
1076 |     backendUrl: BASE_URL,
1077 |     csvPaths: CSV_PATHS,
1078 |     error: error.message,
1079 |     endpointEvidence,
1080 |     finalVerdict: {
1081 |       "Multi-Dataset Support": "FAIL",
1082 |       "Dataset Update Support": "FAIL",
1083 |       "Agentic Architecture": "FAIL",
1084 |       "Dashboard Guardian": "FAIL",
1085 |       "RAG Readiness": "FAIL",
1086 |       "LLM Training Readiness": "FAIL",
1087 |       "Deterministic Analytics": "FAIL",
1088 |       "Production Readiness": "FAIL",
1089 |       "Architecture Score": 0,
1090 |     },
1091 |     finalVerdictLettered: {
1092 |       "A. Multi-Dataset Support": "FAIL",
1093 |       "B. Dataset Update Support": "FAIL",
1094 |       "C. Agentic Architecture": "FAIL",
1095 |       "D. Dashboard Guardian": "FAIL",
1096 |       "E. RAG Readiness": "FAIL",
1097 |       "F. LLM Training Readiness": "FAIL",
1098 |       "G. Deterministic Analytics": "FAIL",
1099 |       "H. Production Readiness": "FAIL",
1100 |       "I. Architecture Score": "0/100",
1101 |       "J. What must be built next": ["Start the backend and rerun the audit."],
1102 |     },
1103 |   };
1104 | 
1105 |   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(REPORT_JSON), { recursive: true });
1106 |   fs.writeFileSync(REPORT_JSON, JSON.stringify(failedReport, null, 2));
1107 |   fs.writeFileSync(
1108 |     REPORT_MD,
1109 |     `# Agentic AI Analytics Salary Stress Audit\n\nGenerated: ${failedReport.generatedAt}\n\nFAIL: ${error.message}\n`
1110 |   );
1111 |   console.error(`FAIL: ${error.message}`);
1112 |   process.exitCode = 1;
1113 | });
1114 | 
```

### FILE: scripts/apply-schema-agent-route.mjs

```js
 1 | import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
 2 | 
 3 | const file = 'apps/backend/src/routes/index.js';
 4 | let code = await fs.readFile(file, 'utf8');
 5 | 
 6 | if (!code.includes("handleSchemaAgentRoutes")) {
 7 |   code = code.replace(
 8 |     /(import .+;\n)/,
 9 |     `$1import { handleSchemaAgentRoutes } from './schema-agent.js';\n`
10 |   );
11 | 
12 |   code = code.replace(
13 |     /(async function setupRoutes[^{]+{)/,
14 |     `$1\n  if (await handleSchemaAgentRoutes(request, response, pathname)) return;\n`
15 |   );
16 | 
17 |   await fs.writeFile(file, code, 'utf8');
18 |   console.log('Schema agent route inserted.');
19 | } else {
20 |   console.log('Schema agent route already exists.');
21 | }
22 | 
```

### FILE: scripts/export-ai-safe.js

Source path: `scripts/export-ai-safe.js`

```js
  1 | import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
  2 | import path from 'node:path';
  3 | 
  4 | const PROJECT_ROOT = process.cwd();
  5 | const OUTPUT_AI_SAFE = path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, 'PROJECT_AI_SAFE.md');
  6 | const OUTPUT_ARCHITECTURE = path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, 'PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.md');
  7 | 
  8 | const INCLUDED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([
  9 |   '.js',
 10 |   '.jsx',
 11 |   '.ts',
 12 |   '.tsx',
 13 |   '.json',
 14 |   '.css',
 15 |   '.scss',
 16 |   '.html',
 17 |   '.md',
 18 | ]);
 19 | 
 20 | const IGNORED_DIRECTORIES = new Set([
 21 |   '.git',
 22 |   '.next',
 23 |   '.firebase',
 24 |   'build',
 25 |   'coverage',
 26 |   'dist',
 27 |   'dist-ssr',
 28 |   'node_modules',
 29 | ]);
 30 | 
 31 | const IGNORED_FILENAMES = new Set([
 32 |   'PROJECT_AI_SAFE.md',
 33 |   'PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.md',
 34 |   'firebase-adminsdk.json',
 35 |   'package-lock.json',
 36 |   'serviceAccountKey.json',
 37 | ]);
 38 | 
 39 | const IGNORED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([
 40 |   '.gif',
 41 |   '.jpeg',
 42 |   '.jpg',
 43 |   '.lock',
 44 |   '.log',
 45 |   '.mp4',
 46 |   '.png',
 47 |   '.zip',
 48 | ]);
 49 | 
 50 | const MAX_FILE_BYTES = 1_000_000;
 51 | 
 52 | const toPosixPath = (filePath) => filePath.split(path.sep).join('/');
 53 | 
 54 | const isIgnoredFile = (relativePath) => {
 55 |   const fileName = path.basename(relativePath);
 56 |   const extension = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
 57 | 
 58 |   if (fileName === '.env' || fileName.startsWith('.env.')) {
 59 |     return true;
 60 |   }
 61 | 
 62 |   if (fileName.startsWith('firebase-adminsdk') && extension === '.json') {
 63 |     return true;
 64 |   }
 65 | 
 66 |   return IGNORED_FILENAMES.has(fileName) || IGNORED_EXTENSIONS.has(extension);
 67 | };
 68 | 
 69 | const isIncludedFile = (relativePath) => {
 70 |   const extension = path.extname(relativePath).toLowerCase();
 71 |   return INCLUDED_EXTENSIONS.has(extension) && !isIgnoredFile(relativePath);
 72 | };
 73 | 
 74 | const redactSecrets = (content) =>
 75 |   content
 76 |     .replace(/AIza[0-9A-Za-z-_]{35}/g, '[REDACTED_FIREBASE_API_KEY]')
 77 |     .replace(/sk_(?:live|test)_[0-9A-Za-z]+/g, '[REDACTED_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY]')
 78 |     .replace(/pk_(?:live|test)_[0-9A-Za-z]+/g, '[REDACTED_STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY]')
 79 |     .replace(/gh[pousr]_[0-9A-Za-z_]{36,255}/g, '[REDACTED_GITHUB_TOKEN]')
 80 |     .replace(/-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----[\s\S]*?-----END [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----/g, '[REDACTED_PRIVATE_KEY]')
 81 |     .replace(
 82 |       /\b((?:postgres|postgresql|mysql|mongodb(?:\+srv)?):\/\/[^:\s/@]+:)[^@\s]+(@[^\s"'`<>)]+)/gi,
 83 |       '$1[REDACTED_PASSWORD]$2',
 84 |     )
 85 |     .replace(
 86 |       /^(\s*["']?[A-Z0-9_]*(?:SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD|PRIVATE_KEY|API_KEY|ACCESS_KEY|REFRESH_TOKEN)[A-Z0-9_]*["']?\s*[:=]\s*)["']?[^"',\n}]+["']?/gim,
 87 |       '$1[REDACTED_SECRET]',
 88 |     );
 89 | 
 90 | const collectFiles = async (directory = PROJECT_ROOT, files = [], skipped = []) => {
 91 |   const entries = await fs.readdir(directory, { withFileTypes: true });
 92 | 
 93 |   for (const entry of entries) {
 94 |     const absolutePath = path.join(directory, entry.name);
 95 |     const relativePath = path.relative(PROJECT_ROOT, absolutePath);
 96 |     const safeRelativePath = toPosixPath(relativePath);
 97 | 
 98 |     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
 99 |       if (!IGNORED_DIRECTORIES.has(entry.name)) {
100 |         await collectFiles(absolutePath, files, skipped);
101 |       }
102 |       continue;
103 |     }
104 | 
105 |     if (!entry.isFile()) {
106 |       continue;
107 |     }
108 | 
109 |     if (!isIncludedFile(relativePath)) {
110 |       continue;
111 |     }
112 | 
113 |     const stats = await fs.stat(absolutePath);
114 | 
115 |     if (stats.size > MAX_FILE_BYTES) {
116 |       skipped.push({
117 |         file: safeRelativePath,
118 |         reason: `larger than ${MAX_FILE_BYTES} bytes`,
119 |       });
120 |       continue;
121 |     }
122 | 
123 |     files.push({
124 |       absolutePath,
125 |       file: safeRelativePath,
126 |       size: stats.size,
127 |     });
128 |   }
129 | 
130 |   return { files, skipped };
131 | };
132 | 
133 | const buildArchitecture = (files, skipped) => {
134 |   let architecture = '# Project Architecture\n\n';
135 |   architecture += `Generated: ${new Date().toISOString()}\n\n`;
136 |   architecture += '## Included Files\n\n';
137 | 
138 |   for (const { file, size } of files) {
139 |     architecture += `- ${file} (${size} bytes)\n`;
140 |   }
141 | 
142 |   if (skipped.length > 0) {
143 |     architecture += '\n## Skipped Files\n\n';
144 | 
145 |     for (const { file, reason } of skipped) {
146 |       architecture += `- ${file}: ${reason}\n`;
147 |     }
148 |   }
149 | 
150 |   architecture += '\n## Safety Rules\n\n';
151 |   architecture += '- Secret-bearing files such as `.env*`, Firebase admin SDK JSON, and service account keys are excluded.\n';
152 |   architecture += '- Build output, dependency folders, logs, lockfiles, and common media archives are excluded.\n';
153 |   architecture += '- Known API keys, tokens, private keys, and database URL passwords are redacted before export.\n';
154 | 
155 |   return architecture;
156 | };
157 | 
158 | const exportCodebase = async () => {
159 |   const { files, skipped } = await collectFiles();
160 |   const sortedFiles = files.sort((a, b) => a.file.localeCompare(b.file));
161 | 
162 |   let output = '# AI Safe Codebase Export\n\n';
163 |   output += `Generated: ${new Date().toISOString()}\n\n`;
164 |   output += 'This export excludes common secret files and applies best-effort redaction. Review before uploading externally.\n\n';
165 | 
166 |   for (const { absolutePath, file } of sortedFiles) {
167 |     const rawContent = await fs.readFile(absolutePath, 'utf8');
168 |     const content = redactSecrets(rawContent);
169 |     const extension = path.extname(file).replace('.', '');
170 | 
171 |     output += '---\n\n';
172 |     output += `# FILE: ${file}\n\n`;
173 |     output += `\`\`\`${extension}\n`;
174 |     output += content;
175 |     output += '\n```\n\n';
176 |   }
177 | 
178 |   await fs.writeFile(OUTPUT_AI_SAFE, output, 'utf8');
179 |   await fs.writeFile(OUTPUT_ARCHITECTURE, buildArchitecture(sortedFiles, skipped), 'utf8');
180 | 
181 |   console.log('PROJECT_AI_SAFE.md generated');
182 |   console.log('PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.md generated');
183 | 
184 |   if (skipped.length > 0) {
185 |     console.log(`${skipped.length} oversized file(s) skipped; see PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.md.`);
186 |   }
187 | };
188 | 
189 | exportCodebase().catch((error) => {
190 |   console.error(error);
191 |   process.exitCode = 1;
192 | });
193 | 
```

 62 | const DEFAULT_IGNORE_EXTENSIONS = new Set([

 87 | const LANGUAGE_BY_EXTENSION = new Map([

 90 |   [".css", "css"],
 91 |   [".html", "html"],
 92 |   [".js", "javascript"],
