 94 |   [".jsx", "jsx"],
 95 |   [".mjs", "javascript"],
 96 |   [".md", "markdown"],
 97 |   [".ps1", "powershell"],
 98 |   [".py", "python"],
 99 |   [".scss", "scss"],
100 |   [".sh", "bash"],
101 |   [".sql", "sql"],
102 |   [".ts", "typescript"],
103 |   [".tsx", "tsx"],
104 |   [".txt", "text"],
105 |   [".yaml", "yaml"],
106 |   [".yml", "yaml"],
107 | ]);
108 | 
109 | const DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_BYTES = 1_200_000;
110 | 
111 | function parseArgs(argv) {
112 |   const options = {
113 |     root: process.cwd(),
114 |     outDir: path.join(process.cwd(), "exports"),
115 |     maxFileBytes: DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_BYTES,
116 |     includeExtensions: DEFAULT_INCLUDE_EXTENSIONS,
117 |     redact: true,
118 |     includeLockfiles: false,
119 |   };
120 | 
121 |   for (let index = 0; index < argv.length; index += 1) {
122 |     const arg = argv[index];
123 |     const next = argv[index + 1];
124 | 
125 |     if (arg === "--root" && next) {
126 |       options.root = path.resolve(next);
127 |       index += 1;
128 |     } else if (arg === "--out-dir" && next) {
129 |       options.outDir = path.resolve(next);
130 |       index += 1;
131 |     } else if (arg === "--max-file-bytes" && next) {
132 |       options.maxFileBytes = Number(next);
133 |       index += 1;
134 |     } else if (arg === "--include-ext" && next) {
135 |       options.includeExtensions = new Set(
136 |         next
137 |           .split(",")
138 |           .map((ext) => ext.trim())
139 |           .filter(Boolean)
140 |           .map((ext) => (ext.startsWith(".") ? ext : `.${ext}`)),
141 |       );
142 |       index += 1;
143 |     } else if (arg === "--include-lockfiles") {
144 |       options.includeLockfiles = true;
145 |     } else if (arg === "--no-redact") {
146 |       options.redact = false;
147 |     } else if (arg === "--help" || arg === "-h") {
148 |       printHelpAndExit();
149 |     }
150 |   }
151 | 
152 |   return options;
153 | }
154 | 
155 | function printHelpAndExit() {
156 |   console.log(`
157 | Usage:
158 |   node scripts/export-codebase-deep.js [options]
159 | 
160 | Options:
161 |   --root <path>             Project root to scan. Default: current working directory.
162 |   --out-dir <path>          Output directory. Default: ./exports
163 |   --max-file-bytes <n>      Skip individual files larger than n bytes. Default: ${DEFAULT_MAX_FILE_BYTES}
164 |   --include-ext <list>      Comma-separated extensions, for example: ts,tsx,js,json,md
165 |   --include-lockfiles       Include package lockfiles.
166 |   --no-redact               Disable best-effort secret redaction.
167 |   --help                    Show this help.
168 | `);
169 |   process.exit(0);
170 | }
171 | 
172 | const toPosix = (value) => value.split(path.sep).join("/");
173 | 
174 | function isSecretFile(fileName) {
175 |   return (
176 |     fileName === ".env" ||
177 |     fileName.startsWith(".env.") ||
178 |     /^firebase-adminsdk.*\.json$/i.test(fileName) ||
179 |     /^.*service.*account.*\.json$/i.test(fileName)
180 |   );
181 | }
182 | 
183 | function shouldIgnorePath(relativePath, options) {
184 |   const normalized = toPosix(relativePath);
185 |   const fileName = path.basename(relativePath);
186 |   const extension = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
187 | 
188 |   if (normalized.startsWith("exports/")) return true;
189 |   if (isSecretFile(fileName)) return true;
190 |   if (DEFAULT_IGNORE_FILES.has(fileName) && !(options.includeLockfiles && fileName.includes("lock"))) return true;
191 |   if (DEFAULT_IGNORE_EXTENSIONS.has(extension) && !(options.includeLockfiles && extension === ".lock")) return true;
192 | 
193 |   return false;
194 | }
195 | 
196 | function shouldIgnoreDirectory(relativePath) {
197 |   const normalized = toPosix(relativePath);
198 |   const parts = normalized.split("/");
199 | 
200 |   return parts.some((part, index) => {
201 |     const candidate = parts.slice(0, index + 1).join("/");
202 |     return DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRS.has(part) || DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRS.has(candidate);
203 |   });
204 | }
205 | 
206 | function isIncludedFile(relativePath, options) {
207 |   const fileName = path.basename(relativePath);
208 |   const extension = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
209 | 
210 |   if (fileName === ".env.example") return options.includeExtensions.has(".env.example");
211 |   return options.includeExtensions.has(extension);
212 | }
213 | 
214 | function hashContent(content) {
215 |   return crypto.createHash("sha256").update(content).digest("hex").slice(0, 16);
216 | }
217 | 
218 | function redactSecrets(content) {
219 |   return content
220 |     .replace(/-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----[\s\S]*?-----END [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----/g, "[REDACTED_PRIVATE_KEY]")
221 |     .replace(/\bgh[pousr]_[0-9A-Za-z_]{36,255}\b/g, "[REDACTED_GITHUB_TOKEN]")
222 |     .replace(/\bsk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b/g, "[REDACTED_OPENAI_STYLE_KEY]")
223 |     .replace(/\bAIza[0-9A-Za-z-_]{35}\b/g, "[REDACTED_GOOGLE_API_KEY]")
224 |     .replace(/\b(xox[baprs]-[0-9A-Za-z-]{20,})\b/g, "[REDACTED_SLACK_TOKEN]")
225 |     .replace(
226 |       /\b((?:postgres|postgresql|mysql|mongodb(?:\+srv)?|redis):\/\/[^:\s/@]+:)[^@\s"'`<>)]+(@[^\s"'`<>)]+)/gi,
227 |       "$1[REDACTED_PASSWORD]$2",
228 |     )
229 |     .replace(
230 |       /^(\s*["']?[A-Z0-9_]*(?:SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD|PRIVATE_KEY|API_KEY|ACCESS_KEY|REFRESH_TOKEN)[A-Z0-9_]*["']?\s*[:=]\s*)["']?[^"',\n}]+["']?/gim,
231 |       "$1[REDACTED_SECRET]",
232 |     );
233 | }
234 | 
235 | async function collectFiles(options, directory = options.root, files = [], skipped = []) {
236 |   const entries = await fs.readdir(directory, { withFileTypes: true });
237 | 
238 |   for (const entry of entries) {
239 |     const absolutePath = path.join(directory, entry.name);
240 |     const relativePath = path.relative(options.root, absolutePath);
241 |     const safePath = toPosix(relativePath);
242 | 
243 |     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
244 |       if (!shouldIgnoreDirectory(relativePath)) {
245 |         await collectFiles(options, absolutePath, files, skipped);
246 |       } else {
247 |         skipped.push({ file: safePath, reason: "ignored directory" });
248 |       }
249 |       continue;
250 |     }
251 | 
252 |     if (!entry.isFile()) continue;
253 | 
254 |     if (shouldIgnorePath(relativePath, options)) {
255 |       skipped.push({ file: safePath, reason: "ignored file or sensitive path" });
256 |       continue;
257 |     }
258 | 
259 |     if (!isIncludedFile(relativePath, options)) {
260 |       skipped.push({ file: safePath, reason: "extension not included" });
261 |       continue;
262 |     }
263 | 
264 |     const stats = await fs.stat(absolutePath);
265 | 
266 |     if (stats.size > options.maxFileBytes) {
267 |       skipped.push({ file: safePath, reason: `larger than ${options.maxFileBytes} bytes` });
268 |       continue;
269 |     }
270 | 
271 |     files.push({
272 |       absolutePath,
273 |       file: safePath,
274 |       size: stats.size,
275 |       extension: path.extname(entry.name).toLowerCase() || path.basename(entry.name),
276 |       modifiedAt: stats.mtime.toISOString(),
277 |     });
278 |   }
279 | 
280 |   return { files, skipped };
281 | }
282 | 
283 | async function readTextFile(file, options) {
284 |   const raw = await fs.readFile(file.absolutePath, "utf8");
285 |   const content = options.redact ? redactSecrets(raw) : raw;
286 | 
287 |   return {
288 |     ...file,
289 |     content,
290 |     hash: hashContent(content),
291 |     lineCount: content.split(/\r?\n/).length,
292 |   };
293 | }
294 | 
295 | function groupBy(items, getKey) {
296 |   const map = new Map();
297 |   for (const item of items) {
298 |     const key = getKey(item);
299 |     map.set(key, [...(map.get(key) || []), item]);
300 |   }
301 |   return map;
302 | }
303 | 
304 | function buildTree(files) {
305 |   const root = {};
306 | 
307 |   for (const file of files) {
308 |     const parts = file.file.split("/");
309 |     let current = root;
310 |     for (const part of parts) {
311 |       current[part] ||= {};
312 |       current = current[part];
313 |     }
314 |   }
315 | 
316 |   function render(node, prefix = "", depth = 0) {
317 |     if (depth > 6) return [];
318 |     const entries = Object.keys(node).sort((left, right) => {
319 |       const leftIsDir = Object.keys(node[left]).length > 0;
320 |       const rightIsDir = Object.keys(node[right]).length > 0;
321 |       if (leftIsDir !== rightIsDir) return leftIsDir ? -1 : 1;
322 |       return left.localeCompare(right);
323 |     });
324 | 
325 |     const lines = [];
326 |     entries.forEach((entry, index) => {
327 |       const isLast = index === entries.length - 1;
328 |       const child = node[entry];
329 |       const isDir = Object.keys(child).length > 0;
330 |       lines.push(`${prefix}${isLast ? "`-- " : "|-- "}${entry}${isDir ? "/" : ""}`);
331 |       if (isDir) {
332 |         lines.push(...render(child, `${prefix}${isLast ? "    " : "|   "}`, depth + 1));
333 |       }
334 |     });
335 |     return lines;
336 |   }
337 | 
338 |   return render(root).join("\n");
339 | }
340 | 
341 | function parsePackageJson(file) {
342 |   try {
343 |     return JSON.parse(file.content);
344 |   } catch {
345 |     return null;
346 |   }
347 | }
348 | 
349 | function detectPackageInfo(files) {
350 |   return files
351 |     .filter((file) => file.file.endsWith("package.json"))
352 |     .map((file) => {
353 |       const parsed = parsePackageJson(file);
354 |       return {
355 |         file: file.file,
356 |         name: parsed?.name || "(unnamed)",
357 |         version: parsed?.version || "",
358 |         scripts: parsed?.scripts || {},
359 |         dependencies: {
360 |           ...parsed?.dependencies,
361 |           ...parsed?.devDependencies,
362 |         },
363 |         workspaces: parsed?.workspaces || [],
364 |       };
365 |     });
366 | }
367 | 
368 | function detectImports(files) {
369 |   const importPattern = /(?:import\s+(?:[^'"]+\s+from\s+)?|export\s+[^'"]+\s+from\s+|require\()\s*["']([^"']+)["']\)?/g;
370 |   const rows = [];
371 | 
372 |   for (const file of files) {
373 |     if (!/\.(cjs|js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)$/.test(file.extension)) continue;
374 |     const imports = new Set();
375 |     let match;
376 |     while ((match = importPattern.exec(file.content)) !== null) {
377 |       imports.add(match[1]);
378 |     }
379 |     if (imports.size) {
380 |       rows.push({
381 |         file: file.file,
382 |         imports: [...imports].sort(),
383 |       });
384 |     }
385 |   }
386 | 
387 |   return rows;
388 | }
389 | 
390 | function detectRoutesAndEndpoints(files) {
391 |   const reactRoutes = [];
392 |   const apiEndpoints = [];
393 | 
394 |   const routePattern = /<Route\b[^>]*\bpath=["']([^"']+)["'][^>]*>/g;
395 |   const apiPattern = /["'`](\/api\/[^"'`\s,)]+)["'`]/g;
396 |   const pathnamePattern = /pathname\s*(?:===|\.match\()\s*["'`](\/api\/[^"'`]+)["'`]/g;
397 | 
398 |   for (const file of files) {
399 |     let match;
400 | 
401 |     while ((match = routePattern.exec(file.content)) !== null) {
402 |       reactRoutes.push({ file: file.file, route: match[1] });
403 |     }
404 | 
405 |     while ((match = apiPattern.exec(file.content)) !== null) {
406 |       apiEndpoints.push({ file: file.file, endpoint: match[1] });
407 |     }
408 | 
409 |     while ((match = pathnamePattern.exec(file.content)) !== null) {
410 |       apiEndpoints.push({ file: file.file, endpoint: match[1] });
411 |     }
412 |   }
413 | 
414 |   return {
415 |     reactRoutes: uniqueObjects(reactRoutes, (item) => `${item.file}:${item.route}`),
416 |     apiEndpoints: uniqueObjects(apiEndpoints, (item) => `${item.file}:${item.endpoint}`),
417 |   };
418 | }
419 | 
420 | function detectEnvVars(files) {
421 |   const envPattern = /\b(?:process\.env|import\.meta\.env)\.([A-Z0-9_]+)/g;
422 |   const envVars = [];
423 | 
424 |   for (const file of files) {
425 |     let match;
426 |     while ((match = envPattern.exec(file.content)) !== null) {
427 |       envVars.push({ file: file.file, name: match[1] });
428 |     }
429 |   }
430 | 
431 |   return uniqueObjects(envVars, (item) => `${item.file}:${item.name}`).sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name));
432 | }
433 | 
434 | function uniqueObjects(items, getKey) {
435 |   const seen = new Set();
436 |   return items.filter((item) => {
437 |     const key = getKey(item);
438 |     if (seen.has(key)) return false;
439 |     seen.add(key);
440 |     return true;
441 |   });
442 | }
443 | 
444 | function inferModuleNotes(files) {
445 |   const notes = [];
446 |   const topFolders = groupBy(files, (file) => file.file.split("/")[0]);
447 | 
448 |   for (const [folder, folderFiles] of [...topFolders.entries()].sort()) {
449 |     const extensions = groupBy(folderFiles, (file) => file.extension);
450 |     notes.push({
451 |       folder,
452 |       files: folderFiles.length,
453 |       lines: folderFiles.reduce((total, file) => total + file.lineCount, 0),
454 |       extensions: [...extensions.entries()]
455 |         .sort((a, b) => b[1].length - a[1].length)
456 |         .slice(0, 5)
457 |         .map(([extension, items]) => `${extension}: ${items.length}`),
458 |     });
459 |   }
460 | 
461 |   return notes;
462 | }
463 | 
464 | function detectEntrypoints(files) {
465 |   const hints = [
466 |     "main.tsx",
467 |     "main.ts",
468 |     "index.tsx",
469 |     "index.ts",
470 |     "server.js",
471 |     "server.ts",
472 |     "app.py",
473 |     "vite.config.ts",
474 |     "AppRouter.tsx",
475 |   ];
476 | 
477 |   return files
478 |     .filter((file) => hints.some((hint) => file.file.endsWith(hint)))
479 |     .map((file) => file.file)
480 |     .sort();
481 | }
482 | 
483 | function buildManifest(files, skipped, options) {
484 |   const packages = detectPackageInfo(files);
485 |   const imports = detectImports(files);
486 |   const routeInfo = detectRoutesAndEndpoints(files);
487 |   const envVars = detectEnvVars(files);
488 |   const byExtension = [...groupBy(files, (file) => file.extension).entries()]
489 |     .map(([extension, items]) => ({
490 |       extension,
491 |       files: items.length,
492 |       lines: items.reduce((total, file) => total + file.lineCount, 0),
493 |       bytes: items.reduce((total, file) => total + file.size, 0),
494 |     }))
495 |     .sort((a, b) => b.files - a.files);
496 | 
497 |   return {
498 |     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
499 |     root: options.root,
500 |     redactionEnabled: options.redact,
501 |     maxFileBytes: options.maxFileBytes,
502 |     totals: {
503 |       files: files.length,
504 |       lines: files.reduce((total, file) => total + file.lineCount, 0),
505 |       bytes: files.reduce((total, file) => total + file.size, 0),
506 |       skipped: skipped.length,
507 |     },
508 |     byExtension,
509 |     packages,
510 |     entrypoints: detectEntrypoints(files),
511 |     routes: routeInfo.reactRoutes,
512 |     apiEndpoints: routeInfo.apiEndpoints,
513 |     envVars,
514 |     moduleNotes: inferModuleNotes(files),
515 |     imports,
516 |     skipped,
517 |     files: files.map(({ content, ...metadata }) => metadata),
518 |   };
519 | }
520 | 
521 | function mdTable(headers, rows) {
522 |   if (!rows.length) return "_None detected._\n";
523 |   const header = `| ${headers.join(" | ")} |`;
524 |   const divider = `| ${headers.map(() => "---").join(" | ")} |`;
525 |   const body = rows.map((row) => `| ${row.map((cell) => String(cell).replace(/\|/g, "\\|")).join(" | ")} |`);
526 |   return [header, divider, ...body].join("\n");
527 | }
528 | 
529 | function buildArchitectureReport(manifest) {
530 |   const lines = [];
531 |   lines.push("# Project Architecture Report");
532 |   lines.push("");
533 |   lines.push(`Generated: ${manifest.generatedAt}`);
534 |   lines.push(`Root: ${manifest.root}`);
535 |   lines.push("");
536 |   lines.push("## Executive Summary");
537 |   lines.push("");
538 |   lines.push(`- Files exported: ${manifest.totals.files}`);
539 |   lines.push(`- Total lines exported: ${manifest.totals.lines.toLocaleString()}`);
540 |   lines.push(`- Total source bytes exported: ${manifest.totals.bytes.toLocaleString()}`);
541 |   lines.push(`- Skipped paths: ${manifest.totals.skipped}`);
542 |   lines.push(`- Secret redaction: ${manifest.redactionEnabled ? "enabled" : "disabled"}`);
543 |   lines.push("");
544 |   lines.push("## Project Tree");
545 |   lines.push("");
546 |   lines.push("```text");
547 |   lines.push(buildTree(manifest.files));
548 |   lines.push("```");
549 |   lines.push("");
550 |   lines.push("## Workspaces And Packages");
551 |   lines.push("");
552 |   lines.push(
553 |     mdTable(
554 |       ["Package", "File", "Scripts", "Dependency Count", "Workspaces"],
555 |       manifest.packages.map((pkg) => [
556 |         pkg.name,
557 |         pkg.file,
558 |         Object.keys(pkg.scripts).join(", ") || "-",
559 |         Object.keys(pkg.dependencies).length,
560 |         Array.isArray(pkg.workspaces) ? pkg.workspaces.join(", ") : "-",
561 |       ]),
562 |     ),
563 |   );
564 |   lines.push("");
565 |   lines.push("## Entrypoints");
566 |   lines.push("");
567 |   lines.push(manifest.entrypoints.length ? manifest.entrypoints.map((item) => `- ${item}`).join("\n") : "_None detected._");
568 |   lines.push("");
569 |   lines.push("## Frontend Routes");
570 |   lines.push("");
571 |   lines.push(mdTable(["Route", "File"], manifest.routes.map((route) => [route.route, route.file])));
572 |   lines.push("");
573 |   lines.push("## API Endpoints");
574 |   lines.push("");
575 |   lines.push(mdTable(["Endpoint", "File"], manifest.apiEndpoints.map((endpoint) => [endpoint.endpoint, endpoint.file])));
576 |   lines.push("");
577 |   lines.push("## Environment Variables Referenced");
578 |   lines.push("");
579 |   lines.push(mdTable(["Variable", "File"], manifest.envVars.map((envVar) => [envVar.name, envVar.file])));
580 |   lines.push("");
581 |   lines.push("## Source Composition");
582 |   lines.push("");
583 |   lines.push(mdTable(["Extension", "Files", "Lines", "Bytes"], manifest.byExtension.map((item) => [item.extension, item.files, item.lines, item.bytes])));
584 |   lines.push("");
585 |   lines.push("## Module Breakdown");
586 |   lines.push("");
587 |   lines.push(mdTable(["Folder", "Files", "Lines", "Top Extensions"], manifest.moduleNotes.map((note) => [note.folder, note.files, note.lines, note.extensions.join(", ")])));
588 |   lines.push("");
589 |   lines.push("## Local Import Map");
590 |   lines.push("");
591 |   lines.push(
592 |     mdTable(
593 |       ["File", "Imports"],
594 |       manifest.imports
595 |         .filter((item) => item.imports.some((specifier) => specifier.startsWith(".") || specifier.startsWith("@/") || specifier.startsWith("@insightflow/")))
596 |         .slice(0, 300)
597 |         .map((item) => [item.file, item.imports.join(", ")]),
598 |     ),
599 |   );
600 |   lines.push("");
601 |   lines.push("## Skipped Paths");
602 |   lines.push("");
603 |   lines.push(mdTable(["Path", "Reason"], manifest.skipped.map((item) => [item.file, item.reason]).slice(0, 500)));
604 |   lines.push("");
605 |   lines.push("## Export Notes");
606 |   lines.push("");
607 |   lines.push("- The code bundle is intended for AI review, migration planning, auditing, and debugging.");
608 |   lines.push("- Generated folders, dependencies, binary/media files, logs, PDFs, SQLite databases, and common secret files are skipped.");
609 |   lines.push("- Secret redaction is best effort. Review the generated bundle before sharing it externally.");
610 |   lines.push("- Use `CODEBASE_MANIFEST.json` for automation and `CODEBASE_ARCHITECTURE.md` for human review.");
611 |   lines.push("");
612 |   return `${lines.join("\n")}\n`;
613 | }
614 | 
615 | function codeFenceLanguage(file) {
616 |   return LANGUAGE_BY_EXTENSION.get(file.extension) || file.extension.replace(".", "") || "text";
617 | }
618 | 
619 | function buildCodeBundle(files, manifest) {
620 |   const lines = [];
621 |   lines.push("# Codebase Deep Export");
622 |   lines.push("");
623 |   lines.push(`Generated: ${manifest.generatedAt}`);
624 |   lines.push(`Root: ${manifest.root}`);
625 |   lines.push("");
626 |   lines.push("This file contains the exported source code for the project. Review redactions and skipped paths before sharing externally.");
627 |   lines.push("");
628 |   lines.push("## Export Summary");
629 |   lines.push("");
630 |   lines.push(`- Files: ${manifest.totals.files}`);
631 |   lines.push(`- Lines: ${manifest.totals.lines.toLocaleString()}`);
632 |   lines.push(`- Bytes: ${manifest.totals.bytes.toLocaleString()}`);
633 |   lines.push(`- Redaction: ${manifest.redactionEnabled ? "enabled" : "disabled"}`);
634 |   lines.push("");
635 |   lines.push("## File Index");
636 |   lines.push("");
637 |   for (const file of files) {
638 |     lines.push(`- ${file.file} (${file.lineCount} lines, ${file.size} bytes, sha256:${file.hash})`);
639 |   }
640 |   lines.push("");
641 | 
642 |   for (const file of files) {
643 |     lines.push("---");
644 |     lines.push("");
645 |     lines.push(`## FILE: ${file.file}`);
646 |     lines.push("");
647 |     lines.push(`- Lines: ${file.lineCount}`);
648 |     lines.push(`- Bytes: ${file.size}`);
649 |     lines.push(`- Modified: ${file.modifiedAt}`);
650 |     lines.push(`- SHA256 short: ${file.hash}`);
651 |     lines.push("");
652 |     lines.push(`\`\`\`${codeFenceLanguage(file)}`);
653 |     lines.push(file.content.replace(/\n```/g, "\n\\`\\`\\`"));
654 |     lines.push("```");
655 |     lines.push("");
656 |   }
657 | 
658 |   return `${lines.join("\n")}\n`;
659 | }
660 | 
661 | async function main() {
662 |   const options = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
663 |   await fs.mkdir(options.outDir, { recursive: true });
664 | 
665 |   const { files, skipped } = await collectFiles(options);
666 |   const sortedFiles = files.sort((left, right) => left.file.localeCompare(right.file));
667 |   const enrichedFiles = [];
668 | 
669 |   for (const file of sortedFiles) {
670 |     try {
671 |       enrichedFiles.push(await readTextFile(file, options));
672 |     } catch (error) {
673 |       skipped.push({ file: file.file, reason: `unreadable as utf8: ${error.message}` });
674 |     }
675 |   }
676 | 
677 |   const manifest = buildManifest(enrichedFiles, skipped.sort((a, b) => a.file.localeCompare(b.file)), options);
678 |   const codeBundle = buildCodeBundle(enrichedFiles, manifest);
679 |   const architectureReport = buildArchitectureReport(manifest);
680 | 
681 |   const codePath = path.join(options.outDir, "CODEBASE_DEEP.md");
682 |   const architecturePath = path.join(options.outDir, "CODEBASE_ARCHITECTURE.md");
683 |   const manifestPath = path.join(options.outDir, "CODEBASE_MANIFEST.json");
684 | 
685 |   await fs.writeFile(codePath, codeBundle, "utf8");
686 |   await fs.writeFile(architecturePath, architectureReport, "utf8");
687 |   await fs.writeFile(manifestPath, JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2), "utf8");
688 | 
689 |   console.log("Deep codebase export complete.");
690 |   console.log(`Code bundle: ${path.relative(process.cwd(), codePath)}`);
691 |   console.log(`Architecture: ${path.relative(process.cwd(), architecturePath)}`);
692 |   console.log(`Manifest: ${path.relative(process.cwd(), manifestPath)}`);
693 |   console.log(`Files exported: ${manifest.totals.files}`);
694 |   console.log(`Skipped paths: ${manifest.totals.skipped}`);
695 | }
696 | 
697 | main().catch((error) => {
698 |   console.error(error);
699 |   process.exitCode = 1;
700 | });
701 | 
```

 30 | const IGNORED_DIRECTORIES = new Set([
 31 |   '.agents',
 32 |   '.claude',
 33 |   '.cursor',
 34 |   '.firebase',
 35 |   '.git',
 36 |   '.next',
 37 |   '.pytest_cache',
 38 |   '.tmp_schema_trained_patch',

 53 | 
 54 | const IGNORED_FILENAMES = new Set([
 55 |   'CODEBASE_ARCHITECTURE.md',
 56 |   'CODEBASE_DEEP.md',
 57 |   'CODEBASE_MANIFEST.json',
 58 |   'firebase-adminsdk.json',
 59 |   'package-lock.json',
 60 |   'pnpm-lock.yaml',
 61 |   'PROJECT_AI_SAFE.md',
 62 |   'PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.md',
 63 |   'PROJECT_FULL_CODEBASE_ONE_FILE.md',
 64 |   'serviceAccountKey.json',
 65 |   'yarn.lock',
 66 | ]);
 67 | 
 68 | const IGNORED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([
 69 |   '.7z',
 70 |   '.avif',
 71 |   '.bmp',
 72 |   '.db',
 73 |   '.docx',
 74 |   '.gif',
 75 |   '.ico',
 76 |   '.jpeg',
 77 |   '.jpg',
 78 |   '.lock',
 79 |   '.log',
 80 |   '.mp3',
 81 |   '.mp4',
 82 |   '.pdf',
 83 |   '.png',
 84 |   '.sqlite',
 85 |   '.svg',
 86 |   '.webm',
 87 |   '.webp',
 88 |   '.woff',
 89 |   '.woff2',
 90 |   '.zip',
 91 | ]);
 92 | 
 93 | const MAX_FILE_BYTES = 1_000_000;
 94 | 
 95 | const toPosixPath = (filePath) => filePath.split(path.sep).join('/');
 96 | 
 97 | const isSecretLikeFilename = (fileName) => {
 98 |   const lowerName = fileName.toLowerCase();
 99 | 
100 |   if (lowerName === '.env.example') return false;
101 | 
102 |   return (
103 |     lowerName === '.env' ||
104 |     lowerName.startsWith('.env.') ||
105 |     lowerName.includes('service-account') ||
106 |     lowerName.includes('service_account') ||
107 |     lowerName.includes('private-key') ||
108 |     lowerName.includes('private_key') ||
109 |     lowerName.includes('credential') ||
110 |     lowerName.includes('secret')
111 |   );
112 | };
113 | 
114 | const isIgnoredFile = (relativePath) => {
115 |   const fileName = path.basename(relativePath);
116 |   const extension = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
117 | 
118 |   if (isSecretLikeFilename(fileName)) {
119 |     return true;
120 |   }
121 | 
122 |   if (fileName.startsWith('firebase-adminsdk') && extension === '.json') {
123 |     return true;
124 |   }
125 | 
126 |   return IGNORED_FILENAMES.has(fileName) || IGNORED_EXTENSIONS.has(extension);
127 | };
128 | 
129 | const EXTENSIONLESS_ALLOWLIST = new Set([
130 |   '.gitignore',
131 |   'Dockerfile',
132 |   'Modelfile',
133 | ]);
134 | 
135 | const isIncludedFile = (relativePath) => {
136 |   const fileName = path.basename(relativePath);
137 |   const extension = path.extname(relativePath).toLowerCase();
138 | 
139 |   if (fileName === '.env.example') return true;
140 |   if (EXTENSIONLESS_ALLOWLIST.has(fileName)) return !isIgnoredFile(relativePath);
141 | 
142 |   return INCLUDED_EXTENSIONS.has(extension) && !isIgnoredFile(relativePath);
143 | };
144 | 
145 | const redactSecrets = (content) =>
146 |   content
147 |     .replace(/AIza[0-9A-Za-z-_]{35}/g, '[REDACTED_FIREBASE_API_KEY]')
148 |     .replace(/sk-(?:proj-)?[0-9A-Za-z_-]{20,}/g, '[REDACTED_OPENAI_SECRET_KEY]')
149 |     .replace(/sk_(?:live|test)_[0-9A-Za-z]+/g, '[REDACTED_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY]')
150 |     .replace(/pk_(?:live|test)_[0-9A-Za-z]+/g, '[REDACTED_STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY]')
151 |     .replace(/gh[pousr]_[0-9A-Za-z_]{36,255}/g, '[REDACTED_GITHUB_TOKEN]')
152 |     .replace(/xox[baprs]-[0-9A-Za-z-]{20,}/g, '[REDACTED_SLACK_TOKEN]')
153 |     .replace(/-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----[\s\S]*?-----END [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----/g, '[REDACTED_PRIVATE_KEY]')
154 |     .replace(
155 |       /\b((?:postgres|postgresql|mysql|mongodb(?:\+srv)?|redis):\/\/[^:\s/@]+:)[^@\s]+(@[^\s"'`<>)]+)/gi,
156 |       '$1[REDACTED_PASSWORD]$2',
157 |     )
158 |     .replace(
159 |       /^(\s*["']?[A-Z0-9_]*(?:SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD|PRIVATE_KEY|API_KEY|ACCESS_KEY|REFRESH_TOKEN|AUTH_KEY|CLIENT_SECRET)[A-Z0-9_]*["']?\s*[:=]\s*)["']?[^"',\n}]+["']?/gim,
160 |       '$1[REDACTED_SECRET]',
161 |     );
162 | 
163 | const withLineNumbers = (content) => {
164 |   const lines = content.split(/\r?\n/);
165 |   const width = String(lines.length).length;
166 | 
167 |   return lines
168 |     .map((line, index) => `${String(index + 1).padStart(width, ' ')} | ${line}`)
169 |     .join('\n');
170 | };
171 | 
172 | const collectFiles = async (directory = PROJECT_ROOT, files = [], skipped = []) => {
173 |   const entries = await fs.readdir(directory, { withFileTypes: true });
174 | 
175 |   for (const entry of entries) {
176 |     const absolutePath = path.join(directory, entry.name);
177 |     const relativePath = path.relative(PROJECT_ROOT, absolutePath);
178 |     const safeRelativePath = toPosixPath(relativePath);
179 |     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
180 |       if (IGNORED_DIRECTORIES.has(entry.name)) {
181 |         skipped.push({
182 |           file: `${safeRelativePath}/`,
183 |           reason: 'generated, dependency, report, or temporary directory',
184 |         });
185 |         continue;
186 |       }
187 | 
188 |       await collectFiles(absolutePath, files, skipped);
189 |       continue;
190 |     }
191 | 
192 |     if (!entry.isFile()) {
193 |       continue;
194 |     }
195 | 
196 |     if (!isIncludedFile(relativePath)) {
197 |       skipped.push({
198 |         file: safeRelativePath,
199 |         reason: 'not an included source/documentation extension or secret-like filename',
200 |       });
201 |       continue;
202 |     }
203 | 
204 |     const stats = await fs.stat(absolutePath);
205 | 
206 |     if (stats.size > MAX_FILE_BYTES) {
207 |       skipped.push({
208 |         file: safeRelativePath,
209 |         reason: `larger than ${MAX_FILE_BYTES} bytes`,
210 |       });
211 |       continue;
212 |     }
213 | 
214 |     files.push({
215 |       absolutePath,
216 |       file: safeRelativePath,
217 |       size: stats.size,
218 |     });
219 |   }
220 | 
221 |   return { files, skipped };
222 | };
223 | 
224 | const buildDirectoryTree = (files) => {
225 |   const tree = new Map();
226 | 
227 |   for (const { file } of files) {
228 |     const parts = file.split('/');
229 |     let current = tree;
230 | 
231 |     for (const part of parts) {
232 |       if (!current.has(part)) {
233 |         current.set(part, new Map());
234 |       }
235 | 
236 |       current = current.get(part);
237 |     }
238 |   }
239 | 
240 |   const render = (node, depth = 0) => {
241 |     const entries = [...node.entries()].sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b));
242 |     const lines = [];
243 | 
244 |     for (const [name, children] of entries) {
245 |       lines.push(`${'  '.repeat(depth)}- ${name}`);
246 | 
247 |       if (children.size > 0) {
248 |         lines.push(...render(children, depth + 1));
249 |       }
250 |     }
251 | 
252 |     return lines;
253 |   };
254 | 
255 |   return render(tree).join('\n');
256 | };
257 | 
258 | const buildSummary = (files, skipped) => {
259 |   let summary = '# Project Full Codebase One File\n\n';
260 |   summary += `Generated: ${new Date().toISOString()}\n\n`;
261 |   summary += 'This single-file export contains project architecture plus redacted source/documentation contents.\n';
262 |   summary += 'Secret-bearing files are excluded, and known token/password/private-key patterns are redacted in included files.\n\n';
263 |   summary += '## Architecture\n\n';
264 |   summary += '### Included File Tree\n\n';
265 |   summary += '```text\n';
266 |   summary += buildDirectoryTree(files);
267 |   summary += '\n```\n\n';
268 |   summary += '### Included Files\n\n';
269 | 
270 |   for (const { file, size } of files) {
271 |     summary += `- ${file} (${size} bytes)\n`;
272 |   }
273 | 
274 |   if (skipped.length > 0) {
275 |     summary += '\n### Skipped Files And Directories\n\n';
276 | 
277 |     for (const { file, reason } of skipped) {
278 |       summary += `- ${file}: ${reason}\n`;
279 |     }
280 |   }
281 | 
282 |   summary += '\n### Safety Rules\n\n';
283 |   summary += '- `.env*`, credential, secret, private-key, Firebase admin SDK, and service-account style files are excluded.\n';
284 |   summary += '- Dependency folders, build output, reports, generated exports, logs, lockfiles, common media, and large files are excluded.\n';
285 |   summary += '- Known API keys, tokens, private keys, and database URL passwords are redacted before writing this export.\n\n';
286 |   summary += '## Code Files\n\n';
287 | 
288 |   return summary;
289 | };
290 | 
291 | const LANG_MAP = new Map([
292 |   ['bat', 'bat'],
293 |   ['cjs', 'js'],
294 |   ['Dockerfile', 'dockerfile'],
295 |   ['mjs', 'js'],
296 |   ['ps1', 'powershell'],
297 |   ['py', 'python'],
298 |   ['sh', 'bash'],
299 |   ['yaml', 'yaml'],
300 |   ['yml', 'yaml'],
301 | ]);
302 | 
303 | const languageFor = (file) => {
304 |   const extension = path.extname(file).replace('.', '');
305 |   const fileName = path.basename(file);
306 | 
307 |   return LANG_MAP.get(extension) || LANG_MAP.get(fileName) || extension || 'text';
308 | };
309 | 
310 | const exportCodebase = async () => {
311 |   const { files, skipped } = await collectFiles();
312 |   const sortedFiles = files.sort((a, b) => a.file.localeCompare(b.file));
313 |   const sortedSkipped = skipped.sort((a, b) => a.file.localeCompare(b.file));
314 | 
315 |   let output = buildSummary(sortedFiles, sortedSkipped);
316 | 
317 |   for (const { absolutePath, file } of sortedFiles) {
318 |     const rawContent = await fs.readFile(absolutePath, 'utf8');
319 |     const content = withLineNumbers(redactSecrets(rawContent));
320 | 
321 |     output += '---\n\n';
322 |     output += `### FILE: ${file}\n\n`;
323 |     output += `Source path: \`${file}\`\n\n`;
324 |     output += `\`\`\`${languageFor(file)}\n`;
325 |     output += content;
326 |     output += '\n```\n\n';
327 |   }
328 | 
329 |   await fs.writeFile(OUTPUT_FILE, output, 'utf8');
330 | 
331 |   console.log(`Generated ${path.relative(PROJECT_ROOT, OUTPUT_FILE)}`);
332 |   console.log(`Included ${sortedFiles.length} file(s).`);
333 |   console.log(`Skipped ${sortedSkipped.length} file(s)/director(ies).`);
334 | };
335 | 
336 | exportCodebase().catch((error) => {
337 |   console.error(error);
338 |   process.exitCode = 1;
339 | });
340 | 
```

### FILE: scripts/generate-codebase-export.mjs

Source path: `scripts/generate-codebase-export.mjs`

```js
  1 | import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
  2 | import path from 'node:path';
  3 | import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
  4 | 
  5 | const PROJECT_ROOT = process.cwd();
  6 | const OUTPUT_FILE = path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, 'CODEBASE_EXPORT.md');
  7 | const IGNORE_FILE = path.join(PROJECT_ROOT, '.gitignore');
  8 | 
  9 | const INCLUDED_EXTENSIONS = new Set([
 10 |   '.bat', '.cjs', '.css', '.env.example', '.html', '.js', '.json',
 11 |   '.jsx', '.mjs', '.md', '.ps1', '.py', '.scss', '.sh', '.sql',
 12 |   '.ts', '.tsx', '.txt', '.yaml', '.yml',
 13 | ]);
 14 | 
 15 | const EXTENSIONLESS_ALLOWLIST = new Set([
 16 |   '.gitignore', 'Dockerfile', 'Modelfile',
 17 | ]);
 18 | 
 19 | const ALWAYS_IGNORE_DIRS = new Set([
 20 |   '.git', 'node_modules', '.next', '.expo', 'dist', 'build',
 21 |   'coverage', 'logs', 'reports', 'exports', '.tmp_schema_trained_patch',
 22 |   '.vscode', '.idea', '.claude', '.windsurf', '.agents',
 23 | ]);
 24 | 
 25 | const ALWAYS_IGNORE_FILES = new Set([
 26 |   'package-lock.json', 'pnpm-lock.yaml', 'yarn.lock',
 27 |   'CODEBASE_EXPORT.md', 'PROJECT_FULL_CODEBASE_ONE_FILE.md',
 28 |   'PROJECT_AI_SAFE.md', 'PROJECT_ARCHITECTURE.md',
 29 |   'CODEBASE_DEEP.md', 'CODEBASE_ARCHITECTURE.md', 'CODEBASE_MANIFEST.json',
 30 |   'firebase-adminsdk.json', 'serviceAccountKey.json', '.env',
 31 | ]);
 32 | 
 33 | const ALWAYS_IGNORE_EXTS = new Set([
 34 |   '.7z', '.avif', '.bmp', '.db', '.docx', '.exe', '.gif', '.ico',
 35 |   '.jpeg', '.jpg', '.lock', '.log', '.mp3', '.mp4', '.pdf',
 36 |   '.png', '.sqlite', '.svg', '.webm', '.webp', '.woff', '.woff2', '.zip',
 37 | ]);
 38 | 
 39 | const MAX_FILE_BYTES = 1_000_000;
 40 | const LANG_MAP = new Map([
 41 |   ['bat', 'bat'], ['cjs', 'js'], ['mjs', 'js'],
 42 |   ['ps1', 'powershell'], ['py', 'python'], ['sh', 'bash'],
 43 |   ['yaml', 'yaml'], ['yml', 'yaml'],
 44 |   ['Dockerfile', 'dockerfile'], ['Modelfile', 'text'],
 45 | ]);
 46 | 
 47 | const SECRET_PATTERNS = [
 48 |   [/(AIza[0-9A-Za-z-_]{35})/g, '[REDACTED_FIREBASE_API_KEY]'],
 49 |   [/(sk-(?:proj-)?[0-9A-Za-z_-]{20,})/g, '[REDACTED_API_KEY]'],
 50 |   [/(sk_(?:live|test)_[0-9A-Za-z]+)/g, '[REDACTED_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY]'],
 51 |   [/(pk_(?:live|test)_[0-9A-Za-z]+)/g, '[REDACTED_STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY]'],
 52 |   [/(gh[pousr]_[0-9A-Za-z_]{36,255})/g, '[REDACTED_GITHUB_TOKEN]'],
 53 |   [/(xox[baprs]-[0-9A-Za-z-]{20,})/g, '[REDACTED_SLACK_TOKEN]'],
 54 |   [/(-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----)[\s\S]*?(-----END [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----)/g, '$1 [REDACTED_PRIVATE_KEY] $2'],
 55 |   [/((?:postgres|postgresql|mysql|mongodb(?:\+srv)?|redis):\/\/[^:\s/@]+:)([^@\s]+)(@[^\s"'`<>)]+)/gi, '$1[REDACTED_PASSWORD]$3'],
 56 | ];
 57 | 
 58 | const ENV_SECRET_PATTERN = /^(\s*["']?[A-Z0-9_]*(?:SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD|PRIVATE_KEY|API_KEY|ACCESS_KEY|REFRESH_TOKEN|AUTH_KEY|CLIENT_SECRET|SERVICE_KEY)[A-Z0-9_]*["']?\s*[:=]\s*)["']?[^"',\n}]+["']?/gim;
 59 | 
 60 | async function parseGitignore() {
 61 |   const patterns = [];
 62 |   try {
 63 |     const content = await fs.readFile(IGNORE_FILE, 'utf8');
 64 |     for (const line of content.split(/\r?\n/)) {
 65 |       const trimmed = line.trim();
 66 |       if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue;
 67 |       patterns.push(trimmed);
 68 |     }
 69 |   } catch {}
 70 |   return patterns;
 71 | }
 72 | 
 73 | function matchesGitignore(relativePath, gitignorePatterns) {
 74 |   const posix = relativePath.split(path.sep).join('/');
 75 |   for (const pattern of gitignorePatterns) {
 76 |     if (pattern.startsWith('!')) continue;
 77 |     const p = pattern.replace(/\\/g, '/');
 78 |     if (posix === p || posix.startsWith(p + '/') || posix.endsWith('/' + p)) return true;
 79 |   }
 80 |   return false;
 81 | }
 82 | 
 83 | function isSecretLikeFile(fileName) {
 84 |   if (fileName === '.env.example') return false;
 85 |   const lower = fileName.toLowerCase();
 86 |   return (
 87 |     lower === '.env' || lower.startsWith('.env.') ||
 88 |     lower.includes('service-account') || lower.includes('service_account') ||
 89 |     lower.includes('private-key') || lower.includes('private_key') ||
 90 |     lower.includes('credential') || lower.includes('secret')
 91 |   );
 92 | }
 93 | 
 94 | function redactSecrets(content) {
 95 |   let result = content;
 96 |   for (const [pattern, replacement] of SECRET_PATTERNS) {
 97 |     result = result.replace(pattern, replacement);
 98 |   }
 99 |   result = result.replace(ENV_SECRET_PATTERN, '$1[REDACTED_SECRET]');
100 |   return result;
101 | }
102 | 
103 | async function collectFiles(directory, gitignorePatterns, files = [], skipped = []) {
104 |   const entries = await fs.readdir(directory, { withFileTypes: true });
105 | 
106 |   for (const entry of entries) {
107 |     if (entry.name.startsWith('.')) continue;
108 |     const absolutePath = path.join(directory, entry.name);
109 |     let relativePath = path.relative(PROJECT_ROOT, absolutePath);
110 |     const posixPath = relativePath.split(path.sep).join('/');
111 | 
112 |     if (entry.name === 'node_modules') continue;
113 |     if (ALWAYS_IGNORE_DIRS.has(entry.name)) {
114 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath + '/', reason: 'ignored directory' });
115 |       continue;
116 |     }
117 | 
118 |     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
119 |       await collectFiles(absolutePath, gitignorePatterns, files, skipped);
120 |       continue;
121 |     }
122 | 
123 |     if (!entry.isFile()) continue;
124 | 
125 |     const fileName = path.basename(relativePath);
126 |     const ext = path.extname(fileName).toLowerCase();
127 | 
128 |     if (isSecretLikeFile(fileName)) {
129 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath, reason: 'secret-like filename excluded' });
130 |       continue;
131 |     }
132 |     if (ALWAYS_IGNORE_FILES.has(fileName)) {
133 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath, reason: 'always ignored file' });
134 |       continue;
135 |     }
136 |     if (ALWAYS_IGNORE_EXTS.has(ext)) {
137 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath, reason: 'ignored extension' });
138 |       continue;
139 |     }
140 |     if (matchesGitignore(relativePath, gitignorePatterns)) {
141 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath, reason: 'matched .gitignore' });
142 |       continue;
143 |     }
144 | 
145 |     const isAllowedExt = INCLUDED_EXTENSIONS.has(ext);
146 |     const isExtensionlessAllowlisted = EXTENSIONLESS_ALLOWLIST.has(fileName);
147 |     if (!isAllowedExt && !isExtensionlessAllowlisted) {
148 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath, reason: 'extension not included' });
149 |       continue;
150 |     }
151 | 
152 |     const stats = await fs.stat(absolutePath);
153 |     if (stats.size > MAX_FILE_BYTES) {
154 |       skipped.push({ file: posixPath, reason: `file > ${MAX_FILE_BYTES} bytes` });
155 |       continue;
156 |     }
157 | 
158 |     files.push({ absolutePath, file: posixPath, size: stats.size, ext });
159 |   }
160 |   return { files, skipped };
161 | }
162 | 
163 | function buildDirectoryTree(files) {
164 |   const tree = new Map();
165 |   for (const { file } of files) {
166 |     const parts = file.split('/');
167 |     let current = tree;
168 |     for (const part of parts) {
169 |       if (!current.has(part)) current.set(part, new Map());
170 |       current = current.get(part);
171 |     }
172 |   }
173 | 
174 |   const render = (node, depth = 0) => {
175 |     const entries = [...node.entries()].sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b));
176 |     const lines = [];
177 |     for (const [name, children] of entries) {
178 |       lines.push('  '.repeat(depth) + '- ' + name);
179 |       if (children.size > 0) lines.push(...render(children, depth + 1));
180 |     }
181 |     return lines;
182 |   };
183 |   return render(tree).join('\n');
184 | }
185 | 
186 | function detectPackageInfo(files) {
187 |   const packages = files.filter(f => f.file.endsWith('package.json'));
188 |   const results = [];
189 |   for (const pkg of packages) {
190 |     try {
191 |       const content = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(pkg.absolutePath, 'utf8'));
192 |       results.push({
193 |         file: pkg.file,
194 |         name: content.name || '(unnamed)',
195 |         version: content.version || '',
196 |         scripts: content.scripts ? Object.keys(content.scripts) : [],
197 |         deps: content.dependencies ? Object.keys(content.dependencies).length : 0,
198 |         devDeps: content.devDependencies ? Object.keys(content.devDependencies).length : 0,
199 |       });
200 |     } catch {}
201 |   }
202 |   return results;
203 | }
204 | 
205 | function detectEntrypoints(files) {
206 |   const hints = ['main.tsx', 'main.ts', 'index.tsx', 'index.ts', 'App.tsx', 'App.tsx',
207 |     'server.js', 'server.ts', 'app.py', 'vite.config.ts', 'vite.config.js'];
208 |   return files.filter(f => hints.some(h => f.file.endsWith(h))).map(f => f.file).sort();
209 | }
210 | 
211 | function detectEnvVars(files) {
212 |   const pattern = /\b(?:process\.env|import\.meta\.env)\.([A-Z0-9_]+)/g;
213 |   const vars = new Map();
214 |   for (const file of files) {
215 |     try {
216 |       const content = fs.readFileSync(file.absolutePath, 'utf8');
217 |       let match;
218 |       while ((match = pattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
219 |         if (!vars.has(match[1])) vars.set(match[1], []);
220 |         vars.get(match[1]).push(file.file);
221 |       }
222 |     } catch {}
223 |   }
224 |   return [...vars.entries()].sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b));
225 | }
226 | 
227 | function detectApiEndpoints(files) {
228 |   const pattern = /["'`](\/api\/[^"'`\s,)]+)["'`]/g;
229 |   const endpoints = new Map();
230 |   for (const file of files) {
231 |     try {
232 |       const content = fs.readFileSync(file.absolutePath, 'utf8');
233 |       let match;
234 |       while ((match = pattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
235 |         if (!endpoints.has(match[1])) endpoints.set(match[1], []);
236 |         endpoints.get(match[1]).push(file.file);
237 |       }
238 |     } catch {}
239 |   }
240 |   return [...endpoints.entries()].sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b));
241 | }
242 | 
243 | function detectRoutes(files) {
244 |   const pattern = /<Route\b[^>]*\bpath=["']([^"']+)["'][^>]*>/g;
245 |   const routes = [];
246 |   for (const file of files) {
247 |     try {
248 |       const content = fs.readFileSync(file.absolutePath, 'utf8');
249 |       let match;
250 |       while ((match = pattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
251 |         routes.push({ route: match[1], file: file.file });
252 |       }
253 |     } catch {}
254 |   }
255 |   return routes;
256 | }
257 | 
258 | function languageFor(file) {
259 |   const fileName = path.basename(file);
260 |   const ext = path.extname(file).replace('.', '');
261 |   return LANG_MAP.get(ext) || LANG_MAP.get(fileName) || ext || 'text';
262 | }
263 | 
264 | function mdTable(headers, rows) {
265 |   if (!rows.length) return '*None detected.*\n';
266 |   const h = '| ' + headers.join(' | ') + ' |';
267 |   const d = '| ' + headers.map(() => '---').join(' | ') + ' |';
268 |   const b = rows.map(r => '| ' + r.map(c => String(c).replace(/\|/g, '\\|')).join(' | ') + ' |');
269 |   return [h, d, ...b].join('\n');
270 | }
271 | 
272 | async function generate() {
273 |   console.log('Scanning project...');
274 |   const gitignorePatterns = await parseGitignore();
275 |   const { files: rawFiles, skipped } = await collectFiles(PROJECT_ROOT, gitignorePatterns);
276 |   const files = rawFiles.sort((a, b) => a.file.localeCompare(b.file));
277 | 
278 |   console.log(`Found ${files.length} files to include, ${skipped.length} skipped.`);
279 | 
280 |   const packages = detectPackageInfo(files);
281 |   const entrypoints = detectEntrypoints(files);
282 |   const envVars = detectEnvVars(files);
283 |   const apiEndpoints = detectApiEndpoints(files);
284 |   const routes = detectRoutes(files);
285 |   const totalLines = files.reduce((sum, f) => sum + f.size, 0);
286 | 
287 |   let output = '';
288 |   output += `# Project Codebase Export\n\n`;
289 |   output += `Generated: ${new Date().toISOString()}\n\n`;
290 |   output += `> This file contains the full project architecture and source code. Sensitive information (API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys) has been redacted automatically.\n\n`;
291 | 
292 |   // ── Architecture Section ──
293 |   output += `---\n\n`;
294 |   output += `# Part 1: Architecture Overview\n\n`;
295 | 
296 |   output += `## Executive Summary\n\n`;
297 |   output += `- Total files included: ${files.length}\n`;
298 |   output += `- Total skipped: ${skipped.length}\n`;
299 |   output += `- Total bytes: ${totalLines.toLocaleString()}\n`;
300 |   output += `- Secret redaction: enabled\n\n`;
301 | 
302 |   output += `## Directory Tree\n\n`;
303 |   output += '```text\n';
304 |   output += buildDirectoryTree(files);
305 |   output += '\n```\n\n';
306 | 
307 |   if (packages.length) {
308 |     output += `## Packages / Workspaces\n\n`;
309 |     output += mdTable(
310 |       ['Name', 'File', 'Version', 'Scripts', 'Deps', 'DevDeps'],
311 |       packages.map(p => [p.name, p.file, p.version, p.scripts.join(', '), p.deps, p.devDeps])
312 |     );
313 |     output += '\n';
314 |   }
315 | 
316 |   if (entrypoints.length) {
317 |     output += `## Entry Points\n\n`;
318 |     for (const ep of entrypoints) output += `- ${ep}\n`;
319 |     output += '\n';
320 |   }
321 | 
322 |   if (routes.length) {
323 |     output += `## Frontend Routes\n\n`;
324 |     output += mdTable(['Route', 'File'], routes.map(r => [r.route, r.file]));
325 |     output += '\n';
326 |   }
327 | 
328 |   if (apiEndpoints.length) {
329 |     output += `## API Endpoints\n\n`;
330 |     output += mdTable(['Endpoint', 'Files'], apiEndpoints.map(([ep, files]) => [ep, files.join(', ')]));
331 |     output += '\n';
332 |   }
333 | 
334 |   if (envVars.length) {
335 |     output += `## Environment Variables Referenced\n\n`;
336 |     output += mdTable(['Variable', 'Files'], envVars.map(([v, f]) => [v, f.join(', ')]));
337 |     output += '\n';
338 |   }
339 | 
340 |   output += `## Skipped Paths\n\n`;
341 |   output += mdTable(['Path', 'Reason'], skipped.map(s => [s.file, s.reason]));
342 |   output += '\n';
343 | 
344 |   // ── Code Section ──
345 |   output += `---\n\n`;
346 |   output += `# Part 2: Full Source Code\n\n`;
347 | 
348 |   for (const file of files) {
349 |     try {
350 |       const rawContent = await fs.readFile(file.absolutePath, 'utf8');
351 |       const content = redactSecrets(rawContent);
352 |       output += `---\n\n## ${file.file}\n\n`;
353 |       output += `\`\`\`${languageFor(file.file)}\n`;
354 |       output += content;
355 |       if (!content.endsWith('\n')) output += '\n';
356 |       output += '```\n\n';
357 |     } catch {
358 |       output += `---\n\n## ${file.file}\n\n*Could not read file.*\n\n`;
359 |     }
360 |   }
361 | 
362 |   output += `---\n\n*Export complete. ${files.length} files, ${skipped.length} skipped.*\n`;
363 | 
364 |   await fs.writeFile(OUTPUT_FILE, output, 'utf8');
365 |   console.log(`\nDone! Generated ${path.relative(PROJECT_ROOT, OUTPUT_FILE)}`);
366 |   console.log(`  Included: ${files.length} files`);
367 |   console.log(`  Skipped: ${skipped.length} paths`);
368 | }
369 | 
370 | generate().catch(err => { console.error(err); process.exitCode = 1; });
371 | 
```

### FILE: scripts/test-agentic-models.mjs

Source path: `scripts/test-agentic-models.mjs`

```js
 1 | const host = process.env.OLLAMA_HOST || process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:11434';
 2 | const expected = [
 3 |   'strict-schema-analyst:latest',
 4 |   'insightflow-dashboard-guardian:latest',
 5 |   'minimax-m2.7:cloud',
 6 |   'insightflow-strict-schema-analyst:latest',
 7 |   'insightflow-master:latest',
 8 |   'nomic-embed-text:latest',
 9 |   'qwen2.5-coder:7b',
10 |   'qwen3:8b',
11 |   'neural-chat:7b',
12 |   'llama3.2:latest',
13 | ];
14 | 
15 | async function main() {
16 |   const res = await fetch(`${host}/api/tags`);
17 |   if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Cannot connect to Ollama at ${host}: ${res.status}`);
18 |   const data = await res.json();
19 |   const installed = new Set((data.models || []).map((m) => m.name));
20 | 
21 |   console.log('\nInsightFlow Agentic Model Check\n');
22 |   for (const model of expected) {
23 |     console.log(`${installed.has(model) ? '✅' : '❌'} ${model}`);
24 |   }
25 | 
26 |   const missing = expected.filter((m) => !installed.has(m));
27 |   if (missing.length) {
28 |     console.log('\nMissing models:', missing.join(', '));
29 |     process.exitCode = 1;
30 |   } else {
31 |     console.log('\nAll configured models are available.');
32 |   }
33 | }
34 | 
35 | main().catch((error) => {
36 |   console.error(error.message);
37 |   process.exit(1);
38 | });
39 | 
```

### FILE: scripts/verify-insightflow-backend.mjs

Source path: `scripts/verify-insightflow-backend.mjs`

```js
   1 | #!/usr/bin/env node
   2 | 
   3 | /**
   4 |  * InsightFlow Backend Verification Test
   5 |  *
   6 |  * Run:
   7 |  *   node scripts/verify-insightflow-backend.mjs
   8 |  *
   9 |  * Optional:
  10 |  *   BACKEND_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3001 node scripts/verify-insightflow-backend.mjs
  11 |  */
  12 | 
  13 | import os from "node:os";
  14 | import { execSync } from "node:child_process";
  15 | 
  16 | const BASE_URL = process.env.BACKEND_URL || "http://127.0.0.1:3001";
  17 | 
  18 | const results = [];
  19 | let importedDatasetId = null;
  20 | 
  21 | function nowMs() {
  22 |   return Number(process.hrtime.bigint() / 1_000_000n);
  23 | }
  24 | 
  25 | function safeJsonString(value) {
  26 |   try {
  27 |     return JSON.stringify(value, null, 2);
  28 |   } catch {
  29 |     return String(value);
  30 |   }
  31 | }
  32 | 
  33 | function getNodeVersion() {
  34 |   try {
  35 |     return execSync("node --version", { encoding: "utf8" }).trim();
  36 |   } catch {
  37 |     return "unknown";
  38 |   }
  39 | }
  40 | 
  41 | function getNpmVersion() {
  42 |   try {
  43 |     return execSync("npm --version", { encoding: "utf8" }).trim();
  44 |   } catch {
  45 |     return "unknown";
  46 |   }
  47 | }
  48 | 
  49 | async function request(method, path, body = undefined) {
  50 |   const url = `${BASE_URL}${path}`;
  51 |   const started = nowMs();
  52 | 
  53 |   const options = {
  54 |     method,
  55 |     headers: {
  56 |       Accept: "application/json",
  57 |     },
  58 |   };
  59 | 
  60 |   if (body !== undefined) {
  61 |     options.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json";
  62 |     options.body = JSON.stringify(body);
  63 |   }
  64 | 
  65 |   let response;
  66 |   let text = "";
  67 |   let json = null;
  68 | 
  69 |   try {
  70 |     response = await fetch(url, options);
  71 |     text = await response.text();
  72 | 
  73 |     try {
  74 |       json = text ? JSON.parse(text) : null;
  75 |     } catch {
  76 |       json = null;
  77 |     }
  78 | 
  79 |     return {
  80 |       ok: response.ok,
  81 |       status: response.status,
  82 |       headers: response.headers,
  83 |       text,
  84 |       json,
  85 |       ms: nowMs() - started,
  86 |       url,
  87 |       method,
  88 |       path,
  89 |     };
  90 |   } catch (error) {
  91 |     return {
  92 |       ok: false,
  93 |       status: 0,
  94 |       headers: new Headers(),
  95 |       text: "",
  96 |       json: null,
  97 |       ms: nowMs() - started,
  98 |       url,
  99 |       method,
 100 |       path,
 101 |       error: error.message,
 102 |     };
 103 |   }
 104 | }
 105 | 
 106 | function pass(name, meta = {}) {
 107 |   results.push({
 108 |     status: "PASS",
 109 |     name,
 110 |     ...meta,
 111 |   });
 112 | 
 113 |   console.log(`✅ ${name}`);
 114 | }
 115 | 
 116 | function fail(name, meta = {}) {
 117 |   results.push({
 118 |     status: "FAIL",
 119 |     name,
 120 |     ...meta,
 121 |   });
 122 | 
 123 |   console.log(`❌ ${name}`);
 124 | 
 125 |   if (meta.route) console.log(`   Route: ${meta.route}`);
 126 |   if (meta.expected) console.log(`   Expected: ${meta.expected}`);
 127 |   if (meta.actual !== undefined) console.log(`   Actual: ${safeJsonString(meta.actual).slice(0, 900)}`);
 128 |   if (meta.suggestion) console.log(`   Fix: ${meta.suggestion}`);
 129 | }
 130 | 
 131 | function assertCheck(condition, name, meta = {}) {
 132 |   if (condition) {
 133 |     pass(name, meta);
 134 |   } else {
 135 |     fail(name, meta);
 136 |   }
 137 | }
 138 | 
 139 | function hasJson(response) {
 140 |   const contentType = response.headers.get("content-type") || "";
 141 |   return contentType.includes("application/json") || response.json !== null;
 142 | }
 143 | 
 144 | function hasErrorMessage(json) {
 145 |   return Boolean(json && (json.error || json.message));
 146 | }
 147 | 
 148 | function getCacheObject(json) {
 149 |   if (!json || typeof json !== "object") return null;
 150 | 
 151 |   if (json.cache && typeof json.cache === "object") return json.cache;
 152 |   if (json.data && typeof json.data === "object") return json.data;
 153 |   if (json.stats && typeof json.stats === "object") return json.stats;
 154 | 
 155 |   return null;
 156 | }
 157 | 
 158 | function validateCacheObject(cache) {
 159 |   return Boolean(
 160 |     cache &&
 161 |       Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(cache, "totalCached") &&
 162 |       Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(cache, "totalHits") &&
 163 |       Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(cache, "hitRate") &&
 164 |       Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(cache, "savedAPICalls") &&
 165 |       Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(cache, "estimatedCostSaved")
 166 |   );
 167 | }
 168 | 
 169 | async function section(title) {
 170 |   console.log("");
 171 |   console.log("═".repeat(60));
 172 |   console.log(title);
 173 |   console.log("═".repeat(60));
 174 | }
 175 | 
 176 | async function testRootAndHealth() {
 177 |   await section("1. ROOT INDEX & HEALTH CHECK");
 178 | 
 179 |   const root = await request("GET", "/");
 180 |   assertCheck(root.status === 200, "GET / returns 200", {
 181 |     route: "GET /",
 182 |     expected: "200",
 183 |     actual: root.status,
 184 |   });
 185 | 
 186 |   assertCheck(hasJson(root), "Root returns JSON", {
 187 |     route: "GET /",
 188 |     expected: "application/json response",
 189 |     actual: root.text,
 190 |   });
 191 | 
 192 |   assertCheck(Boolean(root.json?.name), "Root has name field", {
 193 |     route: "GET /",
 194 |     expected: "body.name exists",
 195 |     actual: root.json,
 196 |   });
 197 | 
 198 |   const health = await request("GET", "/api/health");
 199 | 
 200 |   assertCheck(health.status === 200, "GET /api/health returns 200", {
 201 |     route: "GET /api/health",
 202 |     expected: "200",
 203 |     actual: health.status,
 204 |   });
 205 | 
 206 |   assertCheck(hasJson(health), "Health returns JSON", {
 207 |     route: "GET /api/health",
 208 |     expected: "application/json response",
 209 |     actual: health.text,
 210 |   });
 211 | 
 212 |   const status = String(health.json?.status || health.json?.health || "").toLowerCase();
 213 | 
 214 |   assertCheck(
 215 |     status.includes("healthy") || status.includes("ok") || status.includes("ready"),
 216 |     "Health status is healthy/ok",
 217 |     {
 218 |       route: "GET /api/health",
 219 |       expected: "status healthy/ok/ready",
 220 |       actual: health.json,
 221 |     }
 222 |   );
 223 | }

 319 |     expected: "body.chatMessages is array",

 326 |     name: "Empty Dataset",
 327 |     columns: [],
 328 |     rows: [],

 394 | async function testRowPatch() {
 395 |   await section("5. ROW PATCHING");
 396 | 
 397 |   const patch = await request("PATCH", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/1`, {
 398 |     column: "salary",
 399 |     value: 99999,
 400 |   });
 401 | 
 402 |   assertCheck(patch.status === 200, "PATCH row returns 200", {
 403 |     route: `PATCH /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/1`,
 404 |     expected: "200",
 405 |     actual: patch.status,
 406 |   });
 407 | 
 408 |   const updatedRow = patch.json?.dataset?.rows?.find((row) => Number(row.__rowId) === 1);
 409 | 
 410 |   assertCheck(updatedRow?.salary === 99999, "Updated row has new value", {
 411 |     route: `PATCH /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/1`,
 412 |     expected: "row.salary === 99999",
 413 |     actual: updatedRow,
 414 |   });
 415 | 
 416 |   const missingColumn = await request("PATCH", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/1`, {
 417 |     value: 123,
 418 |   });
 419 | 
 420 |   assertCheck(missingColumn.status === 400, "Patch without column returns 400", {
 421 |     route: `PATCH /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/1`,
 422 |     expected: "400",
 423 |     actual: missingColumn.status,
 424 |   });
 425 | 
 426 |   const missingRow = await request("PATCH", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/9999`, {
 427 |     column: "salary",
 428 |     value: 123,
 429 |   });
 430 | 
 431 |   assertCheck(missingRow.status === 404, "Patch non-existent row returns 404", {
 432 |     route: `PATCH /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/rows/9999`,
 433 |     expected: "404",
 434 |     actual: missingRow.status,
 435 |   });
 436 | }

 438 | async function testChatAndPersistence() {
 439 |   await section("6. CHAT AND CHAT PERSISTENCE");
 440 | 
 441 |   const hello = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`, {
 442 |     query: "hello",
 443 |   });
 444 | 
 445 |   assertCheck(hello.status === 200, "Greeting returns 200", {
 446 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 447 |     expected: "200",
 448 |     actual: hello.status,
 449 |   });
 450 | 
 451 |   assertCheck(hello.json?.userMessage?.role === "user", "Greeting has userMessage", {
 452 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 453 |     expected: "userMessage.role === user",
 454 |     actual: hello.json,
 455 |   });
 456 | 
 457 |   assertCheck(hello.json?.assistantMessage?.role === "assistant", "Greeting has assistantMessage", {
 458 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 459 |     expected: "assistantMessage.role === assistant",
 460 |     actual: hello.json,
 461 |   });
 462 | 
 463 |   assertCheck(Boolean(hello.json?.assistantMessage?.content), "Assistant content is non-empty", {
 464 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 465 |     expected: "assistantMessage.content exists",
 466 |     actual: hello.json?.assistantMessage,
 467 |   });
 468 | 
 469 |   const state = await request("GET", "/api/state");
 470 | 
 471 |   assertCheck(state.status === 200, "GET /api/state after chat returns 200", {
 472 |     route: "GET /api/state",
 473 |     expected: "200",
 474 |     actual: state.status,
 475 |   });
 476 | 
 477 |   assertCheck(Array.isArray(state.json?.chatMessages), "State has chatMessages array", {
 478 |     route: "GET /api/state",
 479 |     expected: "chatMessages array",
 480 |     actual: state.json,
 481 |   });
 482 | 
 483 |   assertCheck(state.json?.chatMessages?.length > 0, "Chat messages are persisted", {
 484 |     route: "GET /api/state",
 485 |     expected: "chatMessages.length > 0 after chat",
 486 |     actual: state.json,
 487 |     suggestion:
 488 |       "In chat route, save messages to store.chatMessages and set store.lastChatDatasetId/currentDatasetId to the chatted dataset id.",
 489 |   });
 490 | 
 491 |   const hasUser = state.json?.chatMessages?.some((message) => message.role === "user");
 492 |   const hasAssistant = state.json?.chatMessages?.some((message) => message.role === "assistant");
 493 | 
 494 |   assertCheck(Boolean(hasUser), "Persisted messages include user message", {
 495 |     route: "GET /api/state",
 496 |     expected: "at least one user message",
 497 |     actual: state.json?.chatMessages,
 498 |   });
 499 | 
 500 |   assertCheck(Boolean(hasAssistant), "Persisted messages include assistant message", {
 501 |     route: "GET /api/state",
 502 |     expected: "at least one assistant message",
 503 |     actual: state.json?.chatMessages,
 504 |   });
 505 | 
 506 |   const empty = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`, {
 507 |     query: "",
 508 |   });
 509 | 
 510 |   assertCheck(empty.status === 400, "Empty query returns 400", {
 511 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 512 |     expected: "400",
 513 |     actual: empty.status,
 514 |   });
 515 | }

 517 | async function testCache() {
 518 |   await section("7. CACHE ROUTES");
 519 | 
 520 |   const first = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`, {
 521 |     query: "count by department",
 522 |   });
 523 | 
 524 |   const second = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`, {
 525 |     query: "count by department",
 526 |   });
 527 | 
 528 |   assertCheck(first.status === 200, "First cacheable query returns 200", {
 529 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 530 |     expected: "200",
 531 |     actual: first.status,
 532 |   });
 533 | 
 534 |   assertCheck(second.status === 200, "Repeated cacheable query returns 200", {
 535 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/chat`,
 536 |     expected: "200",
 537 |     actual: second.status,
 538 |   });
 539 | 
 540 |   const globalStats = await request("GET", "/api/cache/stats");
 541 |   const globalCache = getCacheObject(globalStats.json);
 542 | 
 543 |   assertCheck(globalStats.status === 200, "GET /api/cache/stats returns 200", {
 544 |     route: "GET /api/cache/stats",
 545 |     expected: "200",
 546 |     actual: globalStats.status,
 547 |   });
 548 | 
 549 |   assertCheck(globalStats.json?.success === true, "Cache stats has success true", {
 550 |     route: "GET /api/cache/stats",
 551 |     expected: "success === true",
 552 |     actual: globalStats.json,
 553 |   });
 554 | 
 555 |   assertCheck(validateCacheObject(globalCache), "Cache stats has data object", {
 556 |     route: "GET /api/cache/stats",
 557 |     expected:
 558 |       "body.cache or body.data or body.stats with totalCached,totalHits,hitRate,savedAPICalls,estimatedCostSaved",
 559 |     actual: globalStats.json,
 560 |     suggestion:
 561 |       "Return { success: true, cache, data: cache, stats: cache } from /api/cache/stats.",
 562 |   });
 563 | 
 564 |   const datasetStats = await request("GET", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/stats`);
 565 |   const datasetCache = getCacheObject(datasetStats.json);
 566 | 
 567 |   assertCheck(datasetStats.status === 200, "Dataset cache stats returns 200", {
 568 |     route: `GET /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/stats`,
 569 |     expected: "200",
 570 |     actual: datasetStats.status,
 571 |     suggestion:
 572 |       "Add GET /api/datasets/:datasetId/cache/stats before final route fallback.",
 573 |   });
 574 | 
 575 |   assertCheck(datasetStats.json?.datasetId === importedDatasetId, "Dataset cache has datasetId", {
 576 |     route: `GET /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/stats`,
 577 |     expected: `datasetId === ${importedDatasetId}`,
 578 |     actual: datasetStats.json,
 579 |   });
 580 | 
 581 |   assertCheck(validateCacheObject(datasetCache), "Dataset cache has cache object", {
 582 |     route: `GET /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/stats`,
 583 |     expected: "cache/data/stats object with cache fields",
 584 |     actual: datasetStats.json,
 585 |   });
 586 | 
 587 |   const clear = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/clear`);
 588 | 
 589 |   assertCheck(clear.status === 200, "Clear cache returns 200", {
 590 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/clear`,
 591 |     expected: "200",
 592 |     actual: clear.status,
 593 |     suggestion:
 594 |       "Add POST /api/datasets/:datasetId/cache/clear that clears keys prefixed with datasetId.",
 595 |   });
 596 | 
 597 |   assertCheck(clear.json?.success === true, "Clear cache has success true", {
 598 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/clear`,
 599 |     expected: "success === true",
 600 |     actual: clear.json,
 601 |   });
 602 | 
 603 |   assertCheck(Boolean(clear.json?.message), "Clear cache has success message", {
 604 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/cache/clear`,
 605 |     expected: "body.message exists",
 606 |     actual: clear.json,
 607 |   });
 608 | }

 610 | async function testSchemaOnlyAiQuery() {
 611 |   await section("8. SCHEMA-ONLY AI QUERY");
 612 | 
 613 |   const valid = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/schema-ai-query", {
 614 |     schema: {
 615 |       columns: [
 616 |         { name: "department", type: "string", role: "dimension" },
 617 |         { name: "salary", type: "number", role: "metric" },
 618 |       ],
 619 |       rowCount: 3,
 620 |       columnCount: 2,
 621 |     },
 622 |     query: "average salary by department",
 623 |   });
 624 | 
 625 |   assertCheck(valid.status === 200, "Schema AI query returns 200", {
 626 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 627 |     expected: "200",
 628 |     actual: valid.status,
 629 |   });
 630 | 
 631 |   assertCheck(valid.json?.success === true, "Schema AI query has success true", {
 632 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 633 |     expected: "success === true",
 634 |     actual: valid.json,
 635 |   });
 636 | 
 637 |   assertCheck(Boolean(valid.json?.sql), "Schema AI query has SQL", {
 638 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 639 |     expected: "body.sql exists",
 640 |     actual: valid.json,
 641 |   });
 642 | 
 643 |   assertCheck(Boolean(valid.json?.insight), "Schema AI query has insight", {
 644 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 645 |     expected: "body.insight exists",
 646 |     actual: valid.json,
 647 |   });
 648 | 
 649 |   assertCheck(Boolean(valid.json?.explanation), "Schema AI query has explanation", {
 650 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 651 |     expected: "body.explanation exists",
 652 |     actual: valid.json,
 653 |   });
 654 | 
 655 |   const responseText = safeJsonString(valid.json);
 656 | 
 657 |   assertCheck(!responseText.includes('"rows"'), "Schema AI response does not include raw rows", {
 658 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 659 |     expected: "response must not contain rows",
 660 |     actual: valid.json,
 661 |   });
 662 | 
 663 |   const missingSchema = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/schema-ai-query", {
 664 |     query: "average salary by department",
 665 |   });
 666 | 
 667 |   assertCheck(missingSchema.status === 400, "Missing schema returns 400", {
 668 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 669 |     expected: "400",
 670 |     actual: missingSchema.status,
 671 |   });
 672 | 
 673 |   assertCheck(hasErrorMessage(missingSchema.json), "Missing schema has JSON error", {
 674 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 675 |     expected: "JSON error/message",
 676 |     actual: missingSchema.json,
 677 |   });
 678 | 
 679 |   const missingQuery = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/schema-ai-query", {
 680 |     schema: { columns: [] },
 681 |   });
 682 | 
 683 |   assertCheck(missingQuery.status === 400, "Missing query returns 400", {
 684 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 685 |     expected: "400",
 686 |     actual: missingQuery.status,
 687 |   });
 688 | 
 689 |   assertCheck(hasErrorMessage(missingQuery.json), "Missing query has JSON error", {
 690 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/schema-ai-query",
 691 |     expected: "JSON error/message",
 692 |     actual: missingQuery.json,
 693 |   });
 694 | }

 696 | async function testMlRoutes() {
 697 |   await section("9. ML COMPATIBILITY ENDPOINTS");
 698 | 
 699 |   const health = await request("GET", "/api/ml/health");
 700 | 
 701 |   assertCheck(health.status === 200, "ML health endpoint exists", {
 702 |     route: "GET /api/ml/health",
 703 |     expected: "200",
 704 |     actual: health.status,
 705 |   });
 706 | 
 707 |   assertCheck(health.json?.success === true && Boolean(health.json?.status), "ML health has success/status", {
 708 |     route: "GET /api/ml/health",
 709 |     expected: "success true and status exists",
 710 |     actual: health.json,
 711 |   });
 712 | 
 713 |   const models = await request("GET", "/api/ml/models/list");
 714 | 
 715 |   assertCheck(models.status === 200, "ML models list endpoint exists", {
 716 |     route: "GET /api/ml/models/list",
 717 |     expected: "200",
 718 |     actual: models.status,
 719 |   });
 720 | 
 721 |   assertCheck(models.json?.success === true && Array.isArray(models.json?.models), "ML models list has models array", {
 722 |     route: "GET /api/ml/models/list",
 723 |     expected: "success true and models array",
 724 |     actual: models.json,
 725 |   });
 726 | 
 727 |   const train = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/train`, {
 728 |     targetColumn: "salary",
 729 |     problemType: "regression",
 730 |   });
 731 | 
 732 |   assertCheck(train.status === 200, "ML train endpoint exists", {
 733 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/train`,
 734 |     expected: "200",
 735 |     actual: train.status,
 736 |     suggestion:
 737 |       "Add POST /api/datasets/:datasetId/ml/train and return {success:true,datasetId,model/modelId}.",
 738 |   });
 739 | 
 740 |   assertCheck(train.json?.success === true, "ML train has success true", {
 741 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/train`,
 742 |     expected: "success === true",
 743 |     actual: train.json,
 744 |   });
 745 | 
 746 |   assertCheck(Boolean(train.json?.model || train.json?.modelId), "ML train returns model or modelId", {
 747 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/train`,
 748 |     expected: "body.model or body.modelId exists",
 749 |     actual: train.json,
 750 |   });
 751 | 
 752 |   assertCheck(train.json?.datasetId === importedDatasetId, "ML train returns datasetId", {
 753 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/train`,
 754 |     expected: `datasetId === ${importedDatasetId}`,
 755 |     actual: train.json,
 756 |   });
 757 | 
 758 |   const predict = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`, {
 759 |     inputData: {
 760 |       department: "Engineering",
 761 |       experience: 4,
 762 |     },
 763 |   });
 764 | 
 765 |   assertCheck(predict.status === 200, "ML predict endpoint exists", {
 766 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`,
 767 |     expected: "200",
 768 |     actual: predict.status,
 769 |     suggestion:
 770 |       "Add POST /api/datasets/:datasetId/ml/predict and return {success:true,predictions/prediction}.",
 771 |   });
 772 | 
 773 |   assertCheck(predict.json?.success === true, "ML predict has success true", {
 774 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`,
 775 |     expected: "success === true",
 776 |     actual: predict.json,
 777 |   });
 778 | 
 779 |   assertCheck(Boolean(predict.json?.predictions || predict.json?.prediction !== undefined), "ML predict returns prediction", {
 780 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`,
 781 |     expected: "body.predictions or body.prediction exists",
 782 |     actual: predict.json,
 783 |   });
 784 | 
 785 |   const badPredict = await request("POST", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`, {});
 786 | 
 787 |   assertCheck(badPredict.status === 400, "ML predict without data returns 400", {
 788 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`,
 789 |     expected: "400",
 790 |     actual: badPredict.status,
 791 |   });
 792 | 
 793 |   assertCheck(hasErrorMessage(badPredict.json), "ML predict without data has JSON error", {
 794 |     route: `POST /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/predict`,
 795 |     expected: "JSON error/message",
 796 |     actual: badPredict.json,
 797 |   });
 798 | 
 799 |   const importance = await request("GET", `/api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/feature-importance`);
 800 | 
 801 |   assertCheck(importance.status === 200, "ML feature importance endpoint exists", {
 802 |     route: `GET /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/feature-importance`,
 803 |     expected: "200",
 804 |     actual: importance.status,
 805 |     suggestion:
 806 |       "Add GET /api/datasets/:datasetId/ml/feature-importance and return {success:true,featureImportance/features}.",
 807 |   });
 808 | 
 809 |   assertCheck(importance.json?.success === true, "ML feature importance has success true", {
 810 |     route: `GET /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/feature-importance`,
 811 |     expected: "success === true",
 812 |     actual: importance.json,
 813 |   });
 814 | 
 815 |   assertCheck(Boolean(importance.json?.featureImportance || importance.json?.features), "ML feature importance returns data", {
 816 |     route: `GET /api/datasets/${importedDatasetId}/ml/feature-importance`,
 817 |     expected: "body.featureImportance or body.features exists",
 818 |     actual: importance.json,
 819 |   });
 820 | }

 871 | async function testErrorHandling() {
 872 |   await section("11. ERROR HANDLING & OPTIONS");
 873 | 
 874 |   const notFound = await request("GET", "/api/nonexistent");
 875 | 
 876 |   assertCheck(notFound.status === 404, "Non-existent route returns 404", {
 877 |     route: "GET /api/nonexistent",
 878 |     expected: "404",
 879 |     actual: notFound.status,
 880 |   });
 881 | 
 882 |   assertCheck(hasErrorMessage(notFound.json), "404 has JSON error message", {
 883 |     route: "GET /api/nonexistent",
 884 |     expected: "JSON error/message",
 885 |     actual: notFound.text,
 886 |   });
 887 | 
 888 |   const options = await request("OPTIONS", "/api/health");
 889 | 
 890 |   assertCheck(options.status === 204, "OPTIONS returns 204", {
 891 |     route: "OPTIONS /api/health",
 892 |     expected: "204",
 893 |     actual: options.status,
 894 |   });
 895 | 
 896 |   const allowOrigin = options.headers.get("access-control-allow-origin");
 897 |   const allowMethods = options.headers.get("access-control-allow-methods");
 898 | 
 899 |   assertCheck(Boolean(allowOrigin), "OPTIONS has CORS origin header", {
 900 |     route: "OPTIONS /api/health",
 901 |     expected: "Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists",
 902 |     actual: Object.fromEntries(options.headers.entries()),
 903 |   });
 904 | 
 905 |   assertCheck(Boolean(allowMethods), "OPTIONS has CORS methods header", {
 906 |     route: "OPTIONS /api/health",
 907 |     expected: "Access-Control-Allow-Methods exists",
 908 |     actual: Object.fromEntries(options.headers.entries()),
 909 |   });
 910 | 
 911 |   const fakeChat = await request("POST", "/api/datasets/fake-id/chat", {
 912 |     query: "hello",
 913 |   });
 914 | 
 915 |   assertCheck(fakeChat.status === 404, "Chat on non-existent dataset returns 404", {
 916 |     route: "POST /api/datasets/fake-id/chat",
 917 |     expected: "404",
 918 |     actual: fakeChat.status,
 919 |   });
 920 | 
 921 |   const fakeCorr = await request("GET", "/api/datasets/fake-id/ai-correlations");
 922 | 
 923 |   assertCheck(fakeCorr.status === 404, "Correlations on non-existent dataset returns 404", {
 924 |     route: "GET /api/datasets/fake-id/ai-correlations",
 925 |     expected: "404",
 926 |     actual: fakeCorr.status,
 927 |   });
 928 | 
 929 |   const fakeProfile = await request("GET", "/api/datasets/fake-id/ai/profile");
 930 | 
 931 |   assertCheck(fakeProfile.status === 404, "AI profile on non-existent dataset returns 404", {
 932 |     route: "GET /api/datasets/fake-id/ai/profile",
 933 |     expected: "404",
 934 |     actual: fakeProfile.status,
 935 |   });
 936 | }
